'Mother' takes Sakiko's hand and presses their palms together, remarking that the hand that was less than half the size of her own at birth has, before she knew it, grown bigger than hers. She fondly brings up the photo the three of them took together on high school entrance day, too.
To the hesitant Sakiko, 'Mother' quietly confesses the truth — that she isn't the real mother, and that Sakiko must have already known this all along.

blocks.cap: "Sweetheart, Mommy's been lying to you."

blocks.x: 'Mother' apologizes for not having been there through Sakiko's hardest times. And she says she wants her daughter to go back to her real life, rather than stay here.
I want you to leave this place and find your real life. Go, Sakiko. Go back to your 'world.'
Tears streaming down her face, Sakiko takes a step forward once more — not to run away this time, but to go get her bandmates.
11. The Dolls Awaken — Masquerade, Once More
Back before Morpheus, Sakiko now understands: what her bandmates truly wanted wasn't perfect happiness, but a single person who would see them exactly as they are.

Giving Nyamu back the courage to pick up her sticks again, giving Umiri a reason to lay down her weapon, giving Uika the voice that had missed her, and giving Mutsumi back her own true name, Sakiko unties the knots one by one.

blocks.cap: Fingertips gliding over the keys, and one by one, the string-bound dolls come loose.

blocks.x: We're one band... yes, a community bound by fate. That's my answer.
To Sakiko's playing, Nyamu and Umiri each cut their own knots within their dreams. And so, all four of them finally open their eyes on the same stage.
Epilogue. A Dream to Be Forgotten, A Promise That Still Remains

The joy of reunion lasts only a moment before an enraged Morpheus makes one last desperate stand. The one who saves Sakiko, nearly losing consciousness, is Iris — the castle's true owner, arriving at last.

Iris reveals that Morpheus isn't actually this castle's true owner, and that she's a child who, wounded so deeply, can no longer even believe in fairy-tale dreams herself. She explains that memories of a childhood spent abandoned by her parents and pointed at as a monster are what kept Morpheus confined only within the perfect dream she made for herself.

blocks.cap: The last note rings through the empty castle, and a familiar voice calls out to Sakiko.

blocks.x: Iris promises to ask the Doctor at Rhodes Island about a way for the five of them to return to their original world — though she adds that from the moment they wake, memories of this dream will slowly begin to fade.

That night, the five of them play together one last time on a stage newly built inside the castle. Morpheus grumbles as she carries equipment, Wolf leaps all the way up to the ceiling in beast form to hang the lights, and beside them, the Carlton pack has a grand time too.

blocks.cap: Under the moonlight, Ave Mujica's stage is complete once more.

blocks.x: This is the mysterious tale of our dolls' adventure. Welcome to the world of Ave Mujica.
The crosswalk light Sakiko had been waiting for that day finally turned green again. Even if it's a dream destined to be forgotten, the time they spent together was real for all five of them.

act44side.tagline: An oni comes home after twenty years only to get mistaken for a ghost — and finds out the haunted-house rumors about her own childhood home, and the whole city's gossip mill, were all a real-estate scheme in disguise. Plus, one small firework relit over the ruins.

chars.name: Hoshiguma
chars.desc: An oni shield-warrior who returns to her hometown of Mitsukue after 20 years away. In truth, she's a Lungmen Guard Department officer.

chars.name: Momoka
chars.desc: A beloved national idol and actress known for having ghost stories follow her around. She shot her first film in Kajimachi. · Operator: Haruka

chars.name: Tessai
chars.desc: The aging boss of Kinsekigai who built up Kajimachi, and Hoshiguma's adoptive father. He's also a weapons craftsman.

chars.name: Mifune
chars.desc: The true power behind Kinsekigai and owner of Kinseki Broadcasting. His old nickname was 'Kinbei.'

chars.name: Mio
chars.desc: Mifune's tight-lipped secretary. She wields a blade as if she had three hands.

chars.name: Moriuchi
chars.desc: The owner of an oden food stall on Nita Street, and an information broker who knows every urban legend in town. Speaks in a thick accent.

chars.name: Kichisei
chars.desc: A young man who runs a general store and pachinko parlor in Kajimachi. A die-hard Momoka fan. · Operator: Kichisei

chars.name: Tetsuya
chars.desc: Tessai's son, who always wears a mask. He follows Mifune and hunts down Hokuin spies.

chars.name: Detective Koreto
chars.desc: The Mitsukue police officer who interrogates Hoshiguma. He's the nephew of a council candidate who was murdered 20 years ago.

chars.name: The Curious Deity
chars.desc: A bored deity who rides around on a unicycle. The benefactor Moriuchi met as a child.

terms.name: Kinsekigai
terms.desc: An old yakuza organization based in Kajimachi, in Mitsukue. Tessai was its first boss.

terms.name: Hannya
terms.desc: A large triangular shield forged by Tessai. Hoshiguma's beloved weapon, which she's carried for 20 years.

terms.name: Aooni
terms.desc: Mitsukue's nickname for the 'one-horned oni,' and the name of the murderous demon in the city's ghost stories. In truth, it's Hoshiguma.

terms.name: Kajimachi
terms.desc: The old district where Kinsekigai's people used to live. It's on the verge of vanishing, caught up in a redevelopment plan.

terms.name: Wanyudo
terms.desc: An urban legend about something invisible riding around on a unicycle. In truth, it's a deity who likes watching people.

terms.name: Kinseki Broadcasting
terms.desc: Mitsukue's largest broadcasting station, owned by Mifune. The epicenter that manufactures ghost stories and public opinion alike.

blocks.x: Twenty years ago, there was an oni woman who left Mitsukue. People called her 'the one-horned oni,' or 'Aooni' for short. Rumor even had it that she hunted down and killed people who'd wronged someone, night after night.

In reality, she'd spent that whole time quietly working as a Guard Department officer in Lungmen. Then one day, a letter arrives saying her adoptive father Tessai is holding a funeral for Kajimachi — a chance to catch up on old times before redevelopment wipes it off the map.

So on the first night of her return after 20 years, the very first thing Hoshiguma did was crash a concert and scare an idol half to death. Her hometown, it turns out, had a very unexpected welcome waiting for her.
1. Homecoming Night One: The Original Oni Gets Mistaken for a Ghost

That night, Kajimachi-born actress and singer **Momoka** was in the middle of a concert celebrating *The Birth of the Demon*, the film that gave her her first leading role. Of all things, the film's subject was the 'one-horned oni' legend.
Just as the crowd waited for an encore, a real one-horned oni appeared backstage. Momoka immediately pulled out roasted beans and threw them, shouting.
D-demon, th-th-this is roasted beans! C-c-come any closer and I'll throw them! Demons out, fortune in!
In truth, the 'demon' was just a traveler quietly trying to get home. That oni was none other than Hoshiguma — she'd only ended up shoved onto the stage while trying to dodge the police chasing her.
After that night, Momoka became the talk of the whole city as 'the celebrity who witnessed a real ghost story.'
2. I'm Home... But Nobody Seems Happy About It
Hoshiguma spent the whole night held at the police station, then wolfed down a Higashi-style katsudon that Detective **Koreto** brought her — the first taste of home cooking she'd had in 20 years.

blocks.cap: She'd also had katsudon right before leaving Lungmen, but that didn't matter one bit.

blocks.x: Once released, she headed back to Kajimachi, only to find no familiar faces waiting — just **Mifune**, once one of Tessai's men, throwing his weight around by beheading one of his own subordinates. When Tessai stepped in to scold him, Mifune cut off one of his own fingers on the spot and offered it up, saying, "This should settle it, shouldn't it?" Tessai later put it bluntly.
"Did you think cutting off a finger wasn't just theater? That was an insult."

As it turned out, the funeral letter hadn't come from Tessai at all — Mifune had forged it. Tessai, looking none too pleased, said, "Twenty years ago, we agreed that once we parted ways, there'd be no more contact. I never once thought of breaking that promise." Hoshiguma's old home had already been sitting empty as a haunted house for ten years.

Out on the street, a masked young man named **Tetsuya** picks a fight with her, wooden sword in hand. Turns out he's the son Tessai had after remarrying — Hoshiguma's half-brother. He's subdued in a single joint-lock before they've even traded more than a few blows, though.
Th-this is... some kind of *mutodori*?!
Hoshiguma's deadpan reply follows: "Lay off the manga already — this is basic stuff, it's called qinna."
3. Cleaning the Haunted House, Mistaken for a Ghost Again
Meanwhile, under the pretext of cleaning up last night's mess, Momoka's agency forces her into filming an 'Oni House Exploration' segment — at none other than Hoshiguma's old home.

Hoshiguma, tidying up the house in the dark, runs into Momoka mid-shoot, and Momoka screams and bolts again. In the chaos, she quietly picks up a broken sword lying on the floor and takes it with her.

The trouble starts that night, when the TV in Momoka's room switches on by itself, playing a conversation between Mifune and his secretary **Mio**: "Momoka picked up that broken sword. Get rid of her, quietly." Right up until the moment Mio opened the door and walked in, Momoka had no idea they were talking about her.

blocks.cap: She only repeated "I'll help you, Momoka" three times, expressionless — but in an instant, a familiar face became the scariest one in the room.

blocks.x: 4. Oni, Idol, Info Broker, and a Self-Proclaimed God — the World's Most Mismatched Party

Hoshiguma ends up protecting the fleeing Momoka almost by accident, and the two of them team up with **Moriuchi**, the owner of an oden stall on Nita Street. Moriuchi turned out to be an information broker who'd spent 20 years searching for the deity that saved his family when he was a child.

The clue was 'Wanyudo,' the source of Mitsukue's ghost stories — a unicycle supposedly ridden by something invisible. Chasing it all the way to a shrine, they finally caught it — and found it was just a bored, unenthusiastic deity who'd been chasing other people's ghost stories for fun.
Ever since you disappeared, ghost stories have been floating around Mitsukue. It's not like it's my fault for chasing them around out of boredom, is it?

blocks.cap: A single helmet floated in the shrine pond — the price the oni and the idol paid for tumbling into the water together while trying to catch it.

blocks.x: He had no idea, of course, that he himself was responsible for eighty percent of the ghost stories. The deity Moriuchi had actually been looking for was someone else, and reuniting with them after all this time, that deity offered up an unexpected line: "Wasn't money supposed to be happiness? Take Kinbei's seat and you can have everything you want." After a long pause, Moriuchi answered.
I need a friend too. A real one.
5. Turns Out It Was All About Real Estate

Once the scattered clues came together, the full picture emerged. The council candidate's murder 20 years ago, the haunted-house legend, and the recent wave of public outrage — all of it was a scheme Mifune had orchestrated to seize the land under Kajimachi.
The ghost stories Momoka herself had experienced were no exception. The moment she realizes every 'haunting' on the shows she'd appeared in was staged by Mifune, Momoka completely falls apart.
The ghost stories I believed in... were all fake? Then was Hanyuu Momoka nothing but... bait my agency used to cover for Kinsekigai?
Even 'the Aooni's mansion,' the story that kicked off the whole haunted-house panic, wasn't about any oni or vengeful spirit at all — it was just a tale invented for a real-estate development plan.

While she was at it, Momoka also lets out the real name she'd kept hidden all this time. Her real name was 'Shino Haruka,' and she was from Kogawa, a declining southern city notorious for its water pollution.
Her parents died of illness when she was young, and her agency deliberately built a 'northern' image into her stage name to hide where she really came from.
I can only ever be an entertainer — I can only ever be Hanyuu Momoka. Even if my agency abandons me and Kinbei kills me, I'm still Hanyuu Momoka.
6. The Quietest, Heaviest Verse of All

After a ninja ambush, Tessai's son **Tetsuya** finally learns the truth — that his father Tessai abandoned his mother 20 years ago, and that he himself has just been a tool for Mifune, the man he'd been following all this time.

Mifune waved a photo of Tetsuya in front of Tessai and threatened him — if he didn't put his thumbprint on the deed to the Kajimachi land, Tetsuya would learn the truth. Tessai gave in and pressed his thumbprint on it, but Mifune never kept his end of the bargain.
Listening to his son's cry of anguish through a camera feed, Tessai is stabbed by Mifune's blade. By the time Hoshiguma came running, it was already too late.

blocks.cap: To the very last moment, Tessai heard the sound of a caterpillar tread crushing through the mansion wall as the sound of fireworks at a festival.

blocks.x: Kajimachi is... bustling again, after all this time.
7. Drinking with the Dead, and a Little Martial-Law Cosplay

Hoshiguma pours herself a drink, alone, and empties Tessai's share too. Detective Koreto comes to try to talk her into going back to Lungmen, but instead, Hoshiguma wraps her sword hilt in bandages given to her by old friends. "As long as this bandage is on my hand, I won't go berserk."

Infiltrating the broadcasting station to rescue the captive Momoka and Kichisei gets an unexpected assist — **Sarasa**, a mysterious figure inside the station who'd secretly appeared before the imprisoned Momoka and hacked Mifune's surveillance network. Sarasa sets off a fake 'Hokuin invasion' alert across the whole city, throwing all of Mitsukue into chaos.

blocks.cap: "This is an emergency broadcast... this is not a drill." In truth, it was the work of a hacker who'd hijacked the station's servers.

blocks.x: Storming into the station, Hoshiguma pushes through the gang members, shouting, "Demons out, fortune in? No — back in my day, we shouted it the other way around. Fortune out, demons in." The beans that were once scattered to drive her away, twenty years ago, now scatter on her side.

Meanwhile, Detective Koreto, holding down the fort at the police station, comes under pressure too — an order from above telling him to submit his resignation over today's chaos. The detective tears up the paper and answers.
"What the police protect is that man's disgusting little life — not his filthy money."
8. The Queen of Ghost Stories Quits, Live on Air

Sarasa hijacks the live feed of the inauguration ceremony, and Momoka takes the stage, broadcast across all of Mitsukue. She lays out, in full, that every ghost story she'd ever experienced was staged, and that Mifune was behind it all.

blocks.cap: "I'm going to give everyone hope, and let people going through hard times know... that there's someone out there who feels the same way they do."

blocks.x: Even if it's a life built on lies... it's still our life. I'm going to live the life I love.
The moment she finished speaking, she fainted right there on stage. A coward, it turns out, is still a coward.

Watching all of this play out on the broadcast, Mifune's face turns pale white. Tetsuya, trying to avenge his father, fails — but when he turns his back and walks away, Mifune doesn't bother killing him. "I've got more people to kill than just him."
9. Three Blades vs. Hannya, and a Farewell 20 Years in the Making

While mopping up the station, Hoshiguma clashes with Mio. Even against Mio's three-handed swordplay, Hannya doesn't waver. "It's either hold it back, or go berserk — isn't that how it works?" Mio asks, and Hoshiguma answers.
Sorry, but I don't go berserk. Not as long as I have Hannya. Though, I wouldn't go berserk without it either.

Facing her on the riverbank, before the fight, Mifune goes on at length about how much he hated Tessai. "What I'm talking about isn't chivalry, and it isn't loyalty to the code. What I'm talking about is beauty." It was his own twisted justification for not hesitating to fabricate haunted-house legends and manipulate public opinion, all to keep the organization alive.
You'll have to admit that a wasteland, a riverbank, fireworks, blood spraying in every direction, and a head flying high into the air — that's beauty.
The duel ended quickly. Hoshiguma won, but she didn't bother taking his head.
Instead, she chose to cut the 20-year-old bond herself, with her own hands. When Mifune asked what she'd tell people about his death, Hoshiguma answered.
Tell them it was an oni. An oni who lost her home forever.
On the riverbank as the fireworks festival winds down, Momoka secretly hands over a small firework to Hoshiguma — who'd missed out on both the festival and, in a way, the duel.

blocks.cap: "I guess I can't show you a pretty fireworks display. So don't be too upset about it." That one small firework shone brighter than the whole city.

blocks.x: Epilogue: After That Summer Night

Back in Lungmen, Hoshiguma goes on living with Swire and Lin Wisia as if nothing happened. The two of them are still bickering over a single bowl of oden. Lady Fumizuki, who's come out to join them for a bike race, says.
"It just means Mitsukue is still dreaming, while Lungmen has already woken up."

The oden stall in Kajimachi is as noisy and lively as ever. Momoka is preparing to head for Rhodes Island, and it comes to light that Sarasa was actually the young lady of the Nishigori, one of Hokuin's four great families.

Mio decides to shoulder the job of cleaning up after Kinsekigai all on her own. Tetsuya, who used to go around with his face covered, leaves without a word, and all that's left of him is a rumor that a nameless wandering swordsman took down a gang of con artists in a neighboring city.

The deity Moriuchi reunited with at the shrine leaves behind just one line for the corrupt council member who tried to use them: "That's got nothing to do with me." In the end, not a single person in this city took those words to heart. Only Moriuchi, letting go of a single keychain, proved for himself that he'd chosen people over money.

Mitsukue met Hoshiguma again by mistaking her for a ghost, and she left Kajimachi behind for good, along with twenty years of old grudges. What she carried out of her ruined hometown wasn't revenge or tears, but a single firework handed to her on a riverbank, and a handful of new friends. Maybe that, in the end, was a far more human ending than any ghost story could give her.

act43side.tagline: 촬영장 연쇄살인의 진범이 사실은 죽은 언니를 기억하려던 막내 소품 스태프였던 이야기. 그리고 그 뒤에서 대본을 고쳐 쓰던 진짜 범인은, 단 한 번도 죽어본 적 없는 어떤 신이었습니다.

chars.name: 루시안
chars.desc: 제작팀에 갓 합류한 신인 배우. '전달자' 역을 맡았지만, 정작 관심은 다른 곳에 있어 보인다 · 오퍼레이터 팬텀

chars.name: 미스 크리스틴
chars.desc: 정체불명의 두 꼬리 검은 생물. 말은 못 해도 표정만큼은 또렷하고, 루시안 곁을 떠나지 않는다

chars.name: 티피
chars.desc: 소품팀 인턴 겸 배경화가, 자칭 공포영화 마니아. 모이라의 가장 친한 친구

chars.name: 모이라
chars.desc: 낯선 개척지 출신의 소품팀 스태프. 있는 듯 없는 듯 조용하지만 손끝은 야무지다

chars.name: 그레타
chars.desc: 20년 차 제작자 그레타 스톤. 자신의 안목이 틀린 적은 없다고 믿는다

chars.name: 멜라니
chars.desc: 《미스 듀폰의 죽음》 주연 겸 투자자. 현장 규칙과 배우의 격식에 유난히 엄격하다

chars.name: 애브너
chars.desc: 왕년의 랭크우드 최우수 촬영상 수상자, 지금은 매사에 심드렁한 카메라맨

chars.name: 셸리
chars.desc: 촬영 스케줄을 총괄하는 코디네이터. 요즘 부쩍 신경이 곤두서 있다

chars.name: 스티븐
chars.desc: 이 영화의 감독 겸 원작자. 두 달째 결말을 정하지 못했다

chars.name: 샬렘
chars.desc: 루시안의 오랜 벗. 극단 시절 이름은 '침연'이었다 · 오퍼레이터 샬렘

terms.name: 랭크우드
terms.desc: 컬럼비아의 영화 산업 이동도시. 영화 한 편을 위해 도심부 통째로 촬영용 섹터를 떼어내 이동시킬 만큼, 테라에서 오락 산업이 가장 발달한 곳이다.

terms.name: 이동 섹터
terms.desc: 랭크우드의 촬영 전용 구역. 도심부에서 분리해 원하는 곳으로 옮길 수 있지만, 대여 기한이 끝나면 자동으로 회항한다.

terms.name: 시나리오 경매
terms.desc: 완성되지 않은 시나리오나 아이디어, 판권만 취급하는 랭크우드 특유의 경매. 흥행 여부는 진위 감정 없이 순전히 낙찰자의 안목에 달렸다.

terms.name: 오리지늄 아츠
terms.desc: 오염 물질 오리지늄을 다루는 초자연적 힘. 감정을 물질에 옮겨 담거나, 반대로 물질을 통해 감정을 전달하는 데도 쓰인다.

terms.name: 크림슨 극단
terms.desc: 루시안이 어릴 적 몸담았던 유랑 극단. 무대 위에서 실제로 사람을 해치는 연기를 가르쳤다.

terms.name: 재앙
terms.desc: 오리지늄이 폭주해 일어나는 광역 재해. 랭크우드에서는 이 재앙을 카메라에 담겠다며 목숨을 거는 제작자도 있다.

blocks.x: 컬럼비아의 이동도시 랭크우드는 테라 전역에 꿈을 파는 동네예요. 영화 한 편 찍겠다고 도심부를 통째로 떼어내 이동시킬 정도로, 오락 산업 하나는 확실하게 발달했죠.
그런 랭크우드에서 두 달째 《미스 듀폰의 죽음》을 찍고 있는 제작팀이 있어요. 몰락한 귀족 여인을 삼 형제가 나눠서 구애하는, 어디서 많이 본 치정극이었죠.
그런데 리허설 첫날 밤, 소품용 칼이 진짜 칼로 둔갑해 있었습니다. 그 칼을 쥔 사람은, 시나리오 경매장에서 우연히 캐스팅된 신인 배우 한 명이었어요.
1. 리허설 첫날, 소품 칼이 진짜였습니다
감독 스티븐은 두 달 내내 강행군에 지친 제작팀을 다독이며 리허설을 이어가요. 대사는 안 맞고 다들 예민한데, 그래도 영화는 찍어야 하니까요.
그때 정체불명의 검은 생물 한 마리가 촬영장에 난입해요. 꼬리가 두 개 달린 이 녀석, 소품팀 인턴 **티피**는 '어젯밤 본 공포영화 괴물보다 신기하게 생겼다'며 신나 하죠.
제작자 **그레타**는 이 생물의 정체를 알고 있어요. 자신이 초대한 손님의 '동행'이라면서, 이번 리허설엔 그 손님을 대신 세우겠다고 하죠. 이름은 **루시안**.
루시안은 '전달자' 역을 맡아요. 귀족 저택에 흉흉한 진실을 전하러 온 배역이었죠. 상대역인 노배우 **달튼**은 소품팀이 방금 겨우 구해온 칼을 손에 쥐고 있었고요.
레디, 액션 — 대사가 오가고, 칼이 손에서 손으로 넘어갑니다. 다들 명연기라며 감탄했어요. 그 칼이 진짜라는 걸, 아무도 몰랐죠.

blocks.cap: 감독의 컷 사인이 떨어지기 직전, 소품 칼은 진짜 칼이 되어 상대의 가슴을 파고들었습니다.

blocks.x: 컷 사인이 떨어지고도 달튼은 일어나지 않았어요. 코디네이터 **셸리**가 다가가 핏자국을 만졌을 때, 그건 소품용 피 주머니가 아니라 진짜 사람의 피였습니다.

blocks.cap: 《미스 듀폰의 죽음》 첫 촬영분은, 정말로 사람이 죽는 장면이 되어버렸습니다.

blocks.x: 2. 승진 첫날 밤, 야근 대신 공포영화
그레타는 담담하게 정리해요. 달튼의 죽음은 사고, 소품팀장은 즉시 해고. 그리고 그 빈자리는, 있는 듯 없는 듯 조용히 일하던 소품 스태프 **모이라**가 맡게 됩니다.
모이라는 개척지 출신 자라크예요. 랭크우드에 아는 사람 하나 없이 올라와, 그저 생계를 위해 소품을 만들던 사람이었죠. 갑작스러운 승진에 제일 놀란 건 본인이었고요.
셸리는 그날 이후로 이상해져요. 의자 끄는 소리 하나에도 소스라치고, 팔에는 자해 흔적이 늘어가죠. 5년 전 이 제작팀에서 있었던 추락사 사고 얘기를, 그녀는 자꾸 꺼내요.
그러던 중 촬영장에서 화물 상자가 셸리 머리 위로 떨어져요. 그녀를 구한 건 신인 배우 루시안, 그리고 반대편에서 밧줄을 필사적으로 붙든 소품팀장 모이라였습니다.
밤이 되면 셸리와 티피, 모이라는 소품실에 모여 공포영화를 봐요. 소리 지르고 나면 후련하다면서요. 티피는 모이라를 '몰리'라고 부르고, 몰리는 티피를 제일 친한 친구라고 부르죠.
밖에 살인마가 있다 해도, 친구로서 내가 널 지켜줄게!

blocks.cap: 제작팀에 사건 사고가 잦아도, 공포영화 앞에서만큼은 둘 다 그냥 스무 살이었어요.

blocks.x: 3. 냉동고 미스터리, 그런데 진짜 폭탄은 따로 있었다
그날 밤, 셸리는 낯선 발신자에게 메시지를 받고 소품실을 나서요. 발신자 이름은, 5년 전 이 제작팀에서 죽었다는 그 신인 배우의 이름이었습니다.
다음 날 아침, 셸리는 창고 냉동고 안에서 발견돼요. 동사였지만 손바닥엔 발버둥 친 흔적이 가득했죠. 루시안은 말해요, 그건 저주가 아니라 그냥 살려고 발버둥 친 흔적이라고.
촬영 보조는 신났어요. 연쇄살인마라니, 팔면 큰돈 되는 특종이잖아요. 그런데 사무실에서 주운 서류 조각을 맞춰보다가, 자기가 감당 못 할 폭탄을 하나 더 터뜨리고 말아요.
그레타가 재앙을 촬영하려고 섹터 항로를 몰래 재앙 쪽으로 맞춰뒀다는 것. 그것도 트리마운츠 대학의 재앙 예측 보고서까지 받아가면서요.
카메라맨 애브너는 분노해요. 5년 전에도 그레타는 배우의 죽음을 영화 클라이맥스로 써먹었으니까요. 하지만 그레타는 눈 하나 깜짝하지 않고, 모두를 불러 모아요.
우리에게 부족한 건 기술이 아니라, 배짱이라는 거야.
그러고는 그 자리에서 5년 전 진실을 스스로 밝혀요. 자신이 신인 시절 겪은 몰락과 사기, 이름을 바꿔 랭크우드로 돌아온 사연까지. 사과는 아니고, 그냥 통보였죠.
4. 사랑싸움 끝에, 나란히 목을 맵니다
그날 밤, 신인 시절부터 바람둥이로 유명했던 배우 **마이클**과, 5년째 그를 뒷바라지해온 스타일리스트 **마리온**이 분장실에서 크게 싸워요.
마이클은 '살인마가 무섭다'며 함께 도망치자고 하지만, 마리온은 단칼에 거절해요. 그녀가 정말 화난 이유는 따로 있었죠.
5년 전, 신인 배우가 와이어에 매달린 채 서서히 죽어갈 때, 그 자리를 끝까지 지킨 유일한 사람이 마이클이었거든요. 마리온은 그 사실을 나중에야 알았을 뿐이고요.
마이클은 그날, 낡은 와이어가 곧 끊어질 걸 눈치챘어요. 하지만 마음에 걸리는 여자에게 점수를 딸 기회다 싶어서, 일부러 못 본 척했습니다.
스스로 대가를 감당하기로 한 이상, 이제 나랑은 아무 상관 없겠지.
다음 날 아침, 현장 스태프 호틴은 어두운 스튜디오에서 물방울 떨어지는 소리를 들어요. 손전등을 켰을 때, 마이클과 마리온이 서로 등을 맞댄 채 매달려 있었습니다.

blocks.cap: 다툼도 언쟁도 없이, 두 사람은 그저 조용히 흔들리고 있었어요.

blocks.x: 나중에 밝혀진 사실 하나. 도끼는 원래 마리온을 노렸는데, 마이클이 그녀를 감싸다 대신 급소를 맞았대요. 죽기 직전에야, 둘은 서로의 진심을 확인한 셈이죠.
5. 신을 기다리는 감독, 그리고 다음 주인공
그레타는 남은 사람들의 잔여 보수를 전부 지급하고, 제작팀을 공식 해체해요. 떠나고 싶은 사람은 떠나고, 남고 싶은 사람은 남으라고요. 카메라맨 자리는 자신이 대신하겠다고 하죠.
감독 스티븐은 두 달째 결말을 못 정한 채, 원고지에 파묻혀 있어요. 그런데 그날 밤, 정체불명의 목소리 하나가 그에게 말을 걸어요. 스스로도 헛것인지 진짜인지 구분이 안 될 정도로.
그 목소리는 스티븐에게 속삭여요. 5년 전 그 배우의 죽음이 영화를 살렸던 것처럼, 지금 이 모든 죽음도 이야기의 자양분이 될 수 있다고. 다만 초점을 '전달자'에게 옮겨보라고요.
스티븐은 무릎을 쳐요. 그날부로 신인 배우 루시안이 결말의 주인공이 됩니다. 정작 스티븐은 자신에게 그 아이디어를 준 게 누구인지, 미처 묻지 못했죠.
한편 그레타도 같은 결론에 도착해 있었어요. 루시안을 찾아가, 이 이상한 사건들 속에서도 홀로 침착했던 이유는 묻지 않은 채, 그저 다음 주인공을 맡아 달라고 제안하죠.
루시안은 대답 대신 고개를 끄덕여요. 그는 이미 알고 있었거든요, 이 모든 우연이 결코 우연이 아니라는 걸.
6. '언니'는 처음부터 없었습니다
그날 밤, 루시안은 모이라의 방문을 두드려요. 이불 속엔 아무도 없고, 대신 낮은 그림자 하나가 침대에 누워 있었죠. 다름 아닌 미스 크리스틴, 함정이었던 겁니다.
그레타도 그 자리에 있었어요. 자신을 죽이러 온 사람이 모이라라는 걸 알고 충격받죠. 루시안은 담담히 설명해요, 처음 이상함을 눈치챈 건 미스 크리스틴이었다고.
미스 크리스틴은 감정을 '맛보는' 생물이에요. 억누른 분노, 순간의 살의 같은 복잡한 감정에 유독 끌리죠. 그녀가 유일하게 먼저 다가간 사람이 모이라였고요.
모이라는 부정하지 않아요. 소품용 칼을 바꿔치기한 것도, 화물 상자 밧줄을 붙든 척 셸리를 함정에 빠뜨린 것도, 전부 자기 짓이라고 인정하죠. 이유는 하나, 복수.
저는 이 대지에서 로라를 기억하는 마지막 사람이 될 거예요.
5년 전 죽은 그 신인 배우 이름이 로라였어요. 모이라는 자신이 로라의 동생이라고 말해요. 그런데 그레타가 조사한 로라의 가족 명단에는, '모이라'라는 이름이 없었습니다.
모이라는 낡은 비디오테이프를 꺼내 재생해요. 웃고 있는 로라의 얼굴이 나오죠. 그런데 아무리 떠올리려 해도, 둘이 함께 자란 기억이 어느 한 지점에서 뚝 끊겨 있었어요.

blocks.cap: 테이프가 끝나고, 화면에 비친 건 자신의 얼굴이었습니다. 모이라는 물었죠. 그럼 나는, 누구지?

blocks.x: 모이라는 랭크우드에서 쫓겨나 갈 곳을 잃었던 어느 밤, 다정한 필라인 하나를 만났던 기억을 떠올려요. 그가 떠난 자리엔 비디오테이프 한 편이 놓여 있었대요.

blocks.cap: 그 사람은 빛 한 줄기 없는 삶에, 언니라는 이름의 위로를 놓고 갔습니다.

blocks.x: 루시안은 그 필라인이 누구인지 알아요. 자신을 키운 스승이자, 이 모든 비극의 근원이었죠. 모이라는 웃으며 자신의 조종을 받는 마네킹들과 춤을 추기 시작해요.

blocks.cap: 죽은 달튼, 셸리, 마이클, 마리온의 형상을 두른 마네킹들과, 모이라는 세상에서 가장 슬픈 무도회를 엽니다.

blocks.x: 7. 재앙 속으로 걸어 들어간 사람들
모이라는 이미 섹터의 조종실을 폭파해 뒀어요. 이동 섹터는 방향도, 속도도 바꿀 수 없는 채로 재앙을 향해 질주하기 시작하죠.
티피는 예전에 봤던 공포영화 한 편을 떠올려요. 똑같은 구조의 섹터에서 촬영된 그 영화 속엔, 동력층 안쪽에 예비 항로 조정 장치가 있었거든요.
패닉에 빠진 스태프들이 앞을 막아서요. 살인범의 친구라며 티피를 몰아붙이지만, 그 순간 미스 크리스틴이 나타나 사람들의 극단적인 감정을 슬쩍 '먹어치우죠'.

blocks.cap: 다들 자기가 왜 무기를 들고 있었는지도 잊은 채, 제정신으로 돌아왔습니다.

blocks.x: 티피는 낡은 조종간을 끝까지 돌려요. 섹터가 통째로 비명을 지르며 방향을 틀죠. 성공할지, 시간이 얼마나 걸릴지도 모른 채, 그저 기도할 뿐이었어요.
한편 멜라니는 무너져가는 세트장 한복판으로 걸어 나가요. 만년의 라이벌 마리안을 넘어서지 못했던 세월, 그 모든 열등감을 폭풍 앞에서 벗어던지면서요.
누가 미스 듀폰이 죽는대? 그녀는 절대 죽지 않아. 이 폭풍이 그녀를 태우고 자유를 향해 달려갈 테니까!

blocks.cap: 촬영 기사도, 카메라도 없이. 그저 재앙 속에서 홀로 춤추는 여배우 하나가 있었습니다.

blocks.x: 애브너는 그런 멜라니 곁에 카메라를 든 채 남기로 해요. 5년째 미워했던 자신의 일을, 이번엔 스스로 선택해서요.
8. 스승 시해극, 그리고 이어지는 막
루시안은 무너진 저택 잔해 사이에서, 있어서는 안 될 사람의 형상을 봐요. 죽었던 달튼의 목소리로 말을 거는 그것에게, 루시안은 조용히 고개를 숙입니다.

blocks.cap: 루시안은 모자를 벗고, 오랜 스승에게 예를 갖췄습니다.

blocks.x: 루시안은 지난 몇 달간 테라 곳곳을 돌아다니며 모은 증거들을 하나씩 꺼내요. 가울의 배신자, 라테라노의 추기경, 이베리아의 실종된 선원…… 전부 같은 표식을 남긴 사건들이었죠.
그 표식의 주인은 전설 속 풍요와 축제의 신이 아니라, 죽음과 파멸을 예술이라 부르는 장생자였어요. 이름은 **트라고디아**. 루시안을 거둬 키운 크림슨 극단의 극단장, 바로 그 사람이었죠.
재앙으로 고향을 잃고 홀로 남았던 어린 루시안을 거둬준 것도, 무대에서 진짜 죽음을 연기하도록 가르친 것도 트라고디아였어요.

blocks.cap: "내가 극 중 등장인물일 때만, 나는 돌아갈 곳이 생겨." 어린 루시안은 그렇게 무대에 길들었습니다.

blocks.x: 루시안은 이미 한 번 그를 죽였어요. 그런데도 트라고디아는 살아남아, 이번엔 이 촬영장을 새로운 무대로 골랐던 겁니다.
트라고디아는 웃어요. 달튼도, 마이클과 마리온도, 모이라도, 전부 자기 안에 이미 있던 감정이 죽음 앞에서 터져 나왔을 뿐이라고. 그러니 죽음이야말로 만물의 귀속이라고.
루시안은 되묻습니다. 그 화려한 이론은 사실 신념이 아니라 자기합리화 아니냐고. 장생자인 그가 유일하게 도달할 수 없는 것, 그건 다름 아닌 죽음 그 자체 아니냐고.
폭풍이 두 사람 사이로 불어닥쳐요. 루시안은 목에 채운 오리지늄 결정 억제기를 풀어요. 노랫소리가 재앙을 뚫고 울려 퍼지죠.

blocks.cap: 고통은 나의 칼날이다. 나는 마침내 그 칼날을 내게 그것을 가르친 자에게 겨눴습니다.

blocks.x: 지팡이 끝이 트라고디아의 심장을 꿰뚫어요. 가면 수백 개가 부서져 떨어지고, 그가 서 있던 자리엔 핏자국과 가면만 남습니다.
그런데 트라고디아의 목소리는 사라지지 않아요. 방금 죽은 건 자신의 수많은 존재 중 하나일 뿐이라면서, 은빛 관과 지팡이를 루시안에게 유산으로 남기죠.
시해의 결말이 계승이 되어버린 셈이에요. 루시안은 아무 말도 하지 않은 채, 핏자국을 뒤로하고 계단을 오릅니다. 뒤돌아보지 않고요.
에필로그. 두 달 후, 이색 영화제에서
영화는 완성됐어요. 제목에서 '의 죽음' 세 글자는 지워졌고요. 스티븐도, 멜라니도, 재앙 속에서 자기 몫의 답을 찾은 뒤였으니까요.
티피는 랭크우드 촬영 섹터 복구팀에 뽑혀 바쁘게 지내요. 자신이 정말 좋아하는 일이 배경화라는 걸, 이번 사건을 통해 확실히 알게 됐다면서요.
은퇴한 전설적 배우 마리안, 지금은 야라라는 이름으로 사는 그녀도 상영회를 찾아와요. 오랜 라이벌 멜라니와 마침내 화해하듯 포옹을 나누죠.

blocks.cap: 재앙 속에서 완성된 영화는, 그날 스크린 위에서 다시 한번 폭풍이 되었습니다.

blocks.x: 연방 이동감옥에 있는 모이라도 티피의 면회를 받아요. 용서받진 못했지만, 적어도 더 이상 허상 속에 숨지는 않기로 했다면서요.
루시안은 상영관 밖에서 옛 벗 샬렘, 그리고 극단의 작가를 만나요. 크림슨 극단은 사라졌고, 이제 다들 자유라고 말하죠. 그런데 정작 루시안 자신은, 이 길에서 완전히 벗어나지 못한 눈치예요.
이 대지에 결코 배우가 부족한 적은 없다.

blocks.cap: 루시안은 새 지팡이를 쥔 채, 다음 무대를 향해 다시 걷기 시작했습니다.

blocks.x: 리허설 첫날 밤 진짜였던 소품 칼 한 자루가, 결국 이만큼 많은 사람의 삶을 갈랐어요. 그리고 그 끝에서, 배우 하나가 새로운 신을 자처하며 다시 여행을 떠났습니다.

act42side.tagline: The city vanishes on a new cardinal's first day at work, and it turns out the answer was to become a 'perfect Sankta.' To refuse that answer, all of Laterano takes up arms.

chars.name: Lemuen
chars.desc: The newly appointed Cardinal of the Seventh Ministry. Known by the nickname 'Lemuen the Silent.'

chars.name: Exusiai
chars.desc: A sniper for Penguin Logistics. A Sankta who loves explosions and desserts in equal measure.

chars.name: Andoain
chars.desc: A caregiver who has returned after long wandering outside Laterano. He seems anxious, as if being chased by something.

chars.name: Patrizione
chars.desc: An old gun-knight of Laterano, on the verge of retirement.

chars.name: Federico
chars.desc: A cold-blooded enforcer of the Notary Office. Known by the nickname 'Executor.'

chars.name: Arturia
chars.desc: A cellist imprisoned at Landen Monastery. Has a history of escaping.

chars.name: Mostima
chars.desc: The World Envoy. Has long-standing ties with Lemuen and Andoain.

chars.name: Fiammetta
chars.desc: Patrizione's granddaughter. Still clumsy with a gun.

chars.name: Vannini
chars.desc: An apprentice at the gunsmith's workshop. Poor with words and very shy around strangers.

chars.name: Mon3tr
chars.desc: A Rhodes Island comrade who arrived in Laterano together with the Doctor.

terms.name: Laterano
terms.desc: A sacred city founded by the Sankta, where people are bound together by their Halos and Communion.

terms.name: Halo and Communion
terms.desc: The luminous ring above a Sankta's head, and the shared-emotion ability it grants. The people of Laterano use it to feel one another's hearts.

terms.name: World Summit
terms.desc: A diplomatic event hosted by Laterano, inviting nations from across Terra. This is now its second occurrence.

terms.name: Nurturing Cathedral
terms.desc: A cloud-piercing cathedral completed to coincide with the World Summit. The site where the 'Birth Ceremony' is held.

terms.name: the Saint
terms.desc: The being known as the origin of the Sankta faith. A figure from the scripture 'Journey of the Saint.'

terms.name: the Omen
terms.desc: A great calamity foretold in prophecy, threatening all of Terra. Laterano claims to have long prepared for it.

blocks.x: Laterano is a sacred city founded by the Sankta. Its people feel one another's hearts through the Halo and Communion above their heads, boasting an unshaken order that has lasted thousands of years. Right now, the 2nd World Summit is in full swing, drawing guests from every nation of Terra.

But something's off. Explosions that shouldn't be happening keep going off, and gunsmith workshops that had stood quiet for centuries suddenly shut their doors overnight. And a colossal, cloud-piercing cathedral now stands in the middle of the city, as if it had always been there.
On the newly appointed Cardinal **Lemuen**'s first day at work, all this strangeness erupts at once. And behind it all is someone who wants to test just how much this city loves them.
1. The New Cardinal's First Day, in Which the City Explodes on the Way to Work

blocks.cap: A cloud-piercing, unfamiliar building towers over the sacred city, as if it had stood there from the very beginning.

blocks.x: Lemuen woke up after a five-year coma and immediately became Cardinal of the Seventh Ministry. Her assignment on her very first day: investigate an 'unidentified explosive carrier.' Sure enough, a pillar on Jovita Street in the Stevonus District had been blown to pieces.

They never caught the culprit, but they got one lead: someone in a red hood had slipped through the alley. That night, one of Laterano's autonomous confession vehicles began speaking in a very strange voice.

blocks.cap: "Laterano...... something has clearly changed here, and drastically." A stranger's voice, unmistakably Sankta, drifts out of the confession vehicle's speaker.

blocks.x: The voice belonged to a Sankta who had left Laterano long ago. The explosions, the vanished gunsmith workshops — all of it was this person's doing. But no one yet knew what the purpose was.
If the weather's humid enough, mushrooms might grow on a pillar. But under no circumstances should a bomb grow there. Right?
2. The Whole Nation Shuts Down Every Gunsmith Workshop

The Holy See suddenly shut down every gunsmith workshop in the country. This despite the fact that a guardian gun is, to a Sankta, nearly as precious as the Halo they're born with. The small workshop of the old gunsmith **Paganini** and his quiet apprentice **Vannini** was no exception.

Just before the workshop closed for good, a familiar red-haired Sankta showed up. "You've been at a gunsmith's for a long time — surely you can help pull off an explosion big enough to shake the whole city?" It was Exusiai, in disguise.

blocks.cap: As she takes apart her own guardian gun, Exusiai tells Vannini about the day she first chose it.

blocks.x: Vannini had always been a child who could barely speak. But there was one memory she shared with Exusiai that, even remembered differently by each of them, was equally precious to both. And so the two became the most dangerous duo in the world.
'Never take on a boring job.' That's Penguin Logistics' fine corporate culture for you!

Paganini is a Liberi, yet he's spent decades in Laterano crafting guardian guns for the Sankta. He and the gun-knight Patrizione used to be inseparable drinking buddies, but now they can barely stand to look at each other. No one's said why, not yet.
3. A Baby Is Born at the Cathedral, and No One Smiles
The Nurturing Cathedral. A colossal, cloud-piercing cathedral that Laterano completed to coincide with the World Summit. Archbishop Kef, who is blind, was preparing the 'Birth Ceremony' here.

A pregnant Sarkaz woman from the wastelands, called 'Bridesmaid,' is brought here. The caregiver **Andoain** tends to her day and night, looking somehow desperate — like someone who has his own reason this birth absolutely cannot fail.
It is only human nature to long for peace and joy, and to fear hardship and pain. That is why we all pray together, regardless of race.
On the day of the Birth Ceremony, what was born was not a human child but a baby Abyssbeast. But its eyes never opened, and even its breathing grew faint.

blocks.cap: Instead of a cry, a low sob fills the cathedral — mourning for a pitiful mother and a child gone too soon.

blocks.x: But in Andoain's arms, the dying newborn opens its eyes and lets out its first cry. And above its head, a Halo — until now found only among the Sankta — begins to glow.

blocks.cap: Light condenses above the baby Abyssbeast's head. "We are all born equal, born as one...... as Sankta."

blocks.x: 4. Caught Red-Handed by Her Own Sister While Trying to Blow Up the Cathedral

Exusiai and Vannini secretly infiltrated the Nurturing Cathedral and planted bombs throughout it. Her goal was to stop the Birth Ceremony itself — she just hadn't expected there to be so many people inside.

blocks.cap: The two run into each other in the corridor. Lemuen confirms her sister's identity. "Exusiai. What's your goal? Blowing up this cathedral? Stopping the Birth Ceremony?"

blocks.x: The two sisters defuse the standoff with a childhood code. "Mayonnaise apple pie!" "Bake and eat, bake and eat." It was their own private reconciliation signal, used whenever their arguments dragged on too long.
El. I can feel your emotions. The inner conflict, the impatience, the anxiety, even the feelings you don't know how to put into words......

In the end, Exusiai dumps the remaining bombs into her sister's arms and slips away without a care. Lemuen lets her go, choosing instead to take this whole affair upon herself. Soon after, gunfire begins to ring out across all of Laterano.
5. The Truth No One Can Laugh At: Laterano Was the Omen All Along

Federico is a Notary Office enforcer, and Arturia is the escaped convict he's chasing. While tracking down the missing pregnant woman, the two end up joining forces by chance — and notice something is off. The gunsmith workshops, the endless barley fields, the monastery whose role has suddenly changed......
Everything about Laterano was subtly, slightly wrong.

The moment the Birth Ceremony ends, the two witness all of Laterano freeze in place. Over that scene — devoid of crowds, devoid of noise — unfamiliar creatures begin crawling out of the cathedral's shadow.

blocks.cap: "I was certain — this is what Laterano truly looks like." The same word slips from both their mouths. 'The Omen.'

blocks.x: At that very moment, unexpected visitors arrive from outside the city: the Doctor of Rhodes Island, and Mon3tr. Having watched this disaster unfold from afar, the two of them realized Laterano had fallen into a mass hallucination and rushed in.
6. The Defectors Lined Up Before the City Gate

Before the procession headed for the cathedral, another group was gathering outside Laterano's walls: the gun-knight **Aurela**, who fell from grace and was exiled ten years ago, along with the defectors and children from Bolívar who had followed her.
Federico alone stood in the gate's way. But Arturia used her cello to lead them past the walls, and Hildegard, a nun from Landen Monastery, joined the fight with bow in hand as well.

blocks.cap: Before the city gate, arrows pour down like rain. "Landen escort, Hildegard! Here to support you!"

blocks.x: What we believe might not be Laterano's true doctrine. But this faith is what let us barely hold onto an orderly life, even in a chaotic place like Bolívar.

Aurela said she'd come back for revenge — revenge against Patrizione, the mentor who exiled her. But once she actually stepped inside the walls, what she encountered first was a feeling far more complicated than resentment.
7. The Caregiver Who Killed the Abyssbeast

After the Birth Ceremony, a 'second ceremony' begins, handing out Halos to the entire city — to every race that isn't Sankta, even to streetlamps and trees. Only then does Andoain realize what he had truly created.

Long ago, Andoain left Laterano vowing to find an answer, and wandered for ten years. The moment he realizes this very sight is that answer, he takes the baby Abyssbeast he raised with his own hands into his arms and grabs a bomb's fuse.
If this is a dream, wake up from it. If it's an obsession, it must be smashed. And then we talk about salvation again.

blocks.cap: In the Nurturing Cathedral's first nursery — the very room where it all began — flames burst out.

blocks.x: At the same moment, Investigator Amos — who had been helping Lemuen since her very first day — buys time for others to flee and is shot. To the very last moment, he wonders whether he was truly of use, and quietly closes his eyes.
8. The People of Laterano Answer with Guns
The Birth Ceremony ended in failure, but the procession of new Sankta doesn't stop. The only ones who can now halt that long line marching toward the cathedral are the people of Laterano themselves.
The apprentice Vannini stood in the gun-knight Patrizione's way. But Patrizione was already being consumed by the Communion. He pulls Vannini's gun barrel toward himself of his own will.

blocks.cap: "Don't shake...... just like now. Calm your heart, and pull the trigger." Patrizione's first lesson, and his last.

blocks.x: That single bullet strikes Paganini instead, who was trying to get away. The old gunsmith, who claimed he'd never once truly spoken his mind to Patrizione, finally pours out everything he wanted to say in his final moment.
Well, at least...... I won't have that regret anymore.

blocks.cap: Lemuen arrives in a wheelchair, unloading a hail of bullets. "Because Laterano doesn't need salvation like yours!"

blocks.x: 9. God Is Dead, but the Prayer Remains

The true identity of the procession is 'the Saint' — the origin of the Sankta faith, and the Halo's very owner. Holding Exusiai in its arms, the Saint tells her the true history Laterano had hidden: that it was originally a Tikaz who survived a swamp by devouring its own kind.

blocks.cap: A silver mountain range, and within it, a single glowing ring — the memory of the moment the first Sankta received their Halo.

blocks.x: The Saint asks: does prayer really do any good?
Exusiai was someone who, back in the third grade, had already learned through an experiment that prayer had no effect whatsoever. And yet her answer was already decided.
We'll go on being thankful for the Lord's existence. Even if the Lord doesn't look the way we always imagined.
Exusiai had swapped out every bullet lodged in her body for specially made bombs. The loudest farewell in the world begins.

blocks.cap: "Verus Deo Volente! Gospel Rock'n'Roll! High Voltage Barrage! Char Siu Apple Pie!" The Saint's body scatters amid the chain of explosions.

blocks.x: 10. The Silent God, and the Question with No Answer

At the end of the labyrinth beneath the cathedral, Andoain and Federico finally come face to face with 'the Law' itself — a cold machine. Pope Evangelista XI had already lost the power of speech before it.

The Law tells the two of them: Laterano, the Sankta, all of it was merely a process to return everything to a single existence called Originium. This was the true nature of the 'future where everyone is saved' that Andoain had spent over ten years searching for.

If I've been carrying a false ideal in my heart all along, if I've shamelessly put myself through ten years of solitary penance for nothing...... then I must not pass this cowardice and shamelessness on to anyone else.
Andoain and Federico decide to destroy the machine. In place of a perfect answer, they choose a life with no answers at all.

The Doctor and Mon3tr had also been combing the city, chasing whoever was behind all this. What they ran into was an unexpected face: Pristis. She calmly informed them that this Omen was the result of Originium she herself had tampered with.

Fortunately, Pristis's power wasn't what it used to be either. When Mon3tr risked everything to reconnect with Laterano's machine, light slowly began to return to the city that had lost all its color.
Three Months Later

The Omen retreated, but Laterano never fully returned to how it was. The Halos came back, but the texture of the Communion had changed, and all across the city, people were quietly facing their own share of the cracks.
Paganini's gunsmith workshop now belongs to Vannini. Patrizione shared something like reconciliation with his granddaughter Fiammetta, and Exusiai set up a new company and left Laterano once again.
A round of applause for Lemuel's new adventure!

Amos's last message was recovered, belatedly. Lemuen shows the recording to Aurela, along with a new offer of employment from the Holy See. Instead of answering, Aurela only lets slip a bit of an old story about an argument she once had with Amos.

Hildegard and Landen Monastery went back to baking bread and brewing beer. Nueda gathers the children from Bolívar and the children of Laterano into one room, telling them stories they'd never hear anywhere else in Laterano.

blocks.cap: Andoain and Lemuen's final farewell. "Goodbye, Andoain." "Goodbye."

blocks.x: Andoain was sentenced to exile. No one came to see him off, and no one held it against him. He simply boards an old traveling monastery and once again sets out on a road with no answers.
Laterano still prays. Even after learning that the Lord was, in truth, a silent machine. Even knowing that prayer changes nothing, people still light their Halos for one another, today as ever.

act41side.tagline: In a newly liberated nation where everyone eats nothing but potatoes, no one notices that death itself has crept into their bodies — until someone finally does. And a bittersweet, tender red dragon stabs her own sister to put out that fire.

chars.name: Rochesnie
chars.desc: The younger of Tara's two red dragons. She returns to her homeland, Na Síoraí, under the alias 'Reed' · Operator Reed the Flame Shadow

chars.name: Ebhlana
chars.desc: The 'Leader' who rules Tara, and Rochesnie's older sister, another red dragon. She reclaimed the country with fire, but tells no one what comes next.

chars.name: Brigid
chars.desc: A nomad descended from an exiled king's bloodline. She roams the world delivering letters.

chars.name: Nemos
chars.desc: A Soul Guardian who left the tribe. Skilled in silence and mediation, but comes face to face with her own limits in Na Síoraí.

chars.name: Cullan
chars.desc: A blacksmith hailed as a hero of Na Síoraí. He's building a massive red dragon statue in time for Samhain.

chars.name: Theo
chars.desc: Cullan's young apprentice. Draws the city's resentment for the sole reason of his Victorian blood.

chars.name: Morrin
chars.desc: A wanderer who once dreamed of being a bard. A fellow countryman who returned home alongside Rochesnie.

chars.name: Labhraidh
chars.desc: The ghost of a red dragon wrongfully killed 600 years ago. He calls himself 'the Exiled King.'

terms.name: Na Síoraí
terms.desc: The red dragons' nest and Tara's newly established capital. Hidden deep in the heart of the Black Forest.

terms.name: Tara
terms.desc: A nation that just reclaimed its independence after living under Victorian rule. Home to Rochesnie and Ebhlana.

terms.name: the Black Forest and the Black Mist
terms.desc: The forest surrounding Na Síoraí, and the mist within it. Said to swallow forever anyone who enters without permission.

terms.name: the Flame of Death
terms.desc: A purple fire only red dragons can wield. If it spreads to a living person, it slowly consumes their life until they die and rise again as a zombie.

terms.name: Samhain
terms.desc: A festival marking the end of harvest and the start of winter. Said to be the night when the line between the living and the dead grows thinnest.

terms.name: Hwanhwa
terms.desc: A Taran funeral rite that returns fire to the deceased. Only once this flame is fully extinguished can the dead finally find rest.

terms.name: Dublin
terms.desc: The newly risen bureaucratic body that actually runs Na Síoraí. Said to be made up of war heroes, but...

terms.name: Soul Guardian
terms.desc: A race that lives in seclusion around ruins and presides over funerals. They never meddle in the disputes of the living.

blocks.x: In the world of Arknights, Tara is a nation that spent a long time under Victorian imperial rule. Then the red dragon Ebhlana reclaimed the old land, scattering fire as she went, and not long ago declared independence.

But ahead of the reclaimed nation's first Samhain festival, what filled people's hands wasn't freedom — it was a few potatoes and a nighttime curfew. And without anyone noticing, everyone came to carry a strange little ember inside their bodies.

This story, as it turns out, is a piece of 'fiction' being told by a drunk writer to a woman he's never met, in some run-down tavern. Except this woman keeps insisting she'll decide the ending herself.

The writer's first subject is an old legend — that a greedy red dragon joined hands with the Black Mist to reclaim her nation, and in exchange, the Flame of Death blurred the line between the living and the dead. And this is also the story of the one autumn night when that legend actually happened.
1. A Victory Feast Made of Potatoes
Rochesnie, going by the alias 'Reed,' arrives in Na Síoraí ahead of her sister. In a city just out of a war, people were producing something out of kitchen knives, bowls, even prosthetic legs.

blocks.cap: After days of travel, Na Síoraí at last. The red dragons' nest lay hidden deep in the Black Forest, picture-perfect.

blocks.x: It turns out to be a massive red dragon statue being built by the hero blacksmith **Cullan**. Erected in the square in time for Samhain, it will mark Tara's rebirth. But everyone treats Cullan's young apprentice **Theo** like a thorn in their side, for the sole reason that he's of Victorian blood.
A friendly villager invites Reed to sit down for a 'victory feast.' Meat stew, roasted linsu, oatmeal porridge... the names sound fancy, but the taste, the color, the smell — all of it's identical.
It's all potato.

There's a curfew in the city every night, too. Asked why, she's told the Black Forest's mist sometimes seeps into the streets. Step into that mist uninvited, the friendly villager warns without a flicker of hesitation, and you never come back.

Meanwhile, only the scrap metal for the statue is being gathered with real diligence. A neighbor with an injured leg even insists on donating his own prosthetic — but Cullan flatly refuses, no exceptions.
2. Read as a Blessing, Spelled Out as a Curse

Reed reveals her identity and goes to find her sister. It's been a long time, but the reunion feels awkward. Instead of a warm greeting, Ebhlana asks first where the spear is; Reed dodges, claiming it's safely tucked away in a Rhodes Island storage room.
Looking down at the city from the fortress balcony, Reed finally notices it — that inside everyone laughing, talking, sharing meals, a tiny purple flame is quietly burning.

blocks.cap: A friendly neighbor, a hardworking merchant — inside their bodies, a purple flame burning silently.

blocks.x: Waiting for Reed on the balcony is her sister, **Ebhlana**. She admits the Flame of Death has spread to living people, but won't openly admit any wrongdoing.
Kill me, Rochesnie.
Her sister says she'll die herself and take the fire with her — that it's the only way to save Tara. But Reed has no intention of accepting an answer that easy.
3. Hero, Please Stop Making That Statue

Some quiet digging reveals the real reason the city is starving. An official had been secretly siphoning off food supplies under the name 'Dublin.' Meanwhile, people had no idea, and kept blaming innocent Victorians instead.
Around then, the nomad **Brigid** tries to convince Theo to leave the city with her. Theo refuses, not wanting to be a burden to Cullan — but Brigid's uneasy premonitions have never once been wrong.
As for Brigid herself, she gets bounced back out of the Black Forest five times trying to leave the city. The mist, which always let nomads pass freely before, refuses to let go of her this time.

Suddenly, rumors spread through the city about rooting out 'Victorian spies.' Of all days, that's when Theo disappears. Cullan, Reed, and **Nemos**, a Soul Guardian who left her tribe, split up to search for him.

The truth they uncover is bitter. The story behind Cullan becoming a 'hero' a few years back actually involved an innocent caravan, not scouts as claimed — and Theo was its sole survivor. And now, having been hit by a carriage, beaten in an alley, and left neglected amid cold hostility, Theo finally dies.

Cullan falls apart. In the middle of his workshop, glaring at the statue he built, he blames himself, and finally decides to destroy it. The statue looks down at him with hollow eyes, as if asking him back — are you really a hero?
Once Samhain is over, once the shadow of war has finally lifted... what is it you all want to do?
4. The Statue Finally Finished, and Its Price
Cullan binds himself in chains and drags the statue to the square himself. Before a crowd crying out for revenge, he confesses that he's actually a false hero, built on a lie.

blocks.cap: A hero bound in chains drags the enormous dragon he made all the way to the square.

blocks.x: The crowd erupts in fury. But with his last strength, Cullan strikes the statue's head and collapses, coughing up blood. As he dies, a purple flame rises from his body, and the statue spreads its own wings and flies up to perch on its pedestal.
Reed quietly gathers Cullan's remains and reads aloud the eulogy Nemos wrote for her. It's Na Síoraí's very first Hwanhwa rite — for the first victim of this whole affair.

blocks.cap: "May you and I... meet again in a warm flame." Rochesnie reads aloud the letter Nemos wrote.

blocks.x: Ashes to ashes, dust to dust. Let the dead sleep forever, carrying our memory with them.
5. A Time-Travel Journey with the Exiled King

After the funeral is over, **Labhraidh**, the ghost of a red dragon killed 600 years ago, appears before Reed again. Calling himself 'the Exiled King,' he leads her to the ruins of the old royal castle.

blocks.cap: 600 years ago, in this very corridor, another sibling endured the same tragedy.

blocks.x: He has her put her hand into a furnace and shows her, one by one, everything he went through — killed by his own younger sibling's hand, contaminating pastures even after death, and only ever able to quiet that fire through the Hwanhwa rite.

Labhraidh also shows Reed scenes from her and her sister's childhood that she doesn't yet know — young Rochesnie reviving a dying wound beast and startling her older sister, and young Ebhlana, standing beside her, never once smiling.

He shows her another scene, too. In the guardian Earl Warrick's mansion, young Rochesnie wrapping her injured sister's bandages and reading her poetry. Her sister falls asleep listening to those verses, neither of them realizing how the Earl was using them both.
The conclusion is cruel. As long as Ebhlana lives, the fire keeps spreading. Labhraidh tells her to run. Reed shakes her head.
**Even so, I'm going back.** Reed's answer is short — and just as unshakable.
6. The Sisters' Last Dance
Back in Na Síoraí, Ebhlana makes Reed an unexpected offer — to finally dance the dance they never once danced together as children.

blocks.cap: "Come, dance with me, Rochesnie." For the first time, her sister holds out her hand.

blocks.x: The instant their hands touch, Reed notices — her sister's pale hand, her bloodless face. Ebhlana, too, was being slowly consumed by her own flame.
Reed wants to ask why now, of all times — whether she's forgotten all the books and poems they once read together as children. But Ebhlana laughs off every question.
Reed makes one last plea — let's save the people together, it's not too late yet. But Ebhlana's answer was already decided.

blocks.cap: A sharp spear pierces her sister's body, but she doesn't fall — as if she wants to keep standing right there.

blocks.x: Only then does Ebhlana finally break. What flows from the wound isn't blood but a purple flame, instantly swallowing the whole fortress, and then all of Na Síoraí.
7. A Soul Guardian Sings, Dragging a Coffin
At Reed's request, Nemos places Ebhlana's body in a coffin and drags it through every corner of the city, singing an old mourning song, holding the flame up to every window as she goes.

blocks.cap: Outside the city gates, the formless 'Exiled King' and the nomad Brigid quietly make a wager.

blocks.x: But the response is dismal. Instead of grieving, people just look for someone to take revenge on.
In her despair, Nemos briefly entertains a dark thought, but in the end cries out the truth: the red dragon who killed the red dragon was the red dragon herself — she died to protect all of you.

The first to break down at those words is **Emma**, who runs the grocery. Having lost her lover Niels in the war, and then the baby in her womb to starvation, she instead tries to walk straight into the mist.

Only after hearing a new song that Morrin and Brigid sing together — not a song of reunion, but one meant to light the path for those who've gone — does Emma decide to return her candle's flame to the red dragon after all.

blocks.cap: Candles gather one by one before the coffin, and the entire statue is engulfed in a massive blaze.

blocks.x: When dawn breaks, we go to the Black Forest and call the mist.
8. The Red Dragon, Born a Second Time

Reed walks into the square and, at last, returns her own flame. Then she has one final conversation with the giant dragon-shaped apparition conjured from the Black Mist — really, a conversation with her sister.
Ebhlana admits it — this was the 'parting gift' she'd prepared all along. For Rochesnie to kill her with her own hands, then rise again before everyone's eyes as the one true red dragon.

blocks.cap: Above the ashes of the coffin, the red dragon walks out a second time.

blocks.x: Stepping out of the flames, Reed now stands before the people under the name Rochesnie Dublin. She declares that she has returned from the depths of death thanks to the tears people shed, and vows never again to drive anyone into a place like that.
Tara has one red dragon, and one alone — Rochesnie Dublin.
Rather than revenge, she calls on them to light lanterns instead. To take the flame born of humiliation and grief, and turn it into a flame that warms them and lights their way forward.

blocks.cap: The eve of Samhain, and for the first time in a long while, rain falls over Na Síoraí.

blocks.x: A few days later, a letter bearing an unfamiliar seal arrives for a caster duke in a neighboring nation. It announces the full lifting of martial law across Na Síoraí and the entire southern region. Tara still hasn't fully broken free of Victoria, but at least, now, it can breathe.
And, Outside the Story
...and with that, the drunk writer finishes his tale. But the unfamiliar woman isn't satisfied. The ending's too idealistic, she says — she'll finish the real ending herself.
That's the moment the writer's true identity comes out. He is, in fact, a remnant of Dublin who fled Na Síoraí — 'the Singer.'
And the woman before him is Ebhlana, who should already be dead. Kept alive by the power of the Black Mist, she has known everything from the very start.

She tells him exactly how she deceived Duke Wellington, and even that Rochesnie had secretly slipped outside the city gates to negotiate. Handing over one city that deserved to die to secure a stable Tara — hardly a bad trade, she says.

The Singer tries desperately to argue back, but no matter how hard he drags his pen across the paper, only illegible ink marks remain. In the end, robbed even of the freedom to speak, he falls silent.

In the epilogue, Ebhlana sits across from a Rhodes Island scholar. While Reed goes by 'the Reed Dancer,' she herself has taken a new name — 'Necrass.' Life is short and death is very long, she says, laughing.
Necrass laughs that death will someday bring her back here, to Rhodes Island, again. And she asks, as if out of old habit, what story they'll tell her when that day comes.

In the end, no one knows the true ending of this story — whether Rochesnie truly let go of her sister for good, or merely brought a shadow of ash back to life. The only certain thing is this: the flame of Na Síoraí is no longer purple.

act40side.tagline: A Victorian tourist orders five different dishes, only to discover they're all tofu — and turns out to be an operator hunting for a name erased forty years ago. And the night when two daughters, unremembered by history, finally recognize each other.

chars.name: Blaze
chars.desc: A Rhodes Island operator who speaks Yan with a Victorian accent, going by the alias 'Huang.'

chars.name: Lin Qingyan
chars.desc: The Dali Temple magistrate who brought Blaze to Baekjo, relentless in her pursuit of the truth.

chars.name: Xingzhu
chars.desc: A food critic who tours every corner of Baekjo writing a book, under the pen name 'Wandering Xingzhu.'

chars.name: Yu
chars.desc: A young chef who runs the tiny noodle shop Weimigak alone, with an unusually fierce pride in his cooking.

chars.name: Wang
chars.desc: A customer who lingers near Baekjo calling Yu 'brother.' Hard to read.

chars.name: Gojeon
chars.desc: A man who spent over a decade as a low-ranking official in the Ministry of Rites. Rumored to have already passed away.

chars.name: Woojing
chars.desc: A retired Dali Temple magistrate who suddenly confesses to arson and stands trial.

chars.name: Yeongsul
chars.desc: A former Minister of Rites nearing retirement, living with his granddaughters in an old mansion in Baekjo.

chars.name: Jinlong
chars.desc: The ruler of Yan. Rarely shows his face, even at the Baekjinyeon banquet.

terms.name: Baekjo
terms.desc: A city built atop an Originium vein to hold back its catastrophe. Its name means 'tending to the kitchen of all under heaven,' and it's known as Yan's greatest food city.

terms.name: Baekjinyeon
terms.desc: A banquet Jinlong holds every Mid-Autumn Festival, summoning all officials to discuss affairs of state. It began with an old anecdote of an official who served a shabby soup during a famine to make a bold point, and the dishes served this day are copied citywide.

terms.name: Dali Temple
terms.desc: The government office that handles Yan's judiciary. Its top official is the Dali Qing, with several vice magistrates below who preside over cases.

terms.name: Taishi
terms.desc: The figure who led an education reform forty years ago, recorded in the history books as having taken his own life over charges of treason.

terms.name: Longevity Noodles
terms.desc: Noodles offered to wish someone long life on their birthday. Secretly served each year on Jinlong's true birthday, they recur throughout the story.

terms.name: Candied Tangerine Peel
terms.desc: A dessert made of tangerine peel and sugar, named after an old poem about homesickness. The flavor Blaze remembers from her father as a child feels somehow different from the flavor today.

terms.name: Sui
terms.desc: A being said to have slumbered beneath Baekjo since ancient times. A shadow at the heart of the story whose true nature is hard to grasp.

terms.name: the Four Generations
terms.desc: The name given to the siblings entangled with Sui. Each lives under a different face and a different name, scattered throughout Baekjo.

blocks.x: The people of Yan call Baekjo 'the kitchen of all under heaven.' They say Jinlong himself gave it that name. In this city, every noodle shop, every palace banquet, has its own story to tell.
But the real name of the person who compiled this city's history and refined its script appears nowhere in any history book. Even though they did so much for the world.
This story begins with a retired official who walks into the Dali Temple of his own accord. The charge: arson. But something about this confession is off from the very start.
1. Who Is the Victorian Tourist Who Ordered Five Tofu Dishes?
All of Baekjo was buzzing with excitement ahead of the Mid-Autumn Baekjinyeon banquet. So was Weimigak, the little noodle shop across from the Dali Temple.

Two Dali Temple officials at the next table start chatting. About a mysterious fire that broke out at the Jeongpungnu kitchen. About a burglary at retired official Yeong's mansion where the thief left nothing missing but honey smeared on a painting of an immortal.

Then this rumor comes up: a few days ago, an old man in prison clothes ate twenty bowls of rice, then threw the empty bowls at the Dali Temple's gate, shouting, 'I'm the one who set fire to the archives!'
At the next table sits a Victorian tourist speaking fluent Yan. His name: Huang. Everyone assumes he can't understand the officials' conversation, but he's caught every word.
It turns out the five dishes Huang ordered are all tofu, one way or another. That he didn't even realize it himself only makes things more awkward for the shop's owner, Kang, and the young chef, Yu.

blocks.cap: Ruby-jade tofu, mala tofu, phoenix-jade tofu... all different names, all turned out to be tofu.

blocks.x: Sharing food with the customer next to him, he even helps himself to her dessert (candied tangerine peel) — only to learn she's Xingzhu, the food critic behind the Baekjo Culinary Records.

Provoked by Huang's taunt that maybe his own cooking skills just aren't up to snuff, an angry Yu makes a bet — if Huang finds better food elsewhere, Yu will owe him a free meal and the recipe. This customer's real name, revealed only later, turns out to be Rhodes Island operator Blaze.
2. The Confession About Setting a Wound Beast's Straw on Fire
Next door at the Dali Temple's court, something even stranger is happening. Retired vice magistrate Woojing has turned himself in claiming arson — except his alibi doesn't add up at all.

At the time of the fire, he was already in prison. Yet Woojing calmly rambles on — that he tied a burning bundle of straw to an injured wound beast and set it loose, that he struck the railing with his shackles 863 times (the exact number of years since the Dali Temple was founded!) to make a spark.
Lin Qingyan, his former student now attending as court clerk, grows more and more tense beside her colleague Haejin, who barely holds back a laugh.
Then, in a single sentence, Woojing lets his true feeling slip.
That document was nothing but pretty lies through and through. Reading it dirties the eyes and dirties the heart. So I burned it all away. How clean is that!
Going herself to check the burned-out archive, Lin Qingyan finds a scorched pine branch amid the ashes. It's from the very tree Woojing once said, 'When I die, make my coffin from this wood.'

blocks.cap: An archive said to hold ten thousand books, reduced at last to nothing but ash.

blocks.x: Back at Weimigak, Blaze runs into her guardian at the mansion, Lin Qingyan. Getting scolded for sneaking out, she belatedly brings up her father.

Her father spent over a decade as a low-ranking official in the Ministry of Rites before he passed away. To Lin Qingyan's comforting remark that he 'must have been a good person,' Blaze answers bitterly. 'A good person...'
even good people die all the same.'
3. Two Granddaughters in the Yeong Mansion, Neither Gives a Clean Answer
Former Minister of Rites Yeongsul, nearing retirement, has two granddaughters at his mansion — his blood grandchild Yeong Sachu, and Yeong-in, whom he took in as a child.

By day, Yeong-in organizes historical records at the Hall of History; in secret, she writes food criticism under the pen name 'Wandering Xingzhu.' She's the very customer who bumped into Blaze at Weimigak.

There'd just been a break-in at the mansion, too. Nothing was stolen, but honey had been smeared on the mouth of an immortal in a painting — a burglary that mimicked the Kitchen God, a silencing kind of theft.

Lin Qingyan goes straight to Yeongsul and presses him about a case from ten years ago — the officially recorded death of Gojeon, a low-ranking Ministry of Rites official, who drowned in a river while accompanying a delegation to Victoria.
Yeongsul neither confirms nor denies it, just returning the same question. 'Magistrate Lin, what answer is it you're looking for?'

Meanwhile, Blaze causes her own kind of trouble, leaping out an 18th-floor window to make her escape. And when an invitation arrives at Weimigak for the final chef selection competition at Jeongpungnu for the Baekjinyeon, Blaze's next destination is set.
4. Three 'No Results Found' in a Row on the Academy Registry
Touring Baekjo's dessert shops with Xingzhu, Blaze suddenly confesses — the candied tangerine peel her father used to send her in Victoria as a child tastes different from the one she's eating now.
Her father was from Yan, she says, and died abroad while she was still growing up. Xingzhu starts to say, 'Blaze, your father must have...' — and quickly swallows the rest.
Around this time, Lin Qingyan sneaks into Baekjo Academy under a false identity and searches the registry for 'Gojeon.' The result comes back the same, three times running.
No results found.
Then she finds a record under 'Gojeong,' a name off by one character — top of every class, but never finished his studies. The seal reason reads:
'In 1062, Gojeong and his wife and daughter went missing. This record is hereby sealed.'
In flashback, Lin Qingyan presses Blaze again on the year and place of her birth. Blaze can't answer. 'How would I even remember that...'

Meanwhile, in the Weimigak kitchen, longevity noodle training is in full swing. Blaze and Xingzhu, exhausted from staying up all night, resort to a 'sly trick' of secretly boiling store-bought dry noodles to make it through — but Yu catches on by smell alone and flies into a rage. Even so, he can't deny the taste holds up.
5. The Old Master Who Said Never Add Sugar to Candied Tangerine Peel — Then Suddenly, Fire

The first gate of the Jeongpungnu competition is identifying ingredients in tea; the second, explaining the origins of past Baekjinyeon dishes. Blaze draws 'The Realm's One Hundred' — the story of a humble braised radish dish some Taishi offered Jinlong fifty years ago.
When the story ends, the audience stirs. Jeongpungnu's head chef, Makbulbok, descends the stairs and asks, 'Even after all this time, someone still remembers that.'

At the final gate, tasting the longevity noodles Blaze presents, Makbulbok recognizes the broth's secret and flies into a rage. 'You've made a fool of me!' He suspects a connection to the Weimigak owner.

When she explains sincerely, Makbulbok slips out a hint. 'The one thing you must never do when making candied tangerine peel is add sugar. That's using sweetness to hide poor skill.' At that moment, the chandelier overhead catches fire.

As Blaze uses Arts to put out the flames amid the fire, an arsonist (wearing a mask) is found among the wreckage. Lin Qingyan gives chase but loses them, and Makbulbok, feeling he owes his life to Blaze, finally opens up.

Ten years ago, the cook who made the last meal for a death-row prisoner at the Ministry of Justice's prison was himself — 'Mak Soryuk.' That prisoner's name was Gojeon. And Woojing and Yeongsul, he says, had come to visit him.
For the first time, Blaze learns her father was a death-row prisoner. Even in shock, she doesn't let go of one point. 'Just because it's written in a history book doesn't make it true!
If history books were all true, then what exactly are you even investigating?'
6. Even a Stream Is a Page of History

As a girl, Yeong-in once met a low-ranking official who'd been caught sneaking paper and brushes from the mansion library. His name was Gojeon. He was always alone, and he never once forgot her birthday.
To Yeong-in, who called herself 'just an ordinary person,' Gojeon passed along something his own teacher once told him.
Most people never get their names into the history books, but that doesn't make them the blank spaces in that history. If anything, they are the very pages of the book itself.
That winter, Yeong-in never saw Gojeon again. When she asked the mansion physician how he was, the answer was short. 'He passed away...
in an accident on the way to Victoria.'

Those words line up exactly with the 'official closing report' Lin Qingyan gave Blaze. And yet the physician himself tilts his head, puzzled. 'I never understood why, as sick as he was, he insisted on going all that way.'
Lin Qingyan announces she'll close the case, to keep Blaze from being pulled somewhere more dangerous. But a single line from her colleague Haejin changes her mind.
'If it's something that can't stay hidden, better to bring it into the light yourself.'
That night, Simcheol finds a single document on Lin Qingyan's desk, pinned down by her official robes and an inkstone. It was her letter of resignation.
7. The Night the Baekjinyeon Caught Fire — 'That Woman's Descendant'

blocks.cap: As the horn sounds the hour of the Rooster, the Baekjinyeon begins beneath the moonlight.

blocks.x: Blaze, slipped in as a cook. Yeong-in, entering with a forged invitation. And Lin Qingyan, having removed her official robes to sneak in as well. Each stood in that banquet hall for her own reason.
As the emperor enters, Lin Qingyan cries out, 'I have something to report to Your Majesty!' A moment later, the entire hall bursts into flame.

blocks.cap: And just a few words later, the banquet hall was consumed by fire like a beast baring its teeth and claws.

blocks.x: Fleeing through the palace corridor amid the chaos, Blaze is seized by an unfamiliar 'Imperial Guard instructor.' Crossing blades with her, the words she throws out are like a bolt from the blue.

'The Imperial Guard has been tracking your whereabouts for over forty years now. You're that woman's descendant. Forty years ago, the one child who vanished without a trace from a family convicted of treason.'
Blaze protests that she never knew a thing about her own birth, but gets no answer. Lin Qingyan, arriving late, steps in between them. 'It's for the sake of the truth...
the very truth all of you are turning your backs on.'
The instructor warns that it would take three of them to stop her. Even so, Lin Qingyan doesn't yield. '...Go!' With that one word left behind, Blaze goes flying beyond the city walls.
8. A Tangerine Seed Becomes a Tangerine, No Matter Where It Lands
Blaze wakes at Weimigak after passing out, unable to answer when Yu asks what exactly she's trying to find when she doesn't even know who she is.

A tangerine seed rides the wind from south of the river to north of it. It's not the south, and it's not the north, that made that seed into a tangerine — it's decades of sunlight and rain. Surface and substance can differ. Only once you understand that can you move forward.

Meanwhile, at the Dali Temple, the last conversation between Woojing and Yeongsul continues. Yeongsul finally admits it — that he had the Minister of Justice killed, because the entire Taishi affair was, from start to finish, a false charge.
It's also revealed that on the night Gojeon was caught trying to save a baby girl from the Taishi's mansion, it was Yeongsul who took the child in and raised her instead. That child was Yeong-in.

blocks.cap: The noodle shop and that mansion, it turns out, were separated by nothing more than a single alley.

blocks.x: Yu, Weimigak's young chef, tells her something else — that every dawn, Gojeon used to stand in this very alley, gazing for a long time at the mansion where the child he couldn't protect with his own hands was living.

Only then does Blaze understand her father's other life. 'A man who spent his whole life lying to protect someone he'd never even met, a man who lost his own daughter and lost himself along with her — I've never met anyone like that... What a fool he was.'

It's here that Blaze reveals her real name for the first time: 'Go Chokhwang.' It means the light of a candle flame — faint, but still lighting up what's around it. Yeong-in quietly repeats the name, then holds out her hand.
'From today, you're not alone anymore.'
9. Offering Longevity Noodles to Jinlong

blocks.cap: I made these longevity noodles myself, for Your Majesty. Won't you try some?

blocks.x: In the end, Blaze walks all the way into the Forbidden Palace and sets down a bowl of noodles before Jinlong — for the true birthday no one else bothers to remember.
Blaze asks how someone as remarkable as the Taishi ended up condemned for treason — whether it truly was a false charge. Jinlong's answer isn't what she expects.
'A false charge? That wasn't the truth — it was simply a solution...'

The Taishi truly had taken part in that rebellion. But it was meant to end a tyranny, and the script was already written beforehand for her alone to bear all the blame after the fact. Even the Jinlong of today was someone seated on the throne within that very script.
Blaze doesn't ask for an apology, or for her father's honor to be restored. Instead, she asks for just one thing.
Just make me one promise. Don't forget them.

After a long silence, Jinlong decides not to pursue Blaze's crimes, and orders that 70% of all of Baekjo's energy be mobilized to open Sui's tomb — a dangerous decision to grant clemency to a being sealed away for so long.
Epilogue. It Snowed Over Baekjo That Night
Weimigak's young chef Yu, the customer Wang who often dropped by, and even Nian, Dusk, Ji, and Ling — all of them, it turns out, were siblings split off from a single being called Sui.

blocks.cap: A scene Yu had imagined countless times — the day all the siblings finally gather for a single meal together.

blocks.x: To Wang, who means to enter the tomb alone out of guilt over their dead third sister, Yu insists on feeding him a bowl of soup first, then hands back his own share of the power. 'We were always just 'me,' from the very start.'
Facing his true self inside the tomb, Wang makes a resolve: whoever wins will live and whoever loses will vanish, but if this can make room for his siblings to live as people, then that's enough.
As Baekjo's energy shifts toward the tomb, it snows over the city for the first time in ages. Blaze and Lin Qingyan look up at it together.

blocks.cap: That night, it snowed over Baekjo for the first time in ages.

blocks.x: I just wish this snow would keep falling a little longer.

Woojing accepts a ten-year sentence, and before boarding the transport carriage, asks for one last meal from Weimigak. Lin Qingyan decides to leave the Dali Temple and return to the Ministry of Personnel.

A few days later, Weimigak opens its doors again. Yu thinks everything that happened feels like a dream, but as he checks the broth's flavor one bowl at a time, he quietly breathes a sigh of relief. 'Thank goodness...
same taste as always. Just live looking at what's in front of you — leave tomorrow's worries for tomorrow.'

There were people whose names never made it into history — a father who lied for the sake of the truth, an old man who took in a child in another's place, a being who walked into the tomb on behalf of its siblings. And two daughters who chose never to forget them are, even today, checking the same taste of broth as they prepare the next meal.

act39side.tagline: A story about a drifting swordsman captured by pirates in a salt desert who stumbles into a job as an alchemist and ends up captain of that very pirate ship. A story of someone who never once wanted anything in his life, finally learning to want something with all his heart.

chars.name: Isidro
chars.desc: A drifting swordsman captured by pirates in the salt desert. He's also an operator reported missing from Rhodes Island · Operator Thorns

chars.name: Juana
chars.desc: Captain of a pirate fleet based in the salt desert. An Ægir woman who has lived over a hundred years, her lifelong wish is to return to the sea.

chars.name: Pascuala
chars.desc: A thief girl from a fallen merchant family. She owes Isidro her life after a theft gone wrong · Operator Rose Salt

chars.name: Javier
chars.desc: The fleet's chef and Juana's ward since childhood. He learned the sword before he ever learned to cook.

chars.name: Elysium
chars.desc: Isidro (Thorns)'s colleague from Rhodes Island. He tracked his missing 'brother' all the way to the salt desert · Operator Elysium

chars.name: Weedy
chars.desc: An Engineering operator who came with Elysium. Calm and collected no matter how chaotic things get · Operator Weedy

chars.name: Anastasio
chars.desc: A newly appointed Enforcer the Iberian Tribunal sent to the salt desert. His gentle manner has won the trust of the whole village.

chars.name: Silver
chars.desc: The magistrate who leads Aaron Village in the salt desert. Revered as the one who saved the village from disaster.

chars.name: Titch
chars.desc: The boatswain Juana raised since childhood. The foulest mouth and quickest hands in the fleet.

chars.name: Ruth
chars.desc: The fleet's other boatswain. A realist who worries about keeping the ship running.

terms.name: the Salt Desert
terms.desc: Land that was once a sea. After the Stillness, the water receded and left nothing behind but white salt and bone.

terms.name: Brinescale
terms.desc: A massive creature that swims beneath the salt layer. Its hide, bones, and flesh sustain the pirates' livelihood.

terms.name: Seaborn
terms.desc: A monster that seems part human, part sea creature. Rumor has it they were once people.

terms.name: Corasonics
terms.desc: A mysterious fluid held inside a compass. Said to point toward whatever its bearer wants most.

terms.name: the Tribunal
terms.desc: The religious power that rules Iberia. Its doctrine holds that desire must be suppressed.

terms.name: the Stillness
terms.desc: The great catastrophe that upended all of Iberia. When the seawater receded, it left behind today's salt desert.

terms.name: the Conquest's Declaration
terms.desc: A hundred-year-old, battered salt-ship the fleet sails. The very first salt-ship Juana ever designed.

blocks.x: Iberia used to have a sea. That is, until a catastrophe called the Stillness swallowed all that water whole.
