All that's left now is a desert of white salt and bone. And beneath it, something still swims — something where the line between what was once human and what was once beast has blurred beyond telling.
In this desert lives a drifting swordsman caught by pirates. A man who claims he's never wanted anything — but by the end of this story, there's exactly one thing he wants, more than anything.
1. Escape Attempt No. N, Failed Every Time
**Isidro** is held captive by **Juana**, captain of a pirate fleet. He keeps trying to dig his way out through the floor of the camp, but Juana always scoops him back up without breaking a sweat.
The fleet includes the chef **Javier**, the boatswain **Titch**, and a mysterious creature Juana picked up somewhere called 'Nene.' Everyone just calls Isidro 'the kid.'
When a sailor mutters that Juana is a 'crazy old hag,' Javier chokes him quiet on the spot. Isidro only learns later that Juana took in young Javier and raised him after he lost his mother.
If you want out of here that badly, fix the compass.
The root of it all was an old gold coin Isidro carried. Juana recognized it instantly — the token of a long-vanished school of alchemy.
Isidro swears he knows nothing about alchemy. But every night in his dreams, a man teaches a young boy how to swim and how to practice alchemy, together, by a lakeside.

blocks.cap: Hauled into the salt desert camp hanging upside down, villagers pass by, laughing.

blocks.x: 2. A Fortnight Earlier: Came to Learn Swordplay, Got Bitten by a Monster Instead

Rewind the clock, and it turns out Isidro is actually the Rhodes Island operator 'Thorns.' Ignoring every message from his colleagues, he'd been wandering Iberia alone in search of a legendary swordsmanship said to withstand the raging seas.
Near the uncharted 'Pale Sea,' he's rammed head-on by a massive **Brinescale**. The one who finds him unconscious is **Pascuala**, a thieving girl fond of picking through corpses.

Because of something Pascuala lifted from Isidro's pocket, a newly appointed Enforcer of the Iberian **Tribunal**, **Anastasio**, holds a blade to her throat. Isidro, barely conscious, intervenes with a broken sword and saves her.
In the village, Anastasio is trusted enough that everyone greets him with a handshake. Tending wounds and preaching gently, he earns the villagers' genuine gratitude.

The two fugitives hitch a ride on a passing Brinescale's back and flee to Aaron Village. The villagers, seeing Isidro — whom they call an 'Ægir person' — just look frightened and slam their windows shut.

An old man in the village tells them the swordsman who created that style quietly returned half a year ago, and died. If they want to know his last words, he says — half-joking, half-not — they should go dig up his grave.
3. Turned Grave Robber, Turns Out to Be Famous
Curious about the swordsman's dying words, Isidro digs up the grave — and gets caught red-handed. The village magistrate **Silver** tries to have him hanged, alongside Pascuala.
Which do you want to keep, your right hand or your left? Choose.

Just as they're marched to the gallows, Javier leads a pirate raid to rescue them. Only after leaping off a cliff and landing on Juana's salt-ship sail, spread out like a parachute, does Isidro find himself back in pirate hands.

Meanwhile, in the village, Rhodes Island operators **Elysium** and **Weedy** are searching for the missing 'Thorns.' Silver quietly threatens them, claiming he's colluded with bandits and stolen villagers' corpses.

Seeing the bandits' bodies hung from a pole outside the village, Elysium falls silent for a moment. Realizing he'll die whether he stays with the bandits or returns and is framed as one of them, he finally leaps alone into the salt desert.
4. Hired as an Alchemist, Assistants: A Thief and a Best Friend

Juana becomes certain that the old coin and swordsmanship Isidro carries closely resemble those of her former student, 'Aulus.' In the end, Isidro is put in charge of the fleet's shipboard alchemy workshop.

Elysium, who'd slipped into the village, comes to find him, and the two reunite. They're the kind of friends who call each other 'brother.' Pascuala, meanwhile, pushes her subordinate 'Redfur' in as his assistant, instead of herself.
It'd help if your fingers were as thin as a probe needle.
As it turns out, boatswain Ruth secretly asks him for a favor: just cause enough of a ruckus during alchemy sessions, fail on purpose, and get Juana to give up on it.
Isidro doesn't answer, but his expression grows complicated. Even he doesn't quite understand why he chose to stay instead of running.

blocks.cap: As a child, Isidro would shape the metal at his fingertips into a shimmering little beast, just to earn a piece of bread.

blocks.x: 5. Hunting a Brinescale, and a Wound No One Saw on the Captain
The largest Brinescale in the fleet's history is spotted. Juana insists on personally disembarking to command the hunt.
Boatswain Ruth was uneasy about this hunt from the start. A Brinescale that size, she warns, is always the leader of a pod — for one to appear alone is an ill omen in itself.
Right before dying, the Brinescale goes berserk and drags Juana far away on its back. Everyone assumes she's dead, but the next day she strolls back in, ribs broken, cool as ever.
The crew's all on edge lately. I can't afford to look weak in front of them.

blocks.cap: Leaning against the fallen Brinescale, Juana gulps down water instead of fish sauce, cracking jokes as usual.

blocks.x: On the way back, Tribunal forces led by Silver and Anastasio lie in ambush. A scuffle breaks out over the hunted Brinescale, and Javier refuses to side with the Tribunal.
There's an old rumor among the crew — that Juana once struck a deal with a sea monster and got a long life in exchange. A story half of them don't want to believe.
6. The Seaborn, and a Hundred-Year-Old Goodbye
Juana takes Isidro to a cave and shows him a truth she's kept hidden all this time.

The **Seaborn** trapped inside the cave is actually 'Cruz' — Juana's old captain and lover. He sealed himself in here a hundred years ago after fighting other Seaborn, and Juana, unable to bring herself to kill him, has kept him locked away alone ever since.

Juana asks Isidro to perfect his swordsmanship and, in exchange, end Cruz's life in an honorable duel. Listening to her request, Isidro recalls his old fear — that any relationship, in the end, only devours both people in it.
In the end, Isidro grants her request, and hands over Cruz's sword as a keepsake.
May your soul be freed from this salt desert, riding the clouds and rain back to the sea. Farewell, my love.
7. A Trusted Lieutenant Draws His Blade
As the compass repair drags on, Javier's faction decides to finally hand Pascuala over to the Tribunal. Desperate, Pascuala grabs everyone and begs for just one more week.
You have a compass — why not chase real fortune like your ancestors did? You call yourselves pirates, but none of you have even seen the sea!
The promised week passes and the compass still isn't fixed. In truth, Isidro, afraid of damaging the original, had deliberately been building only replicas — and failing on purpose.
Even if the odds were one in ten thousand, I'd have bet everything on that single chance.
Hearing of the failure, Javier finally crosses the line. He strikes a deal with the Tribunal — hand over the compass and the fleet's location, in exchange for Juana's freedom alone.

blocks.cap: Pascuala returns to find she's been betrayed — familiar faces all around her, blades drawn as one.

blocks.x: But Anastasio never intended to keep his word. Before negotiations even end, he raids the fleet, burns the ship, and captures Juana. Watching the flames rise from across the salt desert, Isidro turns back without a moment's hesitation.
8. The Frozen Ghost Ship, and a Second Chance

Isidro, Titch, and Elysium return, rescue Juana, and flee aboard the hundred-year-old wreck, the **Conquest's Declaration**. With no water or food left, they all huddle under a single blanket to survive the night.
Even then, Isidro keeps working on the compass. Juana, for the first time, tells him a true story about the sea, and asks if he'd like to come along.

As punishment for their betrayal, Javier and Ruth are forced to cooperate with the Tribunal, tending to injured missionaries. Javier, once a chef, refuses to go anywhere near a kitchen anymore, saying he doesn't want to cook a single thing.

Meanwhile, in the village, Silver — killed by his own subordinates' betrayal — mysteriously walks out again. Thanking Anastasio for making him the Tribunal's collaborator, Silver quietly reveals his own ambitions.

blocks.cap: Before the once-dead Silver, a blood-soaked Anastasio walks back in.

blocks.x: Elysium quietly makes the rounds through the village, gathering a communicator, vitamins, waterproof boots — one item at a time. She says nothing to Isidro about it, just slips everything into a single bag.
9. Skull Canyon, a Man Who Judges Himself

The Conquest's Declaration flees the Tribunal's pursuit into 'Skull Canyon,' a place no one is said to have come out of alive. As Juana risks her life at the helm, Isidro tries to finish repairing the compass belowdecks.

blocks.cap: A canyon dyed red with bone, steam columns rising between them — the place no one has ever survived leaving.

blocks.x: A grenade round sets the alchemical device off, and Isidro grabs the Corasonics bare-handed. From that moment, the compass fuses to his arm, becoming part of his flesh and blood.

Inside the canyon, Pascuala, carrying an injured Titch on her back, ends up alone before the pursuing soldiers. Once dismissed as 'just a petty thief,' she now picks up a blade herself and fights back.

blocks.cap: Holding the unconscious Titch in her arms, Pascuala feels, for a brief moment, a warmth that's entirely her own.

blocks.x: In the middle of the canyon, Anastasio reappears for one last duel. He asks Isidro what he truly desires — because only by knowing that, he says, can a man resist death with a clear mind.
Fine, I'll admit it. I want to go out to sea. I want to see it with my own eyes, and conquer it myself.

blocks.cap: Isidro's blade, cutting through the steam, finally turns toward Anastasio's throat.

blocks.x: Meanwhile, aboard the ship, Juana and Javier fight their final duel. Javier, admitting defeat, begs to be killed — but Juana only smiles, offers him her congratulations, and lets him go.
Afterward, Each to Their Own Sea

Javier and Ruth sell off the fleet's remaining salt-ships, split the proceeds among the crew, and scatter to build new lives. Javier plans to open a restaurant somewhere, and Ruth decides, for the first time, to think for herself about where to go.
Pascuala takes a small fortune and slips in among a crowd of strangers dreaming of striking it rich in Columbia.
Elysium and Weedy return to Rhodes Island. Weedy, tempted by the Conquest's Declaration's structure, stays behind a little longer to help with repairs.

blocks.cap: Juana dances atop the mast against the setting sun. The two ships slowly drift apart.

blocks.x: The Conquest's Declaration raises its sails again, with Isidro as its new captain and Juana as first mate. Following the direction pointed by the compass fused to his arm, the crew finally sets sail for the true sea.
To that distant place.
Just then, Nene — thought long dead — finally swims up and climbs aboard. Juana hugs Nene as it licks wildly at her cheek, delighted that they can all go together after all.

And somewhere beyond the salt desert, the headless remains of Anastasio still twitch. Nothing left in it but the desire to live, tangled together with the remains of a Seaborn, doomed to wander the land forever.
On land that was once a sea, someone still struggles to want nothing at all. And someone else, wanting something with all their heart for the very first time, sails on toward the horizon.

act38side.tagline: One night in New Volsinii, a city built by idealists with no families and no vendettas, and the mob that wants to drag it back to the old ways. Everyone wears a mask at Carnevale — and a single gunshot splits the mud from the new order.

chars.name: Leontuccio
chars.desc: Acting mayor of New Volsinii. An idealist who abandoned his Family to dream of a 'city without Families.'

chars.name: Lavinia
chars.desc: Chief justice of New Volsinii, and Leontuccio's partner — a judge who upholds the New City Management Act to the letter. · Operator Penance

chars.name: Texas
chars.desc: Penguin Logistics' driver. Comes to New Volsinii to help her friends — indifferent on the outside, an idealist underneath. · Operator Texas the Omertosa

chars.name: Lappland
chars.desc: A white wolf stripped of her family name by the head of House Saluzzo. She'd rather kick the table over than sit at it. · Operator Lappland the Decadenza

chars.name: Eirene
chars.desc: Leader of the truckers' mutual aid society. A driver who lost everything in Siracusa and fled to New Volsinii.

chars.name: Somer
chars.desc: Eirene's oldest friend and benefactor. Takes on dangerous work for the sake of his sick daughter, Guri.

chars.name: Lyudmila
chars.desc: A trucker from Ursus. A riprovata chasing her master's killer. · Operator Crownslayer

chars.name: Red
chars.desc: A wolf hunter who tracks down the last Fangs on 'Grandma's' orders. Left Kal'tsit's side.

chars.name: Ingrid
chars.desc: A killer back from Higashi. A mother searching for the truth for her Oripathy-stricken daughter, Lisa. · Operator Vulpisfoglia

chars.name: Antonio
chars.desc: The real power behind the Venezia auto company. Fero, who married into the Family as the boss's son-in-law.

chars.name: Venezia
chars.desc: The aging head of the Venezia Family. An old man who's spent his life taking in 'mongrels' and binding them together as one family.

chars.name: Umberto
chars.desc: The old tailor at the De Montano tailor shop. His grandson Lucino is his only family.

chars.name: Lucino
chars.desc: Umberto's grandson. A tailor's apprentice who idolizes Family bigwigs. · Operator Figurino

chars.name: Dmitri
chars.desc: An old friend of Leontuccio's, and the one now responsible for what's left of the Vellone Family.

chars.name: Alberto
chars.desc: Head of the Saluzzo winery. Lappland's father.

terms.name: New Volsinii
terms.desc: Siracusa's 23rd nomadic city, built with Columbian technology. An experimental ground championing a 'city without Families.'

terms.name: New City Management Act
terms.desc: The law that upholds order in New Volsinii. Bans Family violence, smuggling, and vendettas, and runs the city on identity registration and citizen scores.

terms.name: Carnevale
terms.desc: An old Siracusa festival where, once you put on a mask, no one could hold you accountable for anything you did. New Volsinii revived it with new meaning.

terms.name: the Wolf Lord Game
terms.desc: A cruel game the Wolf Lords play, pitting human 'Fangs' against each other until only one is left standing.

blocks.x: There's a city in Siracusa with no **Families**. It's called **New Volsinii** — a nomadic city that's barely a year old.
There's no smuggling here, no vendettas, no back-alley assassinations. Only the **New City Management Act**, and people who just want to live honestly.

For the city's first anniversary, Siracusa revives a festival it had sealed away for sixty or seventy years — **Carnevale**. The very festival where, once you put on a mask, anything you did was forgiven, now dressed in new meaning.
And right before the dazzling opening ceremony, the acting mayor's car gets slammed by a truck. The curtain was already rising.
1. The Ones Who Chose the Hard Road
**Leontuccio** is the acting mayor who abandoned his Family. Together with his partner, Chief Justice **Lavinia**, he's trying to prove that 'order without Families' can work in this city.

But the Twelve Families of Greyhall have slipped into the city under new signboards, still eyeing it hungrily. Just as Leontuccio moves to publicly declare his inauguration as mayor, Lavinia holds him back.

Then, the night they're filming his inauguration promo, Leontuccio's car — running late for a dinner appointment — gets rammed by a container truck and he's left critically injured. It's no ordinary accident.
His friend **Texas** brings Penguin Logistics in to stand watch at his hospital bed. She acts like she can't stand festivals or paperwork, but she couldn't just watch a friend in trouble.

Meanwhile, all over the city, there are people with their own stories — Naomi, a rookie cop who joined the force just to protect the pizzeria she works at; Lucino, a tailor's apprentice who idolizes Family bigwigs... The ideal this new city holds rests, in the end, on the wishes of ordinary people like these.
2. The Secret of the Truckers' Mutual Aid Society
The truck that hit Leontuccio was driven by **Somer**. He's the oldest friend of **Eirene**, leader of the truckers' mutual aid society.

Eirene and Somer used to run a small supermarket in Siracusa together, until a petty dispute got the place burned down by a Family and they lost everything, fleeing here as a result. Somer's daughter Guri caught Oripathy in that fire.
To pay for her treatment, Somer had been secretly hauling smuggled 'tires' for the Venezia Family. And that day, the wheel just suddenly stopped responding.
Trying to protect her friend, Eirene hides the wrecked truck among the Carnevale parade vehicles — but it burns up in the sun anyway. Cornered, she asks Saluzzo head **Alberto** to clean up the mess.

blocks.cap: The moment the cloth-covered parade vehicles are dragged out, the 'wolf' at the end of the line bursts into flame. It was the hidden truck.

blocks.x: In exchange for his help, Alberto tries to bind the trucking guild to his own convenience. Somer is killed to keep him quiet, and all Eirene gets back is his lighter. In the very place she thought she'd escaped the Family, she finds herself stepping right back into the mud.
3. An Unexpected Meeting of Two Unlucky Souls

At the camp is a trucker from Ursus named **Lyudmila**. In truth, she's a former Reunion member, the **Crownslayer**. Having lost her master to the red-hooded assassin **Red**, who chased her all the way to Siracusa, she was drifting aimlessly until Somer took her in.

Red left Kal'tsit's side and now, under 'Grandma's' orders, hunts down the last remaining 'Fangs' as a wolf hunter. Lyudmila keeps throwing herself at her for revenge, only to be subdued every single time.

blocks.cap: The Crownslayer takes off her mask and accepts a sparkling water. The truck camp, home to nothing but unlucky souls, makes a little room for her too, if only for a moment.

blocks.x: "No matter how unlucky you are, life takes care of you eventually" — Somer's words slowly start changing her, someone who's clung to nothing but revenge. But on the night Somer died, Lyudmila was off chasing Red again, and couldn't be there for him.
4. The Last Fang

The last 'Fang' Red was hunting turns out to be an unexpected figure — **Umberto**, the old tailor at the De Montano tailor shop. He's the only family his grandson **Lucino** has left, and once upon a time, he was the legendary 'Tailor' who kept the Family bosses of Montelupo up at night.

He was once Wolf Lord **Vargo's** 'Fang,' but instead of stepping into the game, he spent his whole life raising a son, and then a grandson. With his grandson caught in a Family crisis, Umberto calmly finishes a suit before his old master, Head of House **Venezia**.
He doesn't resist when Red comes for him. Instead, he calmly wraps up the roughly 450 steps of a Siracusa suit and leaves the final touches to Lucino.

blocks.cap: With the new suit finished, the old tailor's breath stops as he lies down on the sofa. Doing the work he'd done his whole life, right to the very end.

blocks.x: In the process, Lucino learns everything — that his grandfather, always so strict, had once been a blood-stained killer; that his father Luca was found dead in a car alongside some actress; and that behind it all, his grandfather had carried every one of those sins and their price while still watching over him.
How many people, right up to the moment of death, are still doing the work they've done their whole life?... At least I was a decent tailor, and a lucky old man.

Sneaking aboard the land ship, Lucino tries to destroy the weapons and gets caught, but instead of becoming some big shot, he shouts that he'll solve his problems with his own two hands. Wolf Lord Vargo even breaks the rules of the game to let the boy go — honoring Umberto's final request.
5. A Mother's Blade
Venezia Family killer **Ingrid** has spent six years hunting down whoever really hurt her daughter **Lisa**, who caught Oripathy in the incident. Lisa is now an Operator at Rhodes Island.

The head of House Venezia is an old man who's spent his life taking in 'mongrels' with no shared blood or kin and binding them together as one family. He handed Ingrid the family crest too, saying "we're family," but for her, that blessing was also a shackle.

Leaving behind the 'family' the head took her into, Ingrid sets down the crest. And on the night of Carnevale, she finally confirms the truth — the one who hurt Lisa and pushed her out of the Family was none other than her own son-in-law, **Antonio**.

blocks.cap: Antonio creates his opening with a flashy speech and a flash of light. But a mother's blade had already read his every move.

blocks.x: Antonio ambushes her with a powerful Arts of light, but Ingrid deliberately misses his vital point. She has no interest in giving him a dignified death. Leaving him to bleed out slowly, the mother walks away from the alley.
6. Antonio's Gamble

Antonio's true identity was a soldier from Bolívar. On the day a drunk noble's careless circle on an amnesty list decided who among his comrades lived and died, he came to hate this entire world, and the kind of power that lets someone casually circle a name that isn't theirs.
In Siracusa, in this filthy mud, is there really anyone with genuine dignity? Not among people like us, at least.

To Antonio, Leontuccio's ideal was nothing more than a privilege — an entire city handed over just because four young people got worked up around a round table. Just like that noble circling a name on the list. So he decided to seize that power for himself.

Under the cover of Carnevale, he smuggles a weapons-laden land ship into the city, aiming to seize the empty-guarded courthouse and city hall. It was a gamble to grab both the surface order and the underworld order at once, and take all of New Volsinii for himself. Not even the old Family head's pleas could stop him.
7. The Wolf Who Kicks Over the Table
But **Lappland** flips his whole plan over. This white wolf, abandoned by the head of House Saluzzo, has been riding around the city with Texas in a taxi, pulling every string from the shadows.

She hijacks Antonio's entire land ship, decks it out in ribbons and balloons, and drives it straight into the middle of the courthouse square. Then, up on deck, she kicks open the crates, raining guns, Arts units, and explosives down on the plaza like a downpour.

blocks.cap: Wearing a gown embroidered with roses and fresh blood, the white wolf takes off her mask. "Want to dance with me? The kind where, if you can't keep up with the steps, your tail gets cut off."

blocks.x: Family members, truckers, and swarms of ordinary citizens — weapons are now within everyone's reach. Lappland makes a bet with Texas: whether, before the midnight parade arrives, these two crowds will end up killing each other.
This is still Siracusa... still stuck in the mud. Don't underestimate this mud's magic, Texas. It can turn absolutely anyone into a wicked wolf.

In truth, Lappland had been pulling nearly every string of the night. Giving Ingrid the thread that led to Antonio, prying the operations her father Alberto controlled out of his hands, making sure Venezia's plot came to light for the whole world to see. Not really revenge — she just wanted to see for herself whether the mud really did turn people into wolves.
8. A Single Gunshot

Eirene lines up truckers armed with weapons to guard the courthouse. Wanting to avenge Somer, she begs Lavinia to condemn the Family in an emergency trial. But that would mean walking, of her own will, straight back into the very mud she'd fled.
Lavinia refuses to sign. She won't let the New City Management Act become anyone's tool for revenge. Lyudmila, too, fights desperately to keep Eirene from crossing that line.
In the very moment the two sides stand locked, weapons raised — a single gunshot rings out from somewhere. No one is hurt, but the sound is enough to reset the trigger on everyone's madness.

It's Eirene who lowers her crossbow first. Soon the midnight parade enters the square, and the two crowds stand side by side, weapons hidden back in their trucks, as if nothing had happened, welcoming the citizens. That gunshot, it turns out, was the sound of the old Family head, Venezia, cutting down the faction of his own traitor, Antonio.

blocks.cap: The old Family head puts a mask on his blood-soaked man before loading him into a car. New Volsinii has its own rules, and Venezia has its own.

blocks.x: 9. As the Curtain Falls
Lappland loses the bet. Texas wins — because justice and conscience, once again, sided with him.

Lappland pulls off Texas's mask and hands it over, passing the lead role of this finale on to him. Then she leaves, taking the Wolf Lords, bored of their tedious games, out into the wasteland with her — so their little pastime never stains Siracusa again.

blocks.cap: Amid the cheers of hundreds of thousands of citizens, Texas raises a hand on the land ship stage. He no longer refuses to be part of the show.

blocks.x: Disappointment presupposes expectation. ... I wanted to see a city, genuinely alive, right in the middle of the mud. I'm done running from it now. I'll stand with my friends.

Leontuccio, now awake, mixes into the parade and reaches the square to give the closing address. He admits, honestly, just how much New Volsinii still lacks, how many problems it still carries — and that even so, he'll keep walking this hard road.

blocks.cap: "Every problem we've run into only tells us that the people of New Volsinii need to keep moving forward."

blocks.x: Epilogue — The Ones Who Remain

After the festival ends, everyone's place falls into line. Lappland leaves her father Alberto behind bars and hands Saluzzo over to the Vellones, while Leontuccio's old friend Dmitri decides to bring the Vellones into the city, 'by your rules.'
Ingrid heads for Rhodes Island to see her daughter Lisa, while Capone and Gambino, their citizen scores wiped clean, dream of a fresh start with the yellow taxi Lappland left behind.

Red finally realizes the truth — that the 'Grandma' who'd been leading her all along was herself a Fang, and that she was a human chosen by the Fangs to hunt Fangs — both hunter and Fang at once. The whole story, and 'Grandma' herself, had been a lie. Rather than kill Red, Lyudmila takes her away alive, saying she'll go ask Kal'tsit for answers instead.
On the night of Carnevale, everyone wore a mask — those trying to drag things back to the old ways, and those refusing to let them, all tangled together.

New Volsinii isn't a vineyard guaranteed a harvest even in a bad year. It's still just one precarious 'possibility,' built on top of the mud. But as long as there are people willing to protect that possibility — people who've decided not to go back to the old ways — Siracusa's new era has, in effect, already begun.

act37side.tagline: The story of a street brawler who turns out to be the deposed king's daughter, and who still throws a punch first even after becoming chairwoman. And an old crown that nobody wants.

chars.name: Vina
chars.desc: Boss of the back-alley gang 'Glasgow Gang' in the Northport District, nicknamed 'Siege.' After the war ends, she becomes chairwoman of the Lundinium Council.

chars.name: Morgan
chars.desc: The Glasgow Gang's strategist and chatterbox. The very person writing this memoir.

chars.name: Indra
chars.desc: The Glasgow Gang's muscle, real name Hannah Jackson.

chars.name: Dagda
chars.desc: The Glasgow Gang's sculptor and homemaker. Rumor has it she once served as a palace guard.

chars.name: Baird
chars.desc: The Glasgow Gang's mediator. Chooses to stay behind alone in the Northport District when everyone else leaves.

chars.name: Delphine
chars.desc: A lady of the Duke of Windermere's house and a Rhodes Island medical officer. Vina's longtime collaborator.

chars.name: Diane Weber
chars.desc: A retired lieutenant who transferred from the allied forces to the Paragon Corps and fought alongside them.

chars.name: Fist
chars.desc: An engineer, formerly a Northport District factory worker. Leads a vigilante militia alongside his grandmother Catherine · Operator Stainless

chars.name: Catherine
chars.desc: The elder who's like a big sister to the factory workers. Prepares a decisive blow together with her grandson Fist.

chars.name: Elysio
chars.desc: A medical-supplies negotiator dispatched from Columbia's Red Cross Medical · Operator Bobbing

terms.name: Paragon Corps
terms.desc: The special unit led by Vina. Its members, regardless of status or origin, fought together to retake Lundinium.

terms.name: Glasgow Gang
terms.desc: Vina's old gang, based in Lundinium's Northport District.

terms.name: 'Milska'
terms.desc: An unidentified crime syndicate that came to dominate Lundinium's underworld after the war ended, and also its leader's codename.

terms.name: The King's Breath
terms.desc: A sword passed down through the Victorian royal family. After breaking in battle, it was reforged by the hands of workers.

terms.name: Steam Knight
terms.desc: The last armored warrior guarding the Victorian royal family. Charles Lynch was its final successor.

terms.name: Unicorn
terms.desc: A mysterious being passed down in Victorian legend. Said to have granted people's wishes from the shadows of history for centuries.

terms.name: Alistair II
terms.desc: Victoria's last king, deposed and executed by the dukes 26 years ago. Vina's biological father.

blocks.x: **Morgan**, strategist of the Lundinium back-alley gang 'Glasgow Gang,' decides one day to set this story down in a book with her own hand. The catch is that the gang's boss turned out to be the sole surviving blood relative of a king deposed twenty-six years ago — and from the moment that came out, everyone started calling her by a different name.

The street brawler 'Siege' became **Vina Victoria**, chairwoman of the Lundinium Council, once the war ended. But the crown above the throne still sits gathering dust, and the old comrades she used to share meals with, just months ago, are heading home one by one.
Then, one day just before Christmas, that old crown vanishes without a trace. The culprit is none other than the soldier who admired her more than anyone else in the world.
1. A back-alley gang's name-guessing game (and a bloodline no one knew about)

Before the war, Vina and her crew — **Indra** ('Hannah'), **Dagda**, and **Baird** — were just small-time hoodlums running the Northport District. Their daily routine was betting on nomadic-city name-guessing games at the bar, with the loser stuck cleaning the back alleys.

blocks.cap: A summer night, back when they'd giggle over nomadic-city name-guessing bets. No one knew it would one day be called 'history.'

blocks.x: That day's bet was 'name as many nomadic cities as you can.' When Indra got stuck, Vina tossed out the name of a dish from some B-movie they'd all watched together as a 'hint,' and once Indra barely scraped together ten, she shouted with excitement, "Vina, you really are the best leader!"

Then one day, word comes in that a corrupt factory foreman, backed by a duke, is abusing his workers. The gang goes to rob the factory without much thought, only to discover unidentified Originium weapons hidden away in a warehouse.

The incident spirals with an unexpected visitor. A stranger calling himself 'Count Moril' appears and blows Vina's cover, addressing her as 'Your Highness.' Vina beats the man down on the spot, but it's already too late — her crew now knows she's the daughter of the deposed King Alistair II.
Wherever you go, Glasgow will always be by your side. Don't forget — someday you'll have to come back and repay that debt to all of us.
That night, the gang decides to leave the city with her. Everyone, that is, except for Baird.
2. As the war nears its end, a single sword passes from soldier to soldier
After years in exile, Vina returns leading the Paragon Corps, all the way to the walls of Lundinium — to reclaim the homeland trampled by the Sarkaz.

On the day of the decisive battle, Vina hands her sword, 'the King's Breath,' to the wounded soldier **Diane Weber**. The sword passes from Diane to a laborer, from the laborer to a nameless Infected citizen, moving from hand to hand until it finally reaches the heart of the Nachzehrer King.
This too is the power of the Victorian people. Now — take hold of it!

Around that time, in the middle of the front lines, a box with no return address arrives for Vina. Inside is nothing but an old ceremonial robe bearing the Victorian royal crest, and a short note reading, "Altered it to fit your size."

blocks.cap: There's no signature. But Vina finally confirms it — Alredale is still alive.

blocks.x: As the tide of the war turns, factory worker **Fist** and his grandmother **Catherine** emerge from underground, driving the massive weapon 'the Crawler.' With the vigilante militia joining the fight, the final battle ends in an instant.

blocks.cap: 'The Crawler,' bursting up through the ground. The ace up Catherine's sleeve, prepared together with her grandson.

blocks.x: 3. The last Steam Knight, and thirty minutes no one else knows about

In front of the west hall of the royal palace, the last Steam Knight, Charles Lynch, stood frozen in place, having just felled the Nachzehrer. His heart had stopped long ago, but he'd kept standing on nothing but his vow to protect Victoria.
When Vina approaches and tells him his mission is over, he finally collapses, venting steam. Standing at his side, unexpectedly, is **Alredale** — someone everyone had believed dead.

blocks.cap: "Knight, your mission is over." She sends off the last Steam Knight, removing his armor as she does.

blocks.x: Your Majesty, all I have ever wanted is a peaceful life... for Victoria's sake.
Those watching from afar each whispered a different name. 'Siege.' 'Vina.' 'Alexandrina.' 'Victoria.' All different names — but in the end, one and the same person.
Amid the cheers, Vina walks alone into the hall. To this day, she has never told anyone what happened inside.
4. They came home, but not everyone made it back
As the walls are breached, every factory across the city vents steam at once to welcome the Paragon Corps home. Before that sight, Vina leaves just one line.
We're home.
But not everyone made it back. Baird, who'd stayed behind to protect the Northport District, lost his life in the final battle against the Sarkaz.

Two months after the war, Vina takes the chair of the provisional council. Morgan, Indra, and Dagda are still by her side, but each of them senses, little by little, that things aren't the same as before.

blocks.cap: "We're home." On that afternoon when steam rose all the way to the sky, this one line was where the whole story began.

blocks.x: Dagda sets up a few statues in the backyard of the Tower Knight's watchtower. On one of them is carved her real name, 'Isabelle Montague' — a sign that she was once one of the 'Tower Knights' who guarded the royal palace.
5. The back-alley brawler turned chairwoman, and the rumor that nearly destroyed her
'Chairwoman' Vina, crownless, wrestles with mountains of paperwork every day. Factory closures, medicine shortages, backlogged rations — a fight far more of a headache than the war itself.

One day, when the worker Matthew picks a fight, going on about 'economic rationality,' Vina's fist flies before she can stop it. It's the kind of thing she'd have once laughed off — but now she's the 'chairwoman,' watched by the entire city.

blocks.cap: A strange candy made by a kid caught mid-robbery. The one comfort Vina could accept without a second thought.

blocks.x: Fortunately, Fist steps in to calm things down. Together with his grandmother Catherine, he represents the workers and decides to stand by Vina's side so she doesn't have to carry everything alone.
Around this time, **Elysio** from Columbia's Red Cross Medical also arrives in Lundinium. He tries hard to somehow make up the medicine shortage, but his arrogant attitude keeps clashing with people.
6. A friend thought dead returns under a new back-alley name
'Milska,' a crime syndicate throwing its weight around Lundinium's underworld. The council chases its identity, but the clue, absurdly, is one Vina herself has been holding all along.
'Milska's' true identity is none other than Alredale Cumberland — the old noblewoman everyone had believed dead. Amid the war, she branded herself a traitor and was reborn under a back-alley name.

blocks.cap: The day she opened her eyes again among the ruins, Alredale chose to survive.

blocks.x: Vina eventually issues a manhunt order for 'Milska.' Rather than surrendering quietly, Alredale even refuses the duke's offer to take her back.
You made the right choice, and you're standing where you're meant to stand. Lundinium doesn't need any more heroes, Vina.
Come to think of it, that mysterious box of ceremonial robes delivered to Vina during the war — it, too, had secretly been a gift from Alredale.
7. Heroes getting by on liquor and painkillers
The war ending didn't mean everyone was fine. Army surgeon Lieutenant Shearer was getting through each day suppressing the aftereffects of an injury he'd suffered below the walls, with painkillers.
He writes to Vina again and again, but no reply ever comes. In the end, it's Delphine who finds him collapsed in the street.

blocks.cap: The purple flames he'd seen below the walls were still burning right in front of his eyes.

blocks.x: Sarah, the nurse who was injured alongside him while trying to save him, was also slowly losing her memory to the aftereffects. The wounds the war left behind lasted far longer than any bullet hole.
Delphine, almost scolding him, decides to send him home to Lincardine, with the words: "The war is over. Every soldier needs to go home."
8. The night of the 'Banquet Address,' the crown disappears

Ahead of the new year, Vina decides to give a radio address across the whole city. People call it the 'Banquet Address,' and for the first time in a while, whole families gather around the radio together.
But partway through the speech, the broadcast abruptly cuts out. Eleanor, the 'young duchess' of House Normandy, had gathered the other dukes and summoned Vina away privately.
The dukes play innocent, but there's only one point to it all: "The throne having sat empty for so long doesn't mean Victoria doesn't want a king."
As Vina returns to her office after the meeting, an urgent radio call from Indra comes flying in. "The crown in the throne room's been stolen!"
9. The one who stole the crown was, in fact, her most devoted fan

According to Morgan's investigation, Diane spent a full thirty minutes in the throne room that day. She never did say what she was thinking, but strangely enough, she didn't even run into the patrol changing shifts on her way out.

The crown's holder turns out, unexpectedly, to be Diane Weber — the very soldier who'd received 'the King's Breath' below the walls. Diane's infection had progressed so far that she had little time left. She simply wanted to see the hero she loved wear an actual crown.

Sharing a final drink in the cell, Vina confides in her, and her alone, the secret of that day: what she'd met inside the hall was the ghost of her dead father, and the fairy 'Unicorn,' said to grant people's wishes.

blocks.cap: "Alexandrina, have you finally returned?" What came from behind the throne was the voice of her dead father.

blocks.x: I chose nothing. Why should I be the one to choose the Unicorn?
Diane smiles quietly. It's nothing like the fairy-tale ending she'd once imagined, but she thinks it's not a bad ending, all the same.
10. A soldier on the gallows — but the story doesn't end there
The next day, Diane is executed for stealing the crown. Protests pour in from every corner of the city, but Vina refuses to place herself above the very law she created.
Instead, she stands alone the whole day before the protesting citizens, hammering out a settlement with the dukes — winning back rations, medicine, and rights, one by one.

blocks.cap: The chairwoman, sitting on the steps watching the sunset instead of at the execution ground. It meant she'd taken care of everything.

blocks.x: As evening falls, Vina, exhausted, sits down on the steps and watches the sunset alongside the citizens. A child who's gathered nearby asks, "Miss, will we get a king?"
That, I can't say. But not having a king isn't a bad thing. You'll understand once you're grown.
Watching that thoroughly unglamorous sight, Catherine thinks things will get better from here, all the same.

blocks.cap: A sunset that looked like it was burning all of Lundinium to the ground. Under it, duke and chairwoman alike looked equally small.

blocks.x: But the real twist came that very night. 'Milska' secretly smuggled out the condemned woman's body and handed it over to Elysio.
A few days later, Diane opens her eyes on a plain far, far from Lundinium. Part of her body was already hardening into Originium — but at least she was alive.
When that day comes, I will bring you home, back to Lundinium.
Epilogue. And once again, walking alone

A few days later, Morgan, Indra, and Dagda return to the Northport District boxing gym. In the beloved old neighborhood, now full of unfamiliar faces, their old friend Kador returns Baird's butterfly knife.
A single worn phrase was left on the blade: "I hope so too." What exactly Baird had hoped for, no one would ever find out.

blocks.cap: "Vina! You're back!" Morgan, nearly spraining an ankle leaping up off the couch.

blocks.x: That night, they all gather together for the first time in a while and raise their glasses — setting aside a seat for the one who isn't there, too.
Yeah, Vina. I hope so too... A toast, for Glasgow, for all of you.
The next morning, Vina changes alone, without any send-off, and leaves for the Central District. Morgan, pretending to be asleep, watches her back for a long, long while.

blocks.cap: A glass filled even for Baird's share. Everyone was awake, but no one went out to see her off.

blocks.x: The crown still lies discarded on the floor of the throne room. Diane, her name erased, is still breathing somewhere out there. Glasgow is still right where it's always been. Morgan puts the memories of that time into a single memoir, and closes its last page hoping that Vina will read these words someday. A hero's story ends the moment she puts on the crown — but Vina's story only truly began the moment she set it down.

act36side.tagline: A culinary road-trip tale of four adventurers who fell from the Dungeon into Terra, searching for a way home as they make their way toward Rhodes Island. Cooking up unfamiliar monsters to share with the people they meet, they prove again and again that food is memory. (Delicious in Dungeon collab)

chars.name: Laios
chars.desc: The party's leader, obsessed with monster cuisine. A sword-wielding fighter. (Delicious in Dungeon)

chars.name: Marcille
chars.desc: An elf mage. Falin's best friend, and utterly opposed to eating monsters. (Delicious in Dungeon)

chars.name: Senshi
chars.desc: A dwarf chef who roams the Dungeon. Always puts his full heart into every ingredient. (Delicious in Dungeon)

chars.name: Chilchuck
chars.desc: A halffoot locksmith. The party's voice of reason, and the one who bears the brunt of the trouble. (Delicious in Dungeon)

chars.name: Ceobe
chars.desc: A perpetually hungry Sami girl from Rhodes Island.

chars.name: Big Bob
chars.desc: An orc who runs an Originium Bug farm in the wasteland. The group's first mentor and benefactor.

chars.name: High Priest
chars.desc: A mystery-shrouded sage revered by the Adakris.

chars.name: Orc Cup
chars.desc: A Sami shaman apprentice. Unbothered by pretty much everything.

chars.name: Arges
chars.desc: A cyclops who foresees nothing but catastrophe.

chars.name: Mudrock
chars.desc: A Rhodes Island Operator. Big Bob's longtime friend.

chars.name: Amiya
chars.desc: Rhodes Island's leader. A caster who reads emotions.

terms.name: the Dungeon
terms.desc: The home of Laios's party. A dungeon teeming with monsters, shaped by the vast magic of the 'Mad Mage.'

terms.name: Falin
terms.desc: Laios's younger sister and Marcille's best friend. Devoured by the Red Dragon — reviving her is the true goal of this journey.

terms.name: Originium Bugs
terms.desc: Insect-like creatures with Originium shells that swarm the wasteland. Tricky to prepare, but excellent ingredients in the right hands.

terms.name: Bob's Gourmet Guide
terms.desc: A notebook Big Bob handed over. Lists whether all manner of Terran creatures are edible.

blocks.x: Having lost his younger sister **Falin** to the Red Dragon, Laios and his party were heading back into the **Dungeon** to revive her.
But when they opened their eyes, they found themselves in an unfamiliar land of sky, sun, and forest. Magic barely works here, and the spirits don't respond.
This was neither the Dungeon nor their own world — it was 'Terra.' And so the four travelers' culinary wanderings begin, in search of a way home.

The rule here is the same as in the Dungeon: live off the land. Which means catching and eating whatever unfamiliar monster is right in front of them. For Laios, who can't resist a monster, it's heaven. For Marcille, it's hell.
At first, Marcille grumbles that they must have been dragged into a living painting. It was half a joke — but looking back, she wasn't entirely wrong.
1. Originium bugs are delicious boiled
There's trouble from the very first encounter. What Marcille steps on, thinking it's a rock, turns out to be a swarm of **Originium Bugs**.

The heavily armored orc **Big Bob** turns out to be the farmer who raises these bugs. He washes the dangerous Originium Bugs five times over and masks their odor with herbs and chili, serving up a spicy stir-fry and grilled shells.

blocks.cap: Originium bug stir-fry washed five times over, shell steamed in beer, and pancakes made with foulbeast eggs. Their first meal on Terra.

blocks.x: Even Marcille, who'd been waving her hands in refusal because it's a monster, caves after one bite. It's thanks to Senshi, who carefully redid a dish that had nearly been rushed and thrown together. Never cutting corners, even in the smallest steps — that's what Senshi says makes cooking so appealing.

Big Bob hands the lost travelers "**Bob's Gourmet Guide**" and tells them about a place full of odd folks — **Rhodes Island**. Seeing them enjoy his cooking so much had given him a real boost of confidence too.
Missing home is only natural, if you're human! I don't understand most of what you told me, but that part, at least, I get.

By the way, if you raise Originium Bugs on barley husks, their shells grow a barley aroma, which can be brewed into beer. Feed them fruit, and their shells turn sweet enough for pancake batter. Every bit of Big Bob's culinary knowledge is solid gold.
2. The High Priest's medicinal brew (actually his bathwater)
The crocodile-headed **Adakris** they meet in the jungle are searching for the **High Priest**, who vanished in an explosion.

The conversation keeps going sideways. The Adakris call Senshi and Chilchuck 'Durin,' and have never heard of the sea or islands. No matter how much Marcille explains, all she gets back are looks that say 'is this person an idiot?' It just goes to show how far from home this land really is.

Meanwhile, Marcille causes an incident. She absentmindedly eats a berry that looks exactly like the raspberries Falin once gave her, and falls into a hallucination where all her companions look like the Red Dragon, sending her into a rampage.
Only the missing High Priest knows the cure. Senshi lures him out by boiling stew in a huge pot — apparently he loves a rowdy feast, so they all sing and dance together while they wait.

blocks.cap: "High Priest! We've prepared a feast, please come!" But the High Priest, as it turned out, was sitting inside the pot enjoying a bath.

blocks.x: It turns out the plump bird was the High Priest all along. The stew was actually his bathwater, but its horrendous taste is exactly what snaps Marcille back to her senses. The High Priest vanishes, leaving only the cryptic words, "You're headed the right way."
3. The golden carrot and the broken Light
At a failing factory in Rim Billiton, the owner can't afford to serve the traditional dish 'golden carrot' at the farewell dinner.

Thanks to a D32 steel magic tool called 'Light,' given to her by the owner, Marcille is able to use magic even on Terra. But the flash spell ends up transforming the corrosive mucus of an acidic Originium Bug into a sweet-and-sour, golden liquid.
In the process, Marcille slowly grasps how magic works in this world — that spells which kept cutting out with just a Dungeon staff actually manifest properly once channeled through D32 steel.

The problem is that Light gets corroded by the acidic mucus and breaks. Senshi, saying "it just changed shape," turns it into a pot without a second thought, and Marcille sobs, "Goodbye, my Light..." But thanks to that sacrifice, the golden carrot gets finished without needing a single pure-gold Originium Bug.

blocks.cap: The full golden carrot course, recreated with magically gilded mucus instead of pure-gold Originium Bugs. The factory's last supper, and a table set for a new beginning.

blocks.x: Thanks to this, the owner picks his abandoned dream back up. He'd finally realized that something of his, which no one had ever appreciated, had made someone smile.
4. Eyes that see catastrophe

While searching for the priest of snow, the party meets **Orc Cup**, an unbothered Sami shaman apprentice. Reading the 'Will of Sami' carved into tree bark, he tells them to seek directions from a northern recluse — a cyclops.

In Sami's black forest, the party meets a grotesque hornbeast with a hollow void where its face should be — a creature corrupted by a **demon**. As the cliff collapses, Chilchuck falls, separated from the rest.
It's **Arges** who saves Chilchuck — a cyclops who foresees nothing but catastrophe. He says he saw an unusually large amount of 'death' in Chilchuck.

And the prophecy comes true. Laios, Marcille, and Senshi, having eaten the corrupted hornbeast's meat, lose their faces and, swallowed by the void, mutter, "Through the north gate, back home," over and over.

blocks.cap: The three companions, faces erased by demonic corruption. As Chilchuck reaches out to them, they vanish into the void.

blocks.x: A chilling silence falls. But Chilchuck, without an ounce of fear, chases furiously after the three of them. Sure, they brought this on themselves — but they're still his companions.

In this forest, the party also meets Ceobe. This perpetually hungry Sami girl, who says she lives on Rhodes Island, tags right along, promising to guide them anywhere as long as she's fed properly. Unexpectedly, she becomes their guide back home.
5. The black slime inside the painting

The next stop is a village. A black slime called 'Muklyang,' which comes back to life no matter how many times it's killed, keeps attacking the village. Yet the villagers show no interest whatsoever in learning how to deal with it.

Laios spends days romping around with the Muklyang he's named Ayao, Xiaoqiao, and Touxian, even picking up their language. By the time the other three find him, he's already turned into a wild child, crawling on all fours and chatting with the Muklyang.
Then Laios realizes something: no habitat, no reproduction, no offspring — everything here just dies and comes back, over and over, a 'fake.' They're inside a painting.
The Muklyang were stray thoughts overflowing from the painting's owner. Senshi even makes jam and fruit wine from the vomit of a Muklyang fed and gotten drunk, and Marcille is horrified.

blocks.cap: Jam and fruit wine brewed from what a drunk Muklyang threw up. "Honey, too, is just something a bee spit up, Marcille."

blocks.x: The village's guide, 'Lai,' is impressed by Laios's way of reading truth in the relationships between all things — how even the Muklyang in the painting each have their own origin and destination. Then the painting's owner, noticing his village had been thrown into chaos, swings his sword and hurls the four of them right out of the painting. Noisy, but it was its own kind of send-off.
6. The starving seaside village
Far away in Iberia, the Abyssal Hunter Skadi drops in like a shooting star to strike down a bishop. The four travelers drift into that dark seaside village, Salviento.
Salviento is a dead village, its senses numbed by long-term hunger. Senshi can't bear to watch its people swallowing rotten shellfish without a second thought.

A missionary from the sea church lures the party into a dangerous cave, urging them to become 'brothers.' His real intent was to kill them, but the cave turns out to be a storehouse of fresh ingredients, teeming with a giant kraken and its symbiotic creatures.
Realizing that mimics exist on Terra too, Chilchuck has one disguised as a box eat the missionary. Laios, eyes sparkling, declares that even a mimic "tastes better the more you chew it."

The four of them lay out a spread of assorted seafood and share it with the starving villagers. In this village that had gone numb, people gather and laugh together for the first time in a long while.

blocks.cap: Sashimi salad and grilled seafood made from what they found in the cave. In the end, it was one warm meal that woke this numbed village up.

blocks.x: 7. When a cornfield turns into a popcorn field

In a wasteland settlement, misunderstandings pile up. A runaway drone, settlers trying to protect their supplies, and even the Rhodes Island Operator Reian all get tangled together into a full-blown brawl.
Marcille lures the drone with a familiar shaped like Laios. Even the settlers are horrified by the sight of 'familiar Laios,' tongue lolling out, sprinting on all fours.
But the drone's bomb happens to land right in the settlers' cornfield, and the entire field pops into popcorn. Drawn by the smell, Ceobe shows up and buries herself in the mountain of popcorn.

blocks.cap: A popcorn blizzard made by the bombing. People who were ready to kill each other by day sit around the same campfire grilling crab legs by night.

blocks.x: What emerges from that mountain of popcorn is Ceobe, whom they'd parted ways with back in Sami. The Operator Reian, who's come to pick her up, takes Ceo along and heads for Rhodes Island. Only then do the travelers realize their destination is right around the corner.
The people who fought so fiercely by day sit around the same fire at night, sharing monster meat. As far as ways to make peace go, it's very much a Terra-meshi thing to do.
8. The Rhodes Island cafeteria
At last, standing on the edge of a cliff, they see it — an enormous structure of steel, a city and another dungeon all in one, a ship that runs across the land: Rhodes Island.

**Mudrock**, who greets the party, turns out to be Big Bob's friend. She tells them that Big Bob, who cooked those hard-shelled bugs so deliciously, had really gained his confidence from people enjoying his food — along with the tenacious story of his life, in and out of prison before finally opening a general store in Sami.

After the mischievous High Priest sends the party flying all over the place via elevator (payback, apparently, for boiling him alive in that Originium Bug stew), the perpetually hungry Ceobe goes berserk. Said to have a thousand heads, she's soothed and calmed down by **Amiya**, who eases her emotions and wins her over with popcorn.

And Rhodes Island turns out to be an absolute paradise for Laios — packed as it is with Operators of every race imaginable. He seriously mutters about whether Lupo lay eggs, or whether a child of a dog beastman and a cat beastman would come out half-and-half, making everyone slowly back away from him.

He gets an earful for trying to roast chestnuts over Skyfire's flames, and Senshi grabs the Doctor to nag them about eating properly. Regular routine, balanced meals, and moderate exercise! And with all the ingredients and memories gathered along the way, they lay out a huge spread of side dishes in the cafeteria.

Chilchuck, squeezed in among giants like Hellagur, SilverAsh, and Hoshiguma, tips back a drink and gets mistaken for a minor yet again, while Marcille and Beeswax hit it off swapping hair-care stories. Before anyone notices, the Delicious in Dungeon crew and the Rhodes Island family have blended together seamlessly.

blocks.cap: A table laden with every ingredient and memory from the journey. The Delicious in Dungeon party and the Rhodes Island family sit together around one table.

blocks.x: Food is tied to memory. That's why eating the same dish again brings back the feeling of that moment.
Epilogue — Thanks for the meal
They still haven't found a way home. The Doctor and Kal'tsit agree to look into it over time, and Amiya invites the four of them to stay at Rhodes Island.

Ceobe is still hungry (well, she does have a thousand heads), and Big Bob, even after a stint in prison, has apparently started a new business in Sami. Wherever they are, everyone keeps living on, tenaciously.

Their journey through Terra became, in the end, a single dish. The Originium Bug stir-fry holds their first mentor, Big Bob; the stew holds the jungle's commotion; the golden carrot holds a factory that dared to dream again.

Looking back, what saved the four of them time and again in this unfamiliar world was Laios's endless love of monsters. That very eccentricity, which made everyone else recoil, became their way out at every moment of crisis.
Every time they taste that same aroma and flavor again, the people and the laughter of that moment will come rushing back — just like the day Marcille will finally revive Falin.
I'll have to tell her that on the way to save her, I met so many good people and made so many wonderful memories... I'm sure she'll be thrilled to hear it!

act35side.tagline: A story about stealing and waking a mummy just to outdo a little sister — and nearly burying an entire city in sand because of it. And the story of a guardian who waited a thousand years, only to close his eyes as a human being in the very last moment.

chars.name: Pepe
chars.desc: A history-obsessed archaeologist · daughter of the Padishah of Sargon · Pepe, true to her nickname

chars.name: Titi
chars.desc: Deputy director of the Menad Hamite Museum · longtime friend of Pepe and Anat · an incorrigible busybody

chars.name: Anat
chars.desc: Acting director of the museum · an anxious stickler for rules · Operator Papyrus

chars.name: Zubayr
chars.desc: The mummy at the museum's special exhibit · in truth, a royal guard officer from the age of the Shah · has been waiting for something for a thousand years

chars.name: Narantuya
chars.desc: A debt-ridden desert bandit leader · self-proclaimed chief · has terrible luck with subordinates

chars.name: Aspasia
chars.desc: A tourist from Minos · holder of ten silver medals · lives and dies by principle

chars.name: Razvar
chars.desc: Young manager of the Grand Bazaar's gem exchange · has a habit of weighing everything on a scale · Operator Sand Reckoner

chars.name: Mio
chars.desc: The cloud leopard Titi dotes on · has quite a temper · somehow a little suspicious

terms.name: Menad Hamite
terms.desc: A desert city that has clung to a single river since the age of the Shah. Just before the Flood Festival, its streets are packed with jewels and crowds.

terms.name: Sargon
terms.desc: The nation this city belongs to. Its monarch bears the title Padishah, and local lords are also called Padishah.

terms.name: Originium Arts
terms.desc: An ancient technology that manipulates memory, emotion, and the body through circuits engraved into gemstones. Traces of it are found all across Sargon.

terms.name: Chronicler
terms.desc: A hereditary post that records the history of the King of Kings at the Padishah's side. Once appointed, it's said one can never leave the Golden City again.

terms.name: The Golden City
terms.desc: Sargon's royal capital. Rumor has it that once someone becomes Chronicler and enters, they never come back out.

terms.name: Knightsmora
terms.desc: A nomadic warrior tribe said to march day and night. Upon reaching adulthood, each member must complete a solitary pilgrimage called the 'Pilgrimage.'

terms.name: The Pilgrimage
terms.desc: The Knightsmora's coming-of-age rite. A rite of passage that only ends once one journeys alone into the Trial Lands and returns alive.

blocks.x: Menad Hamite is a desert city that has held onto a single river and endured for a thousand years since the age of the Shah. With the Flood Festival just around the corner, every street is buzzing with jewels and crowds.

But the archaeologist **Pepe**, longtime friend of the museum's deputy director **Titi**, couldn't care less about the festival. In exactly two months, her younger sibling will leave for the Golden City to become 'Chronicler' in her place.

Before that happens, she needs to prove something. So Pepe's plan was this — secretly stuff an unidentified gemstone into the chest of the thousand-year-old mummy, the star of tomorrow's museum special exhibit.
1. A secret rendezvous with a mummy, and by next day he's imitating a person

Pepe borrows Titi's access pass and sneaks into the museum at midnight. A clue from her father's old research notes said that placing gemstones symbolizing the heart, lungs, stomach, and liver into different parts of the mummy's body would trigger something.

The moment she inserts the first gem, a blue light really does flicker in the mummy's chest. Of all times, a guard chooses that moment to make his rounds. Pepe scrambles to hide, and the mummy, still dazed, ends up greeting opening morning as is.

The next day, in the middle of the opening speech, someone gives acting director **Anat**'s ear a sly tug. She assumes it's just Titi's usual prank — but when she turns around, Titi is all the way over in the audience.

It was the mummy that moved. Pepe had spent all night teaching him to walk like a person (including how to be held, and how to fall over). After the resulting commotion, it comes out that the mummy can even talk, and he introduces himself as **Zubayr**.

He even has a personality — enough to turn walls into sand and pass through them, or to want to sneak off and see the city behind Pepe's back. Amid the chaos, in a corner of the museum, a tourist from Minos, **Aspasia**, is fuming over her homeland's artifacts being displayed all wrong.

Muttering "This is all wrong!" as she gathers the pieces one by one, Aspasia ends up accidentally becoming the hero who calmly guides the panicking crowd. The problem is, someone else was also eyeing this opening — the smuggler **Narantuya**, who's after the Padishah's daughter.

blocks.cap: The hand that seized the kidnapper's wrist belonged to the very mummy that, until moments ago, hadn't moved an inch from the couch.

blocks.x: Narantuya's plan was flawless — save for the one detail that the room she targeted already had a thousand-year-old bodyguard in it. Knocked unconscious, Narantuya gets dragged straight out the window.
2. A five-piece treasure hunt, with a spoonful of bribery on the side

Zubayr tells Pepe his story. He was the guardian who watched over the Shah's treasure vault, and the clues to its location are split across five gemstone fragments. One has already been found; four remain.

But he attaches one condition: he wants to see the sunrise. Pepe loads the mummy into a leaf-collection cart and sneaks him out to the riverbank, where the two sit side by side and watch the sun come up.
Greetings to you, Horakhet.

It was his first greeting in a thousand years. As it happens, they find the third clue at a harvest festival gathering by the river. It's the prize for a contest — but entering requires a rare collectible.

Pepe bribes and threatens the judge with a single golden slipper, a family heirloom, to secure 'exactly second place.' But it turns out the first-place entrant had also arranged in advance to concede the ranking, so Pepe ends up in first place after all.

Meanwhile, once she comes to, Narantuya mistakenly believes she's recruited the Minos tourist **Aspasia**, who saved her, as a "reliable subordinate." Aspasia enters the same contest — staking her ten silver medals on it.
Their conversations always go the same way. No matter what Narantuya asks, Aspasia's answer never changes.
Communication is the cornerstone of understanding.
3. The cloud leopard coughs up the fifth clue
The fourth clue arrives via an anonymous invitation. It's a perfect trap, right down to the line "come alone" — but Pepe goes anyway, unbothered.
Meanwhile, Titi is holding her beloved cloud leopard **Mio** and grumbling, "I wish Anat would just stick to what she believes." Then Mio suddenly coughs up a gem. It was the fourth clue.

Meanwhile, the special exhibit officially opens and the museum turns into a madhouse. Devoted fans showing up in wedding dresses, a kid tapping on the mummy's leg, visitors even tossing coins at him — and through it all, Zubayr diligently explains proper viewing etiquette.

It was Narantuya who set the trap. But once she realizes the bait gem is nothing special, she changes plans — selling a "fake mummy" (actually the real Zubayr, with his own consent) to gem exchange manager **Razvar** to raise a lump sum.
The deal goes off without a hitch. But right after, Razvar's pets Mio and Woof quietly exchange a few words. "Well done, Razvar." — a strange way of speaking, for mere pets.
4. Her sibling already left — and everyone already knew

As Pepe sets out to find the missing Zubayr, Anat belatedly confesses something to her: her sibling's departure date, supposedly two months away, has actually already passed. Their father pulled strings and sent the sibling off early, while Pepe was away on a site survey.
Her sibling left behind only one message.
She said she knows she'll never have the chance to see you again in this life, and that she just hopes you'll remember her voice.
Pepe realizes she never wanted to beat her sibling — she just couldn't stand the thought of her sibling being left alone in that city. And that her sibling felt exactly the same way.

Meanwhile, at Razvar's gem exchange, Zubayr reunites with Mio and Woof. All three have fully returned to their former selves. Zubayr now asks them for one favor — confirm Narantuya's bloodline, and test whether Pepe is worthy of joining them.
5. The mummy is remodeling the city without permission
Breaking news comes over the radio: metal dolls are smashing homes and looting gems all over the city. The culprits are the 'gem constructs' Zubayr had recovered.
Sand pours out of taps instead of water, and enormous sand-sculpted pillars and walls rise up along the streets. Zubayr is reconstructing the old city exactly as he remembers it.

Standing before it, Zubayr tells Razvar the city's true history. Three hundred years ago, a village of gem craftsmen who tried to protect him was wiped out entirely — and Razvar's ancestor was its sole survivor.

blocks.cap: "I will return that gem to you." A promise made three hundred years ago is finally kept, in Razvar's generation.

blocks.x: Ever since, the Razvar family has kept a scale set up at the Grand Bazaar, generation after generation, waiting for the day when all of Sargon's sand would turn to gems and gather in this city. Today was that very day.
6. They called it a test, but the method is swallowing you whole

Even as the city crumbles, the comedy doesn't stop. Titi carries Anat on her back as they flee, saying, "Don't look back — it's bad for your heart to watch your precious relief wall get smashed, Director," while Anat pours out prayers to the gods.

Just as Narantuya is trying to flee, the metal dolls chasing her suddenly go still — meaning she'd already passed the test. Woof confirms it: Narantuya really is a descendant of Karandu Khagan, the Shah's old nemesis.
Pepe, meanwhile, runs into a strange creature in an alley. The animal, which had looked small, instantly transforms into a giant beast and swallows her whole.

blocks.cap: A creature that had barely reached her calf now looked as big as the entire city.

blocks.x: 7. They call it a rite of passage, so why is everyone fighting for their life
In place of the swallowed Pepe, Narantuya is put to the test first. In a dream, she relives her own tribe's departure ceremony, but tries to run away, saying, "The Pilgrimage is just too terrifying."
In the real world, it's Aspasia who protects Narantuya — from an actual beast that attacks her. Even as they bicker through it all, the two slowly get to know each other.

Meanwhile, Anat and Titi solve the historical riddles posed by the rampaging gem constructs one by one, digging through Anat's favorite book, 《Journey Across the Earth》, while recalling something Titi told her back in school.
All you have to do is keep saying what you think is right, Anat.
Meanwhile, inside the beast's belly, Pepe finds herself standing on the shore of an unfamiliar lake. A boy emerges from the water, looks at Pepe, and calls out. "Sister."
8. A thousand years ago, why an older sibling began chronicling their father

From here on, there's no more joking around. Pepe watches Zubayr's entire life unfold from beginning to end — the boy drunk on victory in war, the young man who inherited his father's guard, and the two siblings left alone after their father vanished.

The older sister among the two siblings channeled her grief into compiling a history — recording every single word their father ever said, so that some descendant born long after could carry it on. That is exactly why Pepe's family has served as Chronicler, generation after generation.

It was never a glorious honor or a sacred calling. It was simply the heart of a child who missed her father. And the younger sibling, gravely wounded in the war, chose to permanently change his body into something else, rather than lie in bed and slowly die.

blocks.cap: That moment, so vivid even through the anesthetic, he had failed to forget for a thousand years.

blocks.x: Pepe realizes that what she'd been chasing — 'leaving a name in history' — was, in the end, no different from this.
If it must not be forgotten, then it must be remembered, however much pain comes with it.
9. The vault's real treasure was, in fact, himself

blocks.cap: The Shah's treasure vault. When the door opens, what appears isn't gold and jewels, but hundreds of ghostly recorded images.

blocks.x: Having passed the test, Pepe comes face to face with the real Zubayr. The vault is filled with recordings of their father, Lugalshargus, from when he was alive — an archive of longing for their father, left behind by his sister.
It's also revealed that Pepe's forehead ornament is identical to that of Zubayr's sister. It's no coincidence — Pepe's family carried on that sister's legacy.

blocks.cap: The Shah's words, preserved in the recording: "Pay attention to what made a hero a hero — that is, to the underside of history."

blocks.x: Zubayr offers to grant her eternal life. When Pepe refuses, he says calmly that his circuits carry an inscribed command to "stop the intruder," and that even he himself can no longer stop it now.

In the end, the two clash — a battle between a twenty-two-year-old archaeologist with a hammer and a guardian who has lived a thousand years. Pepe, eyes closed and tracking her opponent by sound alone, finally lands the decisive blow.
10. You have to let go, to set someone free
Where the hammer strikes, the gem in Zubayr's chest cracks — a fracture that can never be repaired. Smiling, he makes one last request: to be carried to the center of the gem.
Pepe, let go of my hand. I'd like you to let me go...

blocks.cap: "I have seen it, young Knightsmora. This is your dream. Go forward."

blocks.x: Water begins to fill the vault. Underwater, Zubayr slowly dissolves into a golden light, and closes his eyes with a smile.

blocks.cap: A body that had endured for a thousand years vanished as easily as if dissolved by a single drop of water.

blocks.x: At the same moment, on the other side of the underground channel, Narantuya dives into the abyss without Aspasia noticing. It was the only way to stop the fissure that would sink the city.
It was also Narantuya who pulled the fading, disoriented Pepe up out of the water.

blocks.cap: "Luck really is on my side. Still managed to fish one out!"

blocks.x: Gold and treasure cascade like a waterfall from the vault door Narantuya threw open. The piled-up treasure fills the crack in the ground, saving the city from sinking.

blocks.cap: Atop the golden waterfall, Narantuya laughed as if dancing.

blocks.x: The city survived. The constructs came to a stop again, and clear water began flowing through the channels once more. Soaking wet, Pepe murmurs.
Horakhet, please grant his soul rest.
Afterward, in the radiant city
Anat decides to step down as acting director. Instead, she becomes a pen pal of the traveler Erikson, whom she's long admired, and plans to see the world. Titi formally takes over the museum.
Narantuya's subordinates Ayazi and Ayani are formally hired at the museum. Narantuya herself declares, "There's no more debt now," freeing her old crew, and sets off alone on her next Pilgrimage.

blocks.cap: The parting gift she left behind was a pair of Minos golden goblets from the museum.

blocks.x: All of this — I chose it myself!
Mio makes an unexpected announcement, revealing that she's been able to talk all along, and declaring she's chosen Titi as her "keeper." Woof decides to stay behind in this still-living city.

blocks.cap: On a sunny afternoon regained after two days, everyone gathers in front of the fountain.

blocks.x: A few days later, a messenger from the Golden City appears before Pepe, delivering an invitation — the King of Kings himself is inviting her to the city.
The city sparkles again, just as before. The river still flows the same. The only difference now is that beneath its currents, someone who waited a thousand years is finally resting in peace.

act34side.tagline: The route drilled through to drive out the Sea Terrors — the irony is, it can only be completed by joining hands with them. Along that road, some stay home, and some choose never to come back.

chars.name: Kal'tsit
chars.desc: The doctor who leads Rhodes Island. She heads into Ægir's deep sea alongside the Doctor, disguised as part of the Iberian delegation.

chars.name: Skadi
chars.desc: An Abyssal Hunter born in Ægir. She vanished five years ago and returns home carrying the Sea Terrors' genes within her body.

chars.name: Specter
chars.desc: An easygoing Abyssal Hunter. Once, he was a boy who dreamed of becoming a sculptor.

chars.name: Gladiia
chars.desc: The de facto leader of the Abyssal Hunters. She's treated as Ægir's honorary legion commander and Chief War Architect.

chars.name: Ulpianus
chars.desc: An Abyssal Hunter who vanished alone. He keeps his distance even from his old comrades, chasing a truth of his own.

chars.name: Secunda
chars.desc: Commander of the Miliarium Marine Patrol. Once a researcher who dreamed of becoming an Abyssal Hunter. · Operator Underflow

chars.name: Irene
chars.desc: An envoy from the Iberian tribunal. She came to see with her own eyes what's happening in the deep sea.

chars.name: Jordi Fontanarrosa
chars.desc: An Iberian clerk from Granfaro. Though he carries Ægir blood in his veins, he knows almost nothing about his ancestral home.

chars.name: Clementia
chars.desc: Miliarium's consul and Chief War Architect of the Sea Route Project.

chars.name: Tullia
chars.desc: A data analyst for Miliarium's Sea Route Project. Tending endangered seaweed is her only joy.

terms.name: Miliarium
terms.desc: Ægir's mobile undersea city. It has sailed all the way to the continental shelf for the Sea Route Project.

terms.name: the Sea Route Project
terms.desc: A national project to open a safe sea lane connecting the Ægir homeland to the continent. Developing a weapon to drive out the Sea Terrors is at its core.

terms.name: Abyssal Hunters
terms.desc: Ægir's program of combatants remade by implanting Sea Terror genes. They were believed wiped out after the operation to hunt the 'Firstborn' five years ago.

terms.name: the Deep Sea Church
terms.desc: A faction that worships the Sea Terrors and operates covertly within Ægir. Less an organized creed than the shared despair of scattered believers.

terms.name: the Self-Adaptive Interface
terms.desc: A modified device inside an Abyssal Hunter's body that suppresses the Sea Terror genes from running wild. It had gone unmaintained for five years.

terms.name: Firstborn
terms.desc: A colossal entity at the root of a Sea Terror swarm. 'Ishamla,' slumbering inside Skadi, is one of them.

terms.name: Weimani
terms.desc: The name the Sea Terrors use for themselves. It's depicted less as an individual than as a single, vast will.

blocks.x: Five years ago, the Abyssal Hunters who went after the 'Firstborn' Ishamla were believed wiped out. But three of them — Gladiia, Skadi, and Specter — come back alive. And they carry traces of the Sea Terrors inside their bodies.

Right on cue, Ægir — long silent — reaches out to Iberia first. Kal'tsit and the Doctor, Iberian clerk Jordi, and tribunal envoy Irene, a delegation from the land, head to the deep-sea city of Miliarium together with the Hunters. Their goal: the 'Sea Route Project' — opening a path linking sea and land.

But that path is shaky from the start. There's a missing researcher, a Hunter who's vanished, and one question nobody dares say out loud. In the end, this story closes on the different answers each of them gives to that question.

blocks.cap: An Ægir biodrone appears on the Iberian coast. The two civilizations' first official contact begins like this — with an unfamiliar voice.

blocks.x: 1. The Ones Who Were Dead Come Back, and Everyone Reaches for an Interrogation First

Miliarium is a completely alien place to Jordi. People eat without knives, do laundry without undressing, and just float through the air. The scholar **Abitus** kindly explains it all, but the more Jordi listens, the more confused he gets.

When a patrol robot called a 'Helper' tells him to clear the area, Jordi reflexively apologizes in the most formal terms he knows. Abitus laughs it off — no need for that, he says, it's just a guide machine.

Jordi even tries to trace his own roots here. The legendary shipwright Breogán once passed through this city. But looking at his face in the old footage, Jordi can't find a single trace of resemblance.
