What's really loud, though, is what's happening with the Hunters. A council convenes in the city's massive circular arena. Over the traces of Sea Terror left in the bodies of the three who returned after five years, the whole city stands before them, firing off questions.

Fortunately, their old comrade **Blandus** steps up to vouch for them. He explains that every Abyssal Hunter's body carries a 'Self-Adaptive Interface' that suppresses the Sea Terror genes, and that the changes they're seeing now all fall within expected limits.
They will not turn into Sea Terrors.
Not everyone buys it, of course. A researcher from the Academy raises a doubt — the information Ulpianus brought back about 'multiple Firstborns' fits together a little too neatly.
An explanation that fits this perfectly is either the one true answer, or one crafted to answer every possible doubt.

Then, from the shadows of the stands, someone threatens to strangle the Doctor. It's Ulpianus, the Abyssal Hunter who's been missing for five years — the most suspicious figure in this whole story. He vanishes after leaving only one warning: watch Skadi closely.

After the hearing, Skadi and Specter take the Doctor sightseeing around the city. Specter used to want to be a sculptor, they say, and Skadi used to be the technician who single-handedly managed the whole city's lighting. Small, warm bits of the past they never got to share five years ago.
2. A Quiet Disappearance, a Loud Premonition

The city's data analyst, **Tullia**, disappears. Marine Patrol Commander Secunda takes charge of the investigation, and Irene volunteers to help. As it turns out, Secunda once wanted to become an Abyssal Hunter herself — her application was simply turned down.

Secunda and Blandus bicker like an old teacher and student — over artificial tears, over tactless jokes. But beneath that warm banter lies a quiet resentment neither of them has ever asked the other about.

In fact, Secunda once applied to undergo the Abyssal Hunter modification herself. Her mentor had been the program's first Hunter, and she could only watch as he came back battered again and again. But her application was rejected, and she was never even given a reason.
Meanwhile, Ulpianus, still in the shadows, quietly lets the Doctor in on something: the one they killed in that battle five years ago wasn't the only 'Firstborn.'
There are 'multiple Firstborns' out in the sea.

Meanwhile, a Sea Terror attack wipes out two whole fleets, leaving only 26 survivors. The Sea Route Project was already in trouble. Gladiia bitterly admits that the war plan drawn up five years ago never left room to admit it could be wrong.
3. The One Who Loved the Jade Vine

Tullia, as it turns out, was an unremarkable person. Cultivating an endangered seaweed was her hobby, and when the city abandoned its volcanic base, she was the one who secretly moved her little plants out to keep raising them.
Her colleagues Lucilla and Cassia worry about her without really knowing her well. But one offhand remark from Cassia quietly redirects Secunda's suspicion toward Tullia — without anyone noticing.
It turns out the Deep Sea Church used her as bait. They framed her and drove her out of the city, and Tullia fell through a drain in the circulation system, where the Sea Terrors took hold of her.
Days later, Irene and Secunda find Tullia, now half turned into a Sea Terror. She's still clutching her nutrient solution, dragging herself painfully toward the crate of seaweed she used to tend.
The moment she sees her own reflection in the aquarium glass, Tullia stops struggling. Secunda sees her off with the very lullaby their Commander used to sing to put the squad to sleep.
Her journey has ended ♪ Her home is right before her eyes ♪
4. The Scariest People Are the Good Ones

While the Hunters are undergoing follow-up surgery, the Deep Sea Church raids the operating room. They're driven off without much trouble — but the real traitor turns out to be Blandus, standing right there.

For five years, he had been secretly researching a phenomenon first observed by accident in Ulpianus's body — a balance point where Sea Terror genes and human biology stop rejecting each other. He quietly embedded that theory into the Sea Route Project's Tier-4 weapon.
Instead of a weapon to kill Sea Terrors, he wanted to build a future where humans and Sea Terrors coexist. Blandus believed that was the true way to save the Hunters.
The Abyssal Hunter program failed, but the Hunters still have a chance to fulfill their purpose — with a solution.
Ulpianus comes to confront him. Neither the excuse of good intentions nor their long friendship makes any difference.

blocks.cap: Ulpianus and Blandus — the last conversation between two men taught by the same mentor.

blocks.x: You have no right to die with regret or lingering attachment.

To the very end, Blandus insists he wasn't wrong — that all he did was build a real bridge between two races, humans and Sea Terrors. Ulpianus listens to all of it without so much as a change in expression.
The moment the weapon Blandus tampered with activates, all of Miliarium begins to pay the price.
5. A Nest Blooms in the Heart of the City
An entire beacon tower turns into a Sea Terror nest. Deathblooms sprout up all across the city, and Deep Sea Church believer **Cassia** reveals herself and tries to destroy the city's core units.
The one who stands in her way, unexpectedly, is her longtime junior **Lucilla**. The junior who was always just cracking jokes grabs hold of the safety valve, risking her own life.
Because — because there's no road left behind me.
Cassia refuses to bend her convictions to the very end. She says that when a person clings too tightly to a value, the moment that value collapses, they collapse right along with it.
When a human life becomes bound to some value, sooner or later it's fated to collapse meaninglessly.
Jordi stays behind with Ulpianus in the beacon tower's central control room. Fixing the facility management frame to detach the whole tower and sink it is the only way out.

While he repairs the device, Abitus holds off the Sea Terrors with nothing but a single lantern. And Jordi finally asks the question he'd never dared to before — was it really Ægir's future that drove you to despair, or was it yourself?

blocks.cap: A young clerk and an old scholar, holding out by the light of a single lantern.

blocks.x: The tower finally sinks, and Ulpianus chooses to stay behind inside it. He wanted to find out — with no one else watching — just how close he'd already come to the Sea Terrors.
6. The Scholar Who Walked Out of a Story
A single Sea Terror appears in the middle of the arena, speaking with the voice of 'Martus,' a legendary scholar who vanished 200 years ago. He was the very origin of the Deep Sea Church.
Martus recognizes Kal'tsit. By an incredibly old name, no less.
The Doctor, too, looks like he half-recognizes him. Martus says the Doctor once visited this place, deep in the sea, long ago.
But the Doctor has no memory of it left. Kal'tsit cuts in to answer for him — for some reason, she says, the Doctor doesn't remember most of that time.

blocks.cap: The colossal being Martus says he encountered deep within the ruins. A single tentacle from it changed the entire course of his life.

blocks.x: Kal'tsit...... AMa-10, as recorded in the archives. You surprise me.
He says he glimpsed the fall of an ancient civilization, and a premonition of an even greater ruin to come. That's why he staked his life on raising the Sea Terrors, on his species' 'survival.'
How can one speak of civilization at all, without survival?
Clementia cuts the conversation short at swordpoint. But Martus's final words have already lodged themselves in everyone's heart.
7. The Route Meant to Drive Out the Sea Terrors Opens With Them Instead
Miliarium sheds its own diseased sections and rises to the surface. On the far side of the city, a true storm of madness — a living swarm of Sea Terrors — surges in right alongside it.

blocks.cap: A city that casts off its own heart to rise alone. On its far side, the true catastrophe rises together with it.

blocks.x: Iberia's long-lost lighthouses flicker back to life one by one — places that had lain silent as the dead for over half a century. Watching that light, Saint Carmen quietly repeats to herself: survival or ruin, there is no other road.

But then something no one expected happens. The floating machines of the beacon tower, dead for 200 years, are reborn in the hands of the Sea Terrors. The Sea Terrors cradle these newborn machines like their own children and swim alongside them.
As those lights link up one by one, a route spanning the entire ocean is finally completed — not the path humanity meant to build, but one built together with the Sea Terrors.

blocks.cap: Billions of lights blanket the sea like a net. The sea route has finally opened — like this.

blocks.x: In truth, the hope Clementia pinned on this route was modest from the start. To show, with their own eyes, that Ægir's main forces could still go to war alive — to a hundred thousand soldiers and civilians, and to Granfaro's punitive army. That alone would have been enough.
Meanwhile, through its drone fleets, Ægir sends its first-ever official declaration to every civilization on land: that humanity must form a single community — under Ægir's leadership, no less.
Under Ægir's leadership, we will surely overcome this crisis together.
Gladiia says there's one more task left now — to capture their old comrade Ulpianus, and hold him accountable, for the last time, as an Abyssal Hunter.
Epilogue: Each Their Own Next Step

**Horatia**, Gladiia's mother and consul of the Scientific Development Planning Bureau, appears via hologram. Now that the route is open, Ægir declares it will 'help' Ursus, Kazdel, and Bolívar alike.
Kal'tsit immediately spots the arrogance hiding behind that goodwill. No one, she says, can guarantee that this help will stay just help.

blocks.cap: Horatia appears with a smile on her face. Beneath the warmth, the calculations are already in place.

blocks.x: Clementia, for her part, keeps Skadi and the Doctor's secret hidden even from Horatia. She and Kal'tsit have already agreed — the moment their identities come to light has to be a moment the two of them choose for themselves.
In a corner of an alley, Skadi runs into Martus alone. He tells her that she might be Ishamla's last hope.
Instead of answering, Skadi swings her sword to cut the conversation short. But even after she sheathes it, her hand is trembling, just slightly.

blocks.cap: Skadi, wearing an expression that says she doesn't even want to guess at the true nature of what she carries.

blocks.x: Now that the route is open, Abitus says he'll set off for even more distant, unknown seas, and asks Jordi to come with him. You don't need to prove your bloodline, he tells him — the first time he's ever said anything so sincerely.
We don't need to prove where we belong.

Jordi still hasn't decided whether to sail off into the unknown with Abitus, or stay in Iberia and shoulder a heavier responsibility. Saint Carmen simply tells him, calmly, that either way, it's his choice to make.
And Ulpianus walks deeper into the Sea Terrors' den, chasing his answer — toward the voice calling out to him.

blocks.cap: Where Ulpianus heads is the deepest part of the den, where no light reaches.

blocks.x: This is how the story began — a Hunter stepping onto the shore, and the question of why he hesitated, why he seemed so uneasy. Now, we think we understand.
Coming back, and having truly come back — those might be, in the end, two completely different stories.

act33side.tagline: A story about waking a savior who had slept for ten thousand years and entrusting him with the Sarkaz's future — only to learn he had already lived through this world's ending once before. And the final farewell of a demon queen who, knowing all that, still called him by name as she died.

chars.name: Theresa
chars.desc: The half-blood Sarkaz demon queen who built Kazdel. An idealist who founded the organization 'Babel' to break down the walls between races and nations.

chars.name: Theresis
chars.desc: Theresa's twin and lifelong blade. The general who holds military power in Kazdel.

chars.name: Ascalon
chars.desc: A feral child found in a snowstorm. Taken in and raised by Theresis, she's an assassin with an expressionless face.

chars.name: Kal'tsit
chars.desc: An Originium lifeform who has lived for over ten thousand years. Babel's co-founder and the Doctor's oldest companion.

chars.name: Odda
chars.desc: A boy raised in Kazdel's back alleys. A warm-hearted troublemaker who secretly looks out for students of other races.

chars.name: Manfred
chars.desc: Ascalon's childhood friend, raised under the same teacher. An optimist who trusts words more than swords.

chars.name: Amiya
chars.desc: A young Cautus girl who alone survived a cargo transport accident. The Doctor takes her in, and she grows up in Babel's embrace.

chars.name: 'Scar Eye'
chars.desc: The one-eyed giant mercenary boss who runs the Scar Market. Famous for buying and selling prophecies.

chars.name: Fortune
chars.desc: An easygoing mercenary who never quite lives up to his name. He lost his wife and raised his son Odda alone.

terms.name: Babel
terms.desc: The organization Theresa built within Kazdel. People gathered under the ideal of solving the Originium problem and Oripathy together, transcending the boundaries of race and nation.

terms.name: Kazdel
terms.desc: The nomadic city the Sarkaz have hauled along for over a hundred years. A homeland where the glow of the Soul Furnace and the scars of war are etched together.

terms.name: the Military Council
terms.desc: A new governing body created by Theresis. It seeks to abolish the privileges of the old monarchies and unite the Sarkaz into a single military force.

terms.name: the Scar Market
terms.desc: An underground mercenary market in Kazdel run by 'Scar Eye.' Commissions, information, even prophecies — anything can be bought here, for the right price.

terms.name: the Black Crown
terms.desc: A symbol of authority passed down through generations of Sarkaz demon kings. It holds vast information, and the power to command Sarkaz souls.

terms.name: the Continuance of Civilization
terms.desc: The name of the Originium plan left behind by the old civilization the Doctor once belonged to. To the Doctor, it's the last hope he's waited ten thousand years for — but to this world, it may mean something else entirely.

blocks.x: There's a final farewell left behind by someone who had already sensed their own death. Deep within one of Kazdel's ships, Kal'tsit was replaying that voice over and over again.

In the year 1094, the Sarkaz nomadic city of Kazdel flew two banners. One was Babel's banner, calling to tear down the walls between races. The other was the Military Council's banner, calling to reclaim what had been lost.

And caught between the two was someone who had slept for ten thousand years before waking. Everyone believed he was a savior who brought victory with him — but what he truly wanted to save may not have been this world at all.
1. In a City of Demons, an Idealist Is Born
Kazdel is the nomadic city the Sarkaz have hauled along for over a hundred years. Beneath the glow of the Soul Furnace, its people lived carrying war and discrimination like an inescapable fate.

The ones who built Kazdel into what it is today were the seamstress **Theresa** and the swordswoman **Theresis** — half-blood twins raised together since childhood. People revere Theresa as one of the legendary 'Six Heroes,' but she's never much cared for the title.
Theresa founds 'Babel,' an organization meant to transcend the boundaries of race and nation. The name is grand, but the goal is simple: care for Kazdel's sick, and teach its children.
Of course, not everyone welcomed it. **'Scar Eye'**, the one-eyed giant who runs the underground market known as the Scar Market, believed war itself was his real bread and butter.
Since when have the Sarkaz ever lived apart from war!

On top of that, Scar Eye has an unusual gift — an Arts that lets him glimpse fragments of the future. Yet he only ever used it to make money, believing all along that he alone could bend his own fate.

blocks.cap: A name both foreign and familiar: Kazdel. Called a den of demons, but to the Sarkaz, it's a home that stirs up complicated feelings.

blocks.x: 2. Waking a Guest Who Slept Ten Thousand Years (No Refunds, No Returns)
Babel actually has two true heads: the demon queen Theresa, and the mysterious being at her side, **Kal'tsit**.

Kal'tsit says there's someone who can solve the Originium problem, and quietly opens a sealed coffin no one else knows about. What wakes inside is a being from a civilization destroyed ten thousand years ago — one who calls himself 'the Doctor.'
Bewildered by this strange world, the Doctor still adapts quickly. Kal'tsit watches him with relief — but a shadow he can't put into words has already fallen across the Doctor's face.
Kal'tsit hides the Doctor's existence from Babel's people for a while, saying there's no need to tell anyone until Theresa and the Doctor have had a chance to size each other up.
For the Doctor's safety, and for the safety of Babel's members, I erased the Doctor's boarding record.

blocks.cap: A door opened after ten thousand years of silence. Is what walked out of it a savior, or...?

blocks.x: 3. One Child Fallen from a Burdenbeast (Two, Counting the Doctor)
Thanks to Kal'tsit's arrangement, the Doctor spends six months traveling across Terra. Along the way, he meets **Amiya**, a young Cautus girl who alone survived a cargo caravan accident.
Amiya was a kid who tried to scare off a Sandbeast by mimicking its cry, badly. When the Doctor solves the problem with an actual whistle, Amiya's eyes light up and she insists on trying it herself.
The peace doesn't last. Minutes after climbing onto a Burdenbeast's back, the Doctor ends up face-first in a puddle.
I'm... already holding on... as tight as I can!

blocks.cap: The Burdenbeast dismount incident. The Doctor's dignity died about halfway here.

blocks.x: The Cautus mercenary **Savage**, who'd been traveling with them, is reluctant to part ways with Amiya, but in the end the Doctor slips away silently in the night — telling himself it's so he won't drag her somewhere even more dangerous.

Amiya wants a promise from him that they'll meet again. Even though she vaguely knows a child with stone growing in her body doesn't have much future left, she says she wants to stay by his side anyway.
Amiya, we'll have a future.
4. Two Sword Hilts Picked Up in a Storm
Theresis has two disciples: **Ascalon**, the feral child found in a snowstorm, and **Manfred**, who came out of the Military Council's foster home.
The two don't dislike each other, exactly — it's more that Ascalon ties Manfred up with rope and strings him up every single time. And yet Manfred keeps coming back for another round.
Theresa was especially gentle with Ascalon. She'd tell her to touch her horns, reassuring her that she too was Sarkaz, no different. Theresa was the one person Ascalon never showed hostility toward.
You don't have to be afraid of me. I have no hostility toward you. See? Go ahead, touch my horns — we're all the same.
But as they grow up, their paths diverge. Ascalon becomes an assassin for Babel; Manfred becomes a minister of the Military Council.

blocks.cap: "Your Highness, look, I won again!" Ascalon's victory report and Manfred's humiliation. Both were a regular occurrence.

blocks.x: 5. Two Banners, a Fracturing Family
Theresis founds the 'Military Council,' vowing to unify Kazdel as one. It's an attempt to abolish the old monarchies' privileges and bind the Sarkaz together into a single force.
The problem is the price of it. To those who carried Kazdel's old grudges, Babel's coexistence with other races was a thorn in the side, and riots and assassinations follow one after another.

Right in the middle of that whirlwind is **Odda**, a boy from the back alleys. The name was originally his badly injured mother's, but his father, **Fortune**, passed it on to his son as he left for revenge — perhaps because he wanted the name to keep being spoken, even if only through his son.
Years later, a grown Odda ends up secretly hiding 'Teacher,' an advocate for other races. But the assassin hired to kill that very Teacher turns out to be none other than his father, Fortune.
Even knowing it's the person his own son is trying to protect, Fortune can't sheathe his blade in the end. And not long after, Fortune himself never comes back either.
These upheavals eventually escalate into disaster when Babel's office takes a direct bombardment. Babel suffers its worst damage since the war eighteen years earlier, and decides to leave Kazdel.
Amid all this, the Banshee queen Rakelamalin entrusts her daughter Aipanil to Theresa. This is how the foreshadowing quietly grows, unnoticed by anyone.

blocks.cap: A procession turning its back on home. People wrapped themselves in banners torn from the city walls, etching into memory a city they might never see again.

blocks.x: 6. The Doctor's Secret Diary (No More Jokes From Here)

The Doctor keeps a record no one else has ever discovered. In the words he murmurs alone in front of the camera are the name of a civilization lost ten thousand years ago, and the name of someone he once loved.
The Doctor's true goal is for 'Originium' to blanket this world completely. He believes that's the only way the civilization that fell ten thousand years ago can ever continue on.
The harder Theresa works to save people with Originium, the more the Doctor burns up inside. Whether he's truly a savior, or the one strangling this world's last breath — he can't find an answer.
Priestess... what should I do?
Kal'tsit, meanwhile, only worries that the Doctor's body keeps growing thinner — with no idea he's been injecting Originium into himself as an experiment.
I don't want you wearing yourself out right now, of all times. Take a little better care of yourself.

What truly exhausted the Doctor wasn't his body, but his heart. A promise made long ago with his lost love, Priestess, and the guilt of having to deceive Kal'tsit and Theresa to keep it — that was what was eating away at him.

blocks.cap: "Originium must rewrite everything, without fail." A plan only the Doctor knows is slowly coming to light.

blocks.x: 7. A Deal Struck Beneath a Rain-Soaked Hero's Statue
The Doctor secretly goes to see Theresis. Beneath the statue of the twins who founded Kazdel, the two of them begin a deal no one else knows about.
The Doctor's condition is simple: bring down Babel, kill Theresa, and he'll end the war right here.
Theresis asks him back: what must be destroyed to build the ladder he wants? The Doctor, knowing full well this is anything but light business, answers that he's already hesitated far too long.
What will you destroy to build your ladder, {@nickname}.
Instead of accepting the offer, Theresis leaves him with one line: if there's anyone toying with the Sarkaz's fate, he'll be the one to pass judgment himself.
If you're worried there's no one to rein in a spirit looking down on everyone else from on high, then I'll be the one to step forward.

blocks.cap: A shadow left alone in the rain. The hero's statue was still gazing off into the distance, as ever.

blocks.x: 8. The Countdown, and a Defense Wall Erased by His Own Hand

Around this time, Ascalon settles an old debt. Scar Eye, the boss of the Scar Market who'd made a living peddling the scent of betrayal, ends up losing his life at the hands of his own underlings. Ascalon comes and finishes him off with the last blow.
Meanwhile, Babel finally launches its operation to retake Kazdel — a once-in-a-lifetime chance, timed to strike while Theresis's main forces are away in Victoria's Londinium.
Under the Doctor's command, the operation runs flawlessly. As the countdown ticks down, Babel's old comrades — Ace, Scout, Mantra, Pith, even Logos — all move in perfect concert, each in their place.

But at the very moment the flagship becomes most defenseless, it's the Doctor himself who disarms its defense systems. While Theresis's assassins silently board the ship, Amiya — knowing nothing — is knocking on Theresa's door, a picture book held to her chest.
9. The Child Who Inherited the Crown

When the assassins burst in, Theresa wraps Amiya in black foam and puts her to sleep, then faces them alone. Even as she cuts them down one by one, she calls each of them by name. Luca, Liens, Levin...
These were assassins who'd erased their own horns and names — but she, at least, would not forget them.
I remember your names.
With her last strength, Theresa transfers her 'Black Crown' to the sleeping Amiya. Then she asks the Doctor, who arrives too late, whether he came to find Amiya — or...
The Doctor can't answer. The assassins pouring in behind him were already the answer.
So this was your answer, Doctor...

blocks.cap: A black blade pierces through Amiya. But what that sword left behind wasn't pain — it was warmth.

blocks.x: Even as she dies, Theresa doesn't resent the Doctor. Instead, she erases his memories thread by thread, so he can live again as his whole self.
Find yourself.
Farewell, and a Beginning
With her last breath, Theresa waits for Kal'tsit. Then, borrowing the body of the sleeping Amiya, she leaves one final farewell — just once.
I killed him. And I saved him too.
The crown, she says, is neither a ruler nor a shackle — it's merely a key toward 'the Continuance of Civilization.' She asks that Amiya not be told, not yet.
Kal'tsit decides to send the Doctor outside of Rhodes Island, to Chernobog in Ursus. She doesn't want him — someone who'll wake with no memory — left in danger any longer.
**Ascalon**, arriving too late, breaks down in front of the empty room. This time, no matter how long she waited, Her Highness never came out to greet her.
Damn it! Damn it! I'll make them pay for this... I promise.
And the story turns back to 196 years earlier. To the day, atop the ruins where an old demon king had died, when a still-young seamstress and swordswoman first shared the crown between them.
We are equals, forever.

blocks.cap: A crown raised together atop the ruins. More than a hundred years later, that same crown would pass once more into the hands of an unfamiliar child.

blocks.x: What makes this story especially bittersweet is that we already know the next chapter after this ending. That the Doctor who woke with no memory is the very same person we now call by that name at Rhodes Island.
And Ascalon's revenge, and the crown Amiya now carries — both are stories that still haven't ended. It's just that, by now, very few people left on this world remember what comes after.

act32side.tagline: 실종된 동료를 찾던 지구의 특수부대가 테라의 휴양도시 천장을 뚫고 추락해, 괴짜 부호의 33번째 경호원으로 취직해버리는 이야기. 그리고 불탄 무도회장에서 다시 춤추는 목각 인형 두 대.

chars.name: Ela
chars.desc: 지구에서 온 특수부대 대원 · 팀의 지휘 담당 · 33번째 경호원

chars.name: Iana
chars.desc: 분신을 만드는 '제미니 복제기'의 주인 · 낙천적인 과학자

chars.name: Doc
chars.desc: 파리 출신 군의관 · 크루아상 발음에 진심

chars.name: Fuze
chars.desc: 과묵한 폭발물 전문가 · 어쩌다 보니 기절 담당

chars.name: 레이넬
chars.desc: 도솔레스의 괴짜 미술상 부호 · 갤러리아 크리슈타와의 주인

chars.name: 미워시
chars.desc: 레이넬의 비서 · 와인병으로도 싸우는 만능 집사

chars.name: 테크노
chars.desc: 예술 거리의 두린 그래피티 아티스트 · 오퍼레이터 테크노

chars.name: 디아즈
chars.desc: 예술 거리를 지키는 전직 소방관 · 다들 '영감'이라 부른다

chars.name: 마테오
chars.desc: 볼리바르 연립정부의 대위 · 예술 거리의 땅을 노린다

chars.name: 테킬라
chars.desc: 로도스 아일랜드 오퍼레이터 · 도솔레스가 고향 · 본명 에르네스토 · 오퍼레이터 테킬라

terms.name: 도솔레스
terms.desc: 볼리바르의 해변 휴양도시. 카지노와 리조트, 그리고 뒷골목이 공존한다. 「도솔레스 홀리데이」의 그 동네.

terms.name: 볼리바르
terms.desc: 테라의 남미풍 국가. 여러 정부가 난립한 내전 상태로, 도솔레스는 연립정부의 영향권이다.

terms.name: 예술 거리
terms.desc: 도솔레스의 슬럼 취급받는 동네. 가난한 예술가와 감염자들이 모여 살며, 매년 자기들만의 예술 축제를 연다.

terms.name: 갤러리아 크리슈타와
terms.desc: 레이넬이 도솔레스에 세운 초호화 유리 갤러리. 개관식 날짜가 하필 예술 거리 축제와 같은 날이다.

terms.name: 광석병 억제제
terms.desc: 감염자의 증상을 억누르는 필수 의약품. 예술 거리에는 이 약 없이 못 사는 주민이 많다.

terms.name: 오리지늄 아츠
terms.desc: 테라의 '마법'. 총을 쓰는 지구인들 눈에는 온 동네 사람이 마법사인 셈이다.

terms.name: 카시미어
terms.desc: 레이넬의 고향인 기사와 상업의 나라. 레이넬 부친은 이곳 최대의 미술상이었다.

blocks.x: 우랄 산맥 남부, 사라진 동료들을 수색하던 지구의 대테러 특수부대 대원 넷 — **Ela, Iana, Fuze, Doc**. 실험실 터에서 갑자기 땅이 울리더니, 정신 차려 보니 웬 펜트하우스 천장을 뚫고 골프공 조형물 위에 떨어져 있습니다.
창밖엔 해변과 카지노, 하늘엔 달이 두 개. 네, 여기는 테라의 휴양도시 도솔레스입니다.
레인보우 식스 시즈 콜라보 2탄 「루센트 애로우헤드」. 총 든 지구인 넷이 아츠와 석궁의 세계에서 겪는, 예술과 폭탄에 관한 이야기예요.
1. 천장으로 들어온 경호원들
같은 시각, 도솔레스의 미술상 부호 **레이넬 코발스키**의 펜트하우스. 정체불명의 무장 괴한들이 계단으로 낑낑대며 올라와(전용 엘리베이터는 경비가 지키거든요) 그를 납치하려는 참이었습니다.
그때 천장이 뚫리고 특수부대가 낙하합니다. 골프공 2천 개로 쌓은 레이넬의 신작 '질서의 붕괴'가 말 그대로 붕괴하죠. 비서 **미워시**의 한마디 — "……붕괴?"
얼떨결에 납치범들과 교전하게 된 네 사람. 수류탄이 안 통하는 적을 연막과 지뢰로 제압하는 동안, 정작 레이넬은 태평하게 술을 따르며 관전합니다. "어느 쪽이 날 납치할지, 싸워서 이기는 쪽이 결정하면 되겠군."

blocks.cap: 발코니 너머, 우랄 산맥이어야 할 풍경. Fuze 왈 — "퍽이나 우랄 산맥이겠다."

blocks.x: 전투가 끝나고 발코니로 나간 대원들을 맞이한 건 노을 진 해변과 아이스크림 트럭. 그리고 지평선 위로 떠오르는 **두 개의 달**이었습니다.
이런 질문을 하게 될 줄은 몰랐지만…… 레이넬 씨, 지금 우리가 있는 이 행성은 지구인가?
볼리바르의 검은 심장…… 도솔레스에 온 것을 환영하지.
2. 경호원 이직률 1위 고용주
낯선 행성에서 숙식이 급한 대원들은 레이넬의 경호원으로 취직합니다. 미워시 말로는 **레이넬에게 싫증 나서 그만둔 경호원이 벌써 32번째**라는데요. 복선입니다.
아니나 다를까, 레이넬은 미워시가 출장 간 틈에 번지점프장으로 직행합니다. 그리고 수면 근처에서 몰래 안전장치를 풀고 자유낙하하죠. "자신을 옭아매는 모든 족쇄를 풀고 낙하하는 그 기분"이라나요.
격노한 Ela는 그 자리에서 사표를 던집니다. 근데 떠나기 전 "특별 전시실 조각상, 모조품이던데"라고 짚어주자 레이넬의 눈이 반짝여요. 그 모조품, 레이넬이 직접 만든 거였고 알아챈 사람은 Ela가 처음이었거든요.
"점잖게 굴기로 약속하면 계속 맡아 주겠나?" — 그렇게 Ela는 33번째 경호원이 됩니다.
한편 TV에서는 레이넬이 지역 예술가들을 "아마추어"라 깎아내리는 인터뷰가 방송 중입니다. 이를 갈며 보는 사람들이 있으니, 예술 거리의 전직 소방관 **디아즈**와 그래피티 아티스트 **테크노**예요. 하필 갤러리 개관식이 예술 거리 축제와 같은 날로 잡혔거든요.
그리고 또 한 명 — 납치극의 진짜 배후, 연립정부의 **마테오 대위**입니다. 예술 거리를 밀어버리고 유흥 지구를 세우는 게 꿈인 이 남자에게, 미워시가 은밀히 협상을 걸어옵니다. 돌아온 미워시의 보고는 "미끼를 물었습니다"였어요.
3. 억제제 강도 사건 (범인: 용병 코스프레)
며칠 뒤, 로도스 아일랜드의 **테킬라**와 캐터펄트가 예술 거리로 광석병 억제제를 배송하다 습격당합니다. 범인들은 총을 쏘고 지구식 전술 용어를 쓰는, 어딘가 어설픈 '용병'들.
마테오가 Ela 팀의 수법을 흉내 내 약을 훔치고, 누명까지 씌우려는 공작이었어요.
같은 시각 Doc과 Iana는 시내에서 길바닥에 쓰러진 테크노를 구합니다. 무릎에 돋은 오리지늄 결정을 내보이며 "이게 무섭지 않아?"라고 묻는 아이에게, 파리 출신 군의관은 태연하게 응급처치를 하죠.
테크노를 데려다준 예술 거리는 겉보기엔 험악해도, 손 안 대고 마사지하는 아츠 명인에 자장가 부르는 아빠까지 사는 동네였습니다. 근데 하필 그때 디아즈가 신문에서 두 사람의 정체를 알아봐요. '레이넬의 경호원 = 약 강도'로 낙인찍힌 두 사람, 온 동네에 쫓기는 신세가 됩니다.
궁지에 몰린 둘을 몰래 빼돌려 준 건 테크노였어요. "난 쓰레기 버리러 나왔을 뿐이고, 아무것도 못 본 거다? 알았지?"
4. 한밤의 창고, BGM은 탱고
훔친 약은 마테오의 손에 의해 레이넬 소유의 외딴 창고로 흘러듭니다. 그런데 그 창고에, 미워시의 의뢰를 받은 Ela 팀과 시장의 정보로 약을 추적한 테킬라 팀과 보안을 뚫으러 온 테크노가 **동시에** 잠입해요.
테크노가 경비원의 커피를 홀짝이다 키보드에 쏟아 방송 버튼이 눌리고, 창고 전체에 탱고가 울려 퍼집니다. 암흑 속에서 서로가 서로를 인질 잡는 대혼전 끝에 — "꼼짝 마!" 가 사방에서 동시에 터져요.
그때 창고 문 밖에서 "어이, 배달 왔는데요!"라는 태평한 목소리가 들립니다. 전원 침묵. 다 같이 배달원을 심문해 마테오의 공작을 밝혀냅니다.
억제제는 무사히 주민들 손에 돌아갑니다. 그리고 마테오는 상부에 깨져요. **누군가 그 배달원을 발가벗겨 부대 정문에 버리고 갔거든요.** 연립정부에 대한 공개적 모욕이라나요.
5. 불탄 무도회장에서, 다시 춤을
오해가 풀린 뒤, 테크노는 대원들에게 자신의 야심작을 보여줍니다. 축제에서 춤추게 할 **거대한 목각 인형 두 대**예요.
예전에 무도회장에 불이 났거든. 그 후로 아무도 춤을 추지 않았어.
그 화재에서 사람들을 구한 게 소방관 디아즈였습니다. 하지만 다 구하지는 못했고, 그날 이후 디아즈는 춤을 추지 않아요. 대신 "사람들이 다시 춤출 수 있게 인형을 만들자"고 한 것도 디아즈였죠.
축제 전날, 인형 팔이 케이블에 엉키는 사고가 터집니다. 8층 높이라 아무도 못 올라가는 상황에서 Ela가 조용히 밧줄을 챙겨요. 20층 건물에서 떨어진 적도 있으니 이 정도는 쉽다면서요.
망상이 뭐 어때서? 스스로에게 부여한 저 불가능할 것 같은 도전에 성공하고, 모두를 놀라게 하면 되는 거잖아.
수리는 성공하고, 그날 밤 두 인형이 처음으로 춤을 춥니다. 리허설도 지휘자도 없이 온 동네가 즉흥 연주로 화답하고, 주민들도 함께 춤을 춰요. 불탄 무도회장 이후 처음으로요.
같은 밤, 레이넬의 저택. 복원사가 고쳐 온 낡은 오르골에서 자장가가 흘러나옵니다. 돌아가신 어머니가 직접 작곡해 어린 레이넬에게 주려던 생일 선물 — 레이넬은 말없이 창밖의 달만 바라봅니다.
6. 폭탄은 이미 설치되어 있습니다
인형의 춤은 다음 날 도솔레스 전체의 화제가 됩니다. '위험한 슬럼'이 하루아침에 뉴스의 주인공이 된 거예요.
미워시는 Ela에게 뜻밖의 부탁을 합니다. 개관식 날짜가 실은 **레이넬 어머니의 기일**이니, 예술 거리 축제를 하루만 미루게 설득해 달라고요. 그러면서 레이넬의 부친 이야기를 들려줍니다 — 카시미어 최대의 미술상이자, 협박과 인신공격으로 예술가들을 짓밟던 남자.
어머니가 항의로 자기 작품을 훼손하자, 그 작품은 오히려 '철학적 의미'가 붙어 더 비싸게 팔렸다는 일화까지.
그리고 그날 밤, 침실에서 레이넬이 미워시에게 조용히 말합니다. "마지막 폭발물까지 모두 설치했다"고.
작품이 세상에 공개되었을 때 불러오는 파장이 아무리 크더라도, 위대한 죽음과는 비교할 수는 없겠지.
이 괴짜 부호는 자기 갤러리 개관식에 폭탄을 깔아둔 거예요. 예술을 돈으로 짓밟는 시장 전체를, 어머니의 기일에, 자기 손으로 날려버리기 위해서.
한편 마테오는 연립정부에서 도솔레스 군 지휘권을 따냅니다. 그리고 완전무장 병력이 '정기 소방 점검'을 명목으로 예술 거리를 포위해요.
7. '소방 점검'이라는 이름의 습격
점검은 파괴였습니다. 병사들은 반년 걸려 만든 작품들을 뜯어내고, 저항하는 예술가들을 폭행하고, 주민들이 보는 앞에서 목각 인형을 해체합니다. 눈알이 전선에 매달려 흔들리는 동안, 디아즈만이 눈을 돌리지 않고 끝까지 지켜봐요.
설계도가 든 저장 장치까지 뺏기려는 순간, 테크노가 그걸 낚아채고 짓밟히면서도 주먹을 펴지 않습니다. "저, 절대…… 안 놓쳐……" 그 순간 지휘관의 이마에 저격 레이저가 찍혀요. Doc입니다.
한 번만 더 밟아 보시지. 총알 세 개가 동시에 박히는 느낌이 궁금하면 다시 밟아 봐도 돼. 사양 말고 해 보라고.

blocks.cap: 추궁하러 온 Ela 앞에서, 태연하게 스윙하는 레이넬. 저 공은 어떻게 쳐도 홀에 들어갑니다.

blocks.x: Ela는 레이넬을 찾아가 따집니다. 알고 보니 레이넬은 마테오에게 '예술 거리를 처리하면 개발권을 주겠다'는 미끼를 던져 놓고, 대위가 주민들에게 당하는 꼴을 구경할 속셈이었어요. 무장 병력이 동원될 줄은 몰랐다지만, 사과도 없죠.

blocks.cap: 습격이 지나간 예술 거리. 주민들은 부서진 작품 앞에 주저앉아 있다.

blocks.x: 폐허가 된 거리에서는 마테오가 심어둔 선동꾼이 "갤러리를 망쳐놓자!"라며 군중을 부추깁니다. 분노한 사람들 앞에 디아즈가 나서요. 짐을 챙겨 그 유리 건물로 간다고.
우리가 춤추는 모습을 보기 싫다면, 볼 수밖에 없게 만들어 주면 돼.
복수 대신 악기와 음향 장치를 들고 가서, 레이넬의 갤러리 앞에서 공연을 하겠다는 겁니다. 같은 시각, 폭발물 운송 기록을 추적하던 테킬라는 갤러리 지하의 숨겨진 방에서 벽을 가득 채운 폭탄과 마주칩니다.
8. 개관식: 테러 두 개가 겹치면 콜라주
개관식 당일. 레이넬은 단상에서 투자자와 브로커들을 앞에 두고 '가상 작품 수집 프로젝트'를 발표합니다. 예술품을 디지털로 복제해 소유권만 사고팔게 하겠다는, 예술 시장 전체를 향한 대놓고 조롱이에요.

blocks.cap: 프로젝트 1호 작품 공개 — 제목 '왕짜증 대위'. 모델료는 무료랍니다.

blocks.x: 관중석의 마테오를 겨냥한 '왕짜증 대위'까지 공개하며 도발하지만, 마테오는 화내지 않습니다. 대신 단말기의 신호를 확인하곤 일어나요. '테러 위험'을 구실로 관객 전원을 갤러리에 가두는 인질극의 시작입니다.
그렇게 레이넬의 폭탄 계획과 마테오의 인질극이, 서로 모른 채 같은 건물에서 겹칩니다. Ela의 명명 — 테러 사건 '콜라주'.
"이건 계획에 없었으니 도망치자"고 설득하던 심복 지휘관을, 마테오는 화살로 쏴 죽입니다. "나는 더 이상 돌이킬 수 없어. 너도 마찬가지고." 이 남자, 이제 진짜 돌아올 수 없는 강을 건넜어요.
갤러리 앞에 도착한 예술 거리 사람들과 Ela 팀, 캐터펄트까지 합류해 구출 작전이 결성됩니다. 디아즈도 나섭니다. "내 직업이 뭐였는지 잊지 마."

blocks.cap: Fuze의 접착식 집속탄이 적 폭발물 창고를 통째로 날린다. 캐터펄트 왈 — "너…… 혹시 천재야?"

blocks.x: 9. 비상구는 위층입니다
돌입한 팀은 인질들을 차례로 구출합니다. Iana는 제미니 복제기로 **총알이 몸을 통과하는 새하얀 산크타 살인마**를 연기해, 공포영화를 보던 경비병들을 싸우지도 않고 항복시켜요.

blocks.cap: 어둠 속에 나타난 광륜의 여인. 방금 본 공포영화랑 너무 똑같은데요.

blocks.x: 그때 미워시가 Ela 앞에 나타나 거래를 청합니다. 폭탄은 네 곳, 폭발은 오전 10시. 해체 정보를 다 넘길 테니 **레이넬을 구해달라**고요.
만약 레이넬 님을 구하지 못하게 된다면…… 저도 이곳에 남아, 레이넬 님의 길동무가 되어드려야죠.
역할 분담 — Fuze는 지하, 테킬라와 캐터펄트는 3층 암실, 디아즈는 6층, Ela 팀은 펜트하우스. 하지만 6층에서 폭탄을 해체하던 디아즈가 발각되어 마테오 앞에 끌려갑니다.
발코니에서 마테오는 레이넬에게 폭발 시각과 전용 엘리베이터 키를 요구합니다. 레이넬의 대답은 조롱이었어요. 당신은 기념관에 난입해 악취나 풍기는 짐승, **버든비스트**라고.

blocks.cap: 골프채가 날아든다. 그래도 레이넬은 조롱을 멈추지 않는다.

blocks.x: 한편 6층 폭탄이 터지며 유일한 계단이 무너지고, Ela가 잔해에 고립됩니다. 그래도 Ela는 혼자서 위층으로 가겠다고 해요.
잘못 생각했어. 난 사람을 구하러 올라가는 거야. 내가 살 길을 찾는 게 아니라.
적병들에게 포위당해 궁지에 몰린 그 순간 — 벽이 폭발하며 뚫립니다. **Ash, Blitz, Frost, Tachanka.** 먼저 테라에 와 있던 실종된 동료들, 바로 그 수색 대상들이 Ela를 구하러 온 거예요.
Ela가 한 명 한 명 본명을 부릅니다. 코언, 티나, 쾨츠, 알렉산드르……

blocks.cap: 불타는 그림 조각이 흩날리는 펜트하우스. 마지막 임무가 시작된다.

blocks.x: 10. 철 좀 들어
10시 직전, 남은 탈출로는 발코니에서 뛰어내리는 것뿐. Blitz가 로도스의 클로저가 만든 글라이딩 제트팩을 꺼내고, 전원이 차례로 뛰어내립니다.
레이넬도 제트팩을 받아 뛰어내립니다. 그리고 공중에서, 스스로 제트팩을 벗어 던집니다.
Ela가 뻗은 손은 닿지 않았어요. 자유낙하하는 레이넬이 자신이 설계한 파멸을 음미하려던 그때 — 골프공들이 빛을 내며 모여들어 그의 몸을 받아냅니다. 눈을 떠 보니 수영장 옆, 골프공 매트 위였습니다.

blocks.cap: 수영장가의 대치. 이 이벤트에서 제일 조용하고, 제일 무거운 대화.

blocks.x: 자신의 파멸을 '명예로운 반항'이라 부르는 레이넬에게, Ela는 정면으로 말합니다. 반항아는 맞지만 명예는 없다고. 아버지가 싫었다면서, 정말 반항할 방법을 생각해 본 적은 있느냐고.
그리고 미워시에게 들은 이야기를 꺼냅니다. 당신 어머니는 진정한 예술가였고, 젊은 시절의 당신은 어머니를 닮았었다고.
당신 어머니라면 어떻게 생각했을지, 당신 스스로에게 물어봐. 당신이 한 일을 자랑스러워했을까? ……레이넬 씨, 제발, 철 좀 들어.

blocks.cap: 대답 대신 주먹. 레이넬은 수영장에 처박힌다.

blocks.x: 물에서 고개를 든 레이넬은 수영장 반대편에서 자신을 바라보다, 조용히 떠나는 사람의 흐릿한 뒷모습을 봅니다. 그 시선은 어머니를 떠올리게 했다고 해요.
결국 레이넬은, 여전히 혼자였습니다.
에필로그: 갤러리아 바비큐 식당
폭탄은 전부 해체되고, 발코니 끝에 매달려 있던 마테오는 경찰에 '구출'되어 체포됩니다. Doc의 평 — "이 정도면 운이 굉장히 좋은 것 같은데."
그날 밤 갤러리 중앙홀은 그대로 축제장이 됩니다. 벽은 그래피티 캔버스, 로큰롤 무대에 무료 푸드 카트까지. 예술가도 인질이었던 관객도 경찰도 함께 춤추는, 예술 거리 역사상 최대의 무도회예요.
홀로 술을 마시던 디아즈 앞에 레이넬이 나타나 "결국 내 갤러리에서 축제를 열었군"이라며 축하 아닌 축하를 건넵니다. 그리고 디아즈가 마지막 한마디를 건네요.
춤을 추고 싶다면 여기 있는 누구와도 출 수 있어. 오직 너만 빼고 말이지.
이후 레이넬은 재판도 없이 고향 카시미어로 송환될 처지가 됩니다. 그는 전 재산을 '지나가는 사람 이름'으로 양도하는데, 그 이름이 디아즈였어요. "진정한 예술을 사랑하는 사람에게 맡기지."
호송 당일, 호송원이 기절하고 은색 차가 멈춰 섭니다. 운전석의 미워시 — "저도 태워주시겠습니까?" 두 사람은 목적지도 없이, 기름이 떨어질 때까지 차가 이끄는 대로 떠납니다.
"하지만 오늘은 아니겠지." — "네. 오늘은 아니겠죠."

blocks.cap: 디아즈가 갤러리를 개조해 차린 바비큐 식당. 그을린 바닥과 그라피티는 인테리어로 남겼습니다.

blocks.x: 그리고 갤러리아 크리슈타와는 바비큐 식당이 됩니다. 그 원형 그릴 테이블에 레인보우 팀 여덟 명이 전원 집합해요. 먼저 온 Ash 일행은 사르곤 사막에 떨어져 로도스 아일랜드에서 '레인보우 팀'이 되었고, 지구로 돌아갈 단서는 아직 못 찾았다고 합니다.
Blitz가 Doc에게 의미심장한 부탁을 남겨요. 나중에 로도스에 오면 후드를 덮어쓰고, 자기가 본명으로 불러도 자연스럽게 행동해 달라고. 이유를 설명하려던 Ash는 "에이 코언, 벌써 스포일러하면 재미없지"라며 제지당합니다.
천장을 뚫고 떨어진 이방인들은 아직 지구로 돌아가지 못했습니다. 하지만 잃어버린 동료를 되찾았고, 두 개의 달 아래에 돌아갈 식당이 하나 생겼죠. 그리고 불탔던 무도회장에는 — 다시 음악이 흐릅니다.

act31side.tagline: A demoted young lord comes to keep watch, and ends up saddled with the nickname "Mr. Candlestick" before he's even unpacked. And a woman who's played god for a thousand years quietly becomes human again — but only after saving a single stalk of rice.

chars.name: Shu
chars.desc: A gentle angel who teaches farming in the Vast Wilderness. Everyone believes she's the returned "Shennong."

chars.name: Nian
chars.desc: Shu's younger brother. An eccentric engineer leading construction of the "Five-Star Twelve Towers," the mobile fortress meant to protect the Vast Wilderness.

chars.name: Dusk
chars.desc: Nian's younger sibling, a painter with the power to freely enter the world inside her own paintings.

chars.name: Zuo Le
chars.desc: A young lord of the Bureau of Generations' Candle-Bearer office, sent to the Vast Wilderness after his father called him out for a mistake.

chars.name: Hesheng
chars.desc: A young agriculture angel of the Vast Wilderness said to have been born in a paddy field. She's researching a strain of rice resistant to Originium. · Operator Wanqing

chars.name: Xiaoman
chars.desc: A cheerful girl of the Vast Wilderness who's convinced she can talk to livestock. · Operator Grain Buds

chars.name: Yun Qingping
chars.desc: Zuo Le's attendant, born frail and trained in recordkeeping instead of martial arts. Xiaoman calls him "Mr. Brush."

chars.name: Ling Sichu
chars.desc: A young official dispatched to the Vast Wilderness by the Board of Rites, always on edge for fear of living in his grandfather's shadow.

chars.name: Vice Minister Wan
chars.desc: An ambitious Vice Minister of Works overseeing construction of the Five-Star Twelve Towers.

terms.name: the Vast Wilderness
terms.desc: A vast granary region at the northern edge of Yan. Blessed with unusually little Originium contamination, it's a precious land steeped in the legend of Shennong.

terms.name: the Five-Star Twelve Towers
terms.desc: A massive mobile fortress being built atop the Vast Wilderness — a national construction project meant to shield the city from both Catastrophes and demons.

terms.name: Shennong
terms.desc: The legendary figure said to have first cultivated the wastelands of the Vast Wilderness a thousand years ago. Every summer harvest, a festival called the "Shennong Festival" is held in his honor.

terms.name: Candle-Bearer
terms.desc: An office under the Bureau of Generations tasked with monitoring and liaising with Agents — beings born from fragments of the Behemoth.

terms.name: the Bureau of Generations
terms.desc: A government office of Yan that manages and monitors the Agents.

terms.name: Agent
terms.desc: Beings born from a piece of the Behemoth who live out their lives as humans. Shu and her siblings belong to this group.

terms.name: the Wanqing Project
terms.desc: Hesheng's long-running research to breed a strain of rice that can grow even in land contaminated by Originium.

blocks.x: Crops grow from seeds — so what did 'we' grow from? Where did we come from? Where do we go when we die?
We grew from the earth, and when we die, we return to the earth. Whether it's a person or a plant, everything is part of the cycle.

The Vast Wilderness sits at the northern edge of Yan, a precious land with unusually little Originium contamination. Its people have built it into Yan's finest granary region, and every summer harvest they hold a festival honoring "Shennong," who is said to have first cultivated this land.

Legend says Shennong set off north a thousand years ago in search of new seeds, and never returned. Even so, people say he descends from the sky each harvest season to check on the year's crop. Most everyone just treats it as an old folk tale.

But Zuo Le, a Candle-Bearer of the Bureau of Generations newly assigned to the Vast Wilderness, happens to read a record suggesting Shennong was a real person. And he can't shake the feeling that the eyes described in that record keep overlapping with those of the gentle angel who waters the paddies beside him every day.
1. A demoted young lord arrives, gets fed, and immediately loses his nickname

blocks.cap: A young lord who can't even properly cut a handful of rice stumbles through his very first task.

blocks.x: Having been sternly scolded by his father Zuo Xuanliao, **Zuo Le** is sent to the Vast Wilderness as punishment for a mistake he made at the Jade Gate. Instead of a written apology, his assigned task turns out to be, of all things, "spring planting."

The moment he arrives, Zuo Le just stands blankly in the paddy — and **Xiaoman**, the cheerful girl who chats freely with livestock, treats him like a scarecrow that hasn't even had lunch, shoving a lunchbox into his hands.

**Shu**, the gentle angel who tends the paddies, introduces him to Hesheng, and Zuo Le's nickname is set in stone almost instantly: "Mr. Candlestick." It was Xiaoman's doing, once she learned that Candle-Bearer (持燭人) literally means "one who holds a candle." His ever-present attendant **Yun Qingping**, frail since birth and trained in recordkeeping instead of martial arts, gets his own nickname too — everyone calls him "Mr. Brush."

One night, the four of them are chasing fireflies when they spot a shooting star. Xiaoman genuinely believes that wherever a star falls, a bountiful harvest follows, and begs everyone to go find the spot with her.

blocks.cap: Fireflies rise and a shooting star falls on a night when the four of them lie side by side.

blocks.x: Even if we never find it, I'll keep looking — until we're old and gray.
2. Shennong, a figure of legend? No, actually just the angel next door

On the day of the Shennong Festival, Zuo Le watches the play *The Contested Heavens* and hears the legend of Shennong from **Hesheng**, the young agriculture angel said to have been born in a paddy field — the story of someone who reclaimed a land ruined by Catastrophe, then set off in search of new seeds and never came back.

But as Zuo Le digs through the records, he finds something unsettling. In the very seat of the "Shennong" who is said to have established the 24 solar terms, appeared during the great famine, and stayed for a thousand years — he keeps seeing Shu's face overlap.

blocks.cap: Trapped inside a painting, three siblings secretly build the nomadic city's "heart." In truth, none of the three are human.

blocks.x: What the three of them are building day and night is the mobile fortress that will protect the entire Vast Wilderness — the "Five-Star Twelve Towers." With the court pushing them to prepare for Catastrophe and war at once, the construction deadline is always brutally tight.
Meanwhile, Shu slips out to the riverbank every night. She brings food to a "mute man" no one else goes near, and tells Xiaoman only that he's someone who's ill.
3. A real bolt from the blue strikes in the middle of the festival

blocks.cap: Fireworks burst and harvesters cut across the paddies — the last festival before disaster strikes.

blocks.x: In the middle of the Shennong Festival, a sudden downpour hits and a dam breaks. Muddy floodwater mixed with Originium debris surges into the paddies.
Hesheng proposes personally piloting the still-unfinished mobile sector to redirect the water, overriding the earth angels who insist it can't be done.
What are you all standing around for! Get it moving, now!
In the end the water recedes and everyone is safe. But no one knows that, that same night, someone collapsed alone on the riverbank. It was Shu.
4. A suspicious traveler sells a girl her own wish
In the wake of the flood, a vagrant who'd been napping in a paddy appears before Xiaoman and the others. He claims to be a merchant — one who'll buy and sell anything.

Xiaoman tosses out a wish as a joke — that the Vast Wilderness would have a bountiful harvest every year — and he takes one of Xiaoman's songs as payment for it. His name is **Ji**. Shu's younger brother.

blocks.cap: Ji returns, sauntering in front of familiar faces of the Vast Wilderness.

blocks.x: Hesheng finds him nothing but suspicious, but Zuo Le recognizes him immediately — a flagged Agent the Bureau of Generations has been watching for a long time.
5. No one died, yet one stalk of rice turned blood-red

In the middle of a paddy left in ruins after the flood, Xiaoman finds a single stalk of rice as red as blood. All the rice around it has fallen over, but this one stands upright — and even the Burden Beast circling nearby has turned bright red.

blocks.cap: Amid the fallen rice, one stalk stands alone, blood-red.

blocks.x: A student from the angel corps secretly asks her to gather these stalks of rice, but won't say what they're for. Around this time, Zuo Le sees, in a vision, an old farmer who's never once harvested a single seed despite a lifetime of farming, and countless nameplates hung from a tree across the river.
In place of an answer, Shu tells an old story instead — of a companion who once helped settle the Vast Wilderness with her, who set off north in her place amid the Catastrophe.
In the end, we couldn't protect any of it.
6. As it turns out, the god was exhausted
Ji keeps coming back to see Shu. He tells her to leave this heavy land behind already — that staking her life on the "heart" Nian is building is far too dangerous a bargain.

Shu refuses every time. But once Ji leaves and she's alone, the smile drops from her face — she's the one who, for a thousand years, has felt this land's Catastrophes in her own body before anyone else.

Zuo Le doesn't miss one thing Ji lets slip: a question about how many angels have died at the Bureau of Heavenly Records in just the last three months. And among the siblings, rumors keep surfacing about a second brother who hasn't shown his face in ages — the one they call the "Go player."
7. An ambitious vice minister joins hands with Catastrophe

**Vice Minister Wan**, the young official put in charge of the Five-Star Twelve Towers construction, was a man consumed by ambition. He was always sharpening his edge against his rival **Ling Sichu**, dispatched from the Board of Rites, but he'd stake his life on holding the construction deadline no matter what.
Ji appears before him and offers a deal: supplies, in exchange for something in return. Rather than deny any wrongdoing, Vice Minister Wan accepts, gladly.
No need to be alarmed. I know full well that Vice Minister Wan climbed from a mere clerk all the way to the second rank.

In the end, on the day the core runs out of control, it's Vice Minister Wan who steps forward alone to try to stop the runaway device — all because of the one thing he'd hated hearing his whole life: "out of control."

They'd always been snarling rivals, but Ling Sichu couldn't let even his death be exploited. He silently grits his teeth and carries the remaining report to the Bureau of Heavenly Records in Wan's place.
8. When the crimson dusk rises, the world turns upside down

blocks.cap: As if the sun had tilted west, half the sky turns the color of blood.

blocks.x: The core's energy suddenly spirals out of control, and unidentifiable monsters pour endlessly out of the ground — the result of Ji forcing Nian and Dusk to activate the "heart" against their will.

The monsters' true nature is the fear born of Catastrophe itself — beings that grow stronger the more people fear them. Rather than flee, Hesheng and Zuo Le call out for everyone to gather and stand together.

Right before the fight, Xiaoman quietly confesses to Hesheng what's really on her mind — she doesn't even know why she's saying it, but she tells Hesheng not to assume that having no family means no one cares about her. Sheer terror had loosened words she'd never normally say.
I told you not to be afraid... but I know full well that's asking the impossible.
9. Silence by the river, and those who never came back

Amid the swarm of monsters, a silent woodcutter who's lived alone by the riverbank for years holds the line with nothing but an axe. Xiaoman risks the danger to seek him out and plays him a tune on her flute.

The woodcutter uses his last strength to protect Xiaoman, but he himself stays behind among the monsters. That he was a father who chose to remain on this riverbank long ago, leaving a daughter behind, is a truth that never gets told to anyone.

At the very same moment, inside the core, Vice Minister Wan is holding back the device alone. He never comes back out — but the dream of a "boundless city" he carried is one Shu picks up in his place.
10. A kite that never got to fly, yet still survives

To rescue the people still trapped in the test fields amid the monsters, Hesheng and Zuo Le race across the gaps of a collapsing sector. Zuo Le carries people one by one, leaping across the widening cracks.
In the final moment, Hesheng is badly hurt. Even so, she turns her angel robe — which holds her research data — into a kite, and sends Zuo Le flying up into the sky alone.
I've said a lot of harsh things to you, but honestly, you're a good kid.
Left alone, Hesheng finds herself surrounded by closing monsters. Fortunately, she's saved by "Fluffy," a Stock Beast she'd rescued together with the others long ago.
11. The god closes her eyes one last time, and becomes human

blocks.cap: "Sui," the child-like consciousness trapped here for a thousand years, opens its eyes.

blocks.x: Shu walks straight into the consciousness of "Sui," the source of the monsters. She's there to gently tell that childlike shadow, which has carried this land's fear on its own for a thousand years, that the world isn't a frightening place.
You're afraid because you've never truly lived.
As Shu finishes speaking, the sky clears and the core's rampage finally stops. Amid the collapsing mobile sector, Zuo Le and Hesheng push each other up and barely make it back alive.

Hesheng confirms that the Originium-resistant rice she'd searched so long for — "Wanqing" — has survived. But Shu, who had raised that rice alongside her, is nowhere to be found anywhere in the Vast Wilderness once the Catastrophe ends.

blocks.cap: The blood-red dusk clears, and the fields turn golden once more.

blocks.x: Epilogue. No one remembered her

In the wake of the Catastrophe, Shu wakes up in an unfamiliar village with no memory of who she is. Hesheng finds it odd how much this "teacher" knows about rice farming — but even Shu herself has no idea who she really is.
Meanwhile, Dusk sets down her brush and quietly disappears, leaving behind only the words that the thousand years felt like a dream — and that having them all around made it never lonely.

blocks.cap: The painter's final brushstroke, and a flock of Fowl Beasts crossing the sky.

blocks.x: Ji leaves the Vast Wilderness carrying the fabric he spent a thousand years weaving — a cloth woven not just with the history of the Vast Wilderness, but with the hearts of people all across Yan. He's off to meet his second older brother, the one who's spent all this time hidden away, playing nothing but Go.

blocks.cap: The silk Ji has been weaving for a thousand years. Now that it's finished, he needs to find someone to sell it to.

blocks.x: Nian and Dusk join forces to corner Ji, but he slips away unharmed in the end. And in that very moment, the second brother long known only as rumor — the "Go player," Wang — appears before Shu for the very first time.
If you trace it back, all of this started a thousand years ago.

Wang says what he's after is a "greater good" for this entire world. Rather than stop him, Shu hands him a handful of seeds — telling him that if he's really walking the right path, these seeds will sprout someday too.

Before leaving, Ji paints a fitting scene for each and every one of his siblings — the eldest brother who guards the border, the free-spirited poet sister, Nian wandering the mulberry roads, and even the sister who has already passed on.
And for Shu, he leaves her exactly as she already is now, living the life she's already chosen.
So this is what it's like to be "human"? Somehow... it feels good.

Zuo Le, too, has changed a little. He gets word from his father that he's been reinstated, and that his next assignment is keeping watch over Rhodes Island, given how often it comes into contact with Agents. This time, instead of rushing off in fear like before, he packs his bags calmly.
Even something you'd call an 'enemy' isn't beyond understanding — so there's no need to be afraid.

blocks.cap: A little brother offering to make winter clothes, and an older sister who doesn't refuse. Some very long-ago day.

blocks.x: Where did we come from, and where do we go when we die? Just as that old saying goes — grown from the earth, and returned to the earth — Shu has now gone back to being part of this land, without a name, without a face.

The people of the Vast Wilderness still hold a festival honoring Shennong every summer. Only now, starting with this harvest, the Originium-resistant rice is placed on the altar too. Where the god once stood, what remains instead is fruit that people themselves planted, and people themselves harvested.

act30side.tagline: 엄마의 유물을 들고 아빠를 찾아온 딸이, 재회하자마자 다짜고짜 벼랑 밑으로 걷어차 버리는 이야기. 그 뒤에서는 나라 하나가 몰래 전함을 숨기고, 어둠의 기사 혼자 2천 명을 막아섭니다.

chars.name: 레토
chars.desc: 엄마의 유물을 품에 안고 홀로 쉐라그를 찾은 우르수스 소녀. '로잘린드'라는 가명을 쓴다

chars.name: 해럴드
chars.desc: 빅토리아에서 온 자작. 버든비스트를 치료하는 수의사 행세를 하며 한 달째 쉐라그에 눌러앉아 있다

chars.name: 데겐블레허
chars.desc: 쉐라그에 정착한 前 카시미어 기사 스포츠 3연속 우승자, '어둠의 기사'. 지금은 카란 무역회사 소속

chars.name: 엔시오데스
chars.desc: 카란 무역회사를 이끄는 쉐라그 청년 가주 · 오퍼레이터 실버애쉬

chars.name: 엔야
chars.desc: 쉐라간드를 섬기는 쉐라그의 성녀 · 오퍼레이터 프라마닉스

chars.name: 노시스
chars.desc: 카란 무역회사의 수석 기술 집행관. 냉철하고 빈틈없다

chars.name: 아크토즈
chars.desc: 쉐라그 페일로쉬 가문의 현 가주. 호탕하고 다혈질인 애주가

chars.name: 늙은 수도사
chars.desc: 산기슭에서 만난 신비로운 노인. 로잘린드에게 하룻밤 묵을 곳을 내준다

chars.name: 시우루스
chars.desc: 브라운테일 가문 가주의 여동생. 국가 사절로 활약하는 명랑한 트러블메이커

chars.name: 회색 모자
chars.desc: 빅토리아 공작의 밀정. 정체를 숨긴 채 쉐라그를 조사하고 다닌다

terms.name: 쉐라간드
terms.desc: 쉐라그인들이 섬기는 이 땅의 수호신. 실존 여부는 확인되지 않았지만, 신도들에게는 흔들리지 않는 믿음 그 자체다

terms.name: 쉐라그
terms.desc: 만년설로 뒤덮인 산악 국가. 실버애쉬 가문의 개방 정책 이후 3년 새 철도와 관광객이 밀려들며 빠르게 변하고 있다

terms.name: 카란 무역회사
terms.desc: 엔시오데스가 빅토리아에서 돌아와 세운 쉐라그의 무역 회사. 광산·철도 등 쉐라그 개발의 중심에 있다

terms.name: 은심호
terms.desc: 쉐라간드의 첫 번째 눈물이라 전해지는 호수. 중앙 섬에 새로 완공된 쉐라간드 동상이 서 있다

terms.name: 만주원
terms.desc: 성녀를 중심으로 한 쉐라그의 종교·행정 자문 기구

terms.name: 쉐라그 3대 가문
terms.desc: 페일로쉬(신앙과 호위), 브라운테일(정치와 처세), 실버애쉬(개방과 무역)로 나뉘어 쉐라그를 떠받쳐 온 세 가문

blocks.x: 쉐라그는 만년설로 뒤덮인 산악 국가예요. 사람들은 이 땅의 수호신 쉐라간드를 섬기며 살아가고, 3년 전 실버애쉬 가문의 청년 가주가 세운 카란 무역회사 덕분에 조용하던 이 나라에도 철도와 관광객이 밀려들기 시작했죠.
그런데 은심호에 쉐라간드 동상 낙성식을 앞둔 지금, 산기슭에는 빅토리아 병사 2천 명이 '축하하러' 야영 중이에요. 다들 그게 그냥 관광은 아니라는 걸 알면서도, 아무도 먼저 입을 열지 않죠.
그 사이로, 우르수스에서 자란 소녀 레토가 엄마의 유물을 품에 안고 홀로 열차에 올라요. 아빠 찾아 삼만 리, 그런데 하필 이 타이밍이에요.
1. 관광객으로 위장한 삼인방
국경 검문소에선 '이상한 모자를 쓴 외국인'이 옆자리 꼬맹이한테 시비 아닌 시비를 겪고 있어요. 객실 인원수를 세고, 수상한 자루를 훔쳐보고, 은근슬쩍 정탐 중이거든요.
그 정체는 빅토리아 공작의 밀정, 별명 '회색 모자'예요. 은심호행 열차 안에 뭔가 있다는 첩보 하나만 들고 온 거죠.
레토는 역에서 도망친 파울비스트 소동에 휘말리다가, 빅토리아 자작 **해럴드**와 얽혀요. 로잘린드라는 가명을 쓰면서요.

blocks.cap: 역에서 병사들 틈으로 불쑥 끼어든 레토. '이 아가씨는 어디서 튀어나온 거야?!'

blocks.x: 해럴드는 버든비스트를 치료하는 수의사 행세를 하며 한 달째 쉐라그에 눌러앉아 있는 자칭 '관광객'이에요. 꿀 취한 친구 얘기로 수다를 떨던 레토는, 회색 모자가 나타나자마자 해럴드가 갑자기 딴생각에 빠지는 걸 눈치채요.
'적은 아니고, 그렇다고 친구도 아닌…… 사이 안 좋은 회사 동료 정도?' 해럴드는 얼버무리지만, 표정은 이미 다 들켰죠.
회색 모자는 마지막 객실 문을 열었다가 그대로 굳어버려요. 손에 핏자국이 묻은 여자, 카시미어 기사 스포츠 3연속 우승자 **데겐블레허**였거든요.

blocks.cap: 실버애쉬 가문 소속 어둠의 기사와 정면으로 마주친 순간. '설마, 저 여자가……'

blocks.x: 알고 보니 데겐블레허가 쫓던 건 회색 모자가 아니라 해럴드였어요. 버든비스트 릴리의 난산을 도와줄 사람이 필요했을 뿐이었거든요.
릴리는…… 사람이 아닙니다!
'애인 이름을 잊었냐'고 놀리던 레토는 릴리가 버든비스트라는 말에 허탈해해요. 온 열차가 이 소동에 휘말려 식당칸까지 뛰어다니는 추격전이 벌어지죠.
정작 아무도 서로를 죽일 생각은 없었다는 게, 이 이야기의 첫 번째 반전이에요.
2. 미남 전설과 수상한 초대장
은심호역에 내린 순간부터 레토는 신기한 걸 봐요. 승객들이 '돈을 떼먹으면 알아서 공격하는' 버든비스트 렌트 시스템에, 그걸 태연하게 파는 목자까지.
시장에서는 더 재밌는 소문을 듣습니다. 쉐라그에서 제일가는 미남이 다름 아닌 카란 무역회사 사장 **엔시오데스**라는 거예요.
레토의 엄마는 늘 '쉐라그의 유명한 미남'이 자기 아빠라고 했었거든요. 설마, 하고 넘겼지만 왠지 찜찜하죠.
동상 앞에서는 브라운테일 가문의 **시우루스**가 말을 걸어와요. 30미터짜리 동상이 '평범한 돌'이 아니라며 자랑하다가, 재료 얘기가 나오자 갑자기 입을 다물어 버리고요.
관광객치고 눈썰미가 좋다며, 시우루스는 얼떨결에 레토를 낙성식에 정식으로 초대해 버려요.
그런데 시우루스는 속으로 좀 다른 생각을 하고 있어요. '라타토스가 이 여자애를 주시하라고 했는데……' 낙성식을 앞둔 세 가문 모두, 레토를 예사롭지 않게 보고 있었던 거예요.
3. 술 취한 삼대, 정체는 안갯속
레토는 산기슭에서 만난 **늙은 수도사** 할아버지에게 하룻밤 신세를 지기로 해요. 그런데 그 집, 알고 보니 페일로쉬 가문의 저택이었어요.
그 저택 주인이 바로 지금 가문의 가주 **아크토즈**. 낙성식 축하주를 고른다며 낮부터 술독에 빠져 있던 아저씨였죠.

blocks.cap: '네가 꿀을 줬으니 나도 뭔가 꺼내야지!' 아크토즈가 술 저장고를 통째로 들고 왔어요.

blocks.x: 레토가 가져온 벌꿀 음료에 취한 아크토즈는, 답례랍시고 지하 저장고를 통째로 꺼내 옵니다. 20년 묵어 시큼해진 결혼 축하주까지 딸 앞에서 스스럼없이 따 버리고요.
취한 아크토즈는 레토의 얼굴을 보고도 알아채지 못해요. 하지만 늙은 수도사는 한눈에 알아보죠. 20년 전 이 산꼭대기에서 아들이 사랑했던 그 여자, 타티야나의 얼굴을요.
다음 날 아침, 술이 덜 깬 아크토즈에게 늙은 수도사가 사실을 말합니다. '로잘린드는 네 딸이다.' 그리고 딸은 이미 산으로 올라간 뒤였어요.
4. 벼랑 끝에서 듣는 출생의 비밀
산 정상, 레토는 엄마의 유물을 나무 틈에 숨기려다 실패해요. 그때 사냥용 함정에 빠진 '존 스미스'를 구해주는데, 그가 다름 아닌 회색 모자라는 걸 눈치채 버려요.
몰아붙이려는 순간 아크토즈가 뛰어들고, 셋 다 눈밭으로 굴러떨어져요. 그리고 아크토즈는 마침내 딸에게 진실을 털어놓습니다.
20년 전 겨울, 이 산에서 우르수스 여인 타티야나를 만나 사랑에 빠졌고, 반대를 무릅쓰고 결혼했다고요.
당시의 쉐라그는 폐쇄적이고 배타적인 나라였어요. 외지인과 결혼한다는 것 자체가 가문의 수치로 여겨질 정도였죠.
하지만 개혁을 주장하던 실버애쉬 부부가 '사고'로 죽은 뒤, 외국인 아내를 향한 적개심이 감당할 수 없이 커졌어요. 페일로쉬 가문도 그 개혁 반대파에 속해 있었거든요.
아크토즈는 두려움에 못 이겨 아내와 딸을 쉐라그 밖으로 떠나보냈습니다. 자신의 곁에 있다가 두 사람마저 '사고'를 당할까 봐요.
'다시 한번, 아빠라고 불러줄 수 있을까?' 아크토즈가 도끼를 딸 앞에 내려놓으며 묻습니다.

blocks.cap: 장황한 사과 끝에 돌아온 대답은 이거였어요.

blocks.x: 웃기지 마! 꿈 깨시지!
레토는 아크토즈가 늘어놓은 변명 속에 정작 타티야나가 없다는 걸 지적해요. 열정적이고, 낯선 곳을 좋아하고, 사람 사귀길 즐기던 사람. 그 사람을 하나도 담지 못한 변명이라고요.
그러고는 등을 걷어차 아크토즈를 눈밭으로 떨어뜨려 버려요. 물론 다치지 않을 정도로 계산된 낙하였죠.
5. 양육비 청구서, 그리고 화해 아닌 화해
'지난 십여 년치 양육비, 계좌로 보내. 안 갚으면 이자까지 붙일 거야.' 레토는 후련한 얼굴로 산을 내려가요.
그런데 통화 중에 아크토즈가 엄마 얘기를 '유물'로 알아듣고 세상을 떠난 줄 오해하는 바람에, 상황이 한 번 더 꼬여버려요.
타티야나는 살아 있어요. 몇 년 전 재앙 구역 사고로 광석병에 걸렸을 뿐, 지금도 레토 곁에 있죠.
그리고 결정타. 레토의 엄마는 단 한 번도 쉐라그를, 아크토즈를 원망한 적이 없다고 해요. 딸에게 기도하는 법까지 가르쳐 줬을 정도로요.
레토는 그걸 '용서'라고 부르지 않아요. 그냥 원망하며 사는 게 손해라서 안 한 거라고, 딱 잘라 말할 뿐이죠.
6. 얼음판 위의 재대결
산에서 도망친 회색 모자는 웃돈까지 얹어 가장 빠른 버든비스트를 빌려 타고 내빼요. 하지만 데겐블레허도 곧장 같은 목장의 다른 버든비스트로 뒤쫓아 오죠.
얼떨결에 남의 스케이트화를 빌려 신은 회색 모자는 태어나서 처음 타보는 스케이트로 어설프게 도망 다녀요. 그런데도 묘하게 금방 요령을 터득해 버리죠.
둘의 결투는 결국 동상 꼭대기까지 이어져요. 성녀의 시녀장은 그걸 구경하며 딴생각을 해요. '저기 조각, 얼굴을 좀 더 갸름하게 다듬으면 좋을 텐데.'

blocks.cap: 머리 위에서 걷어차여 날아가는 회색 모자. 시녀장은 그 와중에도 동상 성형 계획을 짜고 있어요.

blocks.x: 얻어맞고 호수에 처박힌 회색 모자는 결국 살아남아요. 그리고 도망치듯 은심호 뒷골목을 뒤지다가, 결정적인 증거를 찾아내죠.
'술을 나른다'던 통 안에 든 건 술이 아니라 금속이었어요. 동상을 만들고 남았어야 할 자재가, 대체 어디로 실려 가고 있었던 걸까요.
7. 카란 무역회사 삼인방의 뒤풀이
한편 낙성식 전야, 자리를 파한 연회장 로비에는 또 다른 가족이 모여 있어요. 술에 취해 뻗은 **노시스**, 그런 노시스를 걷어차는 데겐블레허, 그리고 옆에서 웃는 엔시오데스.

blocks.cap: 10년째 반복되는 야근 뒤풀이. 데겐블레허가 건네는 건 숙취해소제가 아니라 직접 만든 독약이에요.

blocks.x: 세 사람 다 서로에게 하고 싶은 말은 절대 입 밖으로 안 꺼내요. 그래도 난방도 꺼진 이 로비가, 셋에게는 제일 편한 자리인 것 같아요.
레토가 벼랑에서 마주한 아빠 쪽 가족이 피로 얽힌 애증이라면, 이쪽은 혈연 하나 없이 10년을 함께 버틴 사이. 쉐라그엔 그런 가족이 하나 더 있었던 거예요.
8. 축하한다더니, 발리스타까지 들고 온 손님들
낙성식 당일, 갑자기 대열을 갖춘 빅토리아 병사 2천 명이 은심호로 진군해요. '동상 밑에 금지 물자를 숨겼다'는 명분으로요.
실은 카란 무역회사가 동상 건설을 핑계로 몰래 무언가를 실어 날랐다는 게 사실이었어요. 회색 모자가 이미 그 증거를 손에 넣은 뒤였죠.
엔시오데스의 부하 바이스와 마터호른은 이미 전날 밤 붙잡혀 손발이 묶인 상태였어요. 정보를 전할 방법조차 없었죠.
엔시오데스는 정면충돌 대신 시간을 벌기로 해요. 그리고 그 시간을 버는 역할은, 두말없이 데겐블레허가 자원합니다.

blocks.cap: '이 앞은 통행금지야. 그 누구도 지나갈 생각 마.' 한 명 대 2000명의 저지선이 시작됩니다.

blocks.x: 해럴드가 진심이냐고 묻자, 데겐블레허는 담담하게 대답해요. 라이타니엔 뒷골목에서 이름도 없이 살다가, 날 없는 무기 하나로 스스로 이름을 지었다고요.
나는 데겐블레허야.
그 이름 뒤에는 신념도, 명분도 없어요. 그냥 폭력, 그리고 어쩌다 보니 10년째 정 붙이고 사는 이 추운 나라뿐이죠.
그런데도 해럴드는 협상을 포기하지 않아요. 조건의 30퍼센트만 받아들여도 공작을 설득할 수 있다고 마지막까지 제안하죠.
데겐블레허는 단칼에 자릅니다. 지금 이 순간 자신은 그저 '빅토리아의 적'일 뿐이라고요.
9. 진짜 히든카드는 열차 안에 있었다
회색 모자는 마지막 열차 칸에서 결정타를 준비해요. 실버애쉬가 숨긴 진짜 비밀이 뭔지 알아냈다고 확신하면서요.

blocks.cap: 문을 열자 나온 건 카시미어의 출정 기사, 상업연합회 대변인, 그리고 로도스 아일랜드 사람들.

blocks.x: 게다가 그 칸에는 레토 일행도 함께 타고 있었어요. 레토가 부탁했던 '조금만 더 빨리 가자'는 재촉이, 실은 이 순간을 위한 거였던 거죠.
회색 모자는 그제야 깨달아요. 엔시오데스가 진짜로 숨겨둔 카드는 무기가 아니라, 빅토리아가 함부로 건드릴 수 없는 손님들이었다는 걸요.
낙성식 현장에 카시미어 기사와 상업연합회 대변인이 도착하자, 빅토리아 병사들은 그대로 공격을 멈춰요. 괜히 카시미어까지 적으로 돌릴 순 없으니까요.
피 한 방울 흘리지 않고 끝난 전쟁, 그리고 그 뒤에 남는 건 카드 게임처럼 판을 짜 놓은 한 남자의 미소뿐이었죠.
에필로그: 봄이 오면
연회는 다시 시작되고, 해럴드의 병사들은 얼음으로 꽃을 조각해 프러포즈를 하고, 데겐블레허는 팬들에게 사인을 해줘요. 방금까지 전쟁터였다는 게 믿기지 않을 정도로요.
성녀 엔야는 낙성식 인사말에서 이렇게 말해요. 언젠가 외지인들이 카란 무역회사뿐 아니라, 눈과 산이 아닌 다른 것으로 이 나라를 기억하게 될 거라고요.
라인 랩은 성산에 발사 시설을 짓자고 제안하고, 성녀와 시녀장은 '동상에 대포를 몇 개나 다냐'며 티격태격해요. 국가의 앞날이 걸린 협상치고는 꽤나 태평하죠.
그리고 밝혀지는 3년의 진실. 은심호 동상 밑에는 사실 고속 전함 한 척이 숨겨져 있었어요. 이름은 '넛츠', 시우루스가 지어준 그 유치한 이름 그대로요.

blocks.cap: 쉐라간드 동상 발밑에서, 3년을 숨겨온 첫 번째 전함이 모습을 드러냅니다.

blocks.x: 엔시오데스는 이걸로 쉐라그가 카시미어, 라인 랩 같은 더 큰 나라들과 마주 앉을 자격을 얻었다고 믿어요. 데겐블레허는 그만큼 적도 늘었을 뿐이라고 되받아치지만요.
레토는 아빠에게 양육비 계좌를 던져주고, 엄마에게 안부 전화를 걸어요. 타티야나는 다친 다리로도 벌써 다음 여행지를 고르고 있죠.
쉐라그에선 등산도 할 수 있고, 암벽 등반도 할 수 있어. 로잘린드, 너 해 봤니?
쉐라간드가 정말 이 땅을 지켜주는지는 아무도 몰라요. 그래도 이 겨울, 아무도 죽지 않았고, 딸은 아빠를 벼랑에서 걷어찼고, 봄은 다시 옵니다.

act29side.tagline: The story of the 'Candlelight Knight,' who returns home chasing her father's death and only finds freedom after seeing off, with her own hands, the person who raised her. And before the dead tyrant's final question, everyone rises with an answer of their own.

chars.name: Viviana
chars.desc: A 'Candlelight Knight' retired from Kazimierz. The hidden daughter of the Prince-Elector of Sturmland, she becomes a candidate for Voice of the Empress while chasing the truth of her father's death.

chars.name: Ebenholz
chars.desc: The Count of Urtica and the Witch King's only living bloodline. A caster with Rhodes Island, he sets foot again in the homeland he has fled for so long.

chars.name: Arturia
chars.desc: A Sankta cellist who wanders the borderlands. She wields Originium Arts that resonate with the deepest emotions in a person's heart · Operator Virtuosa

chars.name: Federico
chars.desc: An Executor with Laterano's Notary Office. He has followed the wanted fugitive Arturia all the way to Leithanien · Operator Executor the Ex Foedere

chars.name: Lessing
chars.desc: A young swordsman raised under the lich scholar Fremont. He hunts Ebenholz on the orders of those seeking to reclaim the 'Horn of Origin.'

chars.name: Loris
chars.desc: Captain of the Zwillingstürme gendarmerie. Originally from Laterano, he lives steeped in drink and bribes—a self-proclaimed 'failure.'

chars.name: Brandt
chars.desc: A Gesetzschwerter, a sword of Leithanien. Born of the Hochberg family, he grew up like a brother to Werner in childhood.

chars.name: Michael
chars.desc: A young courier who serves Empress Grimacht alone. He carries a tuba on his back as he keeps watch over the city.

terms.name: Leithanien
terms.desc: A nation that united ten culturally distinct tribes through the 'Güldenes Gesetz,' a movement completed a thousand years ago. Its capital is Zwillingstürme.

terms.name: Güldenes Gesetz
terms.desc: The golden movement that defines Leithanien. A vast Originium formula that shapes the very cognition, morality, and order of the Leithanien people.

terms.name: Zwillingstürme
terms.desc: The capital of Leithanien. Named the 'Twin Towers' for the black tower Grimacht and the white tower Ewigegnade, home to the two empresses, standing side by side.

terms.name: Gesetzschwerter
terms.desc: Knights who have dedicated sword and Arts to the Güldenes Gesetz and Leithanien. They are dispatched across the land to act on the empresses' will.

terms.name: the Witch King
terms.desc: A tyrant of the previous age, felled by the twin empresses 23 years ago. Even in death, followers remain who still chant his name.

terms.name: Voice of the Empress
terms.desc: A courier who acts on behalf of the twin empresses, chosen from among the children of the regional nobility. Formal appointment takes place at the High Tower after the Empress Festival concludes.

terms.name: Horn of Origin
terms.desc: A term referring both to the 'Echo of Herkunftshorn,' the remnant faction that invokes the Witch King's name, and to the tyrant's final legacy that they seek to reclaim.

blocks.x: Leithanien is a nation built on song. What united its ten disparate tribes wasn't an army, but the 'Güldenes Gesetz'—a golden movement completed a thousand years ago. In Zwillingstürme, the city of twin towers that guards that movement, the Empress Festival is held every year.

But this year, three days before the festival, the aging Prince-Elector of Sturmland is murdered in his own tower. And his hidden daughter, Viviana, returns to the unfamiliar capital as a candidate for 'Voice of the Empress,' honoring her father's final wish.

Twenty-three years since the Witch King died, everyone thought his name as a tyrant had been forgotten. But masked figures have begun chanting that name again, and beneath this autumn's sunset wait reunions and farewells no one could have foreseen.
1. Her Father's Death, and a Mission That Begins at Once

On a stormy night in Sturmland, Prince-Elector Werner is murdered in his own tower. That same night, **Viviana**, the 'Candlelight Knight' retired from Kazimierz, is led by a stranger named Cora out through a general store window as they flee.
Only then does Cora tell her the truth: that Viviana is Werner's hidden daughter, that her father has already passed away, and that she must now go to the capital to become 'Voice of the Empress.'
In Kazimierz, Viviana was always just called 'the knight from Leithanien.' But now that she's actually back home, she still feels like a stranger.

That same night, down a different alley, **Ebenholz** returns to his homeland in secret, his identity hidden. The spy tailing him mutters bitterly, "Former Count of Urtica, are you now… waiting for a true 'death'?"

Arriving in Zwillingstürme, Viviana has an audience with Empress Ewigegnade and is ordered to hunt down the 'Echo of Herkunftshorn' that killed her father, alongside the Gesetzschwerter **Brandt**. Her formal appointment won't come until after the festival, so until then she must move under a hidden identity.

blocks.cap: The colors of classical paintings, the timbre of an accordion, the scent of marigolds. A city that had only ever existed in her imagination now stood before her eyes.

blocks.x: 2. A Dead Painter's Final Work, Not Meant to Be Seen

On the opening day of a gallery exhibition, the painter Madam Siman collapses and dies before her own final work. The painting is titled 'The Death of the Witch King.' Guided there by gendarmerie captain **Loris**, this painting carves a black spiral into the mind of anyone who looks at it.

At that moment, the Sankta cellist **Arturia** appears and calms the chaos. She explains that Madam Siman had admired the Witch King in her youth, and that her heart became entirely trapped the moment she watched him fall.
If it's sincere enough, even a painting or a melody can move a person's heart. Don't be afraid… just feel it.
Meanwhile, **Federico**, an Executor from Laterano, also arrives in the city. The fugitive he's chasing turns out, remarkably, to be none other than his own older sister.

Loris finds Federico exasperating, yet quietly looks out for him. The drunken gendarmerie captain and the Sankta unfamiliar with emotion—these two mismatched people end up traveling together almost by accident.

blocks.cap: A black spiral spills out of the canvas, consuming the entire room. A power that can drive people mad with a single name: the 'Witch King.'

blocks.x: 3. An Atonement That Brought Down an Entire School

The Witch King's remnants believe that the 'Note of the Mortal World' remaining within the Urtica bloodline—within Ebenholz himself—is the key to reviving His Majesty. **Lessing**, the remnants' young swordsman, is ordered to capture him.

It's Gerhard, a gentle scholar at Ludwigs University, who ends up saving Ebenholz. A survivor of the assault on the Witch King's tower 23 years ago, he had long been searching for a way to atone. He leads Ebenholz beneath the university, to the hidden chamber where the Witch King once rewrote the old Güldenes Gesetz.

**Fremont**, the lich scholar guarding that chamber, was in fact an old friend of the Witch King, yet he feigns indifference, insisting he has no interest in national affairs. Even he can't help but turn serious in the face of his student's betrayal.
Gerhard raises a brass instrument and brings it down as hard as he can on the head of the very young man he'd just saved. He, too, turns out to be one of the remnants.

blocks.cap: The person who tried to protect me is the same one who has, for a second time, bound me before the Witch King's throne.

blocks.x: His guilt was, in truth, self-consolation. He wanted proof that there had been a 'better choice,' even if it meant reviving His Majesty. Fremont and Lessing join forces to rescue the students from the collapsing university.

Ermengarde, Fremont's younger lich colleague, grumbles placidly even amid the chaos: "My cube's worn down to nothing, and now this whole mess." But when things actually turn dangerous, she's the first to send out her threads and pull people to safety.
4. A Case Unsolved for 15 Years, and Yesterday's Hero
Loris, chasing the remnants, ultimately loses his life in the sewers. His last request before dying was a single one: "someone, please take up Julia's case."

Julia was Loris's lover, who vanished fifteen years ago. The two parted on the morning of a café's reopening—the very day Loris had planned to propose—and Loris had gone on gathering evidence about the case alone for fifteen years since.
Federico accepts the dying wish according to an Executor's rules, and for the first time begins to understand that 'emotion,' too, can be a form of information.
I'm too tired now, old man. In the end, I… can't die like you. Like a hero.

But that night, Loris finally sits face to face with the fear he had always run from. And in his final moment, he stands against the remnants not as the coward he'd always been, but as a hero who chose, willingly, to return.
5. The Empress Festival, a Blade Hidden Within the Song

On the day of the Empress Festival, tens of thousands gather beneath the twin towers to perform the Güldenes Gesetz together. Once this piece begins, the entire city is dyed with a single emotion—but somewhere within that movement, the remnants had infiltrated, secretly layering another song on top.

Viviana and Cora try to stop the performance, but there isn't enough time. While Federico holds off the empress's casters, Viviana and Cora spend the remaining twenty-three seconds severing the connections of some of the musicians.
As the sunset falls, the earth splits open and a dark crimson tower rises up.

blocks.cap: The storm clouds part, and the tower that fell 23 years ago reveals itself once more. The 'Horn of Origin.'

blocks.x: 6. How to See Off the Person You Love Most

Thirteen years ago, it was Cora who took young Viviana, freshly orphaned of her mother, all the way to Kazimierz. From then until now, Cora had always been the one family who stayed by Viviana's side.
But Cora had long been connected to the remnants' 'Chief.' What she wanted wasn't betrayal—only a future where no one would ever be hurt again the way Lucinda and Werner had been.
But that longing had slowly consumed Cora herself, and only one path remained: to meet her end at the hands of someone she loved.
Viviana raised her candlelight. She did not hesitate.

blocks.cap: Cough, cough… Viviana, are you crying? It seems I don't even have the strength left to wipe your tears away.

blocks.x: You deserve to live a better life, Viviana… if such a life truly exists.

"Thanks to you, Viviana and I will go somewhere far away." And with that, Cora vanished into black flame. Brandt left only a single line behind: "No need to explain. You made your own judgment, and you carried it through."
7. The Throne Was Empty

As the tower rises, people all across the city are pulled into the 'Horn of Origin,' where reality and chaos intermingle. At the end of an endless spiral staircase, Ebenholz finally comes face to face with the Echo of the Witch King seated upon the throne.

Before the voice that has tormented him his whole life, the name that bound him to an examination table since childhood, Ebenholz has come prepared with every curse and insult he knows. But the tyrant, unexpectedly calm, simply points out that Ebenholz's true enemy has always been himself.

blocks.cap: "You have defeated your enemy. You have escaped the high tower that once caged you." And so the tyrant acknowledged the descendant he had once despised.

blocks.x: Ebenholz can't help but burst into laughter at those words. When the laughter fades, the throne is empty. The 'Witch King' he had feared for so long had, in the end, existed only within his own imagination.
8. For Hearts Going Hollow, the Sankta's Final Melody

Within that same chaos, Federico once again encounters traces of the dead Julia. As she disappears, forever unaware of the proposal Loris never got to make, even the ever-composed Federico finds himself, for the first time, unable to speak.
Arturia, too, completely loses the sound of her own cello. She, who has always read the hearts of others, comes face to face for the first time with just how fragile she herself is.

blocks.cap: Silence, too, is a performance, and every piece must have an ending. For the first time, the Sankta acknowledged her own tears.

blocks.x: In the square beneath the tower, people who had lost every last emotion were turning into hollow shells. This time, Arturia chooses to play not someone else, but herself.
I will share my feelings with everyone. With this small, unremarkable pride of mine, I will connect every sorrow and every joy.

blocks.cap: At the sound of the Sankta's cello, expression returns once more to the faces of the hollowed-out people.

blocks.x: 9. The Knight in the Sunset, and the Two Empresses' Final Duet
Viviana, too, opens hundreds of doors within that same chaos. Behind them lie lives that could never have been: 'parents who made better choices,' 'a self who came home sooner.'

She even comes face to face with her own childhood self. That child asks her back: isn't wandering in search of a better answer really just an escape—a way of comforting herself with the thought that 'there was never any other way'?
With collapse bearing down on her, Viviana chooses not to run. In her hand, the same light that shone from the knight in the novels she read as a child now takes hold.

blocks.cap: Viviana Droste, don't forget: you are a knight!

blocks.x: Meanwhile, Grimacht and Ewigegnade confront the Echo of the Witch King before the throne. The two empresses bring him down, but through the cracks of the collapsing tower, the gaze of things far more terrifying begins to seep in.

blocks.cap: Black and red, and dazzling gold and violet. Two generations of Leithanien's sovereigns clash before the throne.

blocks.x: Today, or in some distant future, I will lose my life here and become part of the chaos. This is my fate. But it is never… never the fate of Leithanien or its people.

blocks.cap: The sword vanishes together with its shadow. In the end, she never looked back.

blocks.x: The sunset returns. With the instruments in their hands, people begin to play, each their own, an entirely new song—one that is not the Güldenes Gesetz.

blocks.cap: Golden light pours down through the storm clouds. A new sunset for Leithanien.

blocks.x: Having lost Grimacht, Ewigegnade chooses to hold onto that sunset forever, alone. "Because I am more perfect than Herkunftshorn." She said it with a smile, but tears ran down her cheeks.
Michael, recalling Grimacht's final order, plays his tuba alone. Now the mountain of paperwork left to handle, and the one remaining tower to guard, have both fallen to him.
Epilogue: The Sunset, Once More

Two weeks later, after hearing Lessing's long story, Ebenholz decides to return to the Urtica estate. If he was the one to inherit the Witch King's name, he reasoned, then it was also his to remake that name into something entirely different.

Hibiscus, his colleague from Rhodes Island, tracks him down belatedly and scolds him for disappearing without so much as a leave request. But seeing Ebenholz's composed expression, she realizes this is a decision he's already thought through more than enough, and quietly sorts out the paperwork for him.

Viviana politely declines the position of Voice of the Empress. When a reply arrives from the Nearl family of Kazimierz, she leaves the tree in the garden of the small house Cora once bought her to grow freely, and sets out once more, following a new lead.

Even as he takes Arturia back to Laterano, Federico lingers a while at her side, watching the sunset together. Just as she once promised, this was a moment when even the most rational of men could not, in the end, escape the beauty of this city.
Light is eternal, and it is darkness that is fleeting. Our feelings never truly disappear… just as the sunset has never once left the sky.

blocks.cap: Even the answer that the most rational of men arrived at was, without fail, beautiful.

blocks.x: Leithanien remains a nation that stands on song. Only this autumn, that song carries within it a silence someone chose for themselves, and a sorrow someone else chose to bear in another's place. And once the marigolds fall, another sunset will come.

act28side.tagline: Mercenaries who came to fix a single power plant realize that saving the town means robbing a bank first. And this season's gentlest confession: he chose her not for her gun, but for her tears.

chars.name: Jessica
chars.desc: A young sniper with Blacksteel. A soft-hearted soul who can never hold back tears in a crisis · Operator Jessica the Liberated

chars.name: Woodrow Bianchi
chars.desc: A gruff old man who lives and hunts alone on the outskirts of Davis Town. The townsfolk just call him 'Woody'

chars.name: Helena
chars.desc: The proprietress of Davis Town's only restaurant. She's appointed herself guardian of the whole neighborhood · Operator Coldshot

chars.name: Leon Theremin
chars.desc: A demolitions engineer at the mining plant. A father raising two sons alone

chars.name: Benny
chars.desc: Leon's youngest son. A precocious, warm-hearted boy

chars.name: Sylvia
chars.desc: A young, quiet employee at Davis Town's bank

chars.name: 'Clip' Cliff
chars.desc: The head of the Blacksteel mercenary company. A man who keeps business and feeling strictly separate

chars.name: Franka
chars.desc: A commander-ranked mercenary with Blacksteel. Breezy and quick with words

chars.name: Liskarm
chars.desc: Franka's partner. A cautious, principled team leader

chars.name: Laura
chars.desc: Blacksteel's chief engineer. An optimist who talks to herself constantly · Operator Almond

terms.name: Davis Town
terms.desc: A small mining sector stranded in Columbia's eastern forestlands. Its finances collapsed after a power plant accident

terms.name: Blacksteel Worldwide
terms.desc: A mercenary company rooted in Columbia. It looks moderate on the surface, but handles the dirty work behind the scenes

terms.name: Baron Base
terms.desc: Blacksteel's mobile mothership. It ferries mercenary units between sectors

terms.name: the Frontier
terms.desc: A harsh settlement zone where bankrupt debtors or criminals are forcibly sent

terms.name: Sankta
terms.desc: A race bearing rings of light. Said to be able to faintly sense one another's pain

terms.name: Laterano
terms.desc: Homeland of the Sankta, a nation famed for its idealism

terms.name: Colus Camp
terms.desc: A POW camp from the Columbian War of Independence. Infamous for the saying, "of ten who go in, only one comes out"

blocks.x: There's a familiar combo in the world of Arknights: a sector with no money, and the bank that comes to collect it anyway. Davis Town, a mining sector stranded in Columbia's eastern forestlands, was no exception.

A year after its power plant blew, with the government doing nothing but watching the snow pile up, a Blacksteel mercenary unit is dispatched to this town. Their stated mission: repair the power plant.
But rookie mercenary **Jessica** is carrying a suspicious bullet secretly slipped to her by a superior — along with a vague errand: "give it to Woodrow Bianchi of Davis Town," and nothing more.
