A few months later, the mercenaries who came to save this town are robbing its bank. A bit out of order, isn't it? Let's unpack what happened in between.
1. Picked up a hunter in the snow — turns out he had a name after all

Before the power plant mission even starts, Jessica gets separated in a blizzard. What appears with a gunshot is a gruff old hunter. Mid-dressing a deer he'd just shot, he casually offers to walk her to Davis Town.

On the way, they find a fawn caught in a trap. The hunter says to just wring its neck and let it go easy; Jessica insists on holding it in her arms until it dies naturally. "First time I've seen a mercenary do hospice care," the hunter remarks.

blocks.cap: Follow the broken branches through the fog, and a sector appears, gasping for breath like a patient in critical condition.

blocks.x: That evening, at Helena's restaurant, Jessica hears the hunter called 'Woody' and asks, "Do you happen to know Woodrow Bianchi?" Helena and Leon both blink. "Who......?" Nobody knew the real name of the man they saw every day.
2. A single bank has the whole town mortgaged

Davis Town's bank is the kind of place that answers a request for a power of attorney with "the seal suddenly went missing yesterday." The government office squats in the bank's garage, and residents scoop up snow outside their windows to melt and drink, just to save water.
Leon volunteers for the dangerous power plant repair, his eyes lighting up at the mention of a 200,000-check bonus if it's done within a week.
Pressed for the reason, Leon opens up. "Basically the whole sector's in debt. Most of the people who walked into the snow and disappeared did it because of money."

In fact, Jessica had just witnessed one of those missing people herself — a man who didn't turn even when called, walking into the blizzard and vanishing, leaving only footprints. She'd thought this was a cheerful little town; the chill sets in from the very start.
3. The power plant, and the people, were already wrecked

Inside the power plant, things are bad enough that even Laura says, "I must be hallucinating." There's even evidence of 'legal looting' from well before the accident — caravans quietly swapping forklifts and canned goods on the sly.

Laura normally talks to herself nonstop while working. So when she suddenly falls silent, a coworker explains, "When Laura stops talking, that means......" Before he can finish, the evacuation alarm blares.

Thankfully everyone makes it back without major incident. Laura's first words — "Move it, Jessica...... I'm starving to death" — finally let everyone breathe easy. Meanwhile, in the alley behind the bank, Jessica rescues Leon's son **Benny**, who'd nearly been kidnapped by thugs.
4. A friendly town makes for a scarier plan

The bank hands Liskarm a 'public order maintenance list' — essentially treating people in debt as would-be robbers to be dealt with. Liskarm refuses to take the list at face value and insists on re-investigating.

A Blacksteel colleague Jessica meets on patrol lets something slip. "A sector on the verge of bankruptcy like this — debt, tax audits, security loopholes...... plenty of excuses to go around." Once reclaimed by the government, whether it's industry, facilities, or residents, everything would get replaced.
Jessica bristles. "That's illegal!" The reply comes back flat. "Don't care."

That night, working late alone at the bank, **Sylvia** finds it strange that the main power switch sits exactly one hundred steps from the front door. Ever since her mother died, she's had a habit of walking the same route home every day and counting her steps. "No, that can't be...... it doesn't make sense." A suspicion even she doesn't want to believe.
5. Benny decides to leave Davis Town

Leon, Helena, Woodrow, Miles, and Jessica all sit down to dinner together for the first time in a while. Everyone humming an old song about the town — the warmest scene in this whole story. Then Benny quietly raises his hand.
"I'm leaving." A scholarship, he announces, to a school in Ironforge City. Leon just repeats "It's fine...... it's a good thing......" like a machine, unable to string together a proper sentence.

blocks.cap: The son wanted to see anger in his father's eyes. But all that was there was pure devastation.

blocks.x: Right before leaving, Benny drops a bomb on Jessica: his late brother **Karl** was a Blacksteel mercenary, a 'Blackplate.' Jessica brushes it off, saying they didn't know each other long — but she's clearly hiding something.

That night, in the bank vault, Sylvia clutches a necklace and mutters to herself. "Karl...... what am I supposed to do?" Between the dead mercenary and the bank employee, there was a story nobody knew about.
6. Turns out the boss was an old comrade-in-arms

Jessica finally hands that bullet over to Woodrow. The moment 'the boss' in an old photo hidden in his drawer overlaps with Cliff's face, he murmurs low. "So he changed his name...... your boss...... it was him."

blocks.cap: Autumn, year 1019, Colus Camp, Columbia. The man who survived and came back sits face to face again with the man who never came to rescue him.

blocks.x: The fellow Sankta imprisoned alongside them died in Woodrow's arms in the end. Cliff was the one who gave up on the rescue operation. Woodrow goes to Cliff's office and demands he "call back his men and stop driving out the residents," but Cliff won't budge, insisting, "if Blacksteel leaves, the government will just send another mercenary company."
That night, thugs from the sector's east side riot. Miles fends off the intruders with a steam-valve trap, and Woodrow strings one up under the eaves with a noose.
And Leon's house burns down. "Not so bad, being burned down...... one less thing to worry about now," Leon says bitterly, and that's all.

blocks.cap: After a fifteen-day voyage, Baron Base breaks through the thick snow fog and arrives at Davis Town.

blocks.x: 7. A threat by the name of 'public order maintenance'
The bank manager hands Liskarm a document — a three-way contract between the bank, the government, and Blacksteel, making it a legitimate order, she claims.
Liskarm refuses. "I won't be firing my gun. And I won't let anyone on my team fire theirs, either."

blocks.cap: With a single sheet titled "Authority Delegation Agreement," the bank tries to borrow the mercenary company's gun barrels.

blocks.x: Residents gathered outside the bank sing a protest song. "We're the ones starving, we're the ones who gave everything for a worthless promise...... farewell, my hometown ♪" With that song behind them, Helena quietly dances with Woodrow — a brief moment of peace in a town that could vanish any day.
But while sorting documents, Sylvia discovers a stack of blank contracts prepared for people who never signed anything, who never even set foot in the bank.
We don't even care about the paltry interest. What we're after is everything here. That's how it's always been.
8. In the end, everyone decides to become thieves

Jessica confronts Cliff. "It seems like you've lost almost all your humanity just to get those things." Cliff calmly asks back, "Humanity, huh...... what good is a pretty word like that to the head of a mercenary company?"
In the end, Jessica walks away on her own, effectively splitting from Blacksteel.

Sylvia plays her final card: a large sum sitting in the bank vault, set aside for the new settlers. "Do you want us to return that money to its rightful owners, or do you want it taken from you?" Woodrow cuts straight to the point, and Sylvia nods quietly.

Helena is the first to set an old weapon down on the table — the resolve of someone who's survived twenty years wandering the Frontier. "If none of you want in, I'll do it alone." Leon pulls out a wrench too.

Woodrow points his gun at Jessica one last time, telling her to "leave," but Jessica lowers her own weapon and answers instead. "If I don't do something, I'll get used to a life where I never accomplish anything. I don't want that, even if it kills me...... let me in too."
9. How to rob a bank: one hundred steps and some dynamite

Following the bank's layout and passcode Sylvia memorized through years of footsteps, the four of them move. Woodrow and Jessica control the hostages, while Helena and Leon breach their way to the vault — the roles were split that way.

blocks.cap: The black-and-white snowfield regains its color. Asked what they'll do once this is over, neither of them can easily answer.

blocks.x: With their lives on the line, both the bank president and the manager give up the passcode without much fight. The president crumbles under Woodrow's countdown, and the manager, trying to provoke Leon, gets restrained by Jessica instead.
At the final door, Sylvia's instructions are simple. "Easy bonus question — blow it up."

blocks.cap: With the blast, shredded bundles of cash pour down heavier than the snow that ever fell on Davis Town.

blocks.x: The four of them stand speechless in front of the pile of money for a moment. "I've never seen this much money in my life," Helena says, and Leon answers, "But it's still a lot less than what the bank ever squeezed out of us."
Jessica just finds it strange — she's never wanted money this much in her life.
10. Leon stays behind with the power plant
The escape doesn't go as planned. While Woodrow stays behind alone to hold off Blacksteel, Leon and Helena rig the hidden passage with explosives.
With each charge he sets, Leon sees hallucinations — Benny, already gone; Karl, killed in action; and finally, the mother who once left him behind, all passing before his eyes one after another.

In the end, Leon decides to stay and seal off the passage himself. Over the radio, to a desperate Jessica, the last thing he leaves her is a song. "I won't die...... I'll be here forever, living forever in her embrace......"

blocks.cap: From a massive crack in the tower, smoke keeps pouring out without end.

blocks.x: At the same moment, Woodrow stands facing Cliff, guns drawn. For the first time, he calls him by his real name. "Rupert."
Cliff practically begs to be killed. "You can end me." But instead of pulling the trigger, Woodrow shoots and destroys his radio.
I can't kill you. This sin is yours to carry.

blocks.cap: The old man's hat flies off in the blizzard. Gun barrels aimed at one another, neither fires first.

blocks.x: Through her scope, Jessica sees strangers' faces flash past — a praying girl, a woman running through snow, a man who walked off into flames, one after another.

blocks.cap: Faces flashing past through the scope. The last was the man who walked off into the flames.

blocks.x: Jessica fires every bullet she has straight into the sky.
My tears can't stand in for their grief. But my gun will ring out for their grief instead.
In five years, did you ever once realize why I chose you back then? I chose you not for the gun in your hand, but for your tears. Tears are never a weakness, Jessica.

Jessica lets herself be arrested without resistance, as Franka fastens a pair of handcuffs she'd warmed up beforehand. Realizing the night has passed and the snow has stopped, Jessica quietly hums along to the song Leon used to sing.
Epilogue. People starting over
Cliff quietly looks the other way. Jessica admits her guilt and volunteers for the Frontier instead of a reduced sentence; Helena also escapes safely.
Woodrow is offered a new identity and a mansion down south, but he refuses. "I'm going to look for family, not friends."

As they part, Cliff asks one thing of him. "Don't die somewhere I don't know about." Woodrow answers, "Even if there's no undoing it, I suppose I can still call you again sometime." Not a complete break, in other words.

blocks.cap: To a young Jessica crying in front of a collapsed sandcastle, her grandfather says, "Don't be afraid to start over."
The memory fades, and the Jessica of today answers. "...... Yes, let's start over."

blocks.x: Woodrow climbs onto the transport heading to the Frontier alongside Jessica. Fitting a wide-brimmed hat over her head, he says,
Cry if you need to...... every new life is born crying, after all. That's the first step to breathing.

blocks.cap: Under the hat, Jessica finally breaks into tears. The old man looks out at the horizon, blaming it on the morning glow.

blocks.x: Davis Town is still right there, gasping for breath like a patient in critical condition. The only thing different is that it wasn't the bank, and it wasn't the government, that saved this town — it was four people who chose to become thieves. And what brought one of them back to life wasn't a gun, but tears.

act27side.tagline: A mysterious creature promised to grant a wish if three little lost things were found for it — but what it actually gave back to a grieving daughter was her parents' last, true feelings. On the night the volcano burns pure white, everyone learns, little by little, how to smile again.

chars.name: Adel
chars.desc: Daughter of a volcanologist couple. A rookie researcher who carries on her late parents' work despite suffering from Oripathy · Operator Eyjafjalla

chars.name: Ennis
chars.desc: A Siesta boy shouldering his whole family's livelihood alone, juggling four part-time jobs while putting his own health dead last

chars.name: Keller
chars.desc: An old colleague of the Naumann couple and the new volcano museum's curator, whose evasive eyes keep nagging at everyone's mind

chars.name: Swire
chars.desc: Commissioner of the Lungmen Guard Department, insisting he's just on vacation — a claim nobody believes from this conglomerate heir · Operator Swire

chars.name: Bison
chars.desc: Heir to Mountain Dash Logistics, a diligent young master trying to step out of his father's shadow · Operator Bison

chars.name: Ceylon
chars.desc: The mayor's daughter and a doctor with Rhodes Island, distant from her own father · Operator Ceylon

chars.name: Dolly
chars.desc: A fluffy, unidentified creature spotted all over Siesta, who out of nowhere proposes a treasure hunt to Adel

chars.name: Kahn
chars.desc: A volcanologist who once studied under the Naumanns, unable to let go of his suspicions toward his mentor Keller

chars.name: Costa
chars.desc: A city hall clerk going door to door collecting relocation forms, who is secretly the son of a shuttered café

terms.name: Siesta
terms.desc: A resort city-state that once lived off its volcano, obsidian, and rock music. When volcanic activity resumed, the entire city relocated as a nomadic city.

terms.name: Chokomi Sheep
terms.desc: Fluffy avatars Dolly claims to have created. Mischievous little things that carry off anything eye-catching, be it a street sign or a wallet.

terms.name: Volcano Warning Flower
terms.desc: A legendary plant said to change color according to volcanic activity. The very research subject the Naumann couple chased in their lifetime.

terms.name: Obsidian
terms.desc: Ore once mined from the Siesta volcano. Once the city's main source of income, mining was later banned over environmental damage concerns.

terms.name: Obsidian Music Festival
terms.desc: A rock music festival once held in Siesta. It naturally ended when the city relocated.

blocks.x: Siesta was a resort city that lived off its volcano, its obsidian, and a rock festival once said to be the continent's finest. But when that volcano showed real signs of waking up, the entire city packed up and moved 100 kilometers away as a nomadic city.

Adel, daughter of the late volcanologist couple, comes to Siesta to sort through her parents' research materials at the newly built volcano museum. As it happens, that very volcano is on the verge of a real eruption.

But on her very first night there, a talking flock of sheep appears and makes her a proposal. "Just find the three things I lost here." A north wind, a seed, and some fur — that's all it takes, apparently.
1. Is it normal for sheep to talk like this — first time I've seen one talk back

Siesta was originally a beach town at the foot of a volcano. Famous for its hot springs, obsidian, and the **Obsidian Music Festival**, the entire city packed up and relocated 100 kilometers away as the nomadic city 'New Siesta' when the volcano stirred again.

Four years ago, volcanologist couple Katia and Magna Naumann died in an accident while observing Mount Una. Their daughter **Adel** is a rookie researcher carrying on her parents' final work despite suffering from Oripathy. Her hearing has worsened to the point she struggles to converse without a hearing aid, but her eyes light up the moment volcanoes come up.

**Keller**, an old colleague of her parents, called Adel over — asking her to organize the Naumanns' materials at the new volcano museum. With the Siesta volcano also due to erupt soon, she says, it's a perfect opportunity for observation.

blocks.cap: A downtown where buildings rise instead of mountain peaks. New Siesta was built to mimic the shape of the old volcano across the whole city.

blocks.x: A display case in the museum holds an old volcano exploration suit — one all too familiar to Adel, her mother Katia's own gear. She reaches out across the glass, but can't quite touch it.

blocks.cap: A daughter and her mother's suit, separated by a single pane of glass. The hand that reached to touch it pulled back, as if burned.

blocks.x: Strange things start happening from the very first night. A cloud-fluffy creature perches on a mailbox and speaks to her. "Hello there, lovely night."

This creature introduces itself as **Dolly**. It claims to have known Adel's parents long ago, but is vague about what it actually is. All over Siesta, there are fluffy sheep invisible to human eyes — and apparently, they're all Dolly's 'avatars.'
Simply put, I want you to find a few things I lost around Siesta. Think of it as a sort of treasure hunt.

Dolly is after exactly three things: a **north wind**, a **seed**, and **fur**. In return, it promises to give Adel "the thing she's been searching for." Asked what that might be, it never gives an answer.

Meanwhile, several suspicious people had already arrived in town. One of them is **Swire**, Commissioner of the Lungmen Guard Department and heir to one of the biggest merchant houses, insisting this time it's "just a vacation." Naturally, nobody believed her.
2. A boy juggling four part-time jobs, hiding only his own body

**Ennis** is a Siesta boy juggling several part-time jobs at once — freight driver, ticket seller, swim coach. Feeding his two adopted siblings Root and Liv, plus his mother Hailey who runs the bar 'Pure White Volcano,' is his job.
That same Ennis collides head-on with Adel while running down the street. Guilt-ridden, he even covers her treatment costs — but says nothing at all about his own condition.

In truth, Ennis is an Oripathy patient too. Three months ago, he scraped himself on Originium while secretly mining obsidian. He tried to hide it from his family to the end, but it shows plainly on his face.
In exchange for a research sample of **obsidian**, Adel earnestly warns Ennis never to go near the volcano again. That promise, of all things, is one Ennis can't keep.
Later, when his mother Hailey learns the truth, what she says cuts straight to the heart. "You're my son whether you do anything or not — this isn't a relationship that needs a transaction."
Family is family, blood or not.
3. A student suspects his mentor — but the more he suspects, the more suspicious his own heart looks
A letter arrives for Adel, its envelope half torn open, containing a photo of her father taken at Mount Una — as if sent by the father who'd been out of contact for years.

That's not the only strange thing. Among the museum's materials, several pages that should have been in her mother Katia's research notebook are missing entirely. In their place sits an unfamiliar notebook that only Keller ever had.

**Kahn** was a student of the Naumann couple, and he's long harbored suspicions about his mentor Keller. A year before the accident, he says, the Leithanien military kept coming and going from the couple's lab.
By Kahn's account, only one person could freely view the couple's research back then — Keller, and Keller alone. What's more, she suddenly left the expedition the day before the climb.
That's why I absolutely cannot accept their sacrifice being buried under some unknown shadow.

Adel still doesn't know whether Kahn's suspicions are right, or whether Keller has some other circumstance she can't voice. Keller only ever repeats, "I'll tell you everything once the observation is done."
4. Two rich kids in a mudslinging fight who somehow end up business partners

**Bison** is heir to Mountain Dash Logistics. Trying to step out of his father Euril's shadow, he throws himself alone into the bid for Siesta's new district development rights — against, of all people, Swire.
The two clash the moment they meet at a bar, locked in a tense standoff — Swire pushing for a water park, Bison for a logistics center. Yet somehow, each secretly rather likes the other.

Swire recruits shop owner Ennis as an informant, strikes a deal with hot spring hotel owner Felipe using his obsidian collection as bait, and secretly throws together a water park practically overnight. In the process, Dolly's Chokomi Sheep flood the waterway in a disaster involving surfboards flying through the sky.

blocks.cap: On surfboards soaring above the hot spring water park, little lambs invisible to anyone else were having the time of their lives.

blocks.x: In the end, Swire's plan wins the public poll. But Swire had been calculating something other than victory all along — offering to drop out of the bid in exchange for shares in Bison's company.

**"A smart merchant always finds a way for both sides to win."** The two go from rivals to partners. Swire uses this to strengthen her standing within the Lungmen clan, while Bison declares his independence from his father's company. Quite a scale for a couple of rich kids' summer vacation.
5. A café shut for over a year gets a customer — turns out to be the owner's old friend

**Costa** is a city hall clerk going around collecting residents' relocation forms. In truth, he's the son of 'Café Mockingbird,' shuttered for a year now, and once played in a rock band in his younger days.
One night, Costa reopens the empty café and brews some coffee. Then he hears a knock at the door. "Are you still open, by any chance?"

blocks.cap: Under the café's light, lit again after a year, the two of them recognize each other.

blocks.x: Keller was standing at the door. The two reminisce together about the summer twenty years ago, when they first met Katia and Magna at this very café — even Costa's secretly brewed 'volcano coffee' from back then.
That summer Keller suddenly left Siesta, Costa had to see her off without ever knowing why. And the two of them walked separate paths all the way to now.
Costa's grandfather quietly passes away that same night. All he left his grandson was an old kalimba, and a nagging reminder to do what he wants rather than settle for stability.
6. A night walk with two little sheep turns out to be a road leading skyward
One night, wearing her mother's old exploration suit, Adel falls into a strange dream. Two Chokomi Sheep — one gentle, one solemn — lead her walking through a shopping street.
These shops don't sell compasses or tents. They sell 'knowledge' and 'courage,' and a very expensive 'luck.' Oddly enough, the currency here is bottle caps.
The two creatures tell Adel an old story. They once planted a stone and lost it, and that stone eventually became magma deep underground. Asked if they regret it, both shake their heads and smile.
Nothing from the past truly passes away — it just stays by your side in a different form.
Adel vaguely senses this story is really about them. But then, a strange heartbeat echoes from the direction of the volcano, and the two creatures' expressions suddenly change.

blocks.cap: Farewell, Adel. She couldn't hold back the two creatures as they turned toward the volcano.

blocks.x: "We have to reach the volcano before the rain falls." With those words, the two vanish into the night view. The next morning, Adel can't find a single Chokomi Sheep anywhere in Siesta.
7. A Chokomi Sheep suddenly buries its head in someone's chest — the owner of that heartbeat hasn't even been born yet
**Ceylon** is Mayor Herman's daughter and a doctor with Rhodes Island. She returned to Siesta to help the Infected, but stays distant from her own father.
While investigating illegal obsidian mining laborers, Ceylon hears an unexpected name — Barbara, a pregnant scholar who once frequented the mines. The workers say she looked just like Ceylon.
Among the collection of hot spring hotel owner Felipe was a geode of obsidian the workers once tried, and failed, to give to that scholar — a gift meant to celebrate the birth of her baby.
That evening, one of the Chokomi Sheep Adel had been chasing suddenly leaps into Ceylon's arms. It rests its head against her chest and stays still, as if listening for a heartbeat lost long ago.

In that moment, Ceylon feels an inexplicable longing — thoughts of a mother she's never met surface. She begins to vaguely realize whose will Siesta's generous policy toward the Infected really was, all along.
8. An interrogation right at the volcano's foot — but both interrogator and suspect are trembling
Signal cuts off from Observation Post No. 1. The last data it sent shows an unexplainable spike in heat. Keller tries to brush it off as an equipment glitch, but Adel doesn't back down.
"Turning away now is as good as admitting their sacrifice meant nothing, isn't it?" In the end, the two of them risk it and head to the volcano themselves.

But Kahn had already arrived at the observation post ahead of them. In his hand, an old document envelope — letters exchanged between Keller and the Leithanien military, evidence even Adel had never seen.

Even as the volcano trembles, Kahn demands answers from Keller — why she left the expedition right before the accident, and why Katia's research was mixed in with her correspondence with the military.
Mom, Dad...... was your sacrifice really just an accident......?

The truth comes out later. Keller had only tried to buy time by meeting with the military, so the couple could finish their expedition undisturbed. That an accident would happen in the meantime was something even Keller never saw coming.
9. The night the volcano erupts, everyone throws a party instead of running away

A broadcast announces the eruption is imminent. Instead of luggage, the shop owners bring out grills and guitars, throwing one last party for the old Siesta. After all, this is a sight they'll never see again.

In the commotion, little sister Liv goes missing. Ennis chases her all the way to the old shore where seawater is already rising. There, for the first time, he feels the Originium crystal inside his body burn hot.
See...... I told you...... I can do magic......

Ennis, who'd always played at magic with clay dolls for his siblings, actually raises earth and stone to hold back the waves. And countless Chokomi Sheep swarm in, forming a living dam with their own bodies against the tide.

blocks.cap: Fluffy bodies piled layer upon layer, swallowing the wave whole. That's how the siblings survived.

blocks.x: In the distance, the volcano finally erupts. People clasp each other's hands tight and hold their breath. Dark red smoke covers half the sky, then astonishingly turns a brilliant white.

blocks.cap: Farewell, Siesta. Pure white volcanic ash split the sky and settled over the old city.

blocks.x: **The 'pure white volcano,' known only as a legend, finally revealed its true self the moment it erupted.** Everyone, each in their own way, bids farewell to the vanishing hometown.
Epilogue. Summer, again

The day after the eruption, Adel writes a letter to her parents — stories of the old friends she met in Siesta, a confession that she's not as strong as she thought, and a vow that she still loves volcanoes.

blocks.cap: To my beloved mom and dad. Adel wrote out a letter she'd never be able to send.

blocks.x: Keller shows Adel one last remaining box. Inside are documents stamped with a discard order for the 'Shower Project' — evidence that her parents' research had once been caught up in weaponization talks. And bit by bit, Keller begins to let go of the guilt she'd carried, believing the real fault was hers alone.

Ennis finally tells his family he's leaving on a journey. Just as his father once did, he wants to see the world with his own eyes. And instead of being torn down, the bar 'Pure White Volcano' survives as part of the water park.
Ceylon builds an Infected treatment center in New Siesta. What her mother Barbara had tried to do, the daughter now carries on in her own way.

Lastly, Dolly hands Adel her real reward — none other than the **Volcano Warning Flower**. Secretly planted on a slope beyond the volcano, it was the very flower her parents had spent their whole lives searching for.
"I look forward to seeing a truly happy Adel next time we meet." With that farewell, Dolly hurries off to greet the opening of the volcano amusement park.

At the very start of this story, an unknown voice complained it couldn't bear the boredom without something new and fun. Now we can guess that voice belonged to none other than Dolly. This summer, when the volcano burned pure white, both Dolly and Adel finally found their own interesting story to tell.

act26side.tagline: 모든 사고를 합리로만 재단하던 집행자가, 낙원이라 불리는 황야의 수도원에서 법으로는 구할 수 없는 절망과 마주치는 이야기. 그리고 그는 태어나 처음으로, 스스로에게 질문을 던집니다.

chars.name: 페데리코
chars.desc: 감정과 사고를 철저히 분리해 움직이는 라테라노 공증소의 신임 집행자. 최근 '성도' 칭호를 받았다 · 오퍼레이터 이그제큐터 디 엑스 포에데레

chars.name: 르무엔
chars.desc: 라테라노에서 파견됐다가 연락이 끊긴 추기경 보좌관. 다리를 다쳐 휠체어에 의지하고 있다

chars.name: 세르필리아
chars.desc: 총기 정비에 능한 발랄한 산크타 기술자, 르무엔의 오랜 후배 · 오퍼레이터 스푸리아

chars.name: 아르투리아
chars.desc: 어디선가 홀연히 나타나 첼로를 연주하는 정체불명의 여행자 · 오퍼레이터 비르투오사

chars.name: 스테파노 토레그로사
chars.desc: 60년째 이동식 수도원 암브로시우스를 이끌어온 늙은 주교

chars.name: 제럴드
chars.desc: 수도원에 정착한 살카즈 무리를 이끄는 노련한 사냥꾼

chars.name: 클레망
chars.desc: 정성껏 꽃을 가꾸는 수도원의 온화한 정원사

chars.name: 델피나
chars.desc: 산크타 소녀. 포르투나의 소꿉친구이자, 함께 자란 살카즈들의 편에 서는 아이

chars.name: 라이문트
chars.desc: 수도원에서 나고 자란 혈기왕성한 젊은 살카즈

chars.name: 포르투나
chars.desc: 다정하고 사교적인 산크타 소녀. 델피나·라이문트와 어릴 때부터 가까운 사이다

terms.name: 라테라노
terms.desc: 산크타들의 신성한 도시. 세상이 인정하는 유일한 낙원이라 불린다.

terms.name: 산크타
terms.desc: 머리 위에 광륜을 지니고 서로의 마음을 읽는 '교감' 능력을 지닌 종족. 라테라노의 주류 민족이다.

terms.name: 살카즈
terms.desc: 이마에 뿔이 있는 종족. 전쟁과 배척 속에 정착할 곳을 찾아 떠도는 경우가 많다.

terms.name: 성도
terms.desc: 본래 라테라노를 세운 성인들에게만 허락되던 칭호. 교황이 아닌 몸으로 이 칭호를 받은 건 페데리코가 처음이다.

terms.name: 타천사
terms.desc: 금기를 어겨 광륜이 뒤틀리고 이마에 뿔이 돋아난 산크타. 더는 동족과 교감할 수 없게 된다.

terms.name: 심해 교회
terms.desc: 이베리아 인근에서 활동한다는 수상한 신앙 집단. 사람을 알 수 없는 존재로 바꿔놓는다고 전해진다.

blocks.x: 라테라노는 산크타의 낙원이라고 불려요. 광륜을 인 머리, 서로의 마음을 읽는 '교감' 능력, 그리고 천 년째 흔들리지 않는 신앙까지. 이 도시는 스스로를 세상에 하나뿐인 이상향이라 믿어요.
그런데 만약 그 낙원이 정말 하나뿐이라면, 낙원 밖으로 밀려난 이들은 대체 어디로 가야 할까요? 신임 성도 페데리코는 그런 질문을 한 번도 품어본 적 없는 사람이에요. 감정을 배제하고 오직 합리와 규정만으로 움직이는, 라테라노 공증소의 집행자거든요.
교황은 그런 그에게 낯선 임무를 맡겨요. 60년 전 항로를 잃고 황야를 떠돌게 된 이동식 수도원에서, 파견됐다가 연락이 끊긴 특사 두 명을 찾아오라는 것. 도착한 수도원에는 산크타 난민과 살카즈 용병들이 함께 살고 있었고, 라테라노가 내건 귀환 조건은 단 하나였어요: '낙원은 살카즈를 받지 않는다.'
1. 성도 취임식, 그리고 하늘로 승천한 풍선 아저씨
교황 이반젤리스타 11세는 즉위 이래 처음으로, 교황이 아닌 몸에게 '성도' 칭호를 내려요. 후보는 다름 아닌 집행자 페데리코. 정작 본인은 칭호의 무게보다 '대체 무슨 재난이 오길래'에만 관심이 있죠.
교황은 아무에게도 말하지 않은 명부 하나를 갖고 있었어요. 맨 위에 적힌 이름이 바로 오늘의 주인공이었고, 그 아래에는 페데리코조차 알지 못하는 낯익은 이름들이 몇 개 더 적혀 있었죠.

blocks.cap: "위기 등급을 평가 중입니다…… 시뮬레이션 실패. 긴급 대응 시스템을 실행합니다." 즉위식 전날 밤, 정체불명의 경고가 교황청을 훑고 지나갔어요.

blocks.x: 취임식으로 향하던 길, 풍선을 몸에 묶고 날아보겠다며 나섰던 라테라노 시민 한 명이 그대로 하늘로 두둥실 떠올라 버려요. 지나가던 페데리코는 총 한 발로 풍선을 전부 터뜨려 그를 착지시키죠.
현장 상황에 따라 가장 합리적인 판단을 했을 뿐입니다.
고맙다는 인사 대신 '그 제복 어디서 맞췄냐'는 질문만 돌아오는 이 남자에게, 교황은 뜬금없는 한마디를 남겨요. 의식이 끝나면, 명부에 적힌 '친구들'을 만나러 가야겠다고.
2. 황야에 좌초된 낙원
페데리코의 팀은 60년 전 실종된 이동식 수도원 암브로시우스를 찾아 나서요. 함께한 건 수다스러운 동료 집행자 리켈레, 그리고 자원 입대한 산크타 기술자 세르필리아.

blocks.cap: 황야 한복판, 60년째 아무도 돌보지 않은 낙원이 모습을 드러내요.

blocks.x: 정문을 지키던 살카즈 청년 라이문트는 처음부터 노골적으로 적대적이에요. 마침내 마주한 주교 스테파노와 사냥꾼 제럴드는, 이 척박한 땅에서 산크타와 살카즈가 함께 어울려 산 지 10년이 넘었다고 말해요.
우리는 그저 갈 곳 없는 사람들을 받아들였을 뿐이야.
수도원에는 산크타 소녀 포르투나와 델피나, 정성껏 꽃을 가꾸는 정원사 클레망도 있었어요. 다들 저마다의 사정으로 이곳에 흘러들어와, 어느새 한 가족처럼 지내고 있었죠.
하지만 라테라노가 이 수도원을 데려가는 조건은 단 하나였어요. 낙원에는, 살카즈가 없어야 한다는 것.
3. 집행자의 심문, 그리고 어둠 속의 배고픔
페데리코는 사냥꾼치고 지나치게 정제된 제럴드의 몸놀림과, 등 뒤에 감춘 무기에서 위화감을 느껴요. 하지만 지금은 실종된 특사 르무엔과 오렌을 찾는 게 우선이죠.
한편 수도원 지하 어딘가에서는, 정체 모를 무언가가 어둠 속에서 낮게 신음하고 있어요. 그것을 '동포'라 부르며 찾아온 이베리아 선교사 아울루스는, 굶주림과 광석병 사이에서 무너져가는 살카즈들에게 낯선 위로를 건네고 있었죠.
역시, 아직도 내 말이 안 들리는 거야?
또 다른 특사 오렌은 애초에 종적을 감췄어요. 세르필리아는 그런 오렌과 몰래 내통하며, 페데리코 몰래 뭔가를 꾸미고 있었죠.
그리고 예배당 한구석에는, 실종됐던 특사 르무엔이 있었어요. 다리를 다쳐 휠체어에 의지한 채로, 스스로 이곳에 남기를 택한 채로.
4. 총 한 발, 그리고 검은 뿔
소꿉친구 델피나와 포르투나는 매일 티격태격하면서도 서로를 아꼈어요. 하지만 라테라노가 살카즈를 버리려 한다는 걸 안 뒤, 둘 사이엔 좁힐 수 없는 틈이 생기고 말아요.
포르투나는 언젠가 모두가 함께 라테라노로 갈 수 있으리라 믿고 싶었고, 델피나는 그 믿음이 순진한 배신이라 여겼죠. 다투던 델피나는 포르투나의 수호총을 빼앗으려 했고, 포르투나는 안 된다며 총을 붙잡았어요.

blocks.cap: 총성 한 발. 예배당 바닥에 떨어진 총, 그리고 이마에 돋아난 검은 뿔.

blocks.x: 나는 산크타로서 실격일까? 하지만 난 너나 라이문트가 더 걱정돼. 내겐 여기 이 집밖에 없고, 가족도 여기 사람들뿐이야.
방아쇠는 뜻하지 않게 당겨졌어요. 델피나는 그 자리에서 숨을 거뒀고, 포르투나는 더는 동족과 교감할 수 없는 존재, 타천사가 되었어요.
피나는, 피나는 나 때문에 죽었다고! 그게 사고든 아니든 뭐가 달라?!
5. 불타는 성당, 그리고 의심받는 아이
그날 밤, 성당에 원인 모를 불이 나요. 성상이 무너지고 꽃밭이 잿더미가 되는 동안, 사람들의 눈은 자연스럽게 살카즈 라이문트를 향해요. 마침 그 시각 근처에 있었다는 이유만으로.

blocks.cap: 활활 타오르는 예배당. 함께 살아온 세월도 불길 앞에선 힘을 쓰지 못해요.

blocks.x: 페데리코는 불이 붙는 속도가 이상하다며 인위적 방화를 의심하지만, 정작 범인의 흔적은 어디에도 없어요.
종족은 죄를 모면하는 증거가 될 수 없습니다. 성당을 파괴할 필요성이 있다면 저라도 주저하지 않겠죠.
뜻밖에도 이 한마디가 라이문트를 향한 의심을 걷어내요. 하지만 진짜 방화범은, 아무도 눈치채지 못한 곳에서 조용히 이들을 지켜보고 있었죠.
6. 이름을 감춘 사냥꾼, 그리고 다정한 괴물
페데리코는 결국 제럴드의 정체를 밝혀내요. 카즈델 내전의 악명 높은 탈영 용병, 호르스트 티핀달. 제럴드는 순순히 인정하지만 조금도 흔들리지 않아요.
10년이나 불린 이름인데 본명이 아니면 어떤가?
한편 실종된 살카즈 사냥꾼 하이먼의 행방도 드러나요. 지하에서 울부짖던 그 존재가 바로 하이먼이었죠. 그는 몰래 거둔 고아 남매 에스타라와 에렌델을 위해, 사람의 모습을 잃어가면서도 매일 밤 먹을 것을 나눠주고 있었어요.
괴물이 된 하이먼은 그날 밤도 어김없이 아이들 곁을 찾아요. 온기 없는 꼬리로 이불을 끌어다 덮어주며, 거의 다 잊어가는 목소리로도 두 아이의 이름만은 끝까지 놓지 않았죠.
에……렌…… 타라……
페데리코가 총구를 겨누자 제럴드가 그 앞을 막아서요.
하이먼의 뒤를 봐. 저 문 뒤에는 두 아이가 잠들어 있어. 아이들은 너를 믿고 있어.
7. 형제여, 나는 이제 못 도망가
제럴드에게는 오래전 헤어진 동료 에일린이 있었어요. 카즈델의 두 여왕을 등지고 떠나온 날부터, 둘은 살카즈도 평범한 삶을 살 수 있다고 믿으며 버텨왔죠.
만약 정말 무기를 내려놓고 시골 사냥꾼이 된다면, 네가 어떤 모습일지 상상해본 적은 있어?
에일린은 오래전 이 수도원에서 눈을 감았어요. 제럴드는 그날 이후로 종소리조차 두려워하면서도, 해마다 개화기가 되면 그녀를 만나러 갔죠.
라테라노 특수부대가 수도원을 포위했다는 소식이 전해져요. 제럴드는 오랜 벗 클레망에게 마지막 말을 남기고 비수를 들어요.
"내 목으로 다른 사람의 목숨을 바꿀 수 있다면, 그건 가치가 있는 일이야." 그는 라이문트와 포르투나, 그리고 남은 살카즈들을 부탁하며 스스로 목숨을 끊어요.

blocks.cap: 달빛 속에 홀로 선 클레망. 그의 손에는 비수와, 천에 감싸인 무언가가 들려 있었어요.

blocks.x: 이건…… '죄인' 호르스트 티핀달이 자진해서 바치는 머리입니다.
클레망은 제럴드의 마지막 부탁, 포르투나와 라이문트와 남은 살카즈들을 놓아달라는 청을 페데리코에게 전해요. 그리고 다음 날 아침, 아무 일도 없었다는 듯 다시 꽃을 찾아 나서죠.
8. 정원사가 감춘 것
주교 스테파노는 결국 마지막 결심을 해요. 심해 교회가 남긴 살점을 몰래 빵에 섞어, 마지막 조회에서 사람들 모두를 되돌릴 수 없는 존재로 만들어버리겠다는 결심을요. 하지만 그는 마지막 순간 손을 떼고, 그 빵을 지하 창고에 가둬버려요.
문제는 그 창고가 이미 텅 비어 있었다는 것. 누군가 먼저 손을 댄 거예요.
르무엔과 세르필리아는 뒤늦게 단서 하나를 찾아내요. 포르투나가 처음 예배당에 들어섰던 그날, 그 자리엔 분명 한 사람이 더 있었다는 걸요.

blocks.cap: 아르투리아가 웃으며 묻습니다. "너의 그 이성적이고 논리적인 판단이라면, 우선순위가 더 높은 일부터 처리할 줄 알았는데."

blocks.x: 페데리코가 찾아간 곳은 불탄 성당. 그곳엔 온화한 정원사 클레망이 있었어요.

blocks.cap: 성상 밑에서 홀로 살아남은 꽃 한 송이. 하지만 클레망이 찾던 건 이 꽃이 아니었어요.

blocks.x: 오랫동안 사람들의 다정함을 믿고 싶었던 클레망은, 라이문트가 의심받는 걸 보며 또 제럴드의 죽음을 지켜보며 결국 무너져버렸어요. 성당에 불을 지르고 성찬을 훔친 것도 전부 그였죠.
저는 단지 답을 얻고 싶을 뿐입니다. 이 세상에 진정으로 우리를 구원해 줄 존재가 있는지 말이에요.
클레망은 페데리코 앞에서 그 빵을 삼켜요. 아무 일도 일어나지 않아요. 다만 예전에 입은 총상으로 인한 출혈이 서서히 그의 숨을 조여올 뿐.

blocks.cap: 무너진 성상 앞에 쓰러진 정원사. 신도, 낙원도, 결국 아무것도 존재하지 않는다 되뇌며 눈을 감아요.

blocks.x: 라테라노인이여…… 우리의 낙원은 거짓이고, 당신들의 신앙 또한 거짓입니다.
9. 총성 한 발로 멈춘 전쟁
그사이 만국 전달자 오렌은 라테라노 특수부대 50인을 동원해 수도원을 접수하려 해요. 살카즈를 완전히 뿌리 뽑겠다는 명분으로요.
오렌에게는 오렌 나름의 논리가 있었어요. 살카즈와 결탁했다는 소문이 퍼지면, 라테라노가 어렵게 쌓아온 각국과의 우호 관계가 통째로 흔들릴 거라는 논리요.
스테파노는 남은 사람들과 함께 마지막 성찬 의식을 치러요. 이번엔 정말로, 아무 문제 없는 빵과 포도주로.

blocks.cap: "지난 수천 일 동안, 우리는 함께 살아왔네." 늙은 주교의 목소리가 성당 안에 나지막이 퍼져요.

blocks.x: 밖에서는 르무엔이 오렌을 향해 총을 겨누고, 오렌도 물러서지 않아요. 두 사람의 대치가 폭발 직전까지 치닫던 그 순간, 하늘을 가르는 총성 한 발이 모두를 멈춰 세워요.

blocks.cap: 페데리코의 경고 사격. 그가 처음으로, 그 누구의 명령도 없이 스스로 판단해 움직여요.

blocks.x: 저는 당신들의 입장을 이해할 필요도 없고, 충돌을 중재할 필요는 더더욱 없습니다. 하지만 지금 이 수도원에는 질서가 필요합니다. 제가 그 질서를 유지하겠습니다.
봄이 오기 전에
라이문트는 제럴드의 비수를 이어받을 자격이 아직 없다며 사양해요. 대신 페데리코에게 잠시 맡겨두고, 언젠가 다시 찾으러 오겠다 약속하죠.
포르투나는 결국 라이문트를 따라 살카즈 무리와 함께 떠나기로 해요. 세르필리아는 그런 그녀를 눈감아 주며, 대신 앞으로 어디에 있든 계속 연락하라는 약속을 받아내요.
에스타라와 에렌델은 끝내 엄마를 찾지 못해요. 두 아이가 밤새 담요를 덮어준 '잠든 아저씨'가, 사실은 더 이상 사람의 모습이 아니게 된 엄마였다는 것도 모른 채로.
떠나기 전, 르무엔은 스테파노에게 낡은 수도사의 노트 한 권을 건네요. 오래전 이곳에 머물렀던 누군가의 고민이 빼곡히 적힌 기록이었죠.
스테파노는 주교 자리에서 물러나, 홀로 라테라노를 향해 걷기 시작해요. 잃어버린 신앙을 되찾을 수 있을지, 자신의 눈으로 직접 확인하겠다면서.
그 낙원에서 다시 만나도록 하세.
문 앞에서 그를 배웅하던 르무엔은 문득 예감해요. 어쩌면 이 구부정한 뒷모습을 보는 게, 마지막일지도 모른다고.
페데리코는 교황에게 임무를 보고하며, 태어나 처음으로 스스로에게 질문을 던져요. 실수한 곳이 없는데, 대체 왜 최상의 결말에 이르지 못했는지.
우리의 법은 모든 상황에 적용될 수 있습니까? 그게 아니라면, 저는 앞으로 무엇에 근거해 행동해야 합니까?
낙원이 정말 하나뿐이라면, 그 밖으로 밀려난 이들은 대체 어디로 가야 하는가. 페데리코는 아직 그 답을 찾지 못했어요. 다만 이제는, 적어도 그 질문을 품을 줄은 알게 되었죠.

act25side.tagline: 군부가 슈퍼 무기로 착각한 라인 랩의 비밀 병기는, 사실 하늘을 뚫고 별로 향하는 로켓이었어요. 부모의 꿈을 물려받은 한 천재가 자기 목숨을 태워, 모두를 대신해 우주를 바라보는 '눈'이 되기로 한 이야기.

chars.name: 크리스틴
chars.desc: 라인 랩 총괄. 하늘에 도전했다 목숨을 잃은 발명가 부부, 라이트 부부의 딸.

chars.name: 사리아
chars.desc: 라인 랩 전 방위과 주임. 크리스틴의 오랜 친구이자, 이성과 질서를 지키는 와이번.

chars.name: 뮤엘시스
chars.desc: 라인 랩 생태과 주임. 물을 다루는, 얼마 남지 않은 엘프 중 한 명.

chars.name: 사일런스
chars.desc: 라인 랩의 연구원이자 의사. 진지하고 성실한 과학자. · 오퍼레이터 사일런스 더 패러디그매틱

chars.name: 오올헤약
chars.desc: 메이랜더 재단의 특수요원. 고대 쿠쿨칸의 혈통과 힘을 좇는 리베리.

chars.name: 이프리트
chars.desc: 로도스 아일랜드의 사브라 소녀. 사일런스와 사리아를 가족처럼 따른다.

chars.name: 로즈몬티스
chars.desc: 로도스 아일랜드의 필라인 정예 오퍼레이터. 잊고 싶은 것은 잊고, 남길 것은 기록한다.

chars.name: 퍼디낸드
chars.desc: 라인 랩 전 에너지과 주임. 크리스틴을 질투하는, 자칭 최고의 고에너지 물리학자.

chars.name: 야라
chars.desc: 라인 랩 인적자원조사과 주임. 한때 이름을 날린 은막의 배우였다.

chars.name: 내스티
chars.desc: 라인 랩 엔지니어링과 주임. 골필을 다루는 살카즈 밴시.

chars.name: 저스틴
chars.desc: 라인 랩 비즈니스과 주임. 돈과 인맥으로 '미치광이들의 성공'을 돕는 남자.

chars.name: 로켄
chars.desc: 특별구 이동감옥의 30번 죄수. 학계에서 매장된 몰락한 과학자.

chars.name: 블레이크
chars.desc: 컬럼비아 국방부 대령. 볼리바르에서의 실패를 '아크 1호'로 만회하려 한다.

terms.name: 별깍지
terms.desc: 테라의 하늘 약 6,000미터 높이를 막아선 보이지 않는 차단층. 인류는 그 너머로 단 한 번도 가본 적이 없다.

terms.name: 라인 랩
terms.desc: 과학 도시 트리마운츠에 자리한 컬럼비아 최고의 과학 기술 회사. 크리스틴과 사리아가 세웠다.

terms.name: 아크 1호
terms.desc: 군부가 대륙의 판도를 바꿀 슈퍼 무기라 믿는 크리스틴의 프로젝트. 정식 명칭은 '프로젝트 호라이즌 아크'.

terms.name: 트리마운츠
terms.desc: 먹구름이 늘 드리운 컬럼비아의 과학 도시. 라인 랩 본부가 있다.

blocks.x: 테라의 하늘에는 보이지 않는 천장이 있어요. 약 6,000미터 상공, **별깍지**라 불리는 차단층이 사람들을 이 좁은 땅에 가둬 두죠.
그 너머로 가려던 사람은 죄다 실패했어요. 비행 실험 도중 목숨을 잃은 라이트 부부처럼요. 세상은 그들을 세금이나 축낸 미치광이라 비웃었죠.
그런데 어느 날 밤, 트리마운츠의 하늘로 거대한 고리 하나가 떠올라요. 군부는 그걸 슈퍼 무기라 불렀고, 어떤 이는 재앙이라 불렀어요.
하지만 그건, 부모의 꿈을 물려받은 딸이 20년을 준비한 답이었어요.

blocks.cap: "천년만년이 흐른 뒤, 인간이 뭇별의 곁을 거닐게 되는 때가 온다면, 사람들은 필시 그녀의 이름을 칭송하리라."

blocks.x: 1. 하늘에서 별이 떨어졌다
이야기는 트리마운츠 13구역, 어느 화학 공장이 통째로 폭발하며 시작돼요.
원인은 사고가 아니라 하늘에서 떨어진 물체였어요. 거대한 고리 모양의 기계, '**아크 1호**'의 시험 비행체였죠.
라인 랩 총괄 **크리스틴**은 이 기계를 통째로 들고 2주 전 증발한 상태였어요. **블레이크** 대령의 군부는 이걸 대륙을 뒤흔들 슈퍼 무기라 믿고, 부통령 암살까지 무릅쓰며 크리스틴을 쫓았죠.
그 소동 한복판으로, 로도스 아일랜드의 박사와 켈시, 이프리트와 로즈몬티스가 들어와요. 크리스틴의 오랜 친구 **사리아**도, 라인 랩 연구원 **사일런스**도 각자의 이유로 움직이기 시작하죠.
부통령을 노린 군부의 암살은 이프리트가 몸으로 막아내요. 하늘에서 별이 떨어지면 모두의 소원이 이루어진다던 옛이야기를 떠올리면서요. 그 별이, 정말로 떨어지려 하고 있었어요.
2. 미치광이들의 회사
**라인 랩**은 겉보기엔 컬럼비아 최고의 과학 기술 회사예요. 하지만 안을 들여다보면, 저마다의 야망으로 곪은 곳이죠.
질투에 사무친 전 에너지과 주임 **퍼디낸드**는 크리스틴을 끌어내리려 군부의 개가 되고, 구조과 주임 파르비스는 늙어 스러지는 자신을 넘어서려 금단의 실험에 매달려요.
밴시 **내스티**는 살카즈에게 '허락된 땅'을 하늘에서 찾고, 비즈니스과 주임 **저스틴**은 돈과 인맥으로 남의 운명을 손에 쥐려 하죠. 다들 자기가 보는 방향이 곧 '발전'이라 믿어요.
그리고 인적자원조사과 주임 **야라**. 한때 은막을 수놓은 배우였던 그녀만은, 크리스틴을 총괄이 아니라 고집스러운 아이로 바라봐요. 20년 전 그 사고의 죄책감 때문에요.
난 한 번도 엄마가 된 적이 없어. 하지만 나에게는 아이가 한 명 있단다.
더 무서운 건 국가예요. 부통령 잭슨도, 그를 지켜보는 대통령도, 이미 모든 걸 알면서 시간을 끌어요. **블레이크**의 군부가 크리스틴 하나 잡겠다고 발버둥 칠 때, 정치가들에게 라인 랩의 몰락 따위는 허용 범위 안의 손실일 뿐이거든요.
3. 진지한 과학자 사일런스
이 광기의 회사에서, 가장 평범한 연구원 **사일런스**가 조용히 흔들려요.
그녀는 사고 현장을 쫓다 스승 파르비스와 마주쳐요. 파르비스는 '전달물질'로 인공 의식을 만들어, 평범한 자신을 넘어선 '초인'을 빚으려 하고 있었죠.
사일런스는 스승의 야망을 정면으로 막아서요. 발전이라는 이름으로 얼마나 많은 무고한 사람이 소모품이 되어 왔는지, 그녀는 알거든요.
과학은 신이 아니에요. 과학은 사람들이 통행하는 길일 뿐이에요. …… 과학은 모두를 진지하게 바라보고 있어요.
하지만 파르비스는 자신의 의식을 전달물질에 녹여, 스스로 실험의 재료가 되어버려요. 막지 못한 사일런스는 무너지죠.

blocks.cap: "넌 내가 이 땅에서 본 가장 강한 사람이야!" 이프리트가 무너진 사일런스의 멱살을 잡는다.

blocks.x: 그때 이프리트가 그녀의 멱살을 잡아요. 왜 혼자 다 짊어지려 하냐고, 우리가 곁에 있다고. 그 한마디에 사일런스는 다시 일어서요.
4. 나르시사의 심판
한편 **로즈몬티스**에게는 편지 한 통이 도착해요. 감옥에 있어야 할 30번 죄수, **로켄**이 그녀를 '집'으로 부른 거예요.
로켄은 로즈몬티스의 잃어버린 과거였어요. 그녀를 '나르시사'라 부르며 실험대에 올렸던 장본인이자, 매주 꽃을 들고 찾아왔던 다정한 할아버지.

blocks.cap: 죽어가는 로켄은 자신을 위한 '법정'을 열고, 나르시사에게 심판관이 되어 달라 청한다.

blocks.x: 로켄은 죽음을 앞두고 자신을 위한 법정을 열어요. 나르시사가 자신을 원망하고, 그 분노로 자신을 죽여 주길 바라면서요. 외롭게 죽고 싶지 않았던 거죠.
폭주할 뻔한 로즈몬티스를, 이프리트가 붙잡아요. 우리를 조종할 수 있는 건 우리 자신뿐이라고. 로즈몬티스는 로켄을 용서하지도, 그의 무기가 되지도 않아요.
나는 그저 당신이 죽기를 기다리고 있었을 뿐이야. …… 이제, 당신을 잊었어.
5. 물방울의 꿈
박사를 돕던 생태과 주임 **뮤엘시스**에게는, 그녀만의 슬픔이 있어요.
그녀는 오리지늄에 유독 취약한 엘프예요. 광석병 한 번이면 한 달을 못 넘기는 종족이죠. 그녀의 부모도, 마을도 이미 무덤이 되어 있었어요.
그래서 뮤엘시스는 라인 랩의 생태원에 자기만의 정원을 길렀어요. 그리고 크리스틴은 그녀에게 약속했죠. 이 땅이 네 자리가 아니라면, 별 너머에 네 종족이 살 곳이 있을지도 모른다고. 그럼 함께 떠나자고.
박사와 춤을 추며 지난날을 털어놓던 뮤엘시스는, 결국 크리스틴의 편에 서요. 자신의 꿈을 위해, 박사를 문밖으로 밀어내면서요.
그렇게 먼 길을 걸어 마침내 작은 숲을 찾았지만…… 난 나뭇잎이 아니라, 그냥 물방울일 뿐이야.
6. 어제에서 온 여자
메이랜더 재단을 배신하고 크리스틴을 도운 **오올헤약**은, 크리스틴의 정반대 거울이에요.
크리스틴이 미래를 보려 목숨을 걸었다면, 오올헤약은 수백 년의 기억을 물려받으며 오직 과거—고대 쿠쿨칸 혈통의 부활만을 좇았죠. 짧은 수명 안에 옛 영광에 닿는다면, 목숨 따위는 아깝지 않다면서요.
메이랜더의 수장 틴맨이 그녀 앞을 막아서요. 300년, 500년 기억을 이어받았다는 오올헤약에게, 이 레버넌트는 만 년의 무게가 무엇인지 보여주죠.

blocks.cap: 오리지늄 아츠 하나 없는 맨주먹으로, 사리아는 뒤틀린 공기를 뚫고 오올헤약을 쓰러뜨린다.

blocks.x: 결국 오올헤약이 마주한 건 크리스틴의 '신'이 남긴 마지막 계시—거대한 달과, 우주에 떠 있는 작은 테라였어요. 자신의 혈통도, 집념도 그 앞에선 티끌이었죠.
모든 걸 부정당한 그녀는, 처음으로 혈통이 아니라 '자신'을 위해 싸우고 싶다고 깨달아요. 그 편집증적인 미래에의 갈망을 품은 채, 그녀는 로도스 아일랜드에 발을 들이죠.
7. 하늘 밑에 잠든 것
박사와 켈시는 크리스틴의 에너지원을 쫓아, 황무지 지하 깊은 곳으로 내려가요. 그리고 거기서 '보존자'와 마주하죠.
그것은 수만 년 전, 어떤 파멸을 피해 수십만 동포를 얼려 재운 채 홀로 깨어 있던 의식이었어요. 박사의 잃어버린 동포들이 그 관 속에 잠들어 있었죠.
보존자는 박사에게 '변론'을 걸어와요. 사고를 뒤섞는 그 기술 속에서, 박사는 자기 자신과 마주하죠. 기억을 잃기 전의 너는 누구였나. 진실을 되찾을 각오는 있나. 아미야와 테레시아의 목소리가 그를 흔들어요.
크리스틴이 '신'이라 부른 존재가 바로 이 보존자였어요. 그것은 자기 문명의 마지막 피를 크리스틴에게 내주기로 해요. 꺼져가는 등불을 지키느니, 새로운 심장에 바치는 게 낫다면서요.
보존자는 박사에게 오리지늄과 그의 과거에 얽힌 실마리도 흘려요. 박사의 기억 상실이 결코 우연이 아니라고. 다만 그 답은 스스로 마주해야 한다고요. 그리고 죽어가는 순간, 자신을 '트레버 프리스턴'이라는 한 인간으로 지워 달라 부탁하죠.
켈시는 이 땅이 아직 별을 볼 준비가 되지 않았다고, 크리스틴의 불꽃이 다음 정복의 뿔피리가 될까 두렵다고 말해요. 그럼에도 그녀는, 자신은 이제 로도스 아일랜드의 일원이자 테라의 사람이라고 답하죠.
8. 스텔라리아
'아크 1호'는 애초에 존재하지 않았어요. 군부를 속이기 위한 폐지 뭉치였죠. 이 비행체의 진짜 이름은 **스텔라리아**. 별을 향한 방주였어요.

blocks.cap: 쓰레기장에 숨겨졌던 초점 발생기가, 트리마운츠의 하늘을 가르며 별을 향해 떠오른다.

blocks.x: 퍼디낸드는 오직 이 순간의 진리를 기록하려, 군부의 대령마저 비웃으며 계기판에 매달려요. 저마다 미쳐 있는 사람들 사이로, 스텔라리아는 계속 상승하죠.

blocks.cap: 권력에 매달린 블레이크를 향해, 퍼디낸드는 오직 과학만을 바라보며 코웃음 친다.

blocks.x: 그 중심에서 **사리아**가 크리스틴을 따라잡아요. 인공 별이 은하처럼 쏟아지는 플라네타륨 한가운데서, 두 친구가 마주 서죠.

blocks.cap: 인공 별들이 도는 스텔라리아의 심장부. 20년 만에 다시 마주 선 두 친구.

blocks.x: 9. 홀로 가는 별
먼저 뮤엘시스가 크리스틴을 지키려 사리아를 막아서지만, 크리스틴은 뮤엘시스마저 자기 물과 함께 밖으로 밀어내요. 나약한 본체를 아끼는, 크리스틴다운 다정함이었죠.
사리아는 크리스틴을 막으러 온 게 아니었어요. 데리고 내려가려 온 거였죠. 힘으로 맞서는 대신, 그녀는 폭풍을 뚫고 한 걸음씩 걸어가 크리스틴의 손을 잡아요.
진정한 선구자라면 모든 인류를 위해 길을 열어야지, 돌아오지 못할 심연에 먼저 뛰어들어서는 안 돼.
하지만 크리스틴은 마지막 순간, 사리아만을 위해 준비해 둔 탈출구로 그녀를 떨어뜨려요. 스텔라리아에 복귀 프로그램 따위는 없었어요. 처음부터 돌아올 생각이 없었던 거예요.
크리스틴은 자신이 사람들을 이끄는 선구자가 아니라고 했어요. 자신은 그저 눈이라고. 이 땅의 모두를 대신해, 아무도 본 적 없는 하늘을 바라보는 한 쌍의 눈이라고요.
과학자는 꿈이 거짓이라는 걸 믿지 않아. 전 인류에게, 모든 꿈은 이뤄질 수 있다는 걸 증명하는 사람이니까.
마침내 거짓된 하늘이 갈기갈기 찢어지고, 두 개의 오래된 달이 진짜 모습을 드러내요. 그날 밤, 인류는 처음으로 진실을 엿봐요.
에필로그 — 남은 사람들
수천 미터 상공에서 추락한 사리아는, 뮤엘시스가 온몸의 물을 쏟아 만든 물 장벽과 사일런스의 손에 겨우 목숨을 건져요.

blocks.cap: 먹구름이 걷힌 하늘 아래, 사일런스가 만신창이가 된 사리아를 부축한다. "하늘이 정말 예쁘군."

blocks.x: 사일런스는 '트리마운츠 과학 윤리 공동 선언'의 발의자가 되기로 해요. 크리스틴 같은 천재가 아니라, 과학이 폭주하지 않게 지키는 완충대이자 분기기가 되겠다고요. 꿈과 현실을 잇는 다리가 되겠다고.
뮤엘시스는 내스티와 손잡고 하늘을 나는 도시를 꿈꾸고, 야라는 정년을 맞아 와인을 찾아 긴 여행을 떠나요. 로즈몬티스는 로켄을 심판하고, 노트에 한 줄을 남긴 뒤 그를 잊죠.
그림자도 짙게 남아요. 오올헤약은 보존자의 마지막 의식을 지옥에서 건져 달아나고, 그림자 뒤에 있던 컬럼비아 대통령 마크 맥스는 마침내 정체를 드러내죠. 모든 의지를 하나로 통합하고 모든 진리를 접수해, 컬럼비아를 테라의 검과 방패로 만들겠다는 폭군의 야망을요.
그가 노리는 건 박사 일행이 안고 나온 '검은 왕관'이에요. 켈시는 그를 향해 못 박듯 답하죠. 프리스턴은 단 한 번도 폭군을 기대한 적이 없다고. 한편 퍼디낸드는 감옥에서, 죽는 날까지 자기 이론을 적어 내려가요. 100년 뒤의 학자들이 자기 이름을 기릴 거라 믿으면서요.
차단층 너머, 생명유지 캡슐에 홀로 든 크리스틴은 마지막 기록을 남겨요. 우리가 우주의 어디쯤 있는지 깨달아야만, 어디로 갈지 고민할 자격이 생긴다고.

blocks.cap: 별 너머, 살아남은 화초들에 둘러싸여. 크리스틴은 모두를 대신해 우주를 바라보는 한 쌍의 눈이 되었다.

blocks.x: 잘 자, 테라의 여러분. 잘 자, 우주.
옛이야기에선, 하늘에서 별이 떨어지면 사람들의 갈등이 사라지고 모두의 소원이 이루어진다고 했어요. 하지만 크리스틴은 떨어지는 별이 아니라, 스스로 하늘로 올라간 별이 됐죠.
날씨 맑은 밤, 시야가 탁 트인 곳에 망원경을 세워 보세요. 별 하나가 당신을 위해 반짝일 거예요. 그건 소원을 들어주는 별이 아니라, 이 땅의 모두를 대신해 홀로 하늘을 바라보는 한 쌍의 눈이니까요.
천년만년이 흐른 뒤, 인간이 뭇별의 곁을 거닐게 되는 때가 온다면 — 사람들은 필시, 그녀의 이름을 칭송할 거예요.

act24side.tagline: Two Operators chase a fire-breathing 'King of the Skies' into the mountain villages of Higashi, and end up tangled with an old man who's spent seven years hunting nothing but his dead son. A story about burning away a past piled up like fallen leaves, and learning how to survive.

chars.name: Yato
chars.desc: Squad leader of Rhodes Island's A4 Action Team. A former assassin — cold, and always puts the mission first · this event's 6★ Operator Kirin R Yato

chars.name: Noir Corne
chars.desc: Yato's longtime partner and shield-bearer. Warm-hearted and looks out for everyone · this event's 5★ Operator Rathalos S Noir Corne

chars.name: Terra Research Commission
chars.desc: Bipedal cats called Palicoes who crossed over from the Monster Hunter world. This event's reward Operator, made up of the Scholar, Artisan, and Veteran Palicoes

chars.name: Yoshitaka Kashiwau
chars.desc: An old hunter of Roka Village. The only one in the village who still climbs the mountain

chars.name: Akira Kashiwau
chars.desc: Yoshitaka's son, and a young hunter of Roka Village

chars.name: Ataru Takii
chars.desc: The head of Roka Village. The man who lifted the village out of poverty

chars.name: Mirai Takii
chars.desc: The village head's niece. An ecologist who's returned from the city

chars.name: Kazuya
chars.desc: A village boy who idolizes hunters. He calls Yoshitaka 'Grandpa' and follows him everywhere

terms.name: Roka Village
terms.desc: A village at the foot of the mountains in Higashi. Once a hunting community, it's grown prosperous through its specialty tea, ironwork, and woodcraft

terms.name: Rathalos
terms.desc: The 'King of the Skies' from the Monster Hunter world — a massive fire-breathing wyvern the villagers simply call 'the Fire Dragon'

terms.name: Higashi
terms.desc: Terra's Far East. A region resembling Japan in our world, and the homeland of both Yato and Noir Corne

blocks.x: Terra's Far East, Higashi — a mountain hunting village somewhere, in the season of falling leaves. What happens here is **Arknights' first crossover with Monster Hunter**.
**Yato** and **Noir Corne** of Rhodes Island's A4 Action Team came up this mountain for an Oripathy survey. Twenty-three samples in, and nothing had turned up — just an ordinary job.
Then, beneath a pile of fallen leaves, they find a claw mark the size of a house. And from the sky, a shadow descends like a fireball scorching the earth.
1. The King of the Skies, and the Dancing Cats
The first thing to leap out beside the claw mark, unexpectedly, was a pair of talking cats. "The Fire Dragon's coming, nya!" they wave and dance. These creatures are **Palicoes**.

A bipedal cat race that fell into this world alongside the Fire Dragon, straight out of the Monster Hunter world. A self-proclaimed scholar of the Original Royal Paleontology Society, and a quiet, taciturn artisan.

Before the warning even finishes, the King of the Skies swoops down. Yato's blade bounces off its scales, and Noir Corne's shield gets scorched by the flames. Even accounting for how many times these two have fought back to back, this opponent was bad news.

blocks.cap: A forest of drifting embers, two backs pressed together against a threat from the sky. And the mission had only just begun.

blocks.x: Worse still, the Fire Dragon's claws carried poison. Yato collapses, and the Palicoes scramble to give first aid.
The two cats dash off toward the village for an antidote. "You dumb, horned hunter! Sit tight and wait for us, nya!"
2. The Hunting Village With No Hunters

The two who came down to the village were greeted by a boy named **Kazuya**. Seeing the injured Yato, he leads the way, saying, "Let's go to Grandpa's house" — to the hunter grandfather who lives at the village's south end.

That's how they meet the old hunter, **Yoshitaka Kashiwau** — a gruff old man who silently treats the wound of a total stranger. But the moment he hears the two are chasing the Fire Dragon, his expression changes completely.
"That's my quarry."
The old man throws the two out, warning them not to touch his prey. Why is he so furious? Noir Corne can't shake the feeling something's wrong, but Yato brushes it off as none of their concern.

blocks.cap: A village where red maple leaves fall. In the middle of the square, a hunter's statue stands alone, gazing off into the distance.

blocks.x: What's strange is that a hunting village has no hunters left. The hunter's statue in the square sits weathered and neglected, and every villager treats these strangers with wary eyes.
3. The Butterfly You Saw in Autumn Was Only a Fallen Leaf
Village head **Ataru Takii** treats the pair lavishly, yet flatly refuses their advice to evacuate — all while going on about the specialty chilled-grass tea, and about Originium.

Just a few years back, they say, Roka Village barely scraped by on hunting alone. Now, roofs have been replaced with proper wood paneling, streetlamps line the roads, and children can bathe in warm water every day. No one, he says, needs to go into the mountains anymore.

"If you saw a butterfly flying in autumn, it was really just a fallen leaf." The village head gently brushes off Yato's sighting as nothing but that kind of illusion — as if to say what he's been chasing might just be a leaf too.

Meanwhile, a child being treated is rushed in coughing blood. Ever since his father went into 'the cave,' stones had begun growing all over his body — and that father was hauled off by the village head. It's clear the village is desperately hiding something.

Digging for that something, the two are lured into a cave in the back mountain — a trap using a hunk of meat as bait, meant to draw in and finish off the Fire Dragon. They barely escape with explosives, but the cave collapses and seals shut behind them.
4. The Forest Itself Is a Monster

Back into the forest, chasing the Fire Dragon's trail. But this forest isn't normal. Herds of horned beasts rampage for no reason (someone had scattered luring powder), and a strange force that steals your sense of direction traps them wandering the same spot over and over.

A stinky little incident along the way gives the heavy mood some breathing room. The artisan-nya pokes at a rain-bird's eggs, and Noir Corne ends up buried under a bombardment of bird droppings. After that, no one wants to go near him.

blocks.cap: The carpet bombing of the rain-bird flock. From that day on, 'never go near a rain-bird nest marker in the forest' becomes Noir Corne's personal motto.

blocks.x: At an abandoned campsite, the pair find an old group photo of hunters, and a broken hunting spear. The name carved into the spear is **Akira Kashiwau** — a young hunter from the nine-year-old photo, sharing Yoshitaka's family name.

Noir Corne can't stop worrying about the old man. Someone shouldering everything alone, stubbornly running from something — it reminds him of someone. "Don't waste your energy on nonsense," Yato snaps at his partner, but there's something guilty in that scolding too.
5. Is This Outfit Really Armor?
The artisan-nya whips up new gear from Fire Dragon materials — the iconic **Kirin Armor** of Monster Hunter fame. When Yato changes into it, Noir Corne is left utterly speechless.

blocks.cap: "Light, agile, and powerful." Recalling this gear afterward, Yato would always marvel at it this way. "That's not what we were amazed by!" his comrades would always answer.

blocks.x: Right on cue, a seasoned **Veteran Palico** joins them. An old warhorse of a cat with polished manners, except for a 'nya' habit he just can't shake. This old cat teaches Yato how to wield dual blades and the great sword, and the hunt slowly starts to take shape.
6. That's My Quarry

Night at the abandoned lakeside camp. Yoshitaka is talking with the vision of his dead son, **Akira**. Seven years ago, his son left only the words "I'm this village's hunter" before charging into a herd of beasts — and when he came back, he was unrecognizable if not for his clothes and gear.

blocks.cap: By a dying campfire, the old man is still trapped in that night, listening to his dead son's voice.

blocks.x: To Yoshitaka, the Fire Dragon is the chief of every beast that took his son. It's an obsession — to hunt it down alone, to the death, out of revenge.
Yato walks step by step toward the old man aiming his spear, and says: "You can't kill me. You don't have the nerve."

And then Yato brings up his own story. Before coming to Rhodes, he was an assassin who killed his own kind on command. One day, he let a mother and daughter go — but simply not killing them wasn't enough to protect anyone, and another assassin who followed after him killed them instead.
The one who pulled him back up then was the comrade at his side, Noir Corne.
The past is the past. Every regret you have, every sin — pay it all back with whatever future you have left.

What Yato is trying to tell Yoshitaka comes down to the same thing. An obsession trapped in the past can't undo anything — it can only keep you frozen where you stand. The man he's talking to now isn't a hunter. Not anymore.
7. The Mine, and the Massacre Seven Years Ago
Meanwhile, Noir Corne and the scholar-nya, chasing the Fire Dragon deep into the mountain, uncover the truth in the depths of a cave. Beneath it all lay a massive **Originium mine**.

Here, the scholar-nya and Noir Corne share a conversation: Originium is dangerous, yet it's the very power holding up this world's civilization. Just like the monsters of the Monster Hunter world, Originium is both a threat to survival and an opportunity. If it's the only branch drifting your way before a Catastrophe, you have to grab it, thorns and all.

They also meet the village head's niece, **Mirai Takii**, who'd infiltrated the mine. An ecologist returned from the city, she knew excessive mining would invite disaster and had come to blow the mine up. She's also the one who tipped the two off, drawing them to this village in the first place.

And the truth of seven years ago comes to light. This vein was originally home to a burrowing beast called the **Melynx** — Village Head Ataru wiped out their nest to mine Originium, and the beasts, driven mad after losing their home, caused that year's stampede — the very incident that took Akira's life.
But then an accident sets off the Fire Dragon, and every last bit of Originium in the mine activates at once. What that means, the sky answers first.

blocks.cap: A blast of fire swallows the sky. Vegetation turns to ash, the mountain crumbles — this land's punishment, a Catastrophe, has arrived.

blocks.x: 8. We're Hunters Too

The Catastrophe and the beast stampede hit the village at the same time. **Hiroshi Rito**, who'd been embezzling from the village treasury, breaks the defense net and unleashes the beasts to sow chaos — planning to pin all the blame on the village head in the confusion.

Before the villagers, sunk in despair, Mirai steps forward. Holding up a single sprig of chilled grass, she says there's hope for tomorrow even without Originium, even without relying on hunting. First, let's just survive.

As it turns out, Village Head Ataru wasn't entirely a bad man either. Knowing the mine would eventually bring disaster, he'd secretly prepared a shelter, and even now his first concern is the villagers' lives. He reveals that he was once the leader of the old hunters, picks up a hunting spear, and stands before the beast horde.
"Weren't the hunters supposed to be long gone?" a frightened child asks. An adult answers: "No. The hunters were always here. When danger comes, they're the ones who step forward."
One by one, the village men take up spears and fall in behind the village head.
9. Give the Past Back to the Past

Around that time, Yoshitaka is alone, setting his own house on fire. Let the dead stay dead, he thinks — let old regrets burn clean down to a handful of ash. The past, piled up like fallen leaves, burned away by fire.

blocks.cap: With his burning house at his back, the old man grips his son's spear once more, resolved to give the past back to the past.

blocks.x: Then he lures the Fire Dragon out alone to face it. He wounds its head with his son's spear, but his aging body soon gives out.
Young Kazuya steps in front of him, bow drawn. "I'm this village's hunter too! Stay away from Grandpa!"
After saving the boy, it's Yato who takes up the fallen old man's spear — planting it in the ground, standing tall, back straight.

blocks.cap: "Thank you for the time you bought us. Now — leave the rest to us, and rest easy."

blocks.x: Only then does Yoshitaka fully understand his son Akira's final moments, trapped by that beast stampede.

And Village Head Ataru confesses the truth of that day: Akira could have run, but instead smeared himself with luring powder on purpose, drawing the beasts to himself to protect the village and the mine — along with one last message meant for his father.
From now on — lead everyone, and survive.
10. Showdown! The Burning Hunter's Spirit!

Now for the final hunt. Noir Corne puts everything into one blow with his great sword, severing the Fire Dragon's tail and bringing it down, while a flash bomb the artisan-nya whipped up on the spot (made, apparently, from Terran Originium bugs) drags the King down to the ground.

blocks.cap: Demon Mode. The dual-blade secret art of abandoning defense entirely and closing in like a beast — Yato dances through the air, driving the Fire Dragon back.

blocks.x: Yato's dual blades, in Demon Mode, dance through the air for the finishing blow, and the Veteran Palico's rope binds the King fast. The moment they finally subdue the King of the Skies —
It turns out the Fire Dragon was never infected with Oripathy. It was just an old, starving creature, covered head to toe in Originium dust.
The Originium crystals grown on its body were, if anything, traces of its resistance against Originium — and it's revealed to no longer be any kind of threat.

Yato lets the King go. Before a Catastrophe, he says, both people and beasts are equally small — this sky was never ours alone to begin with. Every life on this land is only ever struggling to survive its one thread of life, sometimes gloriously, sometimes not.
Epilogue — Morning Light
Half a month later. The village was rising again, past the Catastrophe.

The 'chilled grass that grows anywhere,' which Mirai had used to sway the villagers, turns out to have been a lie — but now she proposes a real tomorrow to the village head. Not going back to barbarism, turning away from Originium, but respecting it more, handling it properly, and living safely alongside it.
The Palicoes stay behind at Rhodes under the official designation 'Terra Research Commission.' Their captain is the Veteran Palico, though the scholar-nya still insists that he's the one in charge.
And once the mission is done, Yato and Noir Corne secretly return to those ruins on their way back. Yato had come here for one reason alone — to watch the Fire Dragon's fall.

blocks.cap: Ruins at sunset, the Fire Dragon rising into the sky. A flight hanging by a single thread, destined one day to snap and fall to the earth.

blocks.x: The Fire Dragon grows distant against a sky full of drifting ash. Noir Corne quietly reaches out a hand, using the ash on his shoulder as an excuse, and Yato smiles faintly at the sight. The two leave the place without a word.
By the end of the story, the hunter never truly beat the monster. But every villager walked away alive, and the wounded monster, having lost its purpose, returned alone to the forest.

Before it left, the monster seemed to leave behind these words: "I'll come back again, someday." Long years from now, where the old hunter once stood, a young hunter will stand, and a newborn monster will come face it, eye to eye.
Because fallen leaves scatter, and grow anew. **Survive.** From beginning to end, that was the one thing this land had been repeating, all along.

act23side.tagline: The legendary 'Grandmaster' who guarded Yumen for a hundred years turns out to be a fragment of a Catastrophe-class beast. A disciple seeking revenge, a father who abandoned his daughter, and an old man who once threw the city to a Catastrophe out of an old grudge — around a single sword, they all learn to let their grudges go.

chars.name: Chongyue
chars.desc: The legendary martial arts grandmaster who has guarded Yumen for nearly a hundred years. He's about to retire and leave the city.

chars.name: Wei Yanwu
chars.desc: Governor of Lungmen and an old friend of the Grandmaster. He's come all the way to Yumen just to see his old friend off.

chars.name: Lin Yusha
chars.desc: An envoy dispatched from Lungmen, and the Rat King's daughter. She's secretly investigating the dangerous forces hiding in Yumen · Operator Lin

chars.name: Waai Fu
chars.desc: An employee of Lee's Detective Agency and a recent university graduate. She's skilled enough to hold her own on Yumen's training grounds.

chars.name: Jieyun
chars.desc: A girl of the nomadic Anasa tribe. At her late master's last request, she smuggled herself into Yumen carrying a single sword.

chars.name: Zuo Le
chars.desc: Son of Yumen's general and a young agent of the Sishi Bureau. He's tasked with tracking down the stolen sword.

chars.name: Qiubai
chars.desc: The Grandmaster's only disciple. She's stayed at his side learning the sword for five years.

chars.name: Lee
chars.desc: A private detective from Lungmen. He's come to Yumen searching for a sworn brother who's been out of contact for years.

chars.name: Du Yaoya
chars.desc: A rookie boss from Shangshu who's just opened a courier company, 'Xingyou Logistics,' in Yumen. Fearless and always on the move.

chars.name: Waai Tinfu
chars.desc: A hulking, mysterious man working out of a clinic in Yumen. He trusts his own fists more than any weapon.

terms.name: Yumen
terms.desc: A nomadic fortress-city of Yan that sails the desert, guarding the northern frontier. Defenses like the Screenwall hold off both Catastrophes and outside enemies.

terms.name: the Screenwall
terms.desc: Four mobile outer ramparts that encircle Yumen. They stay folded away in normal times, rising only when a Catastrophe-class sandstorm bears down.

terms.name: Sanhaizhong
terms.desc: A fanatical cult that worships the 'Behemoth,' a Catastrophe-class beast that vanished a thousand years ago. It struck Yumen once before, twenty years ago, inflicting heavy damage.

terms.name: Anasa
terms.desc: A nomadic tribe with no fixed settlement, wandering the wasteland. Jieyun comes from this tribe.

terms.name: the Sishi Bureau
terms.desc: A special bureau the court established to handle Behemoth-related cases exclusively. Zuo Le is one of its agents.

terms.name: the Beacon-Watch Festival
terms.desc: A traditional three-day rite held every spring in Yumen. Drums are struck once for every disaster the city weathered that year, so none are forgotten.

terms.name: the Sand Mill
terms.desc: A massive Originium turbine mounted on Yumen's outer wall. It clears the sand from the city's path — and when it slows, there's just enough of a gap for someone to sneak in or out.

blocks.x: Yumen is a mobile fortress-city that sails across the desert. For nearly a hundred years, it's been guarded by a legendary martial arts grandmaster — everyone just calls him 'the Grandmaster.' As this story begins, he happens to be on the verge of retirement.

As luck would have it, right at this moment Yumen docks with its old sister city, Lungmen, and all sorts of people come pouring in. The Lungmen governor come to see off an old friend, a private detective searching for his missing sworn brother, and a desert girl clutching a single sword as she runs for her life.

Then that night, with a coincidence too perfect to be chance, three incidents erupt at once: an assassination attempt on the governor, the theft of the Grandmaster's beloved sword, and the massacre of a patrol outside the walls. With a Catastrophe bearing down, Yumen has no idea yet that all three are actually one and the same thread.
1. The First Night of Docking, Three Disasters at Once

On the inn's TV plays 《Storms Over Yumen》, a drama based on a real incident from fifty years ago — a tale of wandering swordsmen and soldiers joining forces to repel outside invaders. Except half the people in that story are sitting right here in this very inn, watching themselves on screen.

On the training grounds, the Filaine girl **Waai Fu** is sparring with a stranger from another people. Her opponent is **Jieyun**, who's just scaled Yumen's walls for the first time today — a girl of the nomadic Anasa tribe. Waai Fu wins in the end, but Jieyun only asks about a sword before disappearing.

Meanwhile, General Zuo's son **Zuo Le** has come home after a long absence, yet keeps putting off seeing his father. Up on the watchtower, the Grandmaster **Chongyue** is reminiscing with his little sister **Ling**.

A young man dressed in Sargonian clothes, claiming to be sightseeing in Yumen, adds to the chaos. In halting Yan, he boasts, "That Grandmaster taught me martial arts back in Sargon!" — only to get scolded by the innkeeper for mixing up the words for 'inn' and 'tavern.'

blocks.cap: Yumen's night skyline, on the eve of deceleration. Above the siblings' old stories, three fuses are quietly burning down.

blocks.x: Night falls, and all three go off at once. An assassin trailing the scent of peach blossoms targets Lungmen governor **Wei Yanwu**; Jieyun steals Chongyue's sword and flees; and outside the walls, a Catastrophe-relay patrol is wiped out.

blocks.cap: "I said I'm going — who dares stop me?" In the moonlight, the assassin raises her blade.

blocks.x: 2. Zuo Le Gets Knocked Flat Before He Can Even Ask a Question

Having wounded the assassin badly, Zuo Le searches every clinic in Yumen for the culprit. At the last, run-down clinic he visits, a hulking man who won't even give his name turns him away at the door.

Zuo Le tries to force his way in with his martial arts, and gets knocked straight back out. The man, named **Waai Tinfu**, loudly declares he trusts nothing but his own fists — and Jieyun is already hiding inside the room.

But something about this man looks familiar. In fact, there's someone in this city who's been searching for him for over a decade. The story of a father who vanished, leaving his daughter behind, is something no one here knows yet.

It was this very giant of a man who'd secretly taken in and treated the wounded Jieyun. "Don't move. Just be still and let yourself heal." Gruff on the outside, he kept a straight face in front of Zuo Le right to the end.
3. The Detective and the Envoy, Chasing Different Shadows Through Yumen
The Lungmen private detective **Lee** has come to Yumen looking for a sworn brother from long ago. For days, all he's done is grab passing swordsmen and ask, "Have you seen a big, hulking man?"

At the same time, Lungmen envoy **Lin Yusha** is squeezing smugglers for information, chasing down the name 'Sanhaizhong.' It's supposedly a cult of bandits who worship the Behemoth — and they've targeted Yumen once before, twenty years ago.

In the marketplace, Zuo Le is ambushed by Sanhaizhong. Lee, passing by, steps in to help — and in the process, Zuo Le lets slip a crucial clue for the first time: "That big guy, he was a Filaine." Lee's expression goes still.

With that clue in hand, Lee finally tracks Waai Tinfu down, hiding in a clinic across from the inn. And by coincidence, an old friend also shows up there — **Yang Xian**, newly posted to Yumen as a magistrate.
It turns out Lee, Yang Xian, and Waai Tinfu swore brotherhood together over a decade ago. But their reunion over drinks is nothing but awkward.
"It's bad for my health." Waai Tinfu turns down even a drink, and Lee can only sigh. "Waai Tinfu, you idiot — same as any father, and yet somehow..."
4. The Rookie Courier Boss Picks Up an Armband

**Du Yaoya**, who came from Shangshu, has just opened 'Xingyou Logistics' in Yumen. Her very first job was escorting Catastrophe-relay couriers — and two of her own people were in that massacred patrol.
Du Yaoya and Waai Fu make the rounds of the victims' homes together and notice something strange: every single one looks untouched, as if nothing had happened.
Then an armband that Lin Yusha picked up from a blacksmith's storehouse points every suspicion in one direction — toward the elderly owner of the southside smithy, **Meng Zheyi**.
To Du Yaoya, Meng Zheyi is the only adult in Yumen she can truly rely on. But that night, as Du Yaoya and Zuo Le knock on the smithy's door, black-hooded figures burst out in front of them.

After the fighting, the person they find is Jieyun, wounded and hiding. The Grandmaster's disciple **Qiubai** arrives late and makes short work of the Sanhaizhong men — but Meng Zheyi, who should be at the center of all this, is nowhere to be found.
5. A Twenty-Year-Old Reckoning, Uncovered in the Storehouse

Lin Yusha comes face to face with Meng Zheyi in the storehouse. He hands over the real Catastrophe data without resistance and confesses everything: colluding with Sanhaizhong to fake the patrol's deaths, letting the Grandmaster's sword be stolen — it was all his own plan.

The reason goes back twenty years. After Sanhaizhong once infiltrated Yumen disguised as wandering swordsmen, General Zuo Xuanliao pushed every swordsman entirely out of Yumen's affairs. Meng Zheyi never forgot how those who'd bled for Yumen back then were cast aside.
"This whole affair started because of me, and me alone," Meng Zheyi says. But the one who comes for him isn't Sanhaizhong's boss — it's the true master who commands Sanhaizhong himself.
Xingyou Logistics was a fine idea... but even so, I couldn't just stand by and watch a fine young sprout get cut down right in front of me...

In front of Du Yaoya, who arrives too late, Meng Zheyi takes a blade meant for her back. Du Yaoya runs, clutching the data box to her chest, hoping that neither grudges nor apologies have come too late — not yet.
6. Father and Daughter, One Punch in the Middle of the Street

Sanhaizhong appears again, targeting Du Yaoya as she tries to protect the data. This time Waai Fu blocks the way — but her opponent's blade is strange, fast with no form at all, some bizarre kind of Originium Arts.
At the critical moment, a back like a wall steps in front of Waai Fu. It's her father, whom she hasn't seen in over a decade — **Waai Tinfu**.

blocks.cap: An autumn night...? In front of an opponent who commands unnatural weather, this reunion arrives without any warning at all.

blocks.x: Only after shielding his daughter does Waai Tinfu glance back. "Thanks for the save." "Nothing else to say?" "No need." After that short exchange, father and daughter trade a punch.

blocks.cap: A reunion over a decade in the making was sealed not with an embrace, but with a single punch.

blocks.x: "I lost," Waai Fu admits first. But both of them know that the kick she landed on her father's chin wasn't something her father ever taught her.
7. Forty Years of Martial Arts, Settled with a Single Punch

Meanwhile, the Grandmaster's disciple Qiubai keeps a promise she made to herself. As a child, she lost her father when her river village was raided — and she's stayed at the side of Chongyue, who led that band of raiders, learning the sword from him for five years.
On the day the Catastrophe arrives, Qiubai points her sword at Chongyue, knowing full well she can't win.

blocks.cap: Before the sword-aura even fades, she draws back her blade herself.

blocks.x: I struck at you, and this strike failed to kill you. That I couldn't kill you doesn't mean I've given up on revenge — it's only that 'grudge' is no longer the reason I draw my sword.

And then, another duel three years in the making. Waai Tinfu finally throws down his challenge to the Grandmaster, on the eve of the Catastrophe, under the pretext of a friendly bout to boost the soldiers' morale.

blocks.cap: Forty years of martial arts collide with a hundred years of swordsmanship. Victory and defeat are decided by a single exchange.

blocks.x: The Grandmaster wins. But Waai Tinfu, coughing up blood, still laughs. "Took me forty years just to beat one move of yours — give me three hundred sixty more, and I bet I'll win outright!" This man finds optimism in the strangest places.
8. The Catastrophe Descends, the Old Rat's Last Strength

The foretold Catastrophe finally bears down on Yumen. Instead of steering the city around it, General Zuo Xuanliao chooses to meet the Catastrophe head-on, right now, instead of waiting the usual two months.

blocks.cap: After the seventeenth drumbeat, the sky splits open.

blocks.x: One side of the gate is held single-handedly by the Grandmaster's sister, **Ling**. Cracking open a jar of liquor before a storm even hits is just her style. "Glad you came!" She takes on the Catastrophe swigging straight from the jar, no cup needed.

When the Astronomical Bureau casters guarding the core give ground, Lungmen's shadow, **the Rat King** (Lin Yusha's father), steps in himself. Everyone had taken him for just some old man who liked to take walks — but he shows the strength to push back an entire Originium storm.

blocks.cap: Like a hundred-year-old tree, the old rat stands his ground in the middle of the storm.

blocks.x: But the cost of such an effort is steep. As her father collapses, coughing blood, Lin Yusha throws herself in front of him.
Enough talk. Everyone's hurt, so catch your breath. Not much further now. Eyes forward!
9. What the Drawn Sword Revealed

In the middle of the Catastrophe, the unconscious Jieyun unknowingly draws the Grandmaster's sword. The moment she does, memories sealed within it come pouring out. Chongyue, it turns out, is one of twelve fragments of 'Sui,' a Catastrophe-class beast hunted down a thousand years ago, who split and sealed itself into pieces.

blocks.cap: The old sword reveals itself. This was the secret behind how he had separated himself from it.

blocks.x: Chongyue has siblings beyond just **Ling** — Nian and Dusk among them. All of them are fragments of Sui, scattered across the human world in different forms.

And behind all this commotion was yet another fragment, 'Ya.' Badly wounded in that hunt a thousand years ago, he'd vanished without a trace — and had briefly used Sanhaizhong to pry into Sui's whereabouts.
Chongyue meets Ya in a vision, the two of them standing on a small boat, each on their own end, atop a frozen river.

blocks.cap: Eight thousand springs, eight thousand autumns. A conversation between the same kind, who have hated each other the longest.

blocks.x: You sealed yourself in a sword and took on a human body, just to think of yourself as one of them — and in the end, what did you get out of it?
The Catastrophe isn't over yet. People don't even know you exist. Someone has to answer for Yumen's sake — and no matter how I look at it, there's no one left to do that but me.
The conversation ends without resolution. Ya is sent flying with a single punch, and vanishes, leaving only the words: leave Yumen.
Chongyue says nothing at all. He knows well enough that every living thing facing death wears more or less the same face.
Epilogue: Everyone Lays Down Their Own Grudge

After the Catastrophe passes, everyone finds their own path forward. The sword leaves the Grandmaster's hands and passes to Waai Tinfu. "You may as well keep it as a token for our next match." The man who took forty years to win a single exchange accepts the sword, already looking forward to another forty.
Jieyun leaves the sword at a grave, only to come back for it and return it to its true owner — dimly realizing that the sword wasn't the only thing her master had missed.

Lin Yusha decides to stay in Lungmen instead of studying abroad in Victoria. "Whatever burden someone else sets down, someone else has to pick up." Waai Fu keeps training, promising her father a rematch, while Qiubai sets off with her sword in hand, in search of a new goal.

Waai Fu offers to pay off the rest of her father's medical bills for Lee, but the doctor just waves her off. "I figure saving and caring for people beats fighting all day, doesn't it." In the end, Waai Fu stays on and helps with the cleaning.
Last of all, Wei Yanwu shows the Grandmaster one final sword technique — the true final form of the Crimson Night Sword Style, 'Heaven.'
The sword moves with the heart, and there's no regret in a single stroke. The moment you hesitate, even slightly, the blade loses its edge and turns against you instead.
Before the gate, the Grandmaster runs into a stranger playing Go alone. He was, in truth, another 'sibling,' come to say a final goodbye.

blocks.cap: The traveler playing Go alone turns out to have come as a guest, to bid farewell.

blocks.x: Thanks to your sword, I learned something. We'll meet again. Farewell, sibling.

Only then does General Zuo Xuanliao receive, from Lu Wuguan, the Grandmaster's final gift — a manual called 《The Manual of Martial Arts》, complete with breathing techniques for tempering the body through swordplay. By then, the Grandmaster had already left Yumen without a word of farewell, half a watch earlier.
For the first time in a hundred years, the Grandmaster leaves Yumen. As the eighteenth drumbeat sounds, General Zuo Xuanliao himself beats the drum of farewell.

blocks.cap: Everyone else has returned to the frontier fortress — but where is he, alone, headed?

blocks.x: The drama 《Storms Over Yumen》 told of swordsmen who laid down their grudges for the sake of the nation. Yumen's real story turned out no different. Around a single sword, everyone learned how to lay down a grudge of their own.

act22side.tagline: A kind Infected traveler who tends to burns turns out to be the mysterious 'Leader' of the rebel group Dublinn. On the night a woman who's lived her whole life in her sister's shadow finally acknowledges the fire that's hers alone.

chars.name: Reed
chars.desc: An Infected woman who wandered the wilds alone, keeping her identity hidden. Takes in and leads a band of Tarans on the run

chars.name: Finn
chars.desc: A talkative, warm-hearted Taran merchant. Appoints herself the caretaker of the band of fugitives

chars.name: Celmoon
chars.desc: A rough-edged Taran woman who follows and mimics her brother, who joined Dublinn

chars.name: Bagpipe
chars.desc: A retired Victorian soldier turned operator, stubbornly determined to protect commoners

chars.name: Ch'en
chars.desc: A cool-headed inspector skilled with the sword. Bagpipe's colleague

chars.name: Moraine
chars.desc: An Infected woman with night blindness. Quiet, but resolute

chars.name: Earl Warwick
chars.desc: An old earl who hosts a salon for the revival of Taran culture

chars.name: Harmonie
chars.desc: A Felinic spy playing both sides for Victoria and Taran

chars.name: Fisher
chars.desc: A mysterious city council investigator, chasing the covert struggle between Dublinn and the dukes alone — Operator Puzzle

terms.name: Dublinn
terms.desc: An armed rebel group active across southern Victoria, championing the liberation of the Taran people. Led by a mysterious 'Leader'

terms.name: The Wraithguard
terms.desc: A secret group that poses as Dublinn's elite while brutally eliminating witnesses

terms.name: The Iron Rust Guard
terms.desc: An elite unit under Duke Wellington. Moves covertly, posing as Dublinn when necessary

terms.name: The Curfew Bell
terms.desc: The curfew bell that rings at night. Once it tolls, not even a Taran can light a lamp

terms.name: Draco
