terms.desc: The fiery bloodline of King Gael, ruler of the old Taran kingdom. Since Victoria seized the throne, passed down only as legend

terms.name: The Tarans
terms.desc: Natives annexed by Victoria, stripped of their names and language. Drift along with the nomadic city, plagued by discrimination and Oripathy

blocks.x: When night falls, a bell rings. The **Curfew Bell**. Once it rings, a Taran can't so much as light a lamp, for fear of looking like a poacher sneaking onto the hunting grounds.

Somewhere in the Scatana Plains, two Taran drifters had been caught and beaten by a patrol. Then, suddenly, a fire broke out near a warehouse, and the patrol rushed off in that direction. The one who quietly cut the rope binding the two, without ever giving her name, was an unfamiliar wyvern.

Finn and Celmoon simply called this woman the 'kind doctor.' But this woman had never even properly said her own name. And by the end of this journey, her true identity turns out to be tied to the roots of the largest rebel organization in this story.
1. The Night the Curfew Bell Rang, a Nameless Arsonist
Merchant Finn and hot-tempered Celmoon were being caught and beaten by a patrol. Then, out of nowhere, a warehouse caught fire.
While the patrol rushed off to put out the flames, an unfamiliar woman came over and cut Finn's rope. Finn asked her who she was.
……Who are you?
……Go, quickly.
Even after they fled into the marshes, the woman stayed by their side. Finn called her 'Doctor,' but the woman flatly insisted she wasn't one.

Once nine o'clock passes, even the radio stops broadcasting. Nothing makes a sound after the curfew bell. Yet this woman, despite saying she'd been to the Scatana Plains before, says "there was no curfew bell rule back then."
The woman quietly asks if they'd killed anyone. Celmoon readily admits to killing two patrolmen.
Everyone starts to sense this woman isn't just some ordinary wandering doctor.
2. Playing Doctor Without Being One, and a Suspicious Old Poetry Book
The next morning, Finn tries to hand out bread, only to find the woman reading an old Taran poetry anthology. Finn, embarrassed, admits he can't even read Taran script.
When Finn thanks her, the woman mutters, **"To keep the promise made with Rhodes Island, one must cherish the lives of others."** Finn just lets it slide, saying he didn't quite catch it.
Meanwhile, over in Trent County, prosecutor **Ch'en** and former Victorian soldier **Bagpipe** appear. The two are tracking a mysterious group called the 'Wraithguard.'
Then Ch'en suddenly freezes up, saying something reminds her of an incident back in Lungmen.
The Wraithguard is a group in strange garb spotted in Hillock County. Ch'en and Bagpipe cautiously begin to follow the trail.
3. The Day They Robbed a Wagon, Reuniting with an Old Comrade

When Finn's group runs out of food, they decide to rob a wagon. But Celmoon insists on imitating Dublinn while doing it. Finn gets angry — is he putting everyone in danger just to satisfy his own selfishness?
And your selfishness…… I don't like it either. That's the biggest lie of all.

Reed quietly scolds Celmoon. As luck would have it, that very wagon happened to have Ch'en and Bagpipe traveling with it. The moment Celmoon draws his sword, posing as real Wraithguard, Bagpipe's Castbreaker comes flying in.
In the middle of the chaos, Bagpipe runs into a wyvern wielding a spear she's seen somewhere before. The instant they recognize each other, Bagpipe's eyes go wide.
……Reed?
Surprisingly, the two know each other. The fact that they're both Rhodes Island operators comes out naturally in this very moment.
4. Night at the Safe House, Hay Bales, and a Beloved Old Home

With Bagpipe and Ch'en's help, Reed brings the drifters all the way to a safe house. She even gets tents and emergency supplies from Rhodes Island. Celmoon, though, still turns her back, saying, "I'm going back to Dublinn."

blocks.cap: Lying side by side in the hay, Reed brings up her old home for the first time in a long while, pretending she hasn't fallen apart.

blocks.x: At night, Bagpipe pulls Reed down into the hay. "The hay smells nice, doesn't it?" Idle chatter, but here, for the first time, Reed opens up about the home she lost.
Y'know, ye only say 'no' a lot when talkin' about yerself. Like……like yer tryin' to get away from something.
Reed says she has an older sister. But her words trail off beyond that.
Instead she answers quietly — someone once said something similar to her. That person saved her, she says, and she still wants to ask why.
5. Moraine Joins, and They Meet Soldiers Who Walk Even After Death
I don't want to be a burden…… as long as my legs can still carry me forward, I want to walk together with all of you.

Moraine, an Infected woman with night blindness, joins Reed. While on the move, the band of drifters runs into unidentified soldiers. Reed inspects the scene and finds it strange — signs of battle, but not a single body.
Bagpipe and Ch'en say they saw something similar in Hillock County: soldiers with fire blazing from their eyes.
Reed answers quietly. "Those…… are wraiths. People who are surely dead, but couldn't find rest."
Just then, Celmoon, who had been hiding, appears. She's spotted a familiar silhouette among the soldiers.
……Brother.
6. The Shadow Sisters' Old Promise

Celmoon secretly follows her brother. Two years ago, he'd left with the soles of his shoes worn through, heading off to find Dublinn. Celmoon recalls how she'd once tried to follow him, then ran instead.
And overlapping with it, a memory surfaces from long ago — a snowy holiday night. There were two sisters. The elder always excelled at everything; the younger only ever imitated her.
The younger tried mimicking her sister's voice to ask their father for something, but he saw through it right away. "Of course. What kind of father can't tell his own daughters apart?"
That night, the sisters lost their home. The elder took her sister's hand and made her knock on an unfamiliar door.
"Go on, don't be afraid. I'll be watching over you." And so the sisters hid away in their benefactor's study.

blocks.cap: The elder made a weapon of the flame in her hand, while the younger trembled in fear of that very fire.

blocks.x: Their benefactor handed the two of them spears. The elder used hers to strike down silence without hesitation, but the younger's hands trembled every time.
It's all right, little sister. If you don't know where your desire lies, just try being me for now. In the future, you and I will both become 'the Leader.'
Back to the present. Before Celmoon, cudgel in hand, stands the dead man who was once her brother.
No matter how many times she strikes him down, the soldier doesn't fall. In the end, it's Reed's flame that puts him to rest instead of Celmoon, and Celmoon collapses to the ground.
7. Songs by the Campfire, and an Inspector Posing as a Guest
A few days after settling at the safe house, the drifters share a warm dinner in their tent for the first time in a while. Finn goes around excitedly praising Bagpipe's cooking.

blocks.cap: Around the campfire, the fugitives sing for the first time in a long while. Raising empty cups for those not there.

blocks.x: The wanderer, setting off on a long road, wonders where this path might lead♪
At night, they all gather around the campfire and sing. Reed, pushed into it despite herself, hums along to a Taran song.
But amid the singing, an unfamiliar man cuts in. His name is 'Fisher.' He claims to be a city council investigator, and says he's had this camp under watch for quite a while now.

Fisher pulls Reed aside to sit alone with him. "Lovely evening," he says, adding this is a much more pleasant setting than an interrogation room. The song goes on, but underneath it, the interrogation has already begun.
8. The Interrogator's True Identity, and a Fire Erupts
Fisher is a slippery one. He points out that Reed has no identity records whatsoever. "Wouldn't that make you rather like a shadow?"
On top of that, Fisher already knows Reed's Dublinn comrades are coming to rescue her. "Come under our protection, and we won't ask any further about the crimes you've committed in the past."
……I refuse!
Reed refuses firmly. Bagpipe and Ch'en tear down the interrogation tent in a surprise strike, and at that very moment, flames erupt without any warning at all.
Shouldn't there be fire blazing on your spear right now?

Bagpipe sees the flame at the tip of the spear. And it suddenly overlaps with something she'd seen once before, in Hillock County. That Caster was the very same person who once stood watching, indifferent, as the streets burned.
9. That Night Eight Years Ago, and the Burning Forest
We return to eight years ago, the night of Earl Warwick's final gathering. Sensing something was off, the earl had set out to walk through the snowstorm with the poet.

blocks.cap: When the door opened, their benefactor lay dying before a cold flame. The younger sister simply stood there.

blocks.x: That night, the earl accepts a cup of poisoned wine. His counterpart was none other than the elder of the two sisters. "A greedy Draco, a Draco of endless ambition…… so what I've spent half my life investigating wasn't wrong after all." The earl closed his eyes with a smile.
The younger sister stood at the doorway and watched it all. Her reflection in the windowpane was a tangle of fear and pity.
Back to the present. In the fog and smoke, the drifters wander the forest, calling Reed's name.
Celmoon and Moraine also carve a path through, holding off the soldiers. People simply run toward the one light they can see ahead.

blocks.cap: The smoke thickens, the heat draws nearer. No one had ever called out their names before.

blocks.x: Somewhere beyond the fog, someone keeps calling Reed's name. Reed suddenly finds herself wondering. "……Reed. Are they calling for me?"
10. Reed Is, In Fact, Dublinn's 'Leader'

blocks.cap: The two meet through the fog. For the first time, Bagpipe sees Reed's true face.

blocks.x: As the fog clears, Bagpipe sees clearly the flame Reed has made. "Was that you, the Caster in Hillock County? Are ye Dublinn's Leader?"
Everyone waits for her answer. Reed always wanted to shake her head at this kind of question, but this time, she doesn't want to.
……I am Dublinn's 'Leader.'

Reed doesn't hide the wrongs she's committed. That among the countless lives thrown into the fire that was Dublinn, some of the sacrifices weren't even necessary. Even so, she says, starting from this very moment, she wants to live differently.
Bagpipe never really knew how to be disappointed. But right now, she doesn't even know who she's disappointed in anymore. Still, Bagpipe decides not to leave Reed — she just goes her own way instead.
11. Celmoon, Sleeping in the Mud
While escaping, Reed is nearly caught and dragged off again. This time, a woman named Harmonie appears and calls Reed 'Lochsinni' — claiming they're old friends.
Reed doesn't deny the name. Instead, she quietly asks back — did the Leader know? That innocent people would die in Hillock County?
Amid the chaos, Celmoon and Moraine set out to save those who'd fallen behind. Celmoon holds off the soldiers with a broken sword, sending Moraine ahead to safety first.
"They're trying to take our Leader — how could I just stand here?" Celmoon said with a smile. Those were her last words.
At dawn, Moraine returns alone. In her hands, a broken sword and Celmoon's blood-soaked headscarf.
Reed quietly asks what happened to Celmoon. Moraine only shakes her head.
……For everyone rising up from this mire.
Reed raises her hand, and a small flower of flame blooms over the mud, then quietly withers away.
12. In the Ruined Palace, Facing Her Own Shadow
The place they take shelter in the fog turns out to be, of all places, the ruined palace of King Gael. With almost no Originium crystals nearby, it was a safe spot.
Inside the ruins, Reed finds a familiar light. Traces that her sister had passed through.
And something with her sister's voice appears before Reed. "You want to snuff out that flame, don't you."
The shadow presses in on her. "You're nothing but your sister's shadow. She has the ability to reach the peak in every field, but you've always hesitated and shown nothing but weakness."
I'm not my sister's shadow. I'm the shadow of the 'Leader' that lives in people's ideals.
But Reed doesn't back down. Everyone needed a way out, she says, and it just so happened that she was there.
The shadow asks one last question. "Lochsinni, what is it you want?" Instead of answering, Reed reaches out to a wounded drifter. From her hand flows not flame, but a gentle, healing light.
I've simply found my own flame.
13. A Speech Aboard the Warship, and Reed's Answer

Meanwhile, out on Victoria's open sea, her sister Evelina returns to Oak Grove County aboard Duke Wellington's swift warship, after a seven-year voyage. This city, of all places, was where the sisters were born.

blocks.cap: On the deck of the swift warship, Dublinn's 'Leader' stands before the troops.

blocks.x: We do not fight to steal the crown Victoria holds. We fight to reclaim the destiny that was stolen from us, and to hold that destiny in our own hands.
Evelina begins her speech before the soldiers. She vows to reclaim the name and destiny stolen from them for so long.

blocks.cap: At the very moment her sister gives her speech aboard the warship, the younger sister delivers her own answer to the drifters among the reeds.

blocks.x: At the same time, somewhere among the reeds, Reed also stands before the drifters.
Moraine asks — saying it's 'for the sake of the Taran people's lives' feels vague. What exactly are they even supposed to be fighting?
Even if we can't defeat death…… we can still grant dignity to our lives.
Reed answers. She says the Taran deserve a fire to warm themselves on cold nights, and drink to share when they sing.
"Let's go — we're one of them too." Reed sets off, walking together with the drifters.
Asked what she'll do once she moves together with Dublinn, Reed answers calmly. "When that day comes, I'll go meet her too."
Epilogue: A House Built in the Wrong Place
'The Instructor' and Harmonie are still locked in a battle of wits over Lochsinni. Harmonie says she intends to visit, in person, the medical company where Lochsinni once hid herself.

Ch'en and Bagpipe hitch a ride on a cargo truck toward their next mission. Bagpipe fiddles with the old radio Finn gave her. It carries news that the clashes between Victoria and Kazdel are escalating.
Fisher accepts the Caster duke's offer of promotion. His old identity as an investigator is set to be discarded now.
On his way back, Fisher happens to run into Finn. Finn, selling wooden toys, proudly points to the new house he built just yesterday.
Run, Taran. Looks like you picked the wrong spot for your house.

At that moment, a massive roar sounds, and Duke Wellington's fleet rises over the horizon. Fisher leaves one last line and gets into his car. Behind him, the sound of the brick house collapsing is soon swallowed by the warship's din.

When the curfew bell rang, there was once a woman preparing to run. Now, she's become a woman who lights her own fire to warm others. Knowing now that she doesn't have to defeat her sister to be whole, she takes, once again today, one more step out of the shadow.

act21side.tagline: A runaway wolf comes home to settle an old debt, and ends up relearning how not to run. Instead of the fantasy of being a 'Siracusan,' Texas decides to just stay Texas.

chars.name: Texas
chars.desc: Penguin Logistics' reticent dual-blade wielder. Actually the last blood heir of a great Siracusa mafia house — Operator Texas

chars.name: Exusiai
chars.desc: Texas's longtime partner. A Sankta who causes trouble by meddling, then cleans it up by meddling some more — Operator Exusiai

chars.name: Croissant
chars.desc: A warm-hearted, nagging bodyguard. A dependable companion who worries about everyone in her thick accent — Operator Croissant

chars.name: Sora
chars.desc: A Lungmen idol who followed Texas all the way to Siracusa. The grit to go so far as infiltrating a job as a musical actress — Operator Sora

chars.name: Lappland
chars.desc: The daughter expelled from the Salucho family. A wolf whose obsessive fixation on Texas borders on madness — Operator Lappland

chars.name: Lavinia
chars.desc: A young judge in Volsini. An idealist who won't give up on fairness even while under the Belloné family's protection

chars.name: Leontuccio
chars.desc: The young heir of the Belloné family. A young master ahead of his time, who trusts negotiation and profit over violence

chars.name: Bernardo
chars.desc: An elder of the Belloné family, disguised as the art director of Del Alba theater troupe. Leontuccio's father

terms.name: Siracusa
terms.desc: A nation where twelve 'Families' (mafia houses) effectively divide up and rule the cities. There's a court and judges on the surface, but the real order always lies with the Families.

terms.name: Family
terms.desc: The mafia houses rooted in each of Siracusa's cities. They pay lip service to Madame Sicilia, but each moves by its own rules behind the scenes.

terms.name: Volsini
terms.desc: The city where the twelve Families' turf war is hottest, over the construction of a new nomadic city. The stage for this entire story.

terms.name: Grayhall
terms.desc: The council where the heads of the twelve Families gather. A symbol of the rules Madame Sicilia established to prevent all-out war between Families.

terms.name: Madame Sicilia
terms.desc: The absolute ruler who has governed Siracusa for sixty years. She's managed the Families' conflicts with one bare-minimum line: 'no total annihilation.'

terms.name: Wolf Lord
terms.desc: Immortal beings who live in the wilds. Each picks out a human called a 'Fang' to fight in their stead, as part of a game they play.

blocks.x: Siracusa is a city-state that's effectively owned, in its entirety, by twelve 'Families' — mafia houses. There are courts, there are judges, but the shadow of the Families always looms over them both.

**Texas**, quietly making deliveries for Penguin Logistics in Lungmen, spots a wolf standing on a rooftop outside her window one night. That wolf was a visitor come to collect on a debt she'd owed for seven years.
Texas leaves without a word of goodbye — just "I'll be back soon." But what awaits her isn't a nostalgic homecoming; it's the surname she'd abandoned back then.
1. A Wolf Comes to Collect a Debt, In Person No Less

blocks.cap: Above Lungmen's night skyline stood a wolf that had no business being there.

blocks.x: The wolf outside the window was **Zalak** — an immortal being who calls himself a 'Wolf Lord,' and the creditor of the deal Texas struck when she left Columbia.
The demand was simple: return to Siracusa and act as his 'Fang' — his proxy — just once. Texas said yes.

Her boss **Emperor** hurled every ounce of half-baked Konglish curses at her but let her go in the end. "I'll give you vacation, so go take care of that not-at-all important business." And so, one human and one wolf walked off into the dark.
2. A Murderer, the Moment She Comes Back
The one who greets Texas in Siracusa is **Leontuccio** Belloné. The young heir of the Belloné family, he asks Texas to attend tonight's party and show off the name 'Texas' publicly.
But mid-party, Construction Minister Karachi is killed instantly by a car bomb, and Leontuccio himself is attacked. Texas, present at the scene, instantly becomes the prime suspect.

blocks.cap: There's no one left to ask if you're hurt, even after the fighting ends. Texas sinks into an emptiness she's never felt before in her life.

blocks.x: The case falls to **Lavinia** — a young judge who insists on unshakable fairness even under the Belloné family's protection. In the end, she arrests Texas.

The truth is, this attack was orchestrated behind the scenes by Leontuccio's right hand and Belloné's strategist, **Dmitri**. Believing the Family had gone too soft, he had, for the first time, bared his fangs against his own master.
3. A Lungmen Idol Infiltrates a Siracusa Theater Troupe

Meanwhile, back in Lungmen, **Sora** couldn't sit still. Even in the middle of her idol activities, she managed to pass an audition for a Siracusa theater troupe, and chose to head to Siracusa to find Texas.

Coming along were Texas's longtime partner **Exusiai** and **Croissant**, who appointed herself the two of theirs' bodyguard. The three of them get swept up in a Family street brawl the moment they arrive — their first taste of hospitality.

"They're way harder to deal with than the thugs back in Lungmen." Exusiai grumbles, yet still ends up saving a random citizen, while Croissant is too busy chasing after her big sis to do much else. That's about all Siracusa's first impression amounted to.

The Del Alba troupe the three set foot into is led by none other than **Bernardo** as its art director. The title of the new musical he's preparing? None other than *The Death of Texas* — the first work to put the real name Texas Family on stage.

The critic **Ben**, who took it upon himself to coach Sora's acting, was also a puzzling figure. Every offhand remark he tossed out while munching on pizza somehow hit the mark. "Norms build people false pedestals, and true reverence quietly slips away in the cracks between them."
4. Why a Truck Crashed Straight Into the Courtroom
To calm the situation quickly, Texas confesses to being the true culprit — a calculated move to stabilize things and lure out whoever's really behind it, using herself as bait.

But right before the sentencing, a truck comes bursting straight through the courtroom wall. The one who steps out of the driver's seat is **Lappland** — the daughter expelled from the Salucho family. She calmly turns herself in, saying, "I'm the real culprit."

blocks.cap: A truck bursts through the courthouse wall. A direct challenge to the order Madame Sicilia had upheld for so long.

blocks.x: Everyone is puzzled. Why would Salucho's problem child suddenly take the fall for Texas? Lappland herself gives away not a shred of her true intentions.
The trial is suspended and Texas is cleared of suspicion. But the true mastermind is still crouched somewhere in the shadows.
5. The Judge's Trusted Promise — Turns Out the Man Who Made It Was the Mastermind
Lavinia had a secret: on the day the Belloné family achieves final victory, she'd been promised a city where the fairness she believed in would actually be realized.
Meanwhile, Salucho boss **Alberto** also quietly begins to move — a covert alliance with Belloné to take down Rosati and swallow the new city whole.
But the man who made that promise to her was sitting right there at the table plotting with Salucho to strike Rosati. Lavinia storms in and demands to know why — the answer she gets is just one line.
The game is over, Lavinia.

blocks.cap: In a single line, Lavinia's past decade and more came crashing down.

blocks.x: Lavinia leaves the room without looking back, terrified she'll burst into tears. It was the moment the very reason she'd endured until now collapsed entirely.
6. Playwright Caterina — Turns Out She's Texas's Childhood Friend and Greatest Enemy

On the day of Sora's musical debut, the boss of the Rosati family arrives as an honored guest. It turns out that boss is none other than the playwright 'Caterina' herself — the person Sora had been friends with for weeks was the very top of Rosati.
On top of that, Belloné and Salucho conspire to assassinate Rosati right then and there. Texas draws the weapon hidden inside her bass guitar and sprints across the stage.

blocks.cap: Who knew there was a weapon hidden inside that bass. In a theater emptied of fleeing audience, the real show begins.

blocks.x: After a hard-won path to the room, the one who greets Texas has already set down her blade — a childhood friend from long ago. "It's been a while, Celinia." Rosati's boss, **Giovanna**.
7. The Quietest, Heaviest Reunion
Giovanna extends a hand to Texas. "Join me, and we can do something big here in Siracusa." But Texas's answer had already been decided.

"I don't want to be Siracusan or Columbian — **I'm just Celinia Texas.**" The two part ways in the end. Giovanna lets her old friend, now an enemy of Rosati, walk away alive, but leaves behind a warning: this is the last time.

blocks.cap: "Big sis, we're here!" Croissant and Exusiai, having chased her all the way to Siracusa, reunite at last.

blocks.x: The three friends who greet the fleeing Texas ask her nothing at all. "The mark of a good partner is not asking what the other doesn't want to say." The four of them come together once more.
8. The Weakest One Picks Up the Gun

The Construction Minister's seat, left empty since Karachi's death, is taken over by **Rubio**, the Food Safety Minister long stuck in an irrelevant post. On the day of his inaugural speech, he goes on the radio and begins to pour his heart out to the entire city.
His friendship with Karachi, and even the conspiracy Belloné and Salucho had cooked up. A confession delivered calmly, without flashy rhetoric or excitement.
That we are living people. That our lives are real. That we are not animals penned inside a fence.
The moment the speech ends, a gunshot rings out. Rubio protected the final broadcast with his own life.

blocks.cap: Lavinia gently covered her daughter's ears beside her. The gunshot cracked open all of Volsini, and Siracusa's long silence.

blocks.x: 9. Her Father's Will Was, In Fact, the Entire Nation
Rubio's exposé sends the city into uncontrollable upheaval. Belloné and Salucho's secret pact is laid bare for all to see, and all twelve Families begin tearing at each other.

Enraged, Alberto gives Leontuccio just ten seconds: submit to Salucho, or die right here. As the pocket watch's second hand ticks over that short span, Leontuccio has already realized what his father had been aiming for all along.
Leontuccio finally understands his father's true purpose. What Bernardo wanted wasn't the new city, wasn't victory — it was **a Siracusa without Families altogether**.
If the Families disappear, will Siracusa be better off than it is now?
Leontuccio's answer differs from his father's. "That's not enough — you have to let people know what's happening right now, and let them take part in it." The son resolves to go beyond his father.

blocks.cap: A face that took up its father's question, but meant to go past his answer.

blocks.x: Bernardo had already made his peace. Right before Wolf Lord Zalak comes to demand the price of betrayal, he swallows poison of his own accord, ending the game himself.

blocks.cap: He closed his eyes quietly, recalling some summer long ago when he worked, drenched in sweat, at a shoe store.

blocks.x: 10. Trial of the Wolf Lords, and a Farewell
The furious Zalak appears before Leontuccio to demand payment. At that very moment, boss Emperor, who flew in from Lungmen the instant he heard the news, cuts in.

blocks.cap: "Ladies and gentlemen." Lungmen's boss barges into the Wolf Lords' game — the flashiest meddling Siracusa has ever seen.

blocks.x: What Emperor brings with him are other Wolf Lords who've already lost this very game. Zalak, having broken the rules, has no choice but to bow his head before them.
Meanwhile, Lappland bids her father Alberto a final farewell and stands before Texas. "Today, I'm going to kill you." The two swords clash.

blocks.cap: No flourish, only blades aiming purely for each other's lives. A reenactment of that night seven years ago.

blocks.x: "Texas, so you really were just Texas." — "Of course I'm just Texas. What about you?" — "I guess I'm just Lappland too."
Neither of them wins. Lappland holds out her hand, and Texas takes it. As friend and enemy both, the two walk side by side toward Grayhall.
There, Texas, Leontuccio, Lavinia, and Lappland finally come face to face with this nation's true ruler, **Madame Sicilia**.
On the surface, a gentle old woman, but her mere presence was enough to freeze the room solid. Even Wolf Lord Zalak didn't dare act carelessly before her.

blocks.cap: Four people, each wanting to tear Siracusa apart for their own reasons, standing side by side.

blocks.x: Madame Sicilia demands no promises. Instead, she agrees to lend Leontuccio one new city — a fragment of the answer Bernardo had longed for his whole life: 'a city without Families.'
Epilogue: Sutures
On the day of his father's funeral, Leontuccio digs the grave alone. No one else attends, but the one who helps him push the coffin is Dmitri — his old right hand, the one who betrayed him.

blocks.cap: The pocket watch Dmitri tossed onto the grave. A wordless reconciliation, and a promise for what comes next.

blocks.x: In the now-empty Belloné mansion, Lavinia and Leontuccio talk about a new order. "We're going to build new things there. An order that belongs to everyone."

Car washer **Dambron** is still there at the car wash. Lavinia stops by often on her way through, checking in on him. In Siracusa, even the most ordinary conversation can sometimes bring the greatest comfort.
Emperor is still waiting for a party. "I don't know how I survived three thousand years without parties," he grumbles, and ends up settling in Siracusa a few more days anyway.
Sora returns to Giovanna's room with Texas, but she's already gone. What's left is a bundle of scripts — the never-finished Act 3 of *The Death of Texas*.
The first page reads: "I dedicate this script to my dear friend Celinia, and to my newfound friends at Penguin Logistics."

New rumors are already circulating in the theater district. Giovanna — or rather, Caterina — is said to be preparing her next work, *The Siracusan*. A name that points at every single person born in this city, yet one no one can fully define.

The wolf that had stood on that rooftop outside her window that night had, in the end, come only to collect an old debt. But Texas, who went to pay it off, only finished making ready to return to Lungmen once she'd built herself a reason not to need to flee Siracusa. The wolf that used to run now chooses, for herself, where to go home to.

act19side.tagline: A story about going to find missing coworkers, only to discover a paradise every one of them walked into willingly. And the kindest boss in the world tears down her own dream with her own hands, just to set the people she loves most free.

chars.name: Dorothy
chars.desc: Head of Rhine Lab's Applied Originium Arts Division. In charge of Base 359, she's unusually warm toward the pioneers.

chars.name: Elena
chars.desc: Dorothy's assistant and a researcher in Rhine Lab's Energy Division. An ambitious woman who idolizes her boss, Ferdinand — Operator Astgenne

chars.name: Silence
chars.desc: Joyce's attending physician and a Medic cooperating with Rhodes Island. A stickler for principle who trusts only what she sees with her own eyes.

chars.name: Greyy
chars.desc: An electrical engineering intern at Rhodes Island. Once Infected herself, she knows how to take a step forward even in the dark.

chars.name: Ptilopsis
chars.desc: An Infected doctor under contract with Rhine Lab. She has a special chip called the 'No. 9 Device' wired into her nervous system — Operator Ptilopsis

chars.name: Saria
chars.desc: A founding member of Rhine Lab and its former Defense Division head. She left the company, but cleaning up its messes still falls to her.

chars.name: Muelsyse
chars.desc: Head of Rhine Lab's Ecology Division. She disappears while trying to warn the director of danger.

chars.name: Sani
chars.desc: 'Captain' of the pioneer band. A former law student who lost everything after contracting Oripathy.

chars.name: Mary
chars.desc: A sheriff at the Trimounts police station. Sani's childhood friend.

chars.name: Ferdinand
chars.desc: Head of Rhine Lab's Energy Division. An ambitious man with his eye on the director's chair.

terms.name: Rhine Lab
terms.desc: A major Originium Arts research corporation headquartered in Trimounts, Columbia. Under the director, nine division heads each oversee their own research department.

terms.name: Trimounts
terms.desc: A nomadic city that serves as Columbia's center of science and technology. Hundreds of corporations commission and operate experimental facilities here.

terms.name: The Pioneers
terms.desc: A band of laborers who work the wastes outside the nomadic city. Mostly made up of people the city has pushed out — the Infected, debtors, ex-convicts.

terms.name: Base 359
terms.desc: A new experimental facility Rhine Lab built on the outskirts of Trimounts. Home to Dorothy's lab, and the stage for this whole affair.

terms.name: Transmission Substance
terms.desc: A new substance developed by Rhine Lab, said to turn even people with low Originium Arts aptitude into skilled Casters. A silvery liquid that mediates neural signals.

terms.name: the 'Hub'
terms.desc: A massive machine at Base 359 that weaves together the consciousness of the missing pioneers into one.

blocks.x: Rhine Lab, based in Trimounts, Columbia, is a major Originium Arts corporation where nine division heads split five research departments among them. This story begins when three of them go missing at once.

Ecology Division head **Muelsyse** vanished after leaving only a frantic message on the answering machine, while Applied Originium Arts Division head **Dorothy** and her assistant **Elena** disappeared from the new experimental facility. Former Defense Division head **Saria** turned to Rhodes Island for help.
But what the operators found upon reaching the base was neither a hostage situation nor an experiment gone wrong. It was a paradise every one of the missing had walked into on their own two feet.
1. The Last Words Left on the Answering Machine
The incident begins with the answering machine in Rhine Lab's director's private office. "This is bad, Christine. I think I've really done it this time……" It was Muelsyse's voice.
……Christine, this thought just hit me out of nowhere…… if that person is still alive, then we……
The message cuts off there. In truth, Muelsyse had been seized that very night by an unidentified woman and was being interrogated about Rhine Lab's secrets.
When Muelsyse refused to talk, the woman left only a single, short line: "Deal with her." A massive suit of powered armor then appeared and closed in on her.
The next day, Saria's meeting with Muelsyse falls through — she's not at the meeting spot. What Saria gets instead is a missing-person notice, and the place she turns to for help is Rhodes Island.

The Doctor, arriving on scene with the **Mechanist**, is met first with an unenthusiastic briefing. "I'm a mechanic, not a detective's sidekick……" she grumbles, but tags along anyway. "I just hope I can get back to the ship tomorrow and see my beloved workbench again."

It's here they learn that three people vanished from Base 359 at once — pioneer captain **Sani**, assistant Elena, and lab head Dorothy. While searching the back alleys, the two are soon ambushed by energy weapons.
2. Base 359, and a Kind Boss

Inside the base, an entirely different kind of day was unfolding. It was an ordinary morning — Dorothy's assistant Elena handing coffee to her Infected coworker Joyce, listening to a new researcher's grumbling.
Dorothy was the type to secretly make heating modules for the pioneers. Elena wasn't happy about it. "They're just the pioneer band. They don't even think of us as friends."
Dorothy answers with a smile. "They're all just ordinary young people." She seemed like nothing more than a good-natured boss.
But that night, Sani leads a raid on the experimental facility. The targets: the two researchers, Elena and Joyce.
We had no choice but to do this. No one in this facility genuinely cares about us. No one but ourselves.
Sani was a pioneer — once a law student, until Oripathy took everything from him. To him, corporate researchers were just people who stood around with coffee in hand, talking about themselves.
And so the hostage crisis begins. Sani's demand is simple: that he and all his fellow pioneers leave this facility alive.
3. Something Nameless Is Born
The standoff draws an unexpected guest. Behind Sani and his group, an irregular silver mass comes crawling slowly into view.
In an instant, it disassembles an entire pioneer shack. Walls, windows, everything inside — all of it scattered into the sky as silver fragments.
Those things…… split into countless tiny units, break down the matter that makes up that house, carry it off…… then gather back together to form a single shape.
They soon learn it responds to light and sound. Assistant **Greyy**'s Arts manage to lure it away briefly, but the decisive help comes, unexpectedly, from Dorothy.
A 'gentle woman's voice,' rigged through the lab's speakers, plays out. "The doors of the experiment zone will open for you all the same." She had made them an escape route.
4. What Was Learned at the Bar, and What Was Lost
Chasing the disappearances, the Doctor's group follows Saria to her usual back-alley bar. Saria's informant is a man who used to be a lab surgeon.
The man, an Arts unit implanted in his right arm, can only lift his glass with his left hand. Asked about the silver reagent's identity, he goes quiet, gripped by fear.

blocks.cap: An 'old friend' appears and offers a drink. In this city, even a toast can be a threat.

blocks.x: Shaking like that, the bar's AC must be turned up too high. Why don't you drink one more to warm yourself up?
The mysterious woman **Ho'olheyak** appears and forces the man to drink. He collapses in convulsions, revealing a poison fang hidden in his left molar.
Ho'olheyak says she's after the legacy of the ruined lab, the 'Roken Tank.' Then, leaving Saria with a single warning — "don't be hasty about making enemies of anyone" — she vanishes.
5. The Eye Found Inside the Armor
While searching the alleys, the Doctor's group is attacked by yet another suit of powered armor. This time, Saria steps in herself, taking it on bare-handed.
No matter how much the armor broke, it showed no reaction — as if there were no one inside at all.
Just as Saria's fist heads for the core, the Doctor shouts, "Stop, Saria!" With that single cry, Saria halts her hand.

blocks.cap: Through a gap in the broken armor, hair and a single eye come into view. That eye was unmistakably crying out for help.

blocks.x: What emerges from the armor is a person. The Doctor extends a hand and says, "Hello, Muelsyse."
Days after going missing, Muelsyse is alive. She had simply been unconscious, used as a weapon all along.
The one clue left by a now-conscious Muelsyse was a number etched into her water-soaked hem: '359.'
6. A Beautiful Paradise, and Unfamiliar Guests
Inside Base 359's lab, the standoff was still ongoing. Sani's group makes it through the lab door and finally comes face to face with the owner of the voice.
"Hello, everyone. I'm the head of Rhine Lab's Applied Originium Arts Division and the director of Experimental Base 359." Dorothy greets them with a smile.
Sani immediately levels his crossbow. He believes the silver creature Dorothy created grew by feeding on his fellow pioneers' blood.
Dorothy doesn't deny it. "Knowing the truth won't do you any good. The more you know, the more dangerous it gets for you." Instead, she holds out her hand and invites him to join the experiment.
Just as Sani is about to lower his weapon, an arrow flies toward Dorothy. The silver entities guarding the lab react all at once.
Unless we destroy this vile breeding ground, we can't take down those monsters!
Amid the chaos, Elena and Silence notice something else: the silver entities weren't attacking Dorothy at all — they were protecting her.
7. Not a Person, but a Voice
The truth is revealed in the deepest part of the lab. At the end of a tangle of cables, the missing pioneers lay in a row, wearing electrodes.

blocks.cap: Electrodes like plump flower buds glow atop the heads of the sleeping. Every last one of the missing was here.

blocks.x: Derek, Helen, Gale, Sofia…… every single one had been 'saved' by Dorothy. People who'd been on the verge of being cast out for failing to pay their insurance.

Dorothy had connected everyone to a massive machine called the 'Hub,' running an experiment to turn even people with low Originium Arts aptitude into Casters. She wanted to give equal opportunity to the Infected, to pioneers, to everyone born without talent.
Silence pushes back on that dream head-on. "Dozens, hundreds, thousands of people combining their strength? That would become a toy capable of crushing even the sturdiest fortress with ease."
Just then, Joyce, who had been unconscious, suddenly wakes. She speaks in an unfamiliar tone. "Don't hurt Director Franks." As if speaking on behalf of everyone asleep inside.
8. Betrayal, and a Long-Overdue Greeting
While the experiment is briefly halted, Rhine Lab's Defense Division staff burst in. But their goal isn't rescuing hostages — it's injecting every single pioneer with an unidentified substance.
Only then does Elena realize the truth: the 'emergency medical bracelet' issued to the pioneers was actually a surveillance-and-execution device. The reason Joyce had woken was this very bracelet.
Elena contacts her boss, Ferdinand. But what comes back isn't concern — just a cold order to withdraw.
In the end, Elena storms into Rhine Lab headquarters. Ferdinand tries to win her over with promises of a lab and a promotion, but……

blocks.cap: "Just shut your mouth." Elena's first words after stepping out of her role as an assistant.

blocks.x: Take your 'gift' and your phony kindness and get out of my sight. Ferdinand Clooney, you and I are through.
Meanwhile, Saria sneaks into Rhine Lab headquarters with Muelsyse to find the director, Christine. It's a reunion 1,497 days in the making.
"You've come back, Saria." "……Hello, Christine." A short greeting, but the years folded into it were anything but short.
9. What Filled the Sky
Ferdinand's plan had already entered its final stage. He releases the Transmission Substance across the entire experiment zone, forcing the experiment to completion.
Now is the only chance we'll get to seize this.
The lab building tears apart from the inside out. Through the rupture, a colossal silver geometric mass rises, swallowing the entire base.

blocks.cap: The silver mass, rising over the ruins, wraps the entire base in its embrace.

blocks.x: Even Dorothy holds her breath. It was a sight she had designed but never actually seen in person. "So beautiful…… but strangely terrifying too."
The monster's attacks grow faster and faster. In that moment when neither pioneer nor operator can do a thing, assistant Greyy walks out alone.
How can you know if there's a possibility, without ever taking the step forward?

blocks.cap: Greyy shoots light into the sky to draw the monster's gaze — output that surpassed all theoretical limits.

blocks.x: Ptilopsis joins in late to sustain Greyy's Arts. Together, if only for a moment, the two of them manage to pinpoint the monster's exact position.
10. The Story Mother Used to Tell
Behind a wall of sand, Dorothy injects the Transmission Substance into her own body. Silence's protests are of no use. "I want to hear their voices too."
On the other side of consciousness, she meets her childhood self. It was the fairy tale her mother used to tell — the story of a girl crossing the desert in search of a Caster.

blocks.cap: The girl from the fairy tale and her two friends. Wrapped in a Foulbeast's wings and Tin Man's tin shell, they cross the desert.

blocks.x: The fairy tale ends like this. The girl no longer searches for the legendary Caster. "As long as you're by my side, that's my new home."
In the dream, Dorothy also meets the people she trapped through her experiment. Everyone waves and greets her, but they all look somehow exhausted.
"I don't know if we'll ever find another place this good again……" "……No matter how far we run, tomorrow comes anyway, doesn't it?"
Dorothy reaches to wipe away the tears of a young pioneer friend, then pulls her hand back.

blocks.cap: "Your future isn't mine to hold." Dorothy's farewell to herself.

blocks.x: I shouldn't have kept you all trapped here. I shouldn't have……trapped myself here either. I'm sure the view you'll see later on will be far more wonderful than anything I've ever seen.
Outside the lab, the silver entity changes course. Its destination is none other than the very lab where it was born.

blocks.cap: The massive body collapses under its own weight. The moment Dorothy shatters her own dream with her own hands.

blocks.x: And so Ferdinand's experiment ends. The 'Hub' collapses, and he's left a failure, on the run.
11. Afterward

Dorothy was unharmed. But from that day on, the Transmission Substance still flowed through the pioneers' bodies. Neither Silence nor anyone else could say for certain what it would mean going forward.
Sani confesses the wrong he did to his childhood friend Mary four years ago. Mary answers not with words, but by pulling him into a tight embrace.
Enough talk about forgiveness and all that. Your childhood friend came to see you off — shouldn't we be doing something more meaningful?
Sani sets off once more with his pioneer band, carrying a suspicious invitation from someone whose name begins with D.
In his final moment, Ferdinand crosses paths with Ho'olheyak again. She neither kills nor helps him, just leaves behind a single suit of powered armor as she departs. "……Pioneer."
Left alone in the wastes, Ferdinand finally chooses to surrender. Before the pioneers surrounding him, he doesn't resist — for the first time.
Saria and Silence look up at the same sky from two different cities. A hundred kilometers apart, yet the two of them murmur the exact same words.
Looks like it's about to rain in Trimounts.

blocks.cap: Saria looks up at a sky gathering rain clouds. A hundred kilometers away, Silence is watching the very same sky.

blocks.x: On Christine's desk sits a fragment of the 'Hub's' remains, seemingly out of nowhere. Though the report reads 'completely destroyed.'
The still-mysterious Ho'olheyak leaves Christine with a brief word. "Before long…… I might just become the collaborator you need most."

blocks.cap: The night sky is unusually clear after the rain clears. The celestial globe her sister left for Elena begins to glow.

blocks.x: All three of the missing returned. But one stayed where she was, one set off on a new journey, and one, for the first time, walked out of the paradise she'd built herself.

They went to find missing coworkers, only to discover a paradise every one of them had walked into willingly. And the person who built that paradise chose, with her own hands, to set free the people she loved most.

act20side.tagline: Surface-dwellers on vacation get a distress call from an underground city, head down, and end up enjoying the water park before the crisis even crosses their minds. And a shut-in architect finally steps out through a broken door into the world.

chars.name: Gavial
chars.desc: The strongest warrior of the Akafuyara Tiacauh and a Rhodes Island Medic. Solves most problems by beating them with an axe — Operator Gavial

chars.name: Eunectes
chars.desc: Gavial's childhood friend, an engineer who lives and dies for robots. Turns every machine she meets into a 'friend' — Operator Eunectes

chars.name: Tomimi
chars.desc: Chief of the Akafuyara tribe 'Gavial's Will' and a Rhodes Island Operator. A cheerful warrior with a collecting habit — Operator Tomimi

chars.name: Elysium
chars.desc: Rhodes Island's all-purpose guide. Adapts fast anywhere and is very good at pretending to be sober even when drunk — Operator Elysium

chars.name: Inam
chars.desc: Grand chief of the Akafuyara and an emissary planted by the Sargon government. A realist with an uncanny head for business

chars.name: Stitch Canvas
chars.desc: An architect of Ceruercha. Suffers from Oripathy and loves nothing more than holing up at home — a minimalist

chars.name: Abdotiya
chars.desc: Ceruercha's Literature Representative. An immigrant who hides her past behind a Durin-style name

chars.name: Catch Lightrace
chars.desc: Ceruercha's Acting Design Representative. Stitch's longtime rival and closest friend

chars.name: Edge Earthheart
chars.desc: Ceruercha's Environment and Climate Representative. A nagging old-timer in charge of Originium prospecting

chars.name: Croc Diamondface
chars.desc: Ceruercha's Commerce Representative. An all-around MC hopelessly devoted to drink and spectacle

chars.name: Decouture Silvermint
chars.desc: Ceruercha's Industry Representative and robot manager. Nicknamed Decky

terms.name: Ceruercha
terms.desc: A Durin city beneath the Akafuyara jungle. Famous for its massive water park, the 'Great Aquafit'

terms.name: The Durin
terms.desc: A people who build their cities underground. They take it for granted that when disaster strikes, you abandon the city and build anew

terms.name: The Great Aquafit
terms.desc: A giant water park built by citizen vote in Ceruercha. Originally meant just to patch a hole in a collapsed dome

terms.name: The Originium Vein
terms.desc: An active Originium vein discovered in the strata near Ceruercha. No one knows when it might erupt

terms.name: Super Grand Elevator No. 1
terms.desc: The massive elevator connecting the Akafuyara surface to underground Ceruercha

terms.name: Strange Tales and Wondrous Theories
terms.desc: A surface-world magazine popular among the Durin. Most of what the Durin know about the surface comes from here

blocks.x: Deep beneath the Akafuyara jungle in Sargon lies a Durin city surface-dwellers have never once laid eyes on.

One day, the city's young architect **Stitch Canvas** comes up to Akafuyara asking for help, saying "the city is in crisis." Akafuyara's grand chief **Inam**, chief **Tomimi** back from Rhodes Island on vacation, and all-purpose guide **Elysium** head underground first, and once word reaches them late, Akafuyara's strongest warrior **Gavial** and her childhood friend **Eunectes** follow via a shortcut.
But what everyone finds upon arrival is neither a collapsed civilization nor a city in flames. If anything…… it's closer to an amusement park.
1. The Underground City Was Supposedly in Danger — Turns Out It Was an Amusement Park

Stitch had thoroughly scared the surface-dwellers on the way down. "Fireballs falling from the sky, giant mushrooms blotting out the sun," she'd said. Tomimi, Inam, and Elysium steeled themselves all night and sprinted down at full speed.

But when the elevator doors open, what greets them is a giant waterfall, a super spiral slide, and Durin joyfully splashing about. The 'crystallization of wisdom' dome Stitch had been so proud to show off? Nobody's even looking at it — everyone's eyes are glued to the slide behind it.

blocks.cap: Who knew a sight like this existed deep underground. First impression of the Durin city of Ceruercha: not a disaster, but a resort.

blocks.x: Elysium is already in a swimsuit, cheering; Tomimi's eyes light up at a Durin clothing shop. Drunk Durin are locked in a factional war between 'Old Karl Honey Wine' and 'Spirits Number Seven.'
Stitch, the one who caused all this commotion in the first place, cries out in dead earnest: "Not a single person here understands the aesthetics of simplicity!" No one is listening.
2. A Day That Started with a Welcome Vote and Ended in a Drinking Party

In Ceruercha, everything from streetlight color to visitor permits is decided by citizen vote. When design representative **Catch Lightrace** puts forth the motion of whether to welcome the surface-dwellers, everyone checks the glass in their hand first.

blocks.cap: "Raise your hand and show your will." In the middle of the standoff between the Honey Wine and Spirits factions, welcoming the surface-dwellers passes by majority vote.

blocks.x: The result: a near-unanimous pass. The reason is simple — the Durin make friends with strangers fast.

Tomimi gets a free swimsuit, Elysium gets drinks bought by two fan clubs at once, and Inam, in the middle of it all, sells commerce representative **Croc Diamondface** on a business idea: the Super Water Race.
"Do you Rhodes Island people pitch tents and sleep in elevators too?" Inam is baffled, but honestly, the surface-dwellers are adapting to Durin norms faster than expected.
3. Two People Take a Shortcut and Land Right on Top of a Stranger

Meanwhile, Gavial and Eunectes, following the high priest's guidance (or rather, orders to jump), take the shortcut by leaping straight into an underground river. Where they land happens to be right where Durin **Abdotiya** was quietly lost in thought.

Abdotiya, the Literature Representative, ends up ambushed mid-way through drafting the city's ad copy. "Surface-dwellers, so you've finally come." Her first words aren't a welcome — they're a warning.

Abdotiya soon appears before the two and declares to the citizens of Ceruercha: "We don't need help from you 'great surface-dwellers.'" Her nerve — not flinching even at the axe-wielding Gavial — only riles up the crowd of Durin even more.
Abdotiya, in fact, isn't originally Durin at all. Born in Ursus, she draws a firm line: "This is the home I chose." Why, she hasn't told anyone — yet.
4. The Rivals' Taste War: Simplicity vs. Extravagance
Stitch and Catch have been rivals and sworn enemies for years. Both competed over redesign proposals for the dome, Ceruercha's symbol, but neither could win the citizens over.
Catch, visiting Stitch for the first time in a while, shows off her new idea. "Complex geometric patterns, with yellow, green, red, and blue all clashing against each other!"

blocks.cap: "Like one big dazzling gem!" Catch's taste sat directly opposite Stitch's minimalism.

blocks.x: Stitch grimaces, but the moment the dome comes up, she snaps to attention and gets defensive. "Don't you dare touch that dome." They pretend not to give each other an inch, but it's plainly obvious each recognizes the other's skill.

Meanwhile, Eunectes meets the Durin's old assistant robot, unit 0429, and proposes, "Let's be friends." Robot manager **Decouture Silvermint** initially draws a line — "a robot is just a tool for work" — but slowly softens under Eunectes's warmth.
5. Paradise's Last Summer, and a New Landmark Rivalry

Stitch's real goal wasn't the dome at all, but the railway bridge. Fix the earthquake-severed railway and reinstall the Originium prospecting equipment, and the dome need never be touched. The problem: the railway bridge's spot is currently occupied by none other than the famous giant slide.

After a representatives' meeting, a conclusion is reached: blow up the slide, and let Stitch and Catch each build their own new landmark in its place. The citizens are actually delighted — "We were getting a little tired of it anyway."
Right before the demolition, Catch proposes everyone take a group photo.

blocks.cap: "Let's set the complicated stuff aside for now. Let's just have fun!" In front of the playground about to vanish, the noisiest group photo yet.

blocks.x: After the countdown, the slide quietly crumbles into the water. The two rivals then shake hands and formally declare war. "From here on, it's a real showdown."
6. A Swim Meet Turns Up an Actual Disaster

A preliminary survey of the railway bridge somehow morphs into a joint 'Ceruercha–Akafuyara–Rhodes Island Swim Meet.' The representative athletes: Gavial, Elysium, and, unexpectedly, a volunteer entrant, Abdotiya.

The race is heading smoothly toward its climax, when Stitch and **Edge Earthheart** crash in on a stolen boat, throwing everything into chaos. In the end, the out-of-control boat slams straight into the tunnel wall.

blocks.cap: As the price for the boat's rampage, the moment Stitch opens her eyes, two very familiar faces are already staring back.

blocks.x: But the collision reveals something unexpected. Beyond the wall, an active Originium vein. The true disaster that had been slowly waking for a long time was uncovered by pure accident, in the middle of a water park meltdown.
7. The City Blows Up in a Month, and Everyone's Worried About Interior Decorating
By Master Edge's calculations, the vein will erupt in about 25 days. The moment the broadcast goes out, the Durin……calmly start deciding what to pack for the move.

"We built the water park knowing this risk from the very start." For the Durin, disaster isn't unfamiliar. It's their tradition: when a city's destruction is certain, you dress it up in its finest before sending it off, rather than mourn it.

Coincidentally, a similar fuss was happening up on the surface in Akafuyara. Adakris Yuta, given a terminal diagnosis, had spent months building his own tomb-slash-monument, the 'Yutayuta Tower.' That stubbornness — wanting to meet the end at your very best — has something in common with the Durin, somehow.
The real problem comes next. There's not enough time to evacuate hundreds of thousands of people through the narrow tunnels, and it's too late to dig new shelters.
In the end, Gavial proposes: "Just widen the elevator, and everyone can go up to the surface together."
Something that's never happened once in Durin history. Abdotiya is the first to object. "What if we trust you and go to the surface, only to find an even crueler reality waiting for us?"
8. The Invincible Gavial Hesitates for the First Time in Her Life
For the surface migration to succeed, Akafuyara needs grounds to accept the Durin. Inam has long urged Gavial to take the seat of Amir (regional ruler).
"If you really don't want it, the Sargon court will send an Amir of their own. Then Akafuyara can't stay as free as it is now."
For Gavial, who left her homeland with the dream of studying medicine and treating Oripathy patients, this is a first — a choice where both paths are right, and both are what she wants.

blocks.cap: Before someone who always answered with her fists, a problem her fists can't solve, for the first time.

blocks.x: The moment Gavial hesitates on her answer, Tomimi steps forward first. "I'll become Amir!" Inam immediately volunteers too. "Better that I do it, since I've been here all along, than have Tomimi give up going back to Rhodes Island." In the end, the seat of Amir goes to Inam.
Your fist is much bigger than you think it is, Gavial. Because I'm here.
That the axe, and the friend, both belong to Gavial. A quiet, warm line.
9. The Architect in Hiding Gets Dragged Out Through a Broken Door
While Abdotiya prepares her speech for the square, Stitch stays holed up in her room, frozen over a blank page. In truth, there's a story she's told no one.

Eight years ago, Stitch survived an Originium explosion and contracted Oripathy, and former design representative Pinch Canvas took her in as an apprentice. But Pinch vanished without a word a few years back, leaving behind only one assignment: "come see me again only once you've replaced the dome with a design of my own."
Gavial knocks on the door, but Stitch doesn't answer.

blocks.cap: When words don't get through, some people just start breaking down the wall.

blocks.x: Gavial smashes clean through the door and wall to drag Stitch's true feelings out of her. "I've redrawn it over and over and it's never been chosen. Now even the sight of the dome scares me!" In front of the birdcage-like assignment her mentor left behind, Stitch had already been calling herself a failure.
Gavial swings her fist, telling Stitch to just take the one hit.

blocks.cap: Before a fist with nowhere left to run from, Stitch chooses, for the first time, not to dodge it.

blocks.x: Relax, my fist only hits bad guys. You're not a bad guy. You're just bad at using your head.
The fist actually lands not on Stitch, but on the desk. With that one blow that doesn't hurt, Stitch finally decides to let something go.
10. Pouring Out, in the Square, What She'd Never Told Anyone

At the same time, Abdotiya was standing at her own cliff's edge, for different reasons. To get the surface migration passed, the Durin's fear had to be removed — and to do that, she had to bring out her own past first.
Not Abdotiya Razorpen, but Abdotiya Nikolayevna Ivanova. The story of someone born into Ursus nobility who lost everything overnight and fled.

blocks.cap: "This is my past." A woman who has been Durin for so long finally speaks her real name for the first time.

blocks.x: The surface was never beautiful, she says calmly, but it wasn't as awful as they imagined either. And with that, the vote begins.

In the first vote, not a single hand goes up. But once Gavial and Stitch, arriving late, join the square, the mood shifts. In the revote, one hand, then two, then a flood of hands rise, one after another.

blocks.cap: Hands filling the square. For the first time in Durin history, the moment the decision to go to the surface is made.

blocks.x: That day, Catch hands the new design representative seat over to Stitch and leaves the city, saying she's going to search for her missing mentor Pinch.
Stitch stands before everyone for the first time and speaks in her own voice. "Let's go to the surface together."
Paradise Lost, and the Next Paradise

A few days later, Stitch is still out in the square, touching up the dome. Except this time, instead of hiding away, she's out there having full-blown arguments with passing Durin. Master Edge calls it 'saving time,' but everyone knows Stitch has changed.

blocks.cap: A city about to fall, spending its final days dressed in its finest.

blocks.x: A letter Pinch left behind is found at the top of the dome.
The biggest problem you ran into wasn't a design flaw or the difficulty of construction. It was simply…… persuading people.

Her mentor had known the answer all along. Catch, smiling, says that on the day the city is rebuilt, she wants to design something amazing again with Stitch. Abdotiya promises to return to Ceruercha after the rebuilding — not yet knowing that Croc has secretly sold her off as a business partner headed for Rhodes Island.
At last, the final elevator rises. As Gavial walks out carrying Stitch, the Durin and Tiacauh filling the entrance to the cave erupt in cheers all at once.

blocks.cap: On the day an entire city moved in, the entrance to Akafuyara had never been so lively.

blocks.x: Gavial raises her right hand in greeting. "The weather's nice today, so let's all head to the lake and have some fun!"

The underground city said to be in crisis turned out to be an amusement park, and the real crisis was only discovered by accident afterward. And the architect who'd been hiding, and the young woman playing dumb, both finally poured out their own stories in the middle of the square. And so the jungle's summer came to an end, swallowing an entire underground city whole.

act18side.tagline: A story about a real count crashing an unpaid audition. Two people who each carry a fragment of a dead tyrant's melody try to save one another — and in the end, only one survives.

chars.name: Ebenholz
chars.desc: A mysterious young noble. He barges into an unpaid audition and asks to duet on flute.

chars.name: Kreide
chars.desc: An aspiring cellist who has wandered from place to place with his frail grandfather. He entered the unpaid audition to earn money for medical bills.

chars.name: Hibiscus
chars.desc: A Sarkaz Operator dispatched by Rhodes Island. She has come to investigate the strange recovery seen among the Infected of Afterglow.

chars.name: Czerny
chars.desc: The only famous musician Afterglow has ever produced. Despite his failing health, he is preparing a farewell concert.

chars.name: Andante
chars.desc: A local Rhodes Island Operator stationed in Wiesenheim's Afterglow. She gets along easily with the Infected residents.

chars.name: Ursula
chars.desc: A distant relative of Czerny who handles the household. She loves to meddle and is quick to notice things.

chars.name: Gertrude
chars.desc: The lord of Wiesenheim and Czerny's longtime patron. She is putting unusual effort into this concert.

chars.name: Bigler
chars.desc: The owner of a café in Afterglow. He offers coffee with a friendly smile, but something about him feels off.

chars.name: Lava
chars.desc: Hibiscus's younger sister and fellow Infected. She is absorbed in her research on Originium Arts.

terms.name: The Worldly Note
terms.desc: A fragment of melody left behind by the Witch King. It lodges in the body of a select few, granting them the power of Arts — but at the cost of slowly consuming their body and mind.

terms.name: The Witch King
terms.desc: The tyrant of Leithanien who fell to the Twin Empresses twenty years ago. He was deeply versed in music, yet every piece he left behind was destructive.

terms.name: Afterglow
terms.desc: An Infected settlement within Wiesenheim. Once an industrial district, it was later set aside as a district for the Infected.

terms.name: Hall of the Red Sunset
terms.desc: A concert hall in Afterglow. It was originally built as an Arts fortress during the Witch King's era.

terms.name: Pseudo-Recovery
terms.desc: A state in which an Infected's body forcibly compensates for Oripathy damage, appearing outwardly fine. In reality, it's a sign the disease is progressing rapidly.

terms.name: Morning and Evening
terms.desc: Czerny's signature piece, written in memory of a friend who passed away in his youth. It is also the designated piece for this concert's audition.

terms.name: The Twin Empresses
terms.desc: The current rulers who drove out the Witch King and now govern Leithanien.

blocks.x: A strange rumor was going around Wiesenheim's Infected settlement, Afterglow: dying Infected were, one by one, growing stronger. At first listen, it sounded like good news.

Rhodes Island Operator Hibiscus came to investigate this 'recovery,' but the moment she arrived, a stranger among the Infected grabbed her for an impromptu waltz. Before she knew it, she found herself present when a mysterious young noble met a wandering cellist.
That both of them carried a fragment of the dead tyrant's melody within their bodies — and what would happen if those melodies ever met — no one knew yet.
1. A Noble Crashes an Unpaid Audition

Czerny is the only star musician Afterglow has ever produced. The audition for the ensemble piece at his farewell concert — held despite his failing health — offered no prize money at all, yet the line of applicants stretched out since dawn.

Andante, mentioning that it happened to be the day of Czerny's street performance, dragged Hibiscus to the front of the Hall of the Red Sunset. When Count Gertrude's servant appeared to try to stop the performance, Czerny shot back without hesitation.
Tell Gertrude to stop meddling in other people's business and worry about her own.

**Kreide**, the wandering cellist desperate for money to cover medical bills, found out too late but couldn't back down. He had promised his ailing grandfather he would only submit his application and come right back.
He submitted his application without even a cello, and a strange young noble standing nearby suddenly cut in.
The young man introduced himself as **Ebenholz** and asked to duet on flute, on the spot. For no reason other than a chance connection, an unregistered noble and a penniless cellist became a team.

blocks.cap: They'd practiced together for barely a day — how could their timing already be this in sync?

blocks.x: Of course, they failed the first audition spectacularly. Czerny spotted their habit of cutting corners with uncanny precision, and a bruised Ebenholz seethed with wounded pride. Still, thanks to Kreide covering for him, they earned a chance to try again.
2. The Demon Panic — Culprit: A Rhodes Island Operator

As it turned out, it was Hibiscus who stirred up Afterglow. A strange prophecy had been circulating among the Infected: 'When the demon sets foot in the twilight, worms will rise from the dark, and calamity will steal away the last sunlight.'
Hibiscus, being Sarkaz, was instantly branded that 'demon.' A drunken mob of Infected surrounded her, and Andante alone was hopelessly outnumbered.

blocks.cap: Kreide pushed through the crowd, and moments later, Ebenholz's flute calmed the chaos.

blocks.x: The only casualty was Kreide's cello, smashed to pieces — but when Czerny heard what happened, he actually opened up to them. At the next day's practice, one line from him cut straight to Ebenholz's core.
It's not that you lack skill — it seems more like you're insisting you simply don't want to.

Czerny's distant relative **Ursula** was there too. Thanks to this sharp-eyed old woman who ran the household, the state of Czerny's health — which everyone had been keeping quiet — slowly began to leak out.
3. Expedition to Glyphipatio — One Cello and a Spoonful of Friendship

They'd nearly cleared the pass line, but Kreide's cello was already junk. Ebenholz took him all the way to the noble district of Glyphipatio and bought him a top-of-the-line cello — even getting into an argument with a clerk who took issue with their clothes.
On the way back, Kreide brought up the Sankta teacher who had taught him cello. He said that since faces tend to blur with time, he chose one keepsake for each person he wanted to remember.
When asked what keepsake he'd choose for Ebenholz, Kreide laughed and said he hadn't decided yet. At the time, it was truly a trivial little promise.

Meanwhile, Hibiscus kept up her investigation and ran into more hostility. This time, café owner **Bigler** stepped in to smooth things over. His smile looked friendly enough, but something about him left no room for openings.

That evening, everyone gathered at the Rhodes Island office for dinner. With Ursula helping out and Hibiscus serving up a healthy meal, everyone had something to add. Kreide laughed that eating together like this felt just like family.

Hibiscus suddenly brought up her younger sister **Lava**, back on the mothership. She's bad at expressing herself and always causing some kind of trouble, Hibiscus said, but still a good kid at heart. At those words, Ebenholz quietly turned the word 'family' over in his mind.
4. Passing the Audition — But the Patron Seems Suspicious
After the retest, the two of them finally cleared Czerny's minimum bar. But behind the Hall of the Red Sunset, patron Count **Gertrude** was secretly preparing something with her subordinates.

That night, Gertrude quietly called Ebenholz — who was suffering from headaches — and dropped a truth on him: the unidentified melody inside his body, the 'Worldly Note,' was actually inside Kreide's body too.
If they caused a resonance through their duet and transferred the melody to Kreide, Ebenholz could shed his noble title and be free, she said. In exchange, Kreide would……
There's nothing to worry about. I'll take care of him in a suitable way.

Ebenholz couldn't answer on the spot. The word 'freedom' — something he'd never heard directed at himself before — was sweet, but the moment Kreide's name was mixed into it, his stomach turned all the more. All he could do was ask for time to think.
5. The True Cause of the Pseudo-Recovery Was Us All Along

Hibiscus's investigation pointed to the same place. The 'Pseudo-Recovery' — where the Infected looked fine outwardly while their disease progressed faster inside — had its point of origin in Kreide, and Ebenholz was the one amplifying it.
At Hibiscus's warning to leave at once, Ebenholz simply fled. Even knowing that cooperating with Gertrude's plan would make things easier, this time, he refused it.
As he fled, old memories burst open in his mind — starting from the moment at five or six years old when he was dragged to the old tower and told he was the last of the 'Witch King's' bloodline.

Every day he learned hymns praising Her Majesty the Empress, never allowed a single step outside the old tower, trained from childhood to wield that power with ever greater precision. The promise that he could live freely had been a lie from the very start.

blocks.cap: A childhood glimpsed through a crack in the door — for that child, 'freedom' had already been a trap laid in advance.

blocks.x: 6. There Was No Paradise Where He Fled
It was none other than Kreide who laid the unconscious Ebenholz down. From the sound of a distant cello, he recovered a melody he had forgotten.

blocks.cap: That familiar tune — where had he heard it before? A sound he couldn't lose even through the headache.

blocks.x: Only then did Kreide's memories return too — the experiments, the dead, the Witch King's remnants…… and now, this person before him.

Thinking back, Kreide too had wandered aimlessly since childhood. Ever since he learned that staying near anyone too long made their condition worse, he and his grandfather had drifted from town to town, working hard never to grow close to anyone.
Both of them were survivors of that horrific experiment. It's no one's fault, Ebenholz apologized over and over.
7. The Originium Slug Panic — The Cello's Last Audience

Wandering Afterglow alone, Ebenholz spotted the person spreading the prophecy and secretly tailed them into the sewers. There, the one who revealed his true identity was none other than Bigler — not a café owner, but a spy taking orders from the Empress.

blocks.cap: "Think of me as just a lowly spy." The two, crossing paths in an Originium Slug den, ended up on the same side by sheer accident.

blocks.x: After a scuffle, the mastermind got away, but the agitated swarm of Originium Slugs poured out of the sewers. Their gas, far more toxic to the Infected, began to blanket Afterglow. In the middle of that chaos, the sound of Kreide's cello was drawing the slugs in.
Kreide intended to burn away along with the swarm. If his very existence brought disaster, he thought, then that would be better.
Now that you're here…… I guess I won't get the chance, after all.
Ebenholz stopped him, all but pulling him into an embrace. And for the first time, the two of them laid bare their past lives to each other, holding nothing back.

A summer when he couldn't take off his soot-stained clothes for half a month. A day he nearly got locked in a basement. It was a night with nothing to apologize for and nothing to be apologized to over — simply a night of coming to know one another.
8. A Duel Fought With Sincerity on the Line

Once he'd collected himself, Ebenholz went straight to Gertrude to settle things. But the Hall of the Red Sunset had already been converted, top to bottom, into an amplification device — and the negotiation ended in a duel.

blocks.cap: "This time, I'm attacking for real, Miss Strolo!" Count against count — a genuine showdown.

blocks.x: Ebenholz won, but Bigler said there wasn't enough evidence to arrest Gertrude on the spot. That was when Kreide's grandfather appeared and produced a single stolen page of a plan.

It was written there that among the melody fragments the Witch King left behind from his experiments, the last remaining vessels were Ebenholz and Kreide. Czerny made up his mind on the spot: he would draw out both their melodies with a piece of his own composition, and if need be, offer up his own body as a third vessel.
Your sacrifice can sometimes become a burden to the other person, you know. Cry out, howl if you must, and claim the dignity that is rightfully yours!
The piece he completed through a sleepless night was titled 《Light and Shadow》. Coughing up blood, Czerny still didn't let go until the very last note.

blocks.cap: "It's done" — with those words, he collapsed over the piano.

blocks.x: 9. Light and Shadow — The First and Last Stage

blocks.cap: The next day, Czerny, supported by Hibiscus, barely managed to walk to the concert hall.
Flute, cello, piano. This might be the last time all three stood on stage together.

blocks.x: On the day of the concert, it turned out Gertrude had converted the entire hall into an amplification device. Her goal wasn't disruption — it was proof. Proof that this power was beyond anyone's control.
As the piece reached its climax, Kreide's melody spun out of control. While Hibiscus scrambled to evacuate the audience, Kreide decided to draw both melodies entirely into his own body.

blocks.cap: "Mr. Ebenholz, just follow my tempo."

blocks.x: As his body became covered in Originium crystals, Kreide began to transform into something resembling the Witch King — even as, in that voice, he announced the final piece.
We met again, became friends again, and even gave a wonderful performance on stage! So there's no way we can be unhappy, right?

blocks.cap: The final blow in the rain. Ebenholz pushed himself to his absolute limit.

blocks.x: Where the shell collapsed, only a fading Kreide remained. To the very end, he asked Ebenholz not to cry for him, but to smile.
Then you'll be able to stand tall and say — I lived another fulfilling day today…… like that.

Watching it all from a distance, Bigler muttered bitterly to himself. He'd always thought friendship and sacrifice were nothing but childish wordplay — but at least, he thought, there would be someone to cry for these three.
Afterward, Afterglow

Gertrude fell into the trap she herself had dug and was caught by the spy, Bigler. The revenge she'd finally grasped after fifteen years of being manipulated became, in the end, the rope that tightened around her own neck. Instead of arresting Kreide's grandfather, Bigler took him to his own café and treated him to a cup of coffee.

Afterglow was allowed to remain as it was, thanks to a word from the Empress, and Czerny, having barely survived, began a new fight on behalf of the Infected. Andante, meanwhile, began considering a transfer in the wake of it all.
Back on the mothership, Hibiscus was met with a scolding from her sister Lava first thing. Seeing Lava tear up over how reckless she'd been, Hibiscus had no choice but to promise to be more careful.
Count Urtica, Ebenholz, disappeared. In his place, an Operator named 'Ebenholz' joined Rhodes Island.

blocks.cap: His passport, the count's death certificate, Kreide's documents — he burned them all, and picked up the cello.

blocks.x: Afterglow was originally named for how the Infected's remaining time was as brief as a sunset's glow. But now, hearing this name, it sounds instead like a promise that those who remain will go on playing music for those who have died.
And that music was something that could begin again anywhere, as long as there was one cello and one old flute.

act17side.tagline: A story where the son of parents executed by the Inquisition for the crime of being Ægir alone relights the lighthouse they died for. And a night when no one can quite answer the name the sea keeps calling.

chars.name: Jordi
chars.desc: An Ægir youth born and raised in Granfaro. Working as a chapel caretaker, he has lived pretending not to know the mission his parents left sleeping beneath the lighthouse.

chars.name: Kal'tsit
chars.desc: A doctor of Rhodes Island. Even under the Iberian Inquisition's watch, she quietly lays out her own plans alone.

chars.name: Irene
chars.desc: The young disciple of Grand Inquisitor Dario. Her conviction to protect Iberia with sword and lantern is unshakeable.

chars.name: Elysium
chars.desc: An Iberian affiliated with Rhodes Island who has long lingered at the Granfaro chapel. Self-proclaimed handsomest man around.

chars.name: Skadi
chars.desc: Second Battalion Commander of the Abyssal Hunters. A woman of few words who never hesitates to protect her comrades.

chars.name: Gladiia
chars.desc: Chief war planner of the Abyssal Hunters. She coldly pursues any way back to Ægir.

chars.name: Specter
