chars.desc: A member of the Abyssal Hunters. Her lost memories return, little by little, whenever she meets the sea wind.

chars.name: Tiago
chars.desc: Granfaro's village elder, and the man who raised Jordi. Ever since the Inquisition took his lover from him, he has lived resenting the sea.

chars.name: Dario
chars.desc: Iberia's Grand Inquisitor. Irene's mentor, who volunteered for this mission to Granfaro.

chars.name: Ulpianus
chars.desc: The long-missing Fourth Captain of the Abyssal Hunters. Only rumors persist that he wanders the mainland alone, chasing a different answer.

terms.name: Granfaro
terms.desc: A remote fishing village on the Iberian coast, home to the descendants of laborers who once staked their lives on rebuilding the lighthouse.

terms.name: Eye of Iberia
terms.desc: The only lighthouse still working among those built by the Ægir engineer Breogán. Left abandoned since the Hush.

terms.name: Stultifera Navis
terms.desc: The flagship of the Iberian fleet, believed lost sixty years ago. Breogán was involved in its design.

terms.name: the Hush
terms.desc: The catastrophe that struck Iberia some sixty years ago. Ever since, Ægir people have been persecuted as 'islanders.'

terms.name: Sea Terror / Seaborn
terms.desc: Names for the lowly monsters called Sea Terrors that rise from the sea, and their higher-evolved form, the Seaborn, who learn language and come to resemble humans.

terms.name: Glowmark
terms.desc: A phosphorescent trace the sea leaves on land. Wherever it touches slowly turns into a Sea Terror habitat.

terms.name: Abyssal Hunters
terms.desc: Ægir's scouting unit made up of Gladiia, Skadi, and Specter. They roam the mainland searching for a way home to Ægir.

terms.name: Isha-mla
terms.desc: A name and being the Seaborn speak of in unison. No one has yet confirmed what it truly is.

blocks.x: There used to be lighthouses along the Iberian coast. Once numbering in the dozens, nearly all of them collapsed after the catastrophe called the Hush — now, only one remains.
Beneath that last lighthouse lies a small village called Granfaro. The Inquisition forgot this village, and Ægir people are still hunted here for the simple crime of being 'islanders.'

blocks.cap: Are you on this side of the wall, or beyond the forest? A strange exchange between Kal'tsit and an old man at a worn well.

blocks.x: **Jordi**, an Ægir youth raised in Granfaro, has spent his days as a chapel caretaker without ever knowing why his parents vanished beneath that lighthouse.
**Kal'tsit** of Rhodes Island has watched over this village for a long time, believing that the last chance to reconcile Iberia with Ægir across the sea is hidden in this quiet town.
1. A Suspicious Visitor Who Won't Leave the Chapel

blocks.cap: Granfaro's one and only main street. The day a rock band walked into town.

blocks.x: **Tiago**, the village elder who raised Jordi like a father, always warns him to be wary of the sea. Then a self-proclaimed handsome Iberian, **Elysium**, shows up at the chapel and simply refuses to leave.
Claiming to belong to Rhodes Island, he says he's waiting for someone but won't reveal who. Jordi finds him strange, and even a little enviable.
Elysium keeps bragging that he's the most handsome man in Iberia. Every time Jordi looks at him, a laugh slips out before he can stop it.
Then one day, a dead Sea Terror turns up in the village square — a sign that this long-quiet coast is stirring again.
Old fisherman Pedri had actually been talking about 'sea monsters' for years. Everyone used to write it off as drunken rambling, but this time, no one laughs.
2. Alive Until Sunset and the Abyssal Hunters
As it turns out, followers of the Deep Sea Church were already living hidden in the village, moving quietly to avoid the Inquisition's eyes, waiting for the day they could reclaim the lighthouse.
Kal'tsit arrives, and one by one the rock band **Alive Until Sunset** — Alti, Aya, Frost, Dan — and the Ægir scouting unit **Abyssal Hunters** — Skadi, Gladiia, Specter — gather in Granfaro.

Elysium turns out to have been Rhodes Island's eyes and ears, watching this village on Kal'tsit's behalf. Once the group assembles, he hands out communicators of his own Originium Arts make to the Hunters.
Kal'tsit gets back a key she'd once entrusted to the band long ago — an item left behind by an Ægir engineer named Breogán, though no one can guess what it's for.
Meanwhile, it comes out that Grand Inquisitor **Dario** and his disciple **Irene** have also slipped into the village, and in an instant Granfaro becomes a battleground for every faction imaginable.
3. What the Village Elder Was Hiding

blocks.cap: The first sea a four- or five-year-old Jordi ever saw was pitch black.

blocks.x: When rumors spread that an Inquisitor has appeared, Tiago tries to send Jordi away. In doing so, he finally tells the truth — Jordi's parents disappeared trying to revive this lighthouse, and Granfaro once nearly became a fortress for rebuilding Iberia.
But Jordi refuses to leave. Standing before the black sea he's seeing for the first time in his life, he says he wants to see his parents' place with his own hands.
Old blueprints and photographs his parents left behind still sit piled up in Jordi's house. Not fully understanding their meaning, he had always thought of them as nothing more than old belongings.

Granfaro, it turns out, was a dream half a century ago — laborers who wanted to build a coastal defense line against the Catastrophe. Jordi's grandparents and parents, two generations, left for the lighthouse chasing that dream and never came back.

In truth, ever since the Inquisition took his lover **Marin**, Tiago had known about the Deep Sea Church followers and turned a blind eye. That silence is now driving the whole village to the edge of a cliff.
4. The Inquisition's Lantern Burns the Village

Grand Inquisitor Carmen lands and begins mercilessly cutting down the Deep Sea Church followers. Kal'tsit joins hands with Carmen, asking for a path opened to the lighthouse in exchange for the Hunters' strength in cleaning up the village.
Terrified of being branded heretics, some villagers start informing on each other. Even those who were neither Inquisition nor cultist get shaken loose in the panic.

Because he might be a descendant of Breogán, Jordi is spared execution and instead put on the Inquisition's expedition. Meanwhile, the unconscious Tiago, attacked by cultists, is slowly turning into a monster.
By the time Carmen arrives, it's already too late. The dying Tiago asks only for the truth about Marin, the Ægir woman the Inquisition killed, and dies before he even hears the full answer.
Only then does Carmen tell the truth. Sixty years ago, half of the Ægir people captured in Granfaro really were cultists — and their leader was 'Marin' herself. Jordi has no words left.
5. The Boy Who Became the Lighthouse Keeper

blocks.cap: The Eye of Iberia, revealed through the sea mist. For most of them, it's the first time seeing it in sixty years.

blocks.x: Dario, Irene, and Jordi land at the lighthouse, now a Sea Terror den. With nothing but an old blueprint his parents left behind, Jordi starts bringing the control room back to life.
Jordi keeps telling himself he's just an ordinary caretaker, but his hands never stop. Piece by piece, he revives the lighthouse's heart.
Dario guards the door alone, without a single subordinate, buying the two of them time. By the moment the lantern lights again, he has already spent his very last strength.
You must die so humanity may live. This isn't our law or our scripture — it's the 'survival of the fittest' you people speak of.

blocks.cap: By the time Carmen arrived, Dario had already breathed his last, but the lantern was still burning.

blocks.x: Thanks to the signal Jordi revived, the location of the Iberian flagship Stultifera Navis, missing for sixty years, is finally revealed. Carmen quietly tells herself that the lantern her disciple's mentor protected was not lit in vain.
6. The Ship That Never Sank in Sixty Years

blocks.cap: Far off, a golden ship no one had ever searched for floats alone.

blocks.x: The Abyssal Hunters and Irene board the Stultifera Navis. The ship is strangely spotless, and piano music drifts through corridors that should hold no one.
The ship's owner is still alive — **Captain Alfonso**, who insists he's still human even as he turns Seaborn, and his lover **Garcia**, who can no longer speak properly.

blocks.cap: When the bell rings, the hunt begins. Alfonso has guarded this ship this way for sixty years.

blocks.x: For sixty years, Alfonso has called this golden hall 'Iberia' every single day and stared into a mirror. His days were a repeating cycle: hunt, then return to the mirror.
He once dreamed, together with Garcia, watching children on the Iberian coast — a dream where he said 'these children are our future.' Now it visits him only rarely.
Garcia, who has already lost her voice, writes with Sea Terror blood to talk with Alfonso. Even that is a conversation running out of time.
But the moment Alfonso lays eyes on Skadi, there's something else on his mind. "They called you Isha-mla." No one explains what that name means.
If you really did come from the old Iberia, tell me who's the pontiff of the state church now. Carmen? ...Ha, a saint. Isn't he ashamed at all?
7. The Faces Met at the Bottom of the Ship
Deep in the ship's hold, Gladiia comes face to face with someone unexpected — **Ulpianus**, the Fourth Captain of the Abyssal Hunters long believed missing.

Ulpianus says that at the very depths of that den, he saw something he can only call a 'building.' The rest of the First and Fourth Battalion members who went with him all died on the spot; only he survived, hiding inside the body of something dead and enormous.
Since then, he's left the unit to search alone for a different answer. Now he lets slip an ominous guess about Skadi's true nature as well.

blocks.cap: So it really was you. Gladiia levels her spear at her old comrade-in-arms.

blocks.x: "If something goes wrong, we have to kill Skadi." Ulpianus says it flatly. Gladiia doesn't want to believe it, but she can't find grounds to refute it either.
Meanwhile, also aboard the ship — the one behind selling Jordi out to the Inquisition, **Amaia**, who'd been posing as a translator — had already arrived first. She's known Specter for a long time.
8. When a Sleeping Name Comes Back

blocks.cap: A last dance with Amaia. Specter, who kept telling her to focus on the steps, suddenly raises her weapon.

blocks.x: Specter buys time dancing with Amaia, step by step recovering old memories of wanting to be a sculptor — a broken white statue, and even the unfamiliar gesture of 'prayer' she once made before it.
Amaia reveals a cruel truth: the Seaborn blood in Specter's body has actually been holding back an Originium sickness that was slowly consuming her.
But sensing danger, Amaia flees, leaving Irene — whom she'd captured in Jordi's place — in the care of a Seaborn she's tamed.

blocks.cap: Devour me. The Seaborn kneels at Amaia's feet.

blocks.x: Sacrifice isn't sacred. Devotion isn't a virtue. Life is never disorder — that's the last answer I tried to find in the sea.
Leaving only those words, Amaia vanishes along with the Seaborn who follows her.
9. Those Who Wouldn't Abandon Ship
The ship begins collapsing uncontrollably. Alfonso makes his final stand, staking his life against the very Seaborn he's hunted for sixty years.
In the chaos, Garcia — saying she'll die human at least in her final moment — embraces that Seaborn and sinks into the deep sea. "I will die fighting as an Iberian." Those words become her last.
Go, I'll stay here. This is my Iberia. I can't betray my own ship.
Alfonso sits on his throne and closes his eyes. Recalling his homeland's sunlight, the warmth of Garcia's hand, the laughter of old friends one last time, he takes his own life with a rusted blade.
10. A Sinking Ship, a Name With No Answer

blocks.cap: The golden ship sinks after nearly a century of silence.

blocks.x: As it turns out, Jordi had shaken off Kal'tsit's warnings and taken an old boat out to sea alone. When the Sea Terrors swarmed in, he relied on nothing but the lighthouse's guardian Mon3tr, pushing forward on a course full of errors.
Thanks to the small boat Jordi brought, only Irene is barely rescued. Irene also learns that the Stultifera Navis has actually been circling for sixty years directly above an Ægir refugee city.
In truth, ever since the moment Alfonso first spoke that name, Skadi has been forcing herself to stay calm. She'd already guessed the answer herself, but couldn't bring herself to say it out loud.
The Seaborn chasing the ship to the very end asks Skadi one last thing as it dies — whether she isn't the very being they call 'Isha-mla.'
Skadi says no. But the hand rubbing the back of her neck won't stop trembling.
Uimani will be eternal.
Afterward

blocks.cap: Come on up! Irene grabs the hand Jordi holds out.

blocks.x: The Inquisition's tents go up in Granfaro. Irene steps down from her post as Inquisitor, while Jordi agonizes over whether to become 'the first Ægir to join the Inquisition.'
Only victims are said to deserve to be called 'great.' But if it's a person's will that can light this lantern, then I don't think we're so different after all.

Elysium decides to stay with Rhodes Island, while the Abyssal Hunters set off again, without so much as a ship, to find a way back to Ægir. Ulpianus is still out there alone, chasing a different answer.
Kal'tsit tells Gladiia one last thing — that the sea has already become as important a problem as Originium itself, and there's no more time to waste.

blocks.cap: From beneath the sinking ship, a single beam of light spilled out. Irene never manages to ask what it was.

blocks.x: The lighthouse burns bright again after all this time. But no one yet fully knows the answer that light points to — not Skadi, not Jordi, and not whatever it is beneath the sea that still keeps calling that name.

act16side.tagline: 미아 소녀 하나 주웠다가 인생 최대 사고를 쳐버린 신입 집행자, 그리고 천 년 만에 처음 울려버린 종 이야기. 구원이 없어도 걷기로 하는 사람들의 집념에 관한 이야기예요.

chars.name: 체첼리아
chars.desc: 여덟 살, 세상 밖을 궁금해하는 산크타·살카즈 혼혈 소녀

chars.name: 에젤
chars.desc: 정시 퇴근이 인생 목표인 수습 집행자, 그런데 오지랖은 라테라노 제일

chars.name: 피아메타
chars.desc: 라테라노의 리베리 호위, 별명 '미광의 야경꾼'을 죽어라 싫어한다

chars.name: 모스티마
chars.desc: 만사 귀찮아하면서도 계속 라테라노로 돌아오는 타천사

chars.name: 르무엔
chars.desc: 왕년의 명사수, 지금은 휠체어와 서류 업무 사이 어딘가

chars.name: 파티아
chars.desc: 이베리아 출신 리베리, 산크타를 신처럼 떠받드는 리베리가 제일 싫다

chars.name: 오렌
chars.desc: 능글맞고 계산 빠른 만국 전달자, 다 도와주는 척하며 실속은 혼자 챙긴다

chars.name: 벨리브
chars.desc: 젊은 추기경, 계산이 빠르고 뒤끝도 길다

chars.name: 페데리코
chars.desc: 감정 교감을 하지 않는 산크타 집행자, 원칙대로만 움직인다

chars.name: 로셀라
chars.desc: 노래를 좋아하는 살카즈 여성, 체첼리아에게 유독 다정하다

chars.name: 안도아인
chars.desc: 라테라노를 떠나 오래 방랑하다 돌아온 인물, 스스로를 '선도자'라 부른다

chars.name: 교황
chars.desc: 슈크림빵을 좋아하는 라테라노의 최고 지도자, 보기보다 훨씬 능구렁이

terms.name: 라테라노
terms.desc: 산크타의 신성 도시. 만국 전달자 제도로 각국과 교류해온 사실상의 중립국이에요.

terms.name: 만국 전달자
terms.desc: 교황이 세운 특수 부대. 각국을 오가며 정보를 전달하는 라테라노의 외교관들이에요.

terms.name: 계시의 석탑
terms.desc: 라테라노에서 가장 오래됐다는 탑. 전설 속 성종이 걸려 있다고 전해져요.

terms.name: 패스파인더
terms.desc: 안도아인을 따르는 이교 조직. 라테라노가 외면한 이들을 거둔다고 주장해요.

terms.name: 수호총
terms.desc: 산크타가 성인식을 거쳐 받는 개인 총. 계율과 존재 자체가 깊이 얽힌 상징물이에요.

terms.name: 광륜
terms.desc: 산크타 고유의 머리 위 빛. 신앙과 교감 상태를 드러내는 표식이에요.

terms.name: 산크타·리베리·살카즈
terms.desc: 라테라노엔 광륜과 계율을 지닌 '산크타', 같은 도시에 살아도 그 은총은 없는 '리베리', 그리고 오랜 원수 '살카즈'가 있어요.

blocks.x: 라테라노는 산크타들의 신성 도시예요. 계율이 삶의 전부인 이곳이, 이번엔 사상 최초로 테라 각국의 사절을 불러모아 '만국 정상회담'을 열려고 해요.
그런데 이 도시, 계율은 철저히 지키면서 건물 폭파는 신고만 하면 합법이에요. 그런 뻔뻔한 봄날, 도시 어느 골목에서는 여덟 살 **체첼리아**가 아픈 엄마 곁을 지키고 있었어요.
엄마는 죽기 직전 딸에게 딱 한 가지만 신신당부해요. '제복 입은 사람한테는 절대 다가가지 마.' 하지만 정작 체첼리아를 주워가는 사람은, 다름 아닌 그 '제복'이었습니다.
라테라노의 흔한 하루: 폭발은 합법, 사람은 이미 죽어 있었다
이 도시엔 매일 밤 한 남자가 병실을 다녀가요. 휠체어에 앉은 여자는 늘 모른 척, 창밖만 보고요. 둘 사이엔 할 말도, 하지 말아야 할 말도 이미 정해져 있는 것 같아요.

blocks.cap: 책상 위엔 아이리스 한 다발. 창밖 그림자는 끝내 이름을 밝히지 않았어요.

blocks.x: 한편 거리에서는 라테라노 시민들이 기념비적인 기둥을 신나게 폭파하는 중이었어요. 신고도 했고 대피도 시켰고, 심지어 공증소 집행자를 증인으로 세웠으니 완벽하게 '합법'이라나요.
그 소동을 구경하던 컬럼비아 거상을 구해준 건 리베리 **피아메타**와 타천사 **모스티마**였어요. 진짜 문제는 그다음, 대성당에서 벌어져요.
라이타니엔 공작 부인이 행패를 부리자 만국 전달자 **오렌**이 태연하게 맞받아치고, 추기경 **벨리브**가 뒷수습에 나서요. 오렌은 능글맞고 계산적인데, 어쩐지 미워할 수가 없는 타입이에요.

blocks.cap: 만국 전달자 — 교황이 세운 특수 부대. 30년째 각국 사이를 오가며 라테라노의 중립을 지켜왔어요.

blocks.x: 벨리브는 오렌을 딱히 좋아하지 않아요. 그런데도 처벌하겠다며 데려간 별실에서, 둘은 또 티격태격 잘만 지내요. 교황은 그 광경을 보며 그저 웃을 뿐이에요.
같은 날 아침, 스테보누스 구역에서는 **페데리코**라는 원칙주의 집행자가 조용히 시신 한 구를 발견해요. 유언도, 유언 등록도 없는 독신 여성 — 이름은 페오리아.
장례 절차가 진행되는 그 시각, 옆집엔 아무도 모르는 여덟 살짜리 아이가 혼자 남아 있었어요. 엄마 말대로 나가지도, 들키지도 않은 채로요.
수습 집행자, 미아 소녀 주웠다가 인생 최대 사고를 치다
체첼리아는 결국 참지 못하고 거리로 나섰다가 쓰러져요. 아이를 병원에 데려간 사람이 바로 수습 집행자 **에젤**이에요 — 정시 퇴근이 인생 목표인데, 정작 오지랖은 라테라노에서 제일 넓어요.
에젤은 검사 결과를 보고 소름이 돋아요. 이 여덟 살 아이, 분명 산크타인데 고통도 초조함도 전혀 느껴지지 않거든요. 산크타끼리는 서로 감정을 나눌 수 있어야 하는데 말이죠.

blocks.cap: '광륜이…… 어째서……' 에젤이 처음으로 뭔가 잘못됐다는 걸 눈치챈 순간이에요.

blocks.x: 그 사이 병실엔 수상한 간호사가 등장해요. 정체는 리베리 **파티아** — 체첼리아를 '동료'라 부르며 데려가려 하죠. 이베리아 출신인 그녀는, 산크타를 신처럼 모시면서도 자각 못 하는 라테라노 리베리들을 가장 싫어해요.
에젤은 미리 열어둔 창문으로 시간을 벌고, 체첼리아를 업고 도망쳐요. **약속했거든요. 엄마를 찾아주겠다고.**
그런데 그 죽은 여자, 이름이 낯익다
체첼리아를 따라 옛 동네를 헤매던 에젤은 아이 입에서 나온 엄마 이름을 듣고 얼어붙어요. 페오리아 — 오늘 아침 자신이 시신을 확인한, 바로 그 여자였거든요.
한편 교황청은 이미 사건을 접수하고 사람을 보냈어요. 리베리 호위 피아메타가 직접 나서죠.
병원에서 마주친 파티아에게 그녀는 옛정을 걸고 충고해요. '지금 멈추면 아직 늦지 않았어.'
파티아는 코웃음을 쳐요. '산크타의 계율이 리베리한테 제대로 적용된 적 있어?' 피아메타는 대꾸하지 않아요. 대신 총을 겨눠요.

blocks.cap: 같은 시각, 대성당 밖 병실. 타천사 모스티마와 옛 명사수 **르무엔**이 이 소동의 배후 정보를 주고받고 있었어요.

blocks.x: 르무엔은 휠체어 재활 중인 왕년의 사격왕이에요. 5년 전 사고로 이렇게 됐다는데, 정작 본인도 모스티마도 그 얘기만 나오면 묘하게 말을 아껴요.
산크타이면 안 되는 산크타, 그리고 계산이 빠른 남자
결국 에젤은 만국 전달자 오렌과 마주쳐요. 오렌이 순순히 답해줘요 — 체첼리아는 산크타와 살카즈의 혼혈, '되지 말았어야 할 산크타'라고. 원래 이런 혼혈은 부모 한쪽의 종족을 그대로 물려받아야 하는데, 얘는 예외라는 거죠.
오렌의 속내는 따로 있어요. 체첼리아라는 존재 자체가 협상 카드라는 거예요. **'넌 라테라노를 위험에 빠트릴 수도 있어.'** 폭력은 취향이 아니라면서도, 눈빛은 이미 계산기를 두드리고 있어요.
도망치던 길, 노래를 부르던 솜사탕 장수의 뿔은 가발이 아니었어요. 라테라노 한복판에 살카즈가 잠입해 있었던 거죠. 정체가 들통나자 그녀는 도망치는 대신, 동료를 지키려 몸을 던져요.
에젤은 그날 밤, 이 일에서 발을 뺄까 수도 없이 고민해요. 그냥 반듯한 직장에 다니며 제시간에 퇴근하는 삶이 더 낫지 않을까 하고요. 하지만 이미 체첼리아와 약속을 해버렸어요.
위령성당에 온 손님이 하필 지명수배범이라니
체첼리아는 엄마와 제대로 작별하고 싶다며 위령성당에 며칠 머물길 청해요. 그런데 거기, 수도사들도 깍듯이 '선도자님'이라 부르는 수상한 인물이 있어요.
**'나는 이름으로 불러도 괜찮아. 안도아인**이라고.' 그는 자신을 라테라노가 외면한 이들의 문을 열어주는 존재라 소개해요. 버려진 자, 망가진 자, 살카즈들의 편이라고요.
그리고 어제의 그 솜사탕 장수 **로셀라**가 다시 나타나요. 체첼리아에게 살카즈의 고향 카즈델 이야기를 들려주고, 아빠가 왜 라테라노에 못 오는지도 어렴풋이 알려줘요. '사랑하는 게 죄는 아니잖아.'
에젤은 이 상황이 몹시 불편해요. 도리에 어긋난다고 생각하면서도, 정작 안도아인의 말을 반박할 수가 없어요.
체첼리아는 로셀라 옆에서 처음으로 마음 편히 웃어요. 아빠의 뿔을 닮은 그 검은 뿔을 보면서, 언젠가 살카즈들의 고향에도 가볼 수 있지 않을까 상상해요.
8년 전, 웃지 않은 사람들
밤마다 병문안을 오는 모스티마와 피아메타에게 르무엔이 묻지도 않은 이름을 흘려요. '안도아인, 그 사람 만났구나.' 피아메타의 표정이 굳어요.
8년 전, 이 셋과 안도아인은 한 소대였어요. 평범한 소탕 작전 중 피아메타만 잠시 지원을 나갔다 돌아왔을 때, 모든 게 이미 늦어 있었죠. 안도아인은 사라졌고, 르무엔은 5년을 혼수상태로 보냈어요.
그 녀석 얘기를 할 때마다, 너희 태도는 꼭 주말 피크닉 얘기하는 것 같네.

blocks.cap: 감동적인 재회 같은 건 여기 없어요. 그냥 8년째 낫지 않은 상처예요.

blocks.x: 장례식 날, 피아메타와 모스티마는 결국 안도아인을 코앞까지 몰아붙여요. 하지만 그는 '아직 때가 아니다'라는 말만 남기고 유유히 사라져 버려요.
몸싸움은 오렌이, 기적은 체첼리아가 만들다
안도아인은 체첼리아 곁을 떠나기로 해요. 아이에겐 이미 '해야 할 일'이 생겼다면서요.
대신 체첼리아는 혼자 라테라노의 옛 시계탑, '계시의 석탑'으로 향해요. 엄마에게 마지막 인사를 하러요.
가는 길을 막아선 건 오렌이었어요. 카드로 쓸 아이를 순순히 보낼 순 없다는 거죠. 이번엔 에젤이 몸으로 막아서고, 여기에 페데리코가 나타나 오렌의 만국 전달자 직위 해제를 선언해요.

blocks.cap: '저항하지 말고, 저와 함께 제1청으로 가시죠.' 뒤끝 하나 없이 담백한 체포예요.

blocks.x: 그사이 체첼리아는 탑 꼭대기에서 엄마가 가르쳐준 살카즈의 옛 노래를 부르기 시작해요. 서툰 가사, 앳된 목소리로요.

blocks.cap: 그러자 천 년 동안 단 한 번도 울리지 않았던 계시의 석탑 종이, 처음으로 울렸어요.

blocks.x: 영웅은 곧 출발한다, 멀리 향한 길을 따라 자신의 꿈을 실현하러 떠난다…… 아무리 먼 길일지라도, 반드시, 반드시.
이 노래는 이야기 내내 세 번 반복돼요. 부르는 사람도, 이유도 매번 다른데, 뜻은 늘 하나예요. 무사히 돌아오길 바라는 마음이죠.
교황과 방랑자, 신 앞에서 나란히 방아쇠를 당기다
종이 울리자마자 안도아인은 곧장 교황을 만나러 가요. 대성당 안, 두 사람만 남은 자리에서 오래 묵힌 질문이 터져 나와요. 라테라노는 정말 사람을 구원할 수 있는가.
안도아인은 자신의 고향, 로카마레아 이야기를 꺼내요. 라테라노에 도움을 청했지만 '너는 우리 편이지만 그들은 아니다'라는 답만 들었고, 돌아갔을 땐 마을이 통째로 사라져 있었다고요.
낙원이 낙원이라 불리는 까닭은, 바깥의 광야가 너무나도 춥기 때문이다.
말이 안 통하자 두 사람은 동시에 방아쇠를 당겨요. 그런데 둘 다 무사해요. 계율을 어기면 타락한다는 그 법칙 자체가, 지금 이 자리에서 시험대에 올라요.

blocks.cap: 총소리는 거의 동시에 울렸어요. 하지만 승부는 나지 않았어요.

blocks.x: 교황은 안도아인을 대성당 지하, 성인들의 뼈가 잠든 곳보다 더 깊은 곳으로 데려가요. 그곳엔 어떤 책에도 기록되지 않은 무언가가 있었어요.

blocks.cap: '이건 해석이나 믿음의 문제가 아니다. 그저, 이런 방식으로 존재한다.'

blocks.x: 8년 전, 그 빗속에서 무슨 일이 있었나
피아메타, 모스티마, 르무엔, 그리고 안도아인 — 넷은 한 소대였어요. 그날은 그냥 평범한 소탕 작전이었어요. 상대는 살카즈 약탈자 무리, 그런데 이상하게도 적의가 없었대요.
피아메타가 잠깐 지원 요청을 받고 자리를 비운 사이, 안도아인은 대신 그들을 도우러 갔어요. 돌아왔을 땐 이미 모든 게 늦어 있었죠. 르무엔은 총에 맞아 쓰러졌고, 안도아인은 타천사가 되어 사라졌어요.

blocks.cap: 그날 피아메타가 기억하는 건 딱 두 가지예요. 차가운 빗물, 그리고 안도아인의 검은 뿔.

blocks.x: 그 뒤로 8년, 피아메타는 안도아인을 쫓는 데 인생을 썼어요. 복수를 하고 싶어서가 아니라, 그냥 이 일의 끝을 두 눈으로 보고 싶어서요.
모스티마도 르무엔도 그날 이후 이상하리만치 담담해요. 마치 이미 다 이해하고 받아들인 사람들처럼요. 피아메타 눈엔 그게 더 낯설고 서운해요.
집념이라 쓰고 우정이라 읽는다
안도아인이 지상으로 돌아오자 피아메타와 모스티마가 기다리고 있었어요. 그런데 안도아인의 상태가 이상해요. 신념도 참회도 아닌 말들을 횡설수설 쏟아내요.
**'네가 잘못한 게 없다고 생각하는 거야?!'** 피아메타가 소리쳐요. 안도아인은 담담히 답해요. '그때 그 일이 다시 일어난다 해도, 난 또 그렇게 할 거야.'

blocks.cap: '르무엔, 모스티마, 교황님, 심지어 라테라노 전체가 널 용서해도…… 난 절대 용서 안 해.'

blocks.x: 너, 나, 모든 사람, 다들 집념 때문에 여기에 있는 게 아니야?
총구를 겨눈 건 안도아인이 아니라 그의 손에 들린 총이었어요. 피아메타는 죽이는 대신, 무기를 쏴서 떨어뜨려요. 그게 그녀 나름의 결론이었어요.

blocks.cap: 죽이지 않는 것도, 용서하지 않는 것만큼이나 어려운 선택이에요.

blocks.x: 그 틈에 오렌이 끼어들어요. 합법적으로 신청한 폭발(!)로 연막을 치고 안도아인을 놓아줘요. 대신 벨리브 앞에서 자기 정체가 들통나는 대가를 치르죠.
혼란이 가라앉자 교황이 단파 방송으로 각국 사절 앞에 서요. 그리고 훗날 '라테라노 선언'으로 불릴 연설을 시작해요 — 국가 간 안전을 함께 보장하자는, 최초의 제안이었어요.
그 후, 다들 각자의 길을 골랐다
회의는 무사히 끝났고, 다들 다음 걸음을 정해요. 르무엔은 교황청 제7청으로, 오렌은 벨리브 밑에서 근신 중이에요. 배신할 뻔한 전 만국 전달자 신세로요.
페데리코는 그사이에도 조용히 다음 조사를 이어가요. 오렌의 지난 2년 치 외출 기록을 뒤지다가, 낯익은 이름 하나를 발견하고요. 하지만 그 얘기는, 또 다른 날의 몫이에요.
피아메타는 로도스 아일랜드 소속이 돼요. 새 코드네임은 본인이 직접 골랐어요 — '여명의 파괴자'. 모스티마는 여전히 피아메타 감시(?) 역할에서 못 벗어나요.
에젤과 체첼리아는 라테라노를 떠나기로 해요. 도망이 아니라, 아빠를 찾으러 가는 여정이에요. 엄마의 수호총을 챙기고, 교황이 슬쩍 눈감아준 덕분이죠.
너를 위해 기도하도록 하마.
안도아인도 패스파인더들과 함께 다시 길을 떠나요. 파티아가 왜 따라가냐고 묻자 그가 답해요. 오랫동안 자신들이 광야를 외롭게 걷는다 생각했는데, 그건 자신의 오만이었을지도 모른다고요.
체첼리아는 떠나는 길, 노을 속에서 광륜을 쓴 남자의 뒷모습을 보며 문득 '순교자'라는 단어를 떠올려요. 정확한 뜻은 몰라도요. 그리고 에젤의 손을 잡고, 자신도 그 낯선 길 위에 첫발을 내디뎌요 — 엄마가 남긴 그 말, '제복 입은 사람에게 다가가지 마라'를 이미 훌쩍 넘어선 채로.

act15side.tagline: A story that starts with delivering a wine cup as a favor for an old friend, and ends up dragging in a state secret operation and three mythical beings. A ten-year-old grudge times two, and a hundred-year-old loneliness, all meet on the same mountaintop.

chars.name: Lee
chars.desc: A sharp-tongued fixer running a private detective agency in Lungmen, comes to do an old friend a favor and can't seem to pull his foot back out · Operator Lee

chars.name: Kroos
chars.desc: A Rhodes Island field operator who's known Nian long enough that not much surprises her anymore · Operator Kroos

chars.name: Mr. Nothing
chars.desc: A reserve operator who fights with a fan as his weapon, not officially hired yet, but his loyalty's already set its sights on Rhodes Island · Operator Mr. Nothing

chars.name: Yang Xian
chars.desc: The official in charge of the Yang branch office, known for being diligent to a fault, an old schoolmate of Lee's

chars.name: Miss Du
chars.desc: The only daughter of the escort agency's owner, playing the idle rich girl while hiding an agenda of her own

chars.name: Boss Zheng
chars.desc: A warmhearted owner running a handful of inns, rumored to have once been a swordsman leading an escort agency

chars.name: Shang Chong
chars.desc: A porter who hauls loads up and down the Three Mountains, carrying something in his heart for ten years

chars.name: Old Boatman Xin
chars.desc: An old ferryman who knows Shangshu's waterways inside out, and volunteers himself as Lee's guide

chars.name: Zuo Le
chars.desc: A young noble sent to Shangshu as a court envoy, acting mature but not quite grown up yet

chars.name: Taihe
chars.desc: A blunt official escorting Zuo Le, the type whose fists move faster than his words

chars.name: Nian
chars.desc: Kroos's old friend, easygoing and unbothered by just about everything · Operator Nian

chars.name: Dusk
chars.desc: Nian's younger sister, a shy homebody who loves holing up inside paintings · Operator Dusk

chars.name: Ling
chars.desc: The elder sister-figure to Nian and Dusk, in love with wine and scenery, detached from just about everything · Operator Ling

chars.name: Blacknight
chars.desc: A bounty hunter from Rim Billiton who makes a living through theft, always trailed by two Slumberfoots · Operator Blacknight

chars.name: Grand Tutor
chars.desc: The highest-ranking elder in the Yan court, a figure who can untangle a mess with a single appearance

chars.name: Miss Ying
chars.desc: A guest who visits Yang Xian's residence unusually often, warm, yet leaves nothing to chance

terms.name: Shangshu
terms.desc: A region of Yan where towns are scattered between mountains, famed for the Three Mountains, Seventeen Peaks

terms.name: the Three Mountains, Seventeen Peaks
terms.desc: The seventeen (some say eighteen) peaks ringing Shangshu, each holding a legend of its own

terms.name: Cuijiang Peak
terms.desc: One of the Three Mountains, Seventeen Peaks, once called Zhuajiang Peak, with a clearing called 'Wangshui' at its summit

terms.name: the Black Wine Cup
terms.desc: A mysterious antique Lee carries from Lungmen to Shangshu, stirring up strange trouble wherever it touches

terms.name: Chichang
terms.desc: A living, moving apparition that appears wherever the black wine cup has passed

terms.name: Sui
terms.desc: A name for the supernatural beings Yan has dealt with since ancient times, handled exclusively by the Sasedae

terms.name: the Sasedae
terms.desc: A secret court agency dedicated to matters involving the Sui, often at odds with the Ministry of Rites

terms.name: Xingyun Escort Agency
terms.desc: The trading firm run by Boss Zheng, now rebranded as an inn business

blocks.x: Shangshu, in Yan, is a place where towns are scattered between mountain peaks. Even visiting the next town over means riding a cable car or a cliffside plank road. But thanks to that rugged terrain, every peak carries a legend of its own.

blocks.cap: Shangshu, a nomadic city scattered between mountains. Even Rhodes Island's Kroos and Mr. Nothing pause mid-journey, momentarily lost in the view.

blocks.x: The story opens with a snippet of conversation between a father and daughter at some inn. "Dad, why is the rack under the blade empty?" "It's kept for someone." "Who?" "Someone I had a falling out with." Why they fell out, you'll only find out at the very end.

**Lee**, a private detective in Lungmen, boards a boat bound for Shangshu because of a favor asked by **Yang Xian**, a schoolmate he hasn't spoken to in over ten years. The errand is simple: deliver one antique black wine cup.

The problem is, several people are already eyeing this cup. And the moment he steps off the dock, he runs straight into Rhodes Island's **Kroos** and **Mr. Nothing** by pure coincidence. As Mr. Nothing puts it, at this point it's not coincidence, it's a script.
1. Welcomed as the First Guest, Treated as the Prime Suspect

Lee arrives at Shangshu's famous Xingyun Inn together with **Old Boatman Xin**, who came out to meet him. The owner, **Boss Zheng**, looks like a warmhearted old man devoted to his business, but the calluses on his hands are anything but ordinary.

The waiter, Mr. Liu, gets smacked on the back by the boss again today for grumbling about sales. On the surface, this inn is just another popular eatery, but somehow the glares from the guests on the second floor feel far from ordinary.

By coincidence, **Kroos** and **Mr. Nothing** also happen to take a table at the same inn, on the same day. Mr. Nothing, still an operator-in-training who hasn't officially joined yet, is a jack-of-all-trades who settles close combat with nothing but a fan.

blocks.cap: An entrance that kicks a chair over before anything else. Lee thinks the newcomer's fashion sense, at least, isn't bad.

blocks.x: Right on cue, **Miss Du** barges in and grabs Mr. Nothing by the collar. "You're the thief who took the antique box from Lungmen!" She's got completely the wrong man, but her conviction is one hundred percent real.

The brawl only stops once every table and chair in the place is broken. Boss Zheng steps in and barely manages to pull them apart, and only then does everyone finally exchange names. To everyone's surprise, Lee and Kroos already know each other.

Miss Du stamps her foot, unable to swallow her anger, but after a few glances at Boss Zheng, she finally pulls her men back and retreats. "Mr. Nothing, I'll remember you." A first meeting that's already left quite an impression.
2. The Errand His Schoolmate Asked For Was Actually Two Errands

Lee arrives at the local government office and meets **Yang Xian** for the first time in ten years, a curt reunion greeting, followed by some good-natured ribbing over whose drinking tolerance has changed more.

Catching up after so long, the two drift into stories from their school days. Back then, Yang Xian had loudly declared he'd "light a fire for everyone in the world," and Lee had had his own ambitions too. Now, both of them have become somewhat different people from who they were then.
Mid-conversation, a woman named **Miss Ying** suddenly shows up. Yang Xian is caught off guard for a moment but plays it cool, and Lee tactfully steps out, sensing something isn't quite right.

It's only after a drink or two that Yang Xian finally gets to the real reason he called Lee here. "Find the owner of the wine cup." The trouble is, he doesn't know their age, sex, or race. The one thing he's sure of is that they're somewhere in the mountains around Shangshu.

Lee grumbles but takes the job anyway. **I used to be nothing more than a carp.** Borrowing a bit of liquid courage, he lets slip a story from the days when leaping just briefly out of the water made him think the whole world was his.
That night, a teapot with legs shows up outside Lee's room. He shrugs it off, figuring Shangshu must just have a fondness for unusual pets.

blocks.cap: A first visitor with legs, and it didn't even come out of a box. Lee assumes it's just some local pet fad in Shangshu.

blocks.x: 3. The Stranger With a Fan, and an Official Throwing His Weight Around

Meanwhile, **Kroos** and **Mr. Nothing** go out for an evening stroll and run into a suspicious woman commanding a Slumberfoot. Then the burly official chasing her, **Taihe**, promptly mistakes them for Infected and comes at them without a word of explanation.

blocks.cap: An entrance that makes Taihe pull his hand back. The boy **Zuo Le**'s first impression is polite, which somehow makes him even more suspicious.

blocks.x: Zuo Le calmly reveals who he is: an envoy of the **Sasedae**, a secret court agency. And he lets slip something else, too, that Nian and Dusk are beings called "Sui," currently hiding somewhere in Shangshu.

What's more, all of this is apparently tangled up with something that happened at Mount Huiji just days earlier. Kroos ends up becoming, almost by accident, the shield standing between Nian and whatever's chasing her.

Young as he is, Zuo Le is the youngest envoy the Sasedae has ever had. Behind his courteous manner, it's clear every card in the negotiation has already been calculated. Kroos quickly realizes this boy is no naive young lord.

At the same moment, in the courtyard of the government office, a woman named **Miss Ying** is quietly poking around the study, unbeknownst to Lee and Yang Xian. "This book wasn't here before." Without anyone noticing, she quietly takes note of a certain box.
I believe in Rhodes Island~ That's the only reason I believe in that person too~
4. A Detective Invited Into a Hundred-Year-Old Dream

Yang Xian recalls that as a child, during a Catastrophe that struck the mountains, he once saw a woman collapsed drunk in a pavilion. He only now realizes that the wine cup she'd been holding was the very same black wine cup Lee just delivered.

That night, after drinking with Yang Xian, Lee has a truly strange dream, playing a game of Go with someone in a snow-covered pavilion. His opponent introduces herself as "**Ling**" and tells him this is "a dream from a hundred years ago."

blocks.cap: She plays Go alone in a pavilion from a hundred years past. Lee isn't even sure whether the moves he's making are really his own.

blocks.x: What's even more unsettling is that his opponent calls him "the one sitting in for him." **What I saw in the dream was me, but it wasn't me who dreamed it.** Lee turns this over in his mind for a long, long while.
Once he wakes, Lee acts as if nothing happened, calm as ever. But from that day on, his steps toward the mountain start carrying a little more conviction.
5. The Detective Who's Almost Relieved to Be Robbed, and a Ten-Year-Old Grudge
Overnight, the bounty hunter **Blacknight** breaks in and steals the wine cup. Given that she's from Rim Billiton and keeps a pair of Slumberfoots, she's definitely not a local thief.
But Lee isn't disappointed in the slightest. If anything, he's perfectly content just watching to see how many more anglers show up and what kind of big catch gets hooked.

Meanwhile, **Miss Du** has been secretly gathering the escort agency's younger men behind her father's back, building up her own separate operation. She wanted the agency to truly become hers, even if that meant sabotaging her father's big deal to do it.

But one remark from **Shang Chong**, a porter she meets in the mountains, turns everything upside down. Among the people who died in a raid during a downpour ten years ago, apparently, were Shang Chong's son, and Miss Du's real father.

And that the one who abandoned his comrades that day to save only the cargo was none other than **Boss Zheng**. For the first time, Miss Du realizes just how heavy a debt the seat she's meant to inherit is really standing on.
6. An Absurd Coincidence Brings Everyone to the Same Mountaintop

In the end, **Boss Zheng** and Shang Chong cross blades atop Cuijiang Peak, at Wangshui Clearing, finally settling a ten-year-old debt. But of all places, this is exactly where **Zuo Le** and **Taihe**, who've been chasing the wine cup, and **Kroos** and **Mr. Nothing**, trailing right behind them, all converge as well.
Amid the tangle of blade, fan, and club, the wine cup itself ends up rolling right off the cliff. Before anyone even has time to feel deflated, one fact comes to light, late as it is.

This entire mess was **Yang Xian**'s plan all along. If the Sasedae had seized the cup by force, it would have meant a head-on clash with the Ministry of Rites, and Shangshu would have been crushed right in the middle.

So he deliberately let the cup slip out of his own hands. It's also revealed, around this time, that **Miss Ying**, the guest who kept quietly visiting his residence, was actually a Vice Minister of the Ministry of Rites. It turns out there was a good reason Yang Xian had been so tight-lipped about her.

Caught between the friction of two court agencies, the Sasedae and the Ministry of Rites, a small city like Shangshu ended up placed entirely on the scales. And to keep those scales from tipping, Yang Xian chose to shoot himself in the foot with his own hand.
The first thing I ever learned in the Yan court was, 'the mandate of heaven rests with the people.'
7. Big Sister, Little Sister, and the Real Mastermind Hiding in the Mountains

Amid the chaos, **Nian** and **Dusk** make their appearance. They're old acquaintances of Kroos, and a thorn in the Sasedae's side. Zuo Le and Nian trade barbed words, each sizing up whether their interests might actually align.

Meanwhile, Taihe and Mr. Nothing get into a proper duel of their own. Watching Mr. Nothing weather thirty-three strikes in a row, Zuo Le lets slip, almost unconsciously, "the Lian family's Yinqing Fan." Only after truly clashing with him does Taihe start to see this young man with the fan in a new light.

Boss Zheng and Shang Chong's duel keeps going too. When falling rocks threaten Miss Du, it's her instead of Boss Zheng who gets hurt trying to save her, and Shang Chong ends up saving them both at the last second. Old enemy or not, he can't stand watching a child get hurt.
Amid the chaos on the mountaintop, Nian, Dusk, and Kroos's group set off together to find the "big sister" said to be somewhere up there.

blocks.cap: There she is, watching the sunset with a bottle of wine in hand. A hundred years alive, and she's still an idler at heart in front of a good view.

blocks.x: **Ling**, whom they finally meet, is Nian and Dusk's elder sister. Laid-back and half-drunk one moment, she reads the situation faster than anyone the moment it truly matters.

And the three of them find out together: sealed inside the black wine cup is a fragment of consciousness belonging to yet another sibling, missing since long ago, a sinner who, obsessed with Go, split himself into 181 black stones.
This entire commotion turns out to have been that sinner's little game, played out of sheer boredom, going so far as to drag Lee into the board, just to find someone to sit in his place.

Zuo Le keeps pressing the matter, invoking his duty to the Sasedae, but Nian brushes it off with a sly retort. "Aren't our interests already aligned?" A negotiation that isn't quite a negotiation drags on, taut as a bowstring.
8. A Towering Shadow, and the Answer That Erased It
The shadows of the three sisters merge into the towering form of a Sui. If it opens its eyes, they might all vanish. Nian is afraid, and Dusk is trembling.
Still, Nian doesn't back down. **I simply can't accept this.** Don't ask her why, that's just how it is. Dusk doesn't say anything either, but somehow, she seems to be hoping for it a little too.

blocks.cap: A shadow towering over the mountain. The first moment the three sisters, together for a hundred years, are afraid side by side.

blocks.x: But **Ling** calmly pours a cup of wine and holds it out toward the shadow. That thing, she says, is nothing but an illusion reflected in the mind, no need to be jerked around by a shape with no substance.
The world's already been drunk for ten thousand years, can't I dream even once? ...Doesn't matter either way.
The towering shadow vanishes without a trace on a single gust of wind. The answer, as always, was simple. **Who am I?**, whoever answered that question first was always going to win this game.
Epilogue — Shangshu in Spring, Afterward

blocks.cap: An old man in a horned ceremonial cap opens the door and steps inside. The court's most senior elder, the **Grand Tutor**, has arrived.

blocks.x: With a single appearance, the Grand Tutor unravels the whole tangled mess. In exchange for turning a blind eye to the Sasedae's overreach, **Yang Xian** is promoted and transferred to the capital. **Miss Ying** departs ahead of everyone else for her next posting, Yumen.
Under Yan's sky, the only thing I fear is the people living without ease and the nation failing to grow strong and prosperous.
**Nian** is entrusted with a massive wall project to defend the north. **Ling** decides to keep chasing the whereabouts of the sibling who went missing a hundred years ago.
There's one thing Ling has always wondered. Centuries ago, how on earth did the Grand Tutor beat that Go-obsessed sinner?
The answer turns out to be surprisingly anticlimactic. He simply played the match with all of Yan itself as the board, and every one of its people as the stones.

Operator Leizi, arriving late, mediates between the Sasedae and Rhodes Island, and the whole affair is finally wrapped up. Zuo Le gets an earful from Taihe, while Kroos and Mr. Nothing sigh over news of yet another headache from the agency.
**Boss Zheng** and Shang Chong, in the end, never cut each other down. It isn't reconciliation. They simply decide to carry their own debts in their own way.
And **Miss Du** decides to leave her father's side and head somewhere new: Yumen. The sheltered young lady who's only ever lived in a greenhouse is gone for good.
Lee still doesn't head back to Lungmen, choosing instead to chase down the whereabouts of an old friend, **Waai Tin Fooi**.
Kroos suddenly asks, Lee had said Yang Xian resembled Lungmen's Wei Yanwu, so what exactly was similar about them? Lee's answer is short.
Both of them, in front of the person they carry in their heart, can't move an inch.

Oh, and it comes out belatedly that **Blacknight** was actually someone Miss Ying had secretly sent. "I just didn't want him getting tangled up in something complicated without even knowing it," Miss Ying says with a smile, waving off any further questions.
Remember that empty sword rack at the inn, back at the very start of the story? It was a spot kept open for someone he'd had a falling-out with.
Shang Chong still leaves Shangshu, but Boss Zheng tells him, "I'll be waiting for you here in Shangshu." That empty rack, maybe, someday, it'll finally be filled.

act14side.tagline: A brother invites a guest to thank Rhodes Island for curing his sister, then drags that same guest straight into his own nation's coup. The move nobody on the chessboard saw coming came from the sister who climbed the mountain, and the one who walked out of it alone.

chars.name: Ensiodes
chars.desc: The young head of House SilverAsh, one of Kjerag's Three Clans. An ambitious reformer remaking the nation through trade and railways · Operator SilverAsh

chars.name: Enya
chars.desc: The Saint of Kjerag, who lives atop the snowy peak at Manjuwon. She is also Ensiodes's older sister · Operator Pramanix

chars.name: Ensia
chars.desc: The youngest of House SilverAsh, who loves climbing and getting into trouble. She's currently being treated for Oripathy at Rhodes Island · Operator Cliffheart

chars.name: Gnosis
chars.desc: The former Chief Technology Officer, recently dismissed from Karlan Trade CO., LTD. A sharp, arrogant researcher

chars.name: Aktoz
chars.desc: The head of House Paleroche. A hot-blooded, blunt Ursus warrior

chars.name: Latatos
chars.desc: The head of House Browntail. Known for her calm, calculating way of handling things

chars.name: Sharp
chars.desc: An elite Rhodes Island operator guarding the Doctor. Rumored to hail from the Kazimierz Sargon court

chars.name: Aurora
chars.desc: A Kjerag native who joined Rhodes Island through House SilverAsh's talent development program. She's returned home after a long time away

chars.name: Degenbrecher
chars.desc: Ensiodes's personal bodyguard. Known as Kazimierz's 'Knight of Darkness'

chars.name: the Grand Elder
chars.desc: The highest elder of Manjuwon. He's held this seat even longer than the Saint has.

terms.name: Kjerag
terms.desc: A theocratic nation set on a snowy mountain. It has long been governed jointly by the Three Clans and Manjuwon.

terms.name: Kjeragand
terms.desc: The god Kjerag's people have served for a thousand years. Said to command the blizzards and the mountain itself, protecting the nation.

terms.name: Manjuwon
terms.desc: The religious institution devoted to Kjeragand and Kjerag's spiritual center. The Grand Elder and the Saint oversee matters of faith here.

terms.name: the Three Clans
terms.desc: The three houses that make up Kjerag — SilverAsh, Paleroche, and Browntail. They gather each year at the Council of the Three Clans to decide the nation's affairs.

terms.name: Karlan Trade CO., LTD
terms.desc: The trading company Ensiodes founded. It effectively monopolizes Kjerag's foreign trade and serves as the engine of his reforms.

terms.name: the Grand Hunt
terms.desc: Kjerag's greatest festival. The Three Clans hunt together, and the Saint performs the sacred rite of praying to Kjeragand for the year's peace.

terms.name: the Snowmount Fiend
terms.desc: A name borrowed from a legendary monster of the snowy mountains — the private army Ensiodes has secretly raised.

blocks.x: There's a nation that has spent a thousand years shut away in the snowstorm, serving its god. Neither catastrophe nor war ever reached this snowy peak. Instead, the people of Kjerag slowly stiffened inside a stagnation called peace.

The Doctor's group visits this nation at the invitation of **Ensiodes**, the young head of the SilverAsh family. It was meant as thanks for Rhodes Island's help suppressing his sister Ensia's Oripathy.
But the moment they arrive, this so-called older brother announces he'll hand over all the power the three clans share — to the Saint. And he says it with the most devout expression.
1. The Chairman Suddenly Repents — Right in Front of the Three Clans

Ensiodes is the reformer who built trade ports and laid down railways to transform Kjerag. But to the other two clans — **Aktoz** of House Paleroche and **Latatos** of House Browntail — he's nothing but a nouveau riche trampling the faith. Today, at the Council of the Three Clans, the two of them finally corner him.
But cornered, Ensiodes is the one who kneels first. He offers up the valley and the mines to Manjuwon, and hands over all the power of the three clans to the Saint.
The diligent hold power, and the idle suffer.

blocks.cap: The trade port, the mines, the valley, and the sacred mountain. That's the whole of tiny little Kjerag.

blocks.x: The Saint's name is **Enya**, Ensiodes's older sister. After their parents died in an accident, a young Ensiodes led the family and raised his two younger sisters. But five years ago, when Enya became the Saint and left for the mountain, a slow rift opened between the once-close siblings.

The one who actually invited the Doctor's group on this trip is the youngest, **Ensia**. She loves climbing and getting into trouble, and she's the only one who's noticed her brother and sister aren't the way they used to be.
2. Barely Arrived as a Guest, and Already a Hostage

Aktoz drags the Doctor off to the Paleroche mansion by force, saying he'll keep 'Ensiodes's guest' under his own watch. Ensia draws her blade the moment the soldiers barge in, but it's Ensiodes's bodyguard **Degenbrecher** who actually settles the whole rude affair.
Think carefully, outsider. Lord Aktoz said to 'bring you along.' Not everyone gets treated this well.

Meanwhile, **Aurora**, back in her homeland, feels conflicted — she's the one who joined Rhodes Island with the SilverAsh family's backing, and now she's the one standing against the very family she owes it all to. **Sharp** tells her flatly.
From now on, you'll have to treat Ensiodes as an enemy. Can you do that?

And so the Doctor suddenly ends up an 'honored guest' — and hostage — of House Paleroche, saddled with the practical work of handing over rights to the valley and the mines. For such a cutthroat country, the hospitality, at least, isn't bad.

Meanwhile, Aurora's neighbors are actually thrilled at the news that the SilverAsh family is handing power to the Saint — even her father, who's opposed the open-door policy for years, breaks into a wide smile. Aurora alone feels the weight of having to stand against the person she owes a debt of gratitude to.
3. A Protest Breaks Out in Front of the Factory, and Someone's Laughing From the Shadows

A mob of laid-off workers from the valley surrounds the Doctor, blaming him for everything. House SilverAsh's steward Weiss barely manages to calm them down, but then the Doctor abruptly points out the factory's safety problems, flipping the whole mood.
......So this is what you meant by 'help.'

And then the real mastermind is revealed. **Gnosis** — the former Chief Technology Officer, fired from Karlan Trade CO., LTD half a year ago. Hiding behind a wall at the Browntail mansion, eavesdropping, Latatos hears Gnosis drop one loaded line.
Why do you think Ensiodes brought up handing over power? Here's a hint...... the Grand Hunt.
4. In a Ravine Without Even a Bridge, an Entire Train Vanishes

Gnosis takes Latatos to an abandoned road in the gorge and activates a hidden bridge — the secret rail line Ensiodes had been using to quietly ship troops and supplies. Before Latatos can even collect herself, Gnosis blows the entire train sky-high with a flick of his fingers.

blocks.cap: A single sword forged with Victorian smithing techniques, and one entire train, bridge and all, gone. Gnosis's way of saying hello was always this loud.

blocks.x: Gnosis now declares that Latatos is an accomplice too — whether she wanted to be or not, she's on the same boat now. Latatos's cheerful younger sibling Sirus is simply bewildered by the whole situation.
5. A Sacred Hunt, But the Prey Isn't What Anyone Expected

Kjerag's greatest festival, the Grand Hunt, begins. Saint Enya, unprecedentedly, joins the hunt herself, loosing an arrow to save a warrior's life. Even Aktoz and Latatos are struck speechless before her.

During a break, Aktoz, calling her 'friend,' opens up to Enya — how is it that, born a SilverAsh, she walks such a different path from Ensiodes? Enya doesn't deny that SilverAsh blood runs in her too, but she answers like this.
Ever since I became the Saint, everything about me has simply drifted far from the mundane world. And it was Lord Ensiodes who made the name 'SilverAsh' stop meaning anything to me.

In that opening, Ensiodes is attacked by an unidentified assassin. The assassin turns out to be none other than Latatos's attendant, Mönch — a pawn Gnosis had planted. Wounded, Ensiodes doesn't even bother hiding the injury and calmly continues the hunt.
6. A Poisoned Cup for the Grand Elder, and Two Perfectly Framed Traitors

During the Grand Hunt's final rite, the Grand Elder collapses. Poison, in his cup. Ensiodes immediately points the finger at Aktoz, and Sirus, who'd been shielding the captured assassin Mönch, is framed as an accomplice too.
Judge them! Judge them!! Judge them!!!

blocks.cap: One who tries to break the board, and one who keeps the board from breaking. The outcome was decided in an instant.

blocks.x: And Aktoz's confidant Vale learns something else — that the wound his own father supposedly took from the legendary 'Snowmount Fiend' during the sacred hunt was actually a poison herb the Grand Elder had used. The moment he realizes his father's death was Aktoz's own mistake, a crack forms in Vale's loyalty.

In the chaos, Gnosis appears in person to deliver Latatos an ultimatum, but Degenbrecher's blade blocks him and he's dragged away. Enya, confined in Manjuwon, offers no resistance at all and simply walks out quietly.

blocks.cap: The Saint didn't resist. Because she already knew there was no room left to resist.

blocks.x: 7. The Room Grandfather Built to Burn His Enemy Alive

Latatos lures Ensiodes to the old villa of House Edelweiss and triggers a fire trap her grandfather left behind. Die together, that's the plan. Trapped in the burning room, the two of them finally start being honest with each other.

Latatos confesses it wasn't greed but fear that made her turn on him — Ensiodes hadn't looked like someone trying to unite the three clans, but like someone trying to make all of Kjerag his own. That's what frightened her.
It looked less like you were trying to bring the three clans back together, and more like you were trying to make Kjerag your own. That's what scared me.

blocks.cap: The room Grandfather built to burn his enemy alive. Granddaughter and enemy now sit side by side, burning inside it together.

blocks.x: Latatos also lets slip an old truth on top of it — that Gnosis's parents were actually driven out on a false charge, and that it was Latatos's own grandfather who'd targeted Ensiodes's parents. Just before the two of them are about to die, Degenbrecher smashes through the wall and drags them out. Without a word, Ensiodes takes off his own coat and drapes it over Latatos.
8. There Was More Than One Chess Piece

Ensiodes visits the imprisoned Gnosis to discuss what comes after the Grand Hunt. Only then does it come out — Gnosis's dismissal, his betrayal, even the assassination plot using Sirus — all of it was a play the two of them had scripted together from the very start.
I'm not your chess piece. Much less your subordinate. I'm your partner.

blocks.cap: Beneath Lungmen's lights, two boys stand side by side. Twenty years ago, and now, too.

blocks.x: Before their years studying in Victoria, young Ensiodes and Gnosis once promised each other that someday they'd make Kjerag the strongest nation of all. Gnosis, driven out of Kjerag on a false charge, had been paying back that old promise this way all along.

blocks.cap: 'Just like back then,' Gnosis said. The disgrace was always his to carry.

blocks.x: His attendant Mönch, who had believed Gnosis's trust in him was genuine, is left speechless upon learning he was never more than a piece of the plan.
9. The Doctor's Final Move — Gulo Takes the Station, Sirus Takes the Village, and the Little Sister Takes the Mountain
The Doctor secretly frees the imprisoned Aktoz and Latatos. Having been framed as traitors by Ensiodes, they now have no choice but to take Rhodes Island's — their enemy's — hand.

Here's the plan. Aktoz's general Gulo seizes the train station, Latatos's sibling Sirus rescues her husband Yucatan and stirs up the village, and in the chaos that follows, Aktoz's real army advances on the sacred mountain — while Ensia climbs the cliff alone to meet her sister.

blocks.cap: Climbing the sacred mountain with a single ice axe. Having sworn she'd meet her sister, there was no turning back now.

blocks.x: Skirting around the mountainside, Ensia finally reaches Manjuwon and sits face to face with her sister once more. They exchange nothing but small talk at first, until Enya notices the worn bracelet her sister still wears.
Ensia, instead of that...... stay with your sister.

Ensia recalls how, as a child, she used to beg her sister — who never much cared for mountains — to go climbing together. Now that Enya has become the Saint, she's ended up trapped on this very mountain, and Ensia grew up always imagining herself rescuing her. Now, that fantasy has finally come half true.
Meanwhile, Sharp stands against Ensiodes's final line of defense, Degenbrecher. Neither one wins, but Sharp manages to hold him back for a full ten minutes.
Letting personal feelings into a mission is something only an amateur would do.
10. The Saint Walks Out Alone
The dying Grand Elder gathers his last strength for a final word — protect the faith, defeat Ensiodes, and restore Kjerag to what it once was. Enya refuses, quietly but firmly.
I am the Saint. Not the Saint of Manjuwon — the Saint of Kjerag.

As Aktoz's army pushes right up to the sacred mountain, and the two armies stand facing each other, the handmaid Kiyar smiles an unreadable smile and claps her hands twice. Every last guard protecting Manjuwon crumples to the ground on the spot, and only Enya walks out alone.

blocks.cap: Bring down Ensiodes, rescue the Saint! — The battle cry pierced through the snowstorm.

blocks.x: The blizzard stops, the black clouds part, and light lights her path. Every warrior lays down their weapon and kneels. Not the enemy, not even her own brother, is exempted.

blocks.cap: The blizzard stopped. The black clouds parted, and the Saint walked out into that light.

blocks.x: In the name of Manjuwon, Enya declares that foreign faiths and new ways of life are now permitted. Without a war, Kjerag has opened its own doors.

On the way back to Manjuwon, the handmaid Kiyar quietly recalls the past. Watching over the children who came to Manjuwon each year as Saint candidates, enduring their trials, had long been her private habit — and among them all, Enya had been the one she found most fascinating.
Congratulations, my child and my proxy, my friend, my sister.
Epilogue. Chess Ends in a Draw
With everything settled, Ensiodes sits down across from the Doctor for a game of chess. His victory had been all but certain, he admits — except for the one variable he never saw coming.
A draw, huh...... Seems like my luck really hasn't been on my side lately.

blocks.cap: On the chessboard, the outcome had already been decided. Only the fact that it ended in a draw was something even Ensiodes had never experienced before.

blocks.x: He also admits why he had to rush things. Outside Kjerag, nations were already at war, trading, swallowing each other whole. Nowhere was it guaranteed that the peace of these snowy peaks would last forever.
And he keeps one promise: that Rhodes Island will be free to come and go from Kjerag from now on, and that no record of this whole affair will ever bear the Doctor's or Rhodes Island's name.

Aktoz steps down as clan head, taking responsibility for the Grand Elder's poisoning, while Latatos manages to keep her place, if only as a minor branch of the SilverAsh family. Gnosis, acting as Ensiodes's proxy, volunteers to leave for Rhodes Island, and Mönch vanishes without a word — leaving behind nothing but a knife Gnosis had once given him.

Weiss and Matterhorn stand before the Doctor together and bow their heads. They may have served Ensiodes, they say, but they were Rhodes Island operators all along. The Doctor doesn't blame them, but Sharp swears to write every detail of this affair into her report.

At a shop in front of the city gate, Ensia finds a painting of her brother and sister shaking hands — but in the end, she doesn't buy it. She's already realized the reconciliation in that picture is a fake.
Someday, I'm going to put my brother's and sister's relationship back the way it used to be.
Kjerag has broken free of the stagnation called peace. Whether the peace it's earned in exchange is just the beginning of another stagnation, though — no one knows yet.

act13side.tagline: The Radiant Knight was exiled as one of the Infected — except it turns out she was never Infected at all. And still, she stays by the real Infected's side, learning how a candle and the sun can walk together.

chars.name: Margaret
chars.desc: Kazimierz's former champion, exiled six years ago after being branded one of the Infected. The eldest daughter of the Nearl family, she returns to the tournament under the name 'the Radiant Knight' · Operator Nearl the Radiant Knight

chars.name: Maria
chars.desc: Margaret's younger sister and the Nearl family's engineer. She personally maintains her sister's weapons and armor.

chars.name: Sophia
chars.desc: A longtime neighbor of the Nearl sisters and a weapons maintenance expert. She and Młynar are at each other's throats, yet somehow can't stop thinking about one another · Operator Whislash

chars.name: Młynar
chars.desc: Maria's uncle and a former knight living quietly. He plays indifferent to the world, but nothing gets past him · Operator Młynar

chars.name: Gravel
chars.desc: A rank-four knight from Kazimierz. Ever since volunteering to guard the Doctor of Rhodes Island, she's stuck close like a shadow · Operator Gravel

chars.name: Amiya
chars.desc: The leader of Rhodes Island, who has come to Kazimierz as well. She juggles medical support for the Infected and quiet investigation at the same time · Operator Amiya

chars.name: Sona
alias: the Knight of the Blazing Tail
chars.desc: The leader of the 'Pinus Sylvestris' knights, who rallies the Infected knights together. Always smiling, always calculating underneath · Operator Flametail

chars.name: Justina
chars.desc: An Infected sharpshooter who grew up hearing legends of the Radiant Knight from her distant homeland. She stays close by Sona's side · Operator Fartooth

chars.name: Ivona
alias: the Knight of the Wild Mane
chars.desc: A hot-blooded, reckless Infected knight. She never hesitates to go head-to-head with the Armorless Union · Operator Wild Mane

chars.name: Dikaiopolis
chars.desc: The first Infected knight ever to become a Grand Knight, and a former tournament champion. The one who created the system allowing Infected knights to compete.

terms.name: Kavalerielki
terms.desc: The Grand Knight territory of Kazimierz. Every few years it absorbs three other nomadic cities to grow larger — and it's the home base of the tournament.

terms.name: Commercial Federation
terms.desc: A union of merchants that oversees Kazimierz's Knight Sports and commerce as a whole. It tries to control even the outcomes of matches through its spokespeople.

terms.name: Sentiment Council
terms.desc: A political faction of campaign knights and the nobility. It's been locked in a long-running power struggle with the Commercial Federation.

terms.name: Sector Zero
terms.desc: An off-limits zone said to house and treat Infected knights. Many suspect it isn't really just for treatment.

terms.name: Armorless Union
terms.desc: An assassination organization secretly employed by the Commercial Federation. Its operations are run by executives called Lazurites.

terms.name: Dark Iron
terms.desc: A name pointing to the legendary founder of the Armorless Union — or the figure standing at its very peak. No one knows the truth of it.

terms.name: Cheondo
terms.desc: A coming-of-age tradition of the Naitsmora, a branch of the Kuranta people. Carrying only a single weapon given by one's parents, one must walk alone into unknown lands and prove their own fate.

blocks.x: Kazimierz is called the country of knights. But what really drives this nation isn't the sword — it's the Commercial Federation's ledgers.

Six years ago, former champion Margaret Nearl was exiled after being branded one of the Infected. Now she returns under the name 'the Radiant Knight.' In this tournament, tangled up with an uprising of Infected knights and a power struggle among the wealthy, chaos follows wherever she goes.
And underneath all this chaos lies a truth even she doesn't know.
1. The Champion Returns After Six Years, and Nobody's Happy About It

At Kavalerielki National Arena, commentator **Bigmouth Mob**'s voice rings out as the Kazimierz Special Tournament kicks off. The prize pool swells, sucking up every last coin the crowd spends in the city, while Commercial Federation spokesperson Mackie carefully drops the two syllables 'Infected' to the incoming spokesperson Malkiewicz.

The Nearl sisters' longtime neighbors — blacksmith **Sophia** and her younger sister **Maria** — are busy tuning up Margaret's armor and weapons. Maria's uncle **Młynar** lives quietly like an ordinary office worker, but rumor has it he recently lost a serious duel against Margaret without using a single Arts.
That man is looking down on Margaret — and on the Radiant Knight.

Meanwhile, in the cracks of the city, the Infected knights' gathering, the 'Pinus Sylvestris' knights, is on the move. Its leader **Sona** ('the Knight of the Blazing Tail'), **Greinerti** ('the Knight of the Grey Brush-Tail'), and sharpshooter **Justina** ('the Knight of Fangs') take in the young noble Ševčík ('the Knight of Plastic'), who's been hunted by the Armorless Union.

The Armorless Union operatives they're up against are Lazurites **Roy** and **Monique**. On the surface a smooth-talking comedy duo, in truth they're contract killers 'handling' the Infected across the city on the board's orders.

The newly appointed spokesperson **Malkiewicz** doesn't know why his predecessor Czarny was exiled overnight, but he receives one 'unspoken rule' from his senior Mackie: don't ask anything about Sector Zero.
2. A Life Cheaper Than the Championship Prize

On the tournament's first day, a nameless Infected knight steps into the melee event. His noble opponent, Olmer Ingra, mocks him as 'Oripathy trash' and beats him down. In his final moments the Infected knight wrings out every last drop of Originium Arts to fight back, but in the end he dies in the middle of the arena.
His death, broadcast live, sends all of Kazimierz into an uproar. Yet the broadcast itself brushes it off as 'the man's own fault for breaking the rules,' and Ingra walks free within a day.

His name was Jamie. A former Knight Sports fighter, divorced, raising a daughter alone, a man who looked after other people's children every morning. His companion **Ivona** ('the Knight of the Wild Mane') quietly breaks down under the guilt of failing to protect him to the end.
......Please...... help me......

Ivona answers not with words but with a promise: Sona will find his wife and daughter, no matter what. It was a night when everyone was reminded, all over again, of exactly how this city called Kazimierz 'consumes' the Infected.
3. The Nearl Family's Secret, and a Mysterious Guest

At the tavern, old knight **Fogelweide** and blacksmith **Koval** are still trading their usual bickering, while Maria racks her brain over her sister's new weapon. Amid this peaceful scene, Sophia suddenly finds herself wondering — is Młynar really living the way Młynar should?

Then one night, the tavern door opens and a strange armored knight walks in. Muttering something in an ancient tongue, he calls old Fogelweide by an unfamiliar name — 'Batbayar.' A name no one else knew, an old title from the long-forgotten Kuranta people.

This knight — later to be known as 'the Knight of Nightmares' — is **Tola**. With no parents, no kin, he's the last of the Naitsmora, drifting all the way to Kazimierz alone to complete the coming-of-age rite called Cheondo.

Around the same time, a freelance knight from Leithanien, **Viviana Droste** ('the Knight of the Candle'), is being hounded by a baseless scandal. While spokesperson Mackie handles the fallout, her next opponent is decided — none other than the Radiant Knight.

Droste was born the illegitimate child of Leithanien high nobility, locked in a tall tower, raised reading poetry by the light of a single candle she'd secretly stolen. To her, Kazimierz was the first place that ever gave her back the name 'knight.'
The night before the match, she deliberately seeks Margaret out to drop her a quiet warning: that this time, the Federation intends to push the Radiant Knight into the losers' bracket.
A storm's coming soon. Lately when I look up at the sky, the stars have been shining unusually bright.
4. Candle Meets Sun

blocks.cap: Light and shadow collide, and Margaret's swordspear splits the candle's blade clean in two.

blocks.x: Droste's Arts don't control the candle flame itself, but the shadows around it. As the two cross swords, they talk about what it means to be a knight, and Margaret answers plainly.
A knight is a noble being who lights up the whole world.
After a long duel, it's Droste herself who calls it — declaring her own loss. The moment her candle is severed, she accepts defeat and vows a rematch.

The night the match ends, Margaret walks Kazimierz's night streets together with Rhodes Island doctors **Shining** and **Nightingale**. They trade a few jokes, and she even takes Nightingale's hand for a dance.

blocks.cap: Beneath the neon signs, a knight and a Sarkaz clasp hands and dance.

blocks.x: We'll be waiting for you — before the moment comes when you burn through all your own light.

That calm doesn't last. An arrow from Dark Iron flies out of the night sky, and Armorless Union's Roy appears to propose a 'ceasefire': leave the Radiant Knight and Rhodes Island untouched, and they'll take care of the rest of the Infected themselves.

Margaret refuses flatly, on the spot. Pinning the blame on others was never the kind of peace she wanted to begin with. Quietly, the shadow of those willing to trample the Infected to get ahead keeps growing.
5. A Single Night's Sleep Over the Grand Knight Territory

Sona's group raids Kavalerielki's power plant, cutting power to the whole city, and uses the chaos to infiltrate the Commercial Federation building and steal Sector Zero's classified files. Bounty hunter **Tolland Cash** joins in as an unexpected ally.

While Ivona draws the Armorless Union's patrol out head-on, Greinerti raids the energy sector, and Sona slips into the building in the meantime. The Sentiment Council had quietly pulled its own guards out of the way too.

But the Armorless Union had read the whole plan from the start. The instant Roy's arrow sends Sona falling through a gap in the sector, an unknown hand secretly catches her. Ivona goes toe-to-toe with Monique, battered to pieces, but never gives in.

While the blackout drags on, at the tavern, Fogelweide and Koval switch on the backup lights and start beating down Armorless Union agents. Against the assassins who've come looking for the Nearl sisters, the old knights show off skills that, surprisingly, haven't rusted one bit.
I might die. But I won't lose.
As the blackout throws the city into chaos, the Sentiment Council finally breaks its silence and moves. The disguised Silverlance Pegasus Knights march straight into the heart of Kavalerielki.

blocks.cap: Moonlight, forgotten in the age of fluorescent lights, shines silver armor down over the sleeping city.

blocks.x: They are the legendary order once led by Margaret's grandfather. A name that neither the Commercial Federation nor the Armorless Union dares to touch sweeps through and cleans up Kazimierz's night.
6. The Hunter Who Lost His Steppe
Tola's mother lost her life to a beast of prey long ago. From that day on, he chased the legends of the Kagan (king) and Kheshig (royal guard), convinced he was the last heir to that tradition.

To him, Kazimierz didn't even deserve to be called a country of knights. With no beast left to hunt, no kin left to walk beside him, only the Infected, the Minosians, and himself left on an empty steppe — he swallowed his anger alone.

The duel against Margaret ends in a draw on a rules violation, and Tola, unable to control his fury, chases it all the way to the Nearl house. The only one who stands in his way, without so much as armor, is **Maria**.

blocks.cap: There was no time to hesitate. Maria raises her sword high again with an arm covered in wounds.

blocks.x: My sister taught me a knight's shield exists to protect others. Honor......doesn't matter at all!

In the end, Tola backs down. For the man who once said 'you can't grow strong on someone else's beliefs alone,' it's the first time he's ever been shaken — by a fearless girl trying to protect her sister.

A few days later, in the semifinal, Tola's real opponent is the Infected champion, **the Knight of Blood, Dikaiopolis**. Tola blankets the arena with visions of an old army he summons, but Dikaiopolis calmly clears them away with an axe.

blocks.cap: The young Naitsmora stops his chant and cries out. "I......I myself am the Kagan!"

blocks.x: Defeated, Tola quietly heads north. Admitting failure, but no longer afraid of weakness, telling himself over and over that his Cheondo isn't over yet.
7. Light and Blood Meet in the Final

In the final, the two Infected champions, Margaret and Dikaiopolis, stand face to face. Amid the crowd's roar, Dikaiopolis calmly admits it — that in the end Margaret accomplished nothing at all, and yet, she is still 'a great failure.'
Dikaiopolis calls Margaret 'a great failure.' He respects her devotion and fighting spirit, but points out that in the end, she protected no one — and even dragged her own family into it.
Trading blows hard enough to break their weapons, the two share their life stories before the outcome ever settles. And for the first time, Margaret speaks the truth she's kept hidden all this while.

She was never Infected at all. Six years ago, her grandfather Kiril Nearl exiled her on purpose, to protect the young granddaughter who would have become the Commercial Federation's target. The stands erupt instantly.
Even amid the chaos, the match ends in Margaret's victory. She helps the fallen Dikaiopolis back to his feet.
There are......no losers here, Knight of Blood.

The two champions walk side by side toward the Champions' Wall. Arrows from the Armorless Union fly in more than once, but each time, Infected knights, Sarkaz doctors, and the Nearl family's old friends step in to block them.

blocks.cap: In the distance, a dazzling hall of honor comes into view. Loser and winner stand before it, side by side.

blocks.x: In the end, the Silverlance Pegasus Knights escort the two of them, clearing the final path. Grand Knight-Commander Ioleta Rossi looks at Margaret with old eyes and smiles, saying she looks just like her grandfather.
After That, the Light No Longer Walks Alone

Młynar suddenly leaves Kazimierz, saying he's cashing in three months of unused leave. He quietly confides to Tolland alone that he's going to look for an older brother who's been out of touch for years, and someone he once said was as beautiful as a jewel.

The Armorless Union's Roy and Monique quietly open a new shop before long — an ordinary store selling cleaning products, but really a cover identity for people who are about to become Commercial Federation directors. Platinum only now realizes she was supposed to have died as a scapegoat from the very start.

Spokesperson Malkiewicz's face goes stiff after a single phone call. The person on the other end is the head of the Rose Media Group, the true power behind his appointment as spokesperson — and, as it happens, none other than his own father.

Sona's group follows Tolland to a village outside the Grand Knight territory. There, Infected who've turned their backs on the Commercial Federation mingle with farmers, fallen nobles, and bounty hunters. It means the fight against the Commercial Federation is far from over.

Amiya and the Doctor, Shining and Nightingale, all leave Kazimierz safely. Margaret promises that if Rhodes Island calls on her someday, she'll join them again — and that whenever they need her, she'll fight for them without fail.

The lights of Kavalerielki come back on, and the lamps of the Commercial Federation building glow bright once more. The city will keep turning to the tune of the Commercial Federation's ledgers — but now, outside those ledgers too, candle and sun walk side by side.

act12side.tagline: A righteous ex-cop and her pragmatic gangster-boss daughter get dragged into a reunion neither wanted, end up on the same team, and somehow stop a coup in a pleasure city along the way. And in the end, neither of them admits they've made peace.

chars.name: Ch'en
chars.desc: Former captain of the Lungmen Guard Department's Special Inspection Team. Transferred to Rhodes Island, but her sense of justice hasn't changed a bit.

chars.name: Lin Weisha
chars.desc: Daughter of the Ratking who rules the slums. Heads to Dusollese in Wei Yenwu's place · the Operator Lin

chars.name: Ernesto
chars.desc: Deputy director of Dusollese's international trade administration. Volunteers as the pair's guide and interpreter.

chars.name: Raphaela
chars.desc: Captain of 'Grey Feather,' a competing team made up of Dusollese locals.

chars.name: Pancho
chars.desc: A veteran judge of the Grand Prix. Ernesto's father, famed for his cold, cutting commentary.

chars.name: Candela
chars.desc: Mayor of Dusollese. Ruler of this dazzling city, and an acquaintance of Wei Yenwu.

chars.name: Mizuki
chars.desc: A solo competitor in the tournament, famous for her enormous appetite.

chars.name: Hoshiguma
chars.desc: Ch'en's old partner, also of the Lungmen Guard Department. Tags along with Swire and ends up an accidental bystander.

chars.name: Swire
chars.desc: Young lady of the Swire family and Ch'en's successor. Sneaks off for a vacation and ends up digging into the city's secrets.

terms.name: Dusollese
terms.desc: A hedonist paradise built on an artificial sea inside Bolivar. Founded by Mayor Candela Sanchez, a city that knows nothing of war.

terms.name: Bolivar
terms.desc: A country torn endlessly between three factions: the Sanghas Dynasty backed by Leithanien, the coalition government backed by Columbia, and the 'True Bolivarians.'

terms.name: Extreme Iron Man Grand Prix
terms.desc: Dusollese's annual survival variety competition. The winning team earns the honor of pressing the button that opens the city's floodgates.

terms.name: Dragon Rat Brawl
terms.desc: The team name Ch'en threw together on the spot. Meant to signal that two Lungmen natives had teamed up, but the sound of it left something to be desired.

terms.name: True Bolivarians
terms.desc: The independence movement Pancho belonged to a decade ago. Tried to drive out both the coalition government and the Sanghas Dynasty, and failed.

blocks.x: In the middle of a Bolivar torn apart by three warring governments, there's one city that has never known war. Its name is Dusollese — an artificial sea, casinos, liquor, and endless entertainment, all of it Mayor Candela Sanchez's private kingdom.

Every year the city sends Lungmen an invitation, and every year neither the Ratking nor Wei Yenwu bothers to go. This time, though, Wei Yenwu's wife Humizuki secretly pulls some strings, pushing the Ratking's daughter Lin Weisha and Ch'en — currently stationed at Rhodes Island — off to Dusollese separately.
Neither of them has any idea the other is coming. The moment they run into each other at the mayor's residence, they blurt out the exact same line, in perfect unison: 'What are you doing here?!'
1. Came to Sightsee, Left as Tournament Champions

Candela is all warmth from the first meeting. She hands them a credit card with no limit, tells them not to feel awkward standing in for the Lungmen governor, and casually mentions the recent uptick in weapon smuggling — feel free to poke around and investigate if they get bored, she says.

blocks.cap: You'd never guess from the outside, but this is what the city really looks like — an artificial sea and luxury cruise ships adrift in paradise, Dusollese.

blocks.x: Trouble starts the moment Ernesto takes over as their guide. Lin Weisha, hunting for a low-risk casino to look into, cleans out a card-cheating dealer and beats back the thugs sent after her, and somehow ends up in the middle of the day's qualifier finale, 'Total Smash Splash.' Ch'en, arriving late after getting the call, gets swept up too — and before either of them realizes it, they're both qualified for the finals.

They only really come to at the qualifier's award ceremony. Judge Pancho hands them a solid gold trophy, and the team name they're forced to slap together on the spot — 'Dragon Rat Brawl,' Ch'en's improvised pun on a dragon-woman and a rat's daughter fighting it out — draws a look from Lin Weisha that says everything.

The night before the finals, every kind of team takes the stage at the introduction ceremony — the lovey-dovey couple 'Honey Holiday,' big-eater Mizuki's team 'Pac-Man,' the local team 'Grey Feather.' When the MC solemnly announces 'Dragon Rat Brawl,' Ch'en and Lin Weisha can only sigh in unison.
What are you doing here?!
2. Twenty Gold Bars, Zero Friendship

Right up until game day, Ch'en can't warm up to this city — nothing here seems to exist but casinos, bars, and restaurants. Then the TV announces a special guest: the masked pop idol D.D.D. will serve as the tournament's special MC.

Round one of the finals is a scavenger hunt for twenty gold bars hidden across an empty residential district. After a quick discussion, Ch'en and Lin Weisha decide to split up — the one point they agree on perfectly is that neither wants to work with the other.

Ch'en, moving alone, saves a cornered competitor named Mizuki and ends up surrounded by more than a dozen hostile rivals in the middle of an alley. Just as they close in from rooftops and walls on every side, Lin Weisha arrives late, drops silently from a rooftop, and clears out the rest.

blocks.cap: Forget the tedious investigating — this is more like it. A dozen-odd hostile competitors closing in on a single alley.

blocks.x: What Lin Weisha never tells Ch'en is what she actually found that day: she caught Raphaela of Team Grey Feather planting a mass of explosives throughout the residential district. Ch'en, none the wiser, only watches the broadcast confirm that favorites 'Dragon Rat Brawl' have advanced to round two in first place.

blocks.cap: The scoreboard reads 'LUNG wRATh.' Nobody's quite sure how 'Dragon Rat Brawl' ended up like this.

blocks.x: 3. Two Lungmen Ladies Dissect a City on the Sly
