blocks.cap: Yi Nam's trading post. A tribe that prefers business to fighting, but never to be underestimated in numbers.

blocks.x: There's also Croissant, who wraps up negotiations purely through gestures even when the language doesn't connect. She trades away ore for a few seashells and pebbles, then flashes a beaming smile the moment her counterpart turns their back.

Just then, an urgent message comes in — the village has been attacked. The culprit is Utage. She'd simply beaten up some passing Adakris and, before she knew it, somehow ended up the new chief of a massive tree-dwelling tribe.

Meanwhile, Blaze, who'd jumped into the jungle late, has a showdown with a strange girl, Flint. The result is a total win for Blaze. Once she comes to her senses, Flint declares on the spot that she's now Blaze's disciple.
With comrades gathered one by one like this, Gavial's group sets off once more, toward the Eunectes tribe.
7. Will of Gavial
Meanwhile, at that very hour, the other tribal chiefs are gathered in secret. The owner of the voice is, surprisingly, Tomimi. She reveals that she's the one who organized this entire rite herself.

blocks.cap: The only way to make Gavial stay here, Tomimi tells the other chiefs.

blocks.x: "Miss Gavial will surely be able to beat Zumama's giant machine. When that happens, I'll become Great Chief, and I'll make Miss Gavial stay here for good." The chiefs stir at her words, but in the end nod their agreement.
Everything, for Gavial's sake!
8. At the Waterfall, Misunderstanding and Reconciliation

Deep in the jungle, at a great waterfall, the group takes a moment to catch their breath. Utage excitedly shows off her swimsuit, Croissant insists her waterproof gear counts as a swimsuit too, and Ceobe splashes around half out of her mind.

blocks.cap: A brief rest at the jungle waterfall. Next stop, the Eunectes tribe.

blocks.x: Tomimi, who joins them late, seems oddly restless. Even when Gavial asks, she just brushes it off with "I have some things to prepare." No one else notices anything off — except for one person.
Arriving at Eunectes's settlement, Gavial confronts her bluntly. Did you force your people into dangerous mining?

But the truth was the exact opposite. Yota, sick as he was, had gone deep into the mine on his own — a place he'd been told never to enter — just to impress the Great Chief. "You idiot, I told you never to go in there!" Eunectes was the one who got angry first.

Gavial apologizes readily. The misunderstanding is cleared up, but the matter of the engine's return is a separate issue. "I'm not giving it back." "Then I'll have to make you." "That was my plan all along."
9. That Day, She Saw a Nomadic City
Before the duel, Eunectes brings up something from the past. One day when they were children, one of Sargon's massive nomadic cities had passed through this area.

blocks.cap: A giant hunk of iron passing before the eyes of the jungle's children. From that day on, Zumama quietly changed.

blocks.x: The sight that was, to Gavial, nothing more than a "wow, that's big," had been, to Zumama, a shock that turned her whole world upside down. From that day, she began teaching herself machinery and the Sargon language instead of fighting.

"You've always been like this, Gavial. As long as someone as strong as you exists, people will always believe fists are the only answer." Eunectes confesses she built the giant weapon to shatter that very belief.
10. The Final Battle, and the End of the Big Ugly
The rematch begins. The High Priest climbs back into the cockpit and pushes the weapon into maximum output mode. There's even a moment where Gavial's fists start to lose ground.

But suddenly, the overheated weapon starts to run wild. The High Priest is thrown clear, and Eunectes desperately tells Gavial's group to fall back — but Gavial only smiles and steps forward. "Gotta help out, right, Doctor?"
In the end, everyone joins forces to bring the weapon down. Eunectes calmly accepts her defeat, and hands Gavial back the engine — broken, but still usable.

The people hail Gavial, who felled the weapon, as their new Great Chief. After a moment's thought, Gavial declares, "While I'm gone, Tomimi will stand in for me!" The expression on Tomimi's face, now an accidental acting Great Chief, freezes in a strange way.
11. Tomimi's Rebellion, and a Tearful Tail-Spanking
When Gavial says she'll return to Rhodes Island to treat Eunectes, Tomimi does a complete about-face. "No. I can't let you go!"

She confesses that this entire rite was her own scheme, all along. To keep Gavial here, she'd spent years rallying the tribes using methods she'd learned only from books. "If Miss Gavial doesn't stay, I won't stop!"
She even resorts to taking hostages to press her point, but in truth, Dylan and Lancet-2 had been in on the act from the very start. The Doctor had already caught on to Tomimi's plan.

"Doctor, don't tell me you knew all along?" Gavial asks in surprise, and the Doctor lays out the clues gathered along the way, one by one — how Tomimi's words trailed off whenever Eunectes came up, how oddly flustered she got every time Gavial acted on impulse, and finally, the bazooka Dylan had secretly brought along.

That very bazooka turned out to be the one that had shot down the aircraft in the first place — an item found in Tomimi's village. From beginning to end, this whole affair had unfolded entirely within the palm of Tomimi's hand.

"You're not someone who gets cornered this easily, Tomimi." Gavial quietly approaches and makes her kneel. **"A troublemaker who won't listen naturally deserves a tail-spanking!"** Tomimi's crying echoes through the jungle for a good long while.
Back to Rhodes Island Once More

Once the commotion settles, Eunectes comes face to face for the first time with a talking machine, Lancet-2. After studying her intricate joints and smooth lines for a while, she blurts out, "Can I give you a hug?"
Eunectes makes an unexpected proposal to Gavial upon her return. She wants to learn machine engineering at Rhodes Island. Not to be outdone, Tomimi and Flint both tag along too.

The High Priest steps up to settle any worries about the tribe. "Tradition is just a ritual that brings people together — life doesn't collapse just because one custom disappears." The Adakris, meanwhile, have already moved on to arguing over 'who can build the cooler machine.'
Kal'tsit, receiving the return report, threatens to dock the repair costs from her pay, but in the end offers a short greeting.
Welcome back.
Gavial smiles and returns to her everyday life. She still doesn't regret the choice she made to become a doctor.
Whether leaving or returning, Acahualla was, to Gavial, simply home. A home that doesn't need to hold on to her, a home she can always come back to, whenever she likes.

act9d0.tagline: A woman who picked up a dead mercenary's name and belongings to become the new 'W,' and who only begins to set her own price after losing the brief warmth she once had. A bond born in a birch forest fades away beneath the shadow of the same tree.

chars.name: Hoederer
chars.desc: Looks cold, but in truth carries the heaviest sense of responsibility alone. Captain of a Sarkaz mercenary company.

chars.name: Ines
chars.desc: A mercenary vice-captain who wields Originium Arts that read shadows. Hides sharp eyes behind a razor-edged tongue.

chars.name: W
chars.desc: A Sarkaz mercenary who inherited a dead comrade's name and belongings to become the new 'W.' No one knows what she hides behind her playful tone.

chars.name: Theresa
chars.desc: A queen who lost Kazdel's throne but never lost the hearts of the Sarkaz. Fixing a broken door with her own hands, in one corner of an unidentified derelict ship.

chars.name: Kal'tsit
chars.desc: A doctor who stays by Theresa's side. Looks more machine than human, but treats everyone equally.

chars.name: Talulah
chars.desc: The passionate leader of Reunion. Once a pure symbol of resistance, but growing steadily more unrecognizable.

terms.name: Babel
terms.desc: The power controlling Kazdel's underworld. The true client behind the scenes, issuing contracts to the Sarkaz mercenaries while hiding its identity.

terms.name: Rhodes Island
terms.desc: The unidentified massive vessel Theresa discovered and named. Unfinished facilities still remain scattered throughout it.

terms.name: Reunion
terms.desc: An armed force formed under the banner of liberating the oppressed Infected. Led by Talulah.

terms.name: the Regent
terms.desc: Theresa's sibling. Seized Kazdel's throne while she was away.

terms.name: Chernobog
terms.desc: A city in Ursus. Reunion's stronghold, and the stage where much of this story crumbles.

terms.name: Candy Code
terms.desc: A bounty code used among Sarkaz mercenaries. The more candies, the higher the price on someone's head.

blocks.x: For Sarkaz mercenaries born in Kazdel, a name is a luxury. Since everyone expects to die or be forgotten anyway, they don't bother memorizing each other's names.
And yet, some names remain the property of the dead, passed on whole to whoever comes next. The day the sniper 'W' fell fighting from the rear, an unfamiliar woman appeared and picked up that name.
Years later, she loses the first person who ever asked for her real name, and comes face to face with the very things that took him from her.
1. Picking Up a Dead Mercenary's Name
Hoederer, who leads a company of Sarkaz mercenaries, and his vice-captain Ines have just lost the codename 'W' in battle. A sniper who fell buying time from the rear.
On the retreat, an unfamiliar Sarkaz woman follows on foot from behind. Dressed in rags, holding the dead W's sword and gun.
Ines wants to kill her on the spot, but Hoederer is thinking otherwise. He'd noticed she'd deliberately thrown stones at him several times along the way.
'Anyone in their right mind wouldn't take in some Sarkaz of unknown origin.' Ines snaps, and Hoederer laughs. 'Right, that's the difference between you and me.'
'You do know what it means to inherit a fallen soldier's weapon?' Hoederer asks, and the woman answers without hesitation. 'Of course.'
And so she inherits the dead mercenary's name and weapon, exactly as they were. A common thing in Kazdel—a nameless Sarkaz reborn under a dead person's name.

blocks.cap: From the moment he first saw her in the ruins about to collapse, he was certain. That she would become the finest Sarkaz warrior of all.

blocks.x: 2. How Many Candies on Your Head?
A few months later, a note with a code written on it turns up on an assassin's corpse. Instead of real names, there are aliases and codenames, with a few candy drawings beside each.

'The candy count is the bounty. The one with the most candies has the priciest head,' Hoederer explains, and the new W finds her own entry. 'The last one—settled. New one—annoying, ten, plus more depending on circumstances.'
'Seems your reputation is quite generous.' 'Probably because I buried a bunch of arms dealers alive in the forest last month.'

Hoederer also tells her about the dead previous W. An oddball who arbitrarily declared the day he killed a Laterano man and took his gun as his 'birthday,' always grinning yet cutting deep with his words.
'That guy had a stubborn streak. The type who fell for traps other people dug.' 'That's just being stupid, isn't it.'
From that day on, W became W.

blocks.cap: He'd only given her an excuse, but that was the first time she smiled. A smile the spitting image of the dead previous W's.

blocks.x: 3. The Tuition for Close Combat Is One Forearm
By the campfire, Ines quietly probes the new W. 'Where were you attached to before joining our team?' 'What, are you interviewing me now, after the fact?'
W pokes at Ines's weak spot. 'Heard you're pretty weak in close quarters, huh?' No sooner has she finished than Ines's blade grazes W's arm.
'I thought you were weak in melee—guess I let my guard down a little.' W admits it with a smile. 'A lesson learned for the price of one scratch on the arm—cheap, all things considered.'

blocks.cap: 'You and Hoederer really are alike, working the angles and leaving little traps behind.'

blocks.x: The joking is brief—a new mission comes down. Escort an unidentified transport team. Neither destination nor cargo disclosed.
That's when Hoederer warns her in a low voice. 'Using your Arts to scan the transport team would be a breach of contract. Don't underestimate them.'
During the escort, Ines can't help herself and scans the transport team with her Arts. A massive shadow—not a ship, more like some kind of 'frame.'
4. The Birch Forest, and the Identity of the Frame
There's a birch forest in northern Kazdel. Born in spring and dead by winter, a land where only Originium crystals remain, glinting under the moonlight.

blocks.cap: This was where the previous W died, and where the new W was born.

blocks.x: They're ambushed near that forest. The team scatters, and only W remains beside the unconscious Ines.
'What if I kept you alive to use as bait?' 'If you and I fought right now, who do you think would win?' Neither trusts the other, yet they fight back-to-back.
Unidentified elite soldiers keep pouring in without end. It felt like taking on the whole of Kazdel.
One of the elites grabs W and tries to win her over. 'The general will take a liking to you.' W scoffs. 'Isn't the more accurate word disposable—easy to use and easy to throw away?'
5. The Woman Unbothered in the Middle of a Battlefield
In the middle of the encirclement, a woman suddenly appears. An extremely strange person—not even Sarkaz, yet her expression doesn't change one bit even at the heart of the battlefield.
Ines was trembling. What made her tremble so, W couldn't tell.

blocks.cap: Everyone was looking in a different direction, at a different person. And just before losing consciousness, W saw a frail Sarkaz woman, one who radiated no hostility at all.

blocks.x: 6. A Queen Who Can't Even Fix a Broken Door
When she comes to, she's aboard an unfamiliar vessel. The main body of the transport team, temporarily taking in the wounded—called 'Rhodes Island.'
Wandering the corridors, W runs into an engineer wrestling with an automatic door, and beside her, an unidentified woman wrestling with the same headache. 'Call me Theresa.'
I don't want to forget anyone. And I shouldn't.
This Theresa—the one who fought the usurping Regent and united the Sarkaz—was fixing a broken automatic door. W can't help but laugh.
Theresa asks W her real name. 'W is one mercenary's name. What represents you is your name.' W has no name to answer with.
'It's common enough for a Sarkaz born in Kazdel.' 'If the day ever comes when you no longer have to live as W, you might get a new name then.'
Kal'tsit sends W back to the sickbay with a warning. 'Not everyone here welcomes you. I looked over your history—it's quite dangerous.' 'Isn't that mutual?'
Down the corridor, her eyes meet another figure with a hood pulled low. The one everyone calls 'Doctor.' W feels an inexplicable chill.
7. A Banner Must Fall
Hoederer confesses to Kal'tsit that Theresa was the only person who remembered his name and his family. And so, most of the Sarkaz decide to remain on Rhodes Island.
Only Hoederer, Ines, and a handful of mercenaries decide to leave. Of all the old comrades who started out together, only the two of them, in the end, remained alive.

In that old battle where they'd defended Rhodes Island's main ship and been decimated, the one holding the banner had been a child. Hoederer could have grabbed that banner, but he didn't. 'I never once liked that banner, not for a single moment.'
Ines sums up the three people she read with her Arts: Theresa was strong and gentle even amid sorrow, and Kal'tsit looked more machine than human, yet treated everyone equally.
Chess pieces just end up in the box, don't they. We're objects—lifeless objects.
And the last person, the one called 'Doctor,' wasn't commanding the battlefield—he was commanding the war itself.
8. The War Ended Too Fast
A few months later, Kazdel's fortunes turn overnight. A war that had held on for half a year comes to an end far too quickly.
The place that brings this news back to W is none other than the very ruins where she was first born. Completely collapsed.

W had grown unrecognizably composed. Quietly disposing of the Sarkaz who'd betrayed them one by one, she flatly declares none of this against her is revenge. 'Just think of it as an unemployed woman venting her frustration.'
Theresa was already dead, and the Regent now sits on Kazdel's throne. The three of them have nowhere left to return to.
'Now's our chance to get out of Kazdel.' Hoederer tells W. The plan is to infiltrate Reunion, the rising Infected faction in Ursus.

blocks.cap: The Regent had no interest in an emptied-out Kazdel either. Reunion, gathering the oppressed races together, was exactly the card he needed right now.
...They left Kazdel. W changed a great deal, and Ines was stronger than expected. So then, what about me?

blocks.x: 9. Talulah's Two Shadows
A few months into infiltrating Reunion, W has already become a field commander in Chernobog. Outwardly calm now, but that very composure only made Ines more uneasy.
Reunion soldiers press W over a failure. 'We're only in a partnership. If I flip this table, do you think Talulah would be pleased?' W fires back, unfazed.
Ines looks into Reunion's leader Talulah with her Arts. Once a pure symbol of resistance, she's now tearing down with her own hands the very thing she'd built.
'Her shadow... there are two of them. Not a trace of Arts—it looked more like an old ruin.' Ines tells Hoederer. But it's already too late.
Saying they must tell W, Ines shakes off Hoederer's restraint for the first time and rushes out alone.
A Reunion soldier crosses her path along the way. 'Talulah would have it, surely.' The moment those words are spoken, the entire street explodes.
It was birches... everything, so much like that place, even the tangled shadows...
Chernobog's roadside trees were birches. Exactly like that forest, where she and Hoederer had first picked W up.
10. Scattered
No one confirmed Ines's death. No body, no trace of an enemy remained. Hoederer cuts down, on the spot, the Reunion soldier who delivered that news.
He makes up his mind. To report all of this directly to the Regent, he decides to head for Londinium in Victoria.
'You're definitely going to die.' W tries to stop him, but Hoederer doesn't waver. 'Her Majesty Theresa remembered my name. Now, I finally know what I have to do.'
The one who greets him in Londinium is the Regent's confessor. The Regent already knew everything—Rhodes Island's return, and that W had taken over Reunion.
'There isn't enough chaos yet,' the confessor says. The Regent had no intention of destroying Reunion. If anything, his plan was to use it as an even bigger spark of chaos.
His feet held in place, Hoederer decides to secretly send a signal to W through a hidden transmitter buried in Kazdel's ruins, using a method prepared from the very beginning.
Before he left, W had once asked him a question. 'Can a mercenary's life be measured in money?' 'War decides that.' 'Is that so... but not anymore.'
Every Sarkaz mercenary has to set their own price for themselves. No one else needs to appraise it, and no one needs to be controlled by others' judgment.
To Live
After the massacre, in the quiet ruins of Chernobog, W meets two children carrying weapons. A boy named 'Rublev' with an injured leg, and his timid friend.
W points out the medicine left in a hospital and eggs the timid child on to go there alone. Rublev desperately tries to stop it. 'He can't do anything by himself!'
As time passes and his friend doesn't return, Rublev begins to fall apart. Resentment, anxiety, and even the belief that he himself was indispensable.
'You saved him, so isn't he probably trying hard for your sake too?' W asks, and Rublev grits his teeth. 'He can't do anything at all! It's all his fault!'
In this world, there is no one who can live without others. Absolutely no one.
'What decides a person's actions isn't where they're from, their status, or their past. It's the influences they've taken in through life, and how much they believe in those convictions,' W says.
In the moment he's dying, the friend comes running with a first-aid kit. Only then do the two children ask each other their real names for the first time.
Watching the two children smile, W murmurs quietly to herself.
Right? ...Talulah.

A woman born without a name survived by picking up a dead person's name. Only after losing both the man who nearly gave her a name and the people who protected it, did she finally begin to set her own name's price.
A bond born in a birch forest faded away beneath the same shadow. But that shadow is still speaking to someone, even now.

act11d0.tagline: A doctor sets out to find her missing best friend, and ends up taking on an entire village's troubles instead. She searches everywhere for the culprit — only to find the true culprit had already paid for his own crime, and was gone.

chars.name: Folinic
chars.desc: A Rhodes Island doctor who came to Wallmond searching for her missing best friend, Atro

chars.name: Suzuran
chars.desc: A young nine-tailed Vulpo operator, Folinic's junior, who tagged along with her

chars.name: Severin Hawthorne
chars.desc: Never without a cigarette — the governor (mayor) of Wallmond

chars.name: Tatiana
chars.desc: Severin's aide, and a village resident who was once the fiancée of his late son, Thorvald

chars.name: Atro
chars.desc: The Rhodes Island doctor hailed as a hero to the Infected, whom Folinic and Suzuran are desperately searching for

chars.name: GreyThroat
chars.desc: A Rhodes Island archer who grew afraid of the Infected after a past Reunion incident

chars.name: Ayerscarpe
chars.desc: The blunt bodyguard operator assigned to Catastrophe Messenger Leonhardt

chars.name: Click
chars.desc: An aspiring creator operator, rescued after getting stranded in the mountains while filming content for her channel

chars.name: Mudrock
chars.desc: Leader of 'Mudrock's Squad,' an armed band of Infected lingering around Wallmond

terms.name: Wallmond
terms.desc: A nomadic city drifting through western Leithanien. Once a bustling commercial district, now stranded in the aftermath of a Catastrophe.

terms.name: the Grand Rift
terms.desc: A massive canyon, thousands of meters deep, where active Originium lies exposed. Wallmond's attempt to avoid it sent it completely off its normal course.

terms.name: Zwölfton Street
terms.desc: The district in Wallmond where the Infected live together. A place that shows how Leithanien's 'generous' policy toward the Infected actually plays out.

terms.name: the Winterwish
terms.desc: The tribe that originally owned this mountain range, centuries ago. Most have since intermarried with Wallmond's people, leaving only a few of pure blood.

terms.name: Reunion
terms.desc: An armed organization advocating for the liberation of the Infected. Its name has come up repeatedly as the force behind the Wallmond riots.

terms.name: the Crisis Accord
terms.desc: A system through which Catastrophe Messengers trade requests for Catastrophe response. On the surface it looks like goodwill, but some within it will use any means necessary.

terms.name: L-44 'the Phonograph'
terms.desc: Wallmond's Arts-generating device for city defense. Any well-educated Leithanien citizen can operate it — and if overloaded, it becomes a weapon capable of vaporizing a person.

terms.name: Sarkaz
terms.desc: A demon-kin from Kazdel. The core fighting force of the armed Infected squad led by Mudrock, lingering around Wallmond.

blocks.x: Wallmond, a nomadic city of Leithanien, is famous for being unusually generous to the Infected — generous enough to provide them their own clinics and housing.

But in that very city, Rhodes Island's doctor Atro had gone silent for a month. Her close colleague Folinic, after twenty hours holed up in the comms room waiting for word, finally heads for Wallmond herself, bringing her junior Suzuran along.
The moment they arrive, the worst news lands on them: a 'Catastrophe' had struck the village where Atro was last seen, a month ago.

Wallmond's name literally means "the Eighth Moon," a nomadic city that once formed a bustling commercial district in western Leithanien alongside seven neighboring cities — until a massive canyon called the Grand Rift appeared and changed everything.
1. The Smoking Slacker Turns Out to Be the Governor

The first person Folinic and Suzuran run into in Wallmond is a rude middle-aged man clocking out with a cigarette in his mouth. He points out the general direction and walks off without a second glance.
It turns out that man was none other than Severin Hawthorne, governor of Wallmond — someone who could brazenly claim "I've already clocked out" with a straight face.

Leithanien is famous as a nation that treats the Infected generously. But to Folinic's eyes, that 'generosity' looked suspicious somehow — the shops and cafés long shuttered, the Infected quarter standing empty.

blocks.cap: A city once said to be a bustling commercial district. Wallmond's first impression now — a nomadic city where hardly a soul stirs.

blocks.x: Severin's aide Tatiana shows up late to guide the two of them to the council hall. But the way she calls Severin 'Minister' every single time, with almost excessive formality, hinted at some story behind it.

In truth, that very morning Severin had come back from a defeat at the village council. Against the decision to bury the Infected dead outside the village with no funeral rites, he alone had held out, refusing to let that happen to his dead son.
2. "This Isn't a Disappearance — It's a Murder"
A protest erupts in Zwölfton Street, the Infected quarter, and Folinic and Suzuran end up helping quell it before they know it. Only then does Severin reluctantly open his mouth.

A fire broke out at the makeshift Infected clinic Atro had set up, and her whereabouts have been unknown ever since. Eight people died — four village residents, Severin's only son Thorvald, and three Infected.
At Severin's claim of a 'simple accident,' Folinic finally can't hold back. "This is a murder case."

Worse, a rebellious Infected they meet during the crackdown drops a shocking line: "Not a single person made it out of that tent the day of the fire." A story completely at odds with Severin's account of an accident.

In the end, Tatiana decides to tell them everything. Clear traces of Arts were found at the fire scene, and the bodies showed no sign of having had any chance to flee — they were struck down in an instant.
Tatiana also passes along something Atro had said before. When Severin once asked if she wouldn't leave with the refugees, Atro had answered like this.
Rhodes Island does not abandon you.
3. The ID Card Found in the Ashes

Folinic's group wasn't the only one searching the burned-out clinic. Outside the village, the archer GreyThroat, the blunt bodyguard Ayerscarpe, and Click — an aspiring creator rescued after getting stranded in the mountains — were combing the same site.

Click was originally a "genius" who'd wandered into the Winterwish mountains to film content for her channel and ended up stranded. In front of her rescuers GreyThroat and Ayerscarpe, she talked a big game — though what she actually did was mostly just sneak a few photos.
What this group found was also a Rhodes Island supply crate. And on Folinic's side, amid a spot with no ash left at all, they pick up one item that, strangely, hadn't burned.

blocks.cap: "Ah... Big Sister Atro's ID card. This didn't burn..."

blocks.x: It was the very ID card she'd always hated wearing around her neck. As Suzuran quietly steps aside, Folinic allows herself exactly one moment to cry aloud, then returns to her doctor's face.

Folinic tilts her head at Severin's claim of suddenly knowing 'from experience' that the site was safe, despite saying he had no experience handling bodies. She doesn't forget to add a little jab either. "Quite composed, for someone who lost family."
4. Turns Out the Rioter Boss Is Actually Kind?
An armed band of Infected was also lingering at the burned-out site. Self-proclaimed 'Mudrock's Squad,' led by the Sarkaz warrior Mudrock.

blocks.cap: The burned clinic site. Before Folinic's group even arrived, other operators were already standing before the same ashes.

blocks.x: The villagers treated Mudrock as an object of fear, but all he actually did was lay down a single flower and mourn quietly. "Autumn here is bitterly cold... rest in peace."
He barely lets his guard down around either GreyThroat's group or Folinic's — "Talk without force behind it means nothing," he says — yet oddly, he never picks a fight first either.

Ayerscarpe kept scolding Click at every turn. Even as Click grumbled, "Did you really have to needle me at a time like this," it was always Ayerscarpe, behind GreyThroat's back, quietly looking out for her.
5. Severin's Second Secret
Severin takes Folinic and Suzuran down to the underground freezer. Among the eight frozen bodies, Folinic finally recognizes Atro.

blocks.cap: "If we don't keep the Infected bodies here, no one can feel at ease" — the chilling first impression of the underground freezer.

blocks.x: But partway through the conversation, Suzuran notices something unexpected about Severin's body. Severin, too, was actually an Infected with Oripathy. He only asks that this one fact be kept secret for the time being.
And then Severin produces the item — the core of L-44 'the Phonograph,' the city's Arts-based defense device. If overloaded, it's a weapon capable of vaporizing a person in an instant.

blocks.cap: "This much destructive power would obviously be illegal for civilian use, but..." The identity of the weapon that likely started the fire.

blocks.x: Severin also lets slip a passing remark. "A thirst for revenge — isn't that a feeling everyone carries?" At the time it sounded like idle talk, but looking back, it was a loaded line.
Folinic finally hardens her resolve. Suzuran quietly tries to stop her, but Folinic doesn't stop.
I will find the culprit. And after that... they'll pay the price.
6. The Last of the Winterwish
In truth, Folinic carried a story of her own. As a child, her mother had left alone for the Ursus wilderness, vowing to avenge her father — and never came back.
What am I supposed to take revenge on?
Right in front of that same Folinic, an unfamiliar song rings out through the streets — a lullaby-like tune laced with Arts, sung in some ancient language no one recognizes.

An elderly man who's come in for treatment confesses to Suzuran. He is the very last of the 'Winterwish,' he says — once he killed countless people out of a thirst for revenge, but in the end changed nothing.

The old man goes on. That revenge never once bent Leithanien's laws, nor the nomadic city's roaring engines, and one by one his fellow elders were put down by Severin. He alone had built a hideout beneath the village and lived there in hiding ever since.
And yet, on the very day of the fire, he confesses he felt neither rage nor relief. All he can recall, standing dazed before the raging flames, is that he was crying — nothing else.

And then, the decisive line: "I saw Biedermann, the Catastrophe Messenger I thought was dead, leading the rioters as they brought down the power plant." The list of suspects Folinic had been chasing for the fire had just gained one more, all of a sudden.

Biedermann had originally taken the blame for the Grand Rift accident, and had been assaulted by villagers more than once as a result. Severin himself admits that the one who'd first reached out to offer him work was none other than Atro.
7. The Burning Street
The riot on Zwölfton Street spirals out of control. Amid streets packed with wooden buildings, an unidentified giant stone-statue Arts construct even appears.

As Tatiana tries to protect the wounded, she watches, right before her eyes, the last scream of a resident engulfed in flames. Over that voice crying for help, the final moments of her dead fiancé Thorvald overlap.
At the scene, Suzuran also has to hear plenty shouted at her. To a rioter pressing her — why aren't you on our side, you're Infected too? — Suzuran finally snaps back.
Don't go deciding other people's fates for them!
Folinic and Suzuran both throw themselves into the fight. But no matter how many rioters they subdue, the flames consuming the street and the hatred everyone holds for one another refuse to die down.
8. The True Culprit, Already Dead
GreyThroat finally finds Biedermann. But he's already a cold corpse, struck down by frost Arts.
The riot doesn't stop even after Biedermann's death. Faced with the fact that the person she'd believed to be the true culprit was already dead, Folinic's rage has nowhere left to go.

Traces of the 'Crisis Accord' are also found in Biedermann's home. Ayerscarpe knows that organization's true nature well. Under the banner of standing against Catastrophes, some Catastrophe Messengers take things to an extreme, weighing human lives on a scale.

But even Ayerscarpe can't quite make sense of why 'the emotions of a Rhodes Island operator' specifically were factored into the calculation. It meant this fire might have been, from the very start, someone's calculated scheme to 'draw more attention.'
9. The Final Battle, and a Single Matchstick
As a last resort, Mudrock burns away his own life to raise a massive earthen giant. Rioters, villagers, and Rhodes Island alike all freeze before it.
Severin steps forward as bait, ready to die, and confronts Mudrock. "If you die, I'll leave this place," Mudrock had said — and in the end, he spares Severin and withdraws his squad.
The Sarkaz warrior mutters bitterly. "This village has never once come together with one heart, it seems." Even she, in the end, eases her hand rather than cut down the innocent.
Just before leaving, the Sarkaz warrior hands Severin a single matchstick. And with that, the riot that nearly swallowed Wallmond whole finally dies down.
Epilogue. So, Who Was the Culprit?

Even after the riot ends, the true culprit is never revealed. Biedermann had been the prime suspect, but all that's confirmed is that someone who'd only just arrived half a year earlier couldn't possibly have handled the latest defense equipment with such skill.

But the truth, in fact, had been far closer than anyone realized. Severin's dead son Thorvald had teamed up with the Crisis Accord to blackmail Biedermann into engineering this very riot — and had thrown himself into the fire as his own act of atonement. Neither Severin nor Folinic ever learns this truth, and the story closes without it.

Severin also shares an old story of his own — from the days he hunted down the Winterwish elders, the expression he saw on the last one's face right before they both went under the lake. Whether it was regret or release, he says that face still hasn't left him.

Tatiana's wounds run deeper in her heart than on her skin, and Severin decides to keep hiding that he's Infected while remaining governor. Rhodes Island, in the end, decides to lend a hand to Wallmond, with famine now looming.

Before leaving, GreyThroat crosses paths once more with Mudrock, who'd come alone to the ruins. To her, who'd grown afraid of the Infected ever since a past Reunion incident, Mudrock bids farewell, calling her 'Swallow.'
We'll probably meet again... since you're someone who fights for the Infected.

That night, an exhausted Severin falls asleep and dreams — of his dead son turned into a giant, sweeping away the clouds of Catastrophe, sealing the cracks in the earth, and carrying his homeland onward. In the dream he was smiling, but at the same time, for the first time since losing his son, he was crying.

In the end, Folinic never finds anyone left to take revenge on. The true culprit had already paid his own price, and all that remained was the village Atro had wanted to protect, and the people who would go on living in it.

act5d0.tagline: One night that starts with an entire highway blown sky-high and ends with candy raining from the heavens—the story of a rule-abiding new hire learning the rules at what looks like the least rule-bound company in the world. Underneath it all, there was an even older rule.

chars.name: Bison
chars.desc: Young heir of Mountain Dash Logistics. Sent tonight on his father's orders to intern at Penguin Logistics.

chars.name: Texas
chars.desc: Penguin Logistics' wolf-eared courier. A woman of few words who goes all-in the moment she's behind a wheel or holding a blade.

chars.name: Exusiai
chars.desc: Penguin Logistics' sharpshooter. Cheerful, free-spirited, and prone to causing trouble.

chars.name: Croissant
chars.desc: A Penguin Logistics staffer who swings a hammer. Thick accent, quick with the nagging and the math.

chars.name: Mostima
chars.desc: A Sankta courier back in Lungmen after a long absence. Rarely reveals her true identity.

chars.name: Sora
chars.desc: A senior courier who keeps watch over Penguin Logistics' bar, 'End of the Earth.'

chars.name: Jaye
chars.desc: A young man running a fish cake food stall in Lungmen. Keeps the shop with Waai Fu.

chars.name: Waai Fu
chars.desc: A college student working part-time at the detective agency. Can't stand seeing injustice.

chars.name: Emperor
chars.desc: Boss of Penguin Logistics. A self-proclaimed artist who calls himself the emperor of rap.

chars.name: Rat King
chars.desc: An old man rumored to rule Lungmen's slums. No one is certain of his true identity.

terms.name: Sourin
terms.desc: An annual festival held in Lungmen. Originally a day to see off the souls of the dead, but these days it's better known as a festival full of candles and candy.

terms.name: Penguin Logistics
terms.desc: A private logistics company in Lungmen. On paper, an ordinary delivery service, but in reality it's known as the most unpredictable armed group in Lungmen.

terms.name: End of the Earth
terms.desc: The bar Penguin Logistics actually uses as its base. Despite the grand name, it's just an ordinary bar.

terms.name: Mountain Dash Logistics
terms.desc: A conglomerate that controls much of Lungmen's private courier business. Led by Bison's father.

terms.name: Sicilian
terms.desc: What the mafiosi who came from Siracusa call themselves. Originally a term for one of the roots of Siracusa's Twelve Families.

terms.name: Siracusa
terms.desc: The mafia's home city. Both Capone and Gambino were pushed out of there and drifted into Lungmen.

terms.name: Lungmen Guard Department
terms.desc: The agency in charge of Lungmen's public order. Rarely steps in on a commotion like tonight's.

blocks.x: Lungmen's nights have two faces. Beneath the daytime order, another set of rules flows through the back alleys. Tonight is one of the special ones—Sourin, the festival honoring the souls of the dead.

And today, Bison, the young heir of Mountain Dash Logistics, has been sent by his father's order to intern at a company called 'Penguin Logistics.' On paper, an ordinary logistics company—by rumor, a place no one can quite pin down.
Tonight, Bison is about to learn firsthand why those rumors run so thick.
1. First Day of the Internship, Blown Up Alive on the Highway
The story begins in a shabby bar on Yalu Road. Two Siracusa mafia bosses who crossed over, **Capone** and **Gambino**, were in the middle of a card game, growling at each other.
Who would take Lungmen, who was the 'real' Sicilian—that was the argument. Then a customer at the next table cuts in. "How about you two take it outside with a bottle?"

blocks.cap: The shop owner's true identity: Emperor, boss of Penguin Logistics. He answers with two gunshots instead of a greeting.

blocks.x: At the same moment, **Bison**, the heir of Mountain Dash Logistics, was dozing off in his car, ahead of his first meeting with Penguin Logistics.

blocks.cap: The young master, sleeping soundly. He still has no idea someone has already planted a bomb beneath the highway.

blocks.x: Boom. The entire highway explodes. It was an attack aimed at Bison.

Emerging from the smoke is Penguin Logistics' sharpshooter **Exusiai**. Right behind her come the wolf-eared driver **Texas** and hammer-wielding **Croissant**, but the place they all end up fleeing to is, of all spots, a dead-end alley.

blocks.cap: "Welcome to Penguin Logistics, my tourists!" The boss, appearing with a spotlight, offers the mafia their first greeting.

blocks.x: 2. The New Hire's Welcome Party Comes With a Candy Bomb
The place they barely escape to is Penguin Logistics' base. In the chaotic interior, Bison tries to politely introduce himself, but nobody's listening.
The boss is looking for a cigar, Croissant confesses to having sold off a box of LPs, and Texas just sighs, "We're a logistics company—why do we keep getting dragged into these turf wars?"
Then someone finds a cute candy box under a chair. Just as Exusiai, delighted, is about to open the lid—
Texas shouts, "Get away, it might be a trap." But it's already too late.
As it explodes, everyone scatters into the street, and the real target of the Siracusa mafia is revealed. It was Bison, all along.
If even we stopped following the rules, Lungmen would've ascended to the heavens long ago. Yo Texas, pop the sunroof!
What follows is a car chase armed with rubber bullets and slingshots. "No live rounds in Lungmen city limits—dis is da rule, okay?" is apparently the boss's one and only principle.
3. The Young Master Chases a Motorcycle on a Bicycle
After a frantic chase, the Penguin Logistics crew ends up leaving Bison behind on the roadside entirely. "Where's Bison?" "Wasn't he supposed to be with you guys?"
Left alone, Bison borrows a bicycle from a passerby and puts on quite a show chasing down the motorcycle gang, but he ends up caught by mafia boss **Gambino** in the end.
Meanwhile, across town, at a fish cake stall in Lungmen Central Park. Owner **Jaye** and part-timer **Waai Fu** are serving customers.
But one unfamiliar customer particularly stands out. A woman with horns, taking her time, saying she has plenty of it while she waits for her fish cakes.

blocks.cap: "Take your time, I've got plenty of it." A courier back in Lungmen after a long while, quietly taking in the scenery.

blocks.x: This woman is **Mostima**. Before long, an explosion echoes from the direction of the highway, and Mostima murmurs to herself. "A bit earlier than scheduled."
4. A Candle Stuck in Her Horn, and a Mysterious Savior
Bison, being dragged off by Gambino, is thrown off the road and crashes onto a pile of Sourin decorative candles. He survives, but with a candle stuck right in his horn.
The one who saved him is none other than Mostima. "Hey there, Mr. Candle? Just hold still for a second." They have to hide together to avoid a mafia patrol.
Bison asks who she is. Mostima answers with a smile instead.
"Penguin Logistics' courier, name's Mostima. I just prefer working alone, is all."
A name he'd heard from his father, but not with good rumors attached. Bison finds himself, half-dazed, following her through the streets of Lungmen at night.
5. The True Meaning of Sourin, Told in a Graveyard
Claiming it's a shortcut, Mostima takes Bison to a graveyard in the middle of the city. "Lungmen might be the only city on Terra that builds a graveyard right in its center."

blocks.cap: Amid mourners dressed in black, the living and the dead walk side by side on the night of Sourin.

blocks.x: Mostima tells him in a low voice. Sourin was originally a day to send off the souls of those who had passed and soothe their lingering attachments. "Though everyone's forgotten that by now."

At that moment, in the slums, mafia men were grabbing a resident and roughing him up while demanding to know Rat King's whereabouts. Passing by and witnessing this were fish cake shop's Jaye and Waai Fu.
The two of them easily beat down the mafia and chase them off without a word. "Feels like a sea urchin left three days in a fridge with the power cut off," Jaye adds, in his own way of trash-talking.
The fallen resident, barely catching his breath, warns them. The mafia's next target is the fish cake shop owner, Uncle Dong.
Somewhere in that same graveyard, that very **Uncle Dong** is waiting for an old friend. The old man rumored to rule the slums, **Rat King**.
"Guess we should start thinking about where we're gonna settle down soon," Uncle Dong jokes. Rat King answers briefly. "As long as Rat King's still alive, it's not your time to die yet."

And Bison learns one more thing. That today's whole internship was actually a favor his father had secretly asked of Mostima. "Take Bison and show him the world." Even his grades were apparently in Mostima's hands.
6. Why the Bar Is Called 'End of the Earth'
After all the twists and turns, they finally reach Penguin Logistics' real hideout, the bar 'End of the Earth.' Despite the name, it's just an ordinary bar—only the interior is oddly shiny.
Texas and the others, gathered mid-party, welcome Bison warmly. "Three drinks as a lateness penalty—but since you can't drink, make it nine sodas."
A merry party with not a shred of remorse in sight. Then, from somewhere, a familiar voice calls out. "So, what kind of trouble did you Texas lot get into this time?"
Mostima joins them as if it's nothing at all. Everyone treats their reunion after four years as no big deal. Only Bison is left utterly baffled.
That's short-lived, though—mafia boss Gambino's raid begins. Bottles fly through a hail of bullets, and every expensive liquor in the bar becomes a weapon.
Let's make these fine drinks bloom—by alcohol, and by chemistry both.
In the end, Gambino is thoroughly beaten by Texas and flees. The bar is completely wrecked, but as long as you call it 'Emperor style,' that's apparently the end of it.
7. Capone's Betrayal, Gambino's Final Hour

In truth, Gambino's subordinate **Capone** had been harboring his own hidden agenda. He believed he'd held his ground in Lungmen far longer than Gambino, who came in leading an organization that had already failed in Siracusa.
Capone sends a subordinate to sabotage the plan, and finally shows up before Gambino himself, drawing his blade. A duel between two men from the same family.
As the remaining mafia thugs who'd been beating people up in the slums get tangled in too, it turns into total chaos—and the one who shows up to sort it all out is none other than Rat King.
You've already crossed the line.
In the end, Gambino loses his life at Capone's hand, and Capone kneels before Rat King. But this whole betrayal drama, it turns out, had been Rat King's script from the very start.
Even the subordinates standing by Capone's side were secretly Rat King's people all along. "You'd already been standing on our ground." The mafia from Siracusa never had a chance from the start.
8. The Sourin Party, and the Boss's Sudden Death
Meanwhile, downtown, the real Sourin festival was in full swing. Fish cake shop owner Jaye and part-timer Waai Fu suddenly pick a fight with Texas and her crew, whom they haven't seen in ages.

"I'm going to fix that rotten habit of yours dragging innocent citizens into things every single time," Waai Fu declares, launching a one-on-one showdown with Texas. Onlookers, mistaking it for street theater, cheer them on.
Then suddenly a voice booms out from the speakers. "Alright everyone, wash out your ears and get ready to worship me." The boss has started an impromptu performance.

blocks.cap: In the middle of the festival crowd, the boss takes the stage—and a shadow quietly closes in from behind.

blocks.x: At that instant, a blade sinks in from behind. "Sleep tight, Mr. Emperor. Happy Sourin." It was Rat King.
The crowd freezes. Lungmen's greatest star has just been murdered right in front of them. But not a single member of Penguin Logistics is even remotely surprised.
9. Sand and Flame, and the Mysterious Box

Rat King grabs the mic and calmly makes an announcement. "Somewhere in this party venue, the Siracusans have hidden a little gift—see if you can find it before time runs out." A bomb-hunting game has begun.
At the same time, Penguin Logistics finds itself surrounded, layer upon layer, by the crowd and the remaining mafia. The one who cuts straight through the situation head-on is Mostima.

blocks.cap: "Is there anything in this whole city of Lungmen that could actually take you down?" Mostima, having reclaimed her Arts staff, faces Rat King head-on.

blocks.x: Rat King, in the body of an old man, was a true monster who freely commanded sandstorms and barriers. Yet Mostima doesn't back down.

Bison, who'd been watching nervously from a corner, makes up his mind too. "When it comes to enduring things, I won't lose to anyone." Even giving his butler an order, he dives into the fight on his own judgment for the first time.
In the end, a single sniper shot forces Rat King to retreat, and the remaining mafia scatter in the confusion. What's left is nothing but a large, unidentified iron box the Siracusans supposedly hid.
10. Happy Sourin, a Sky Raining Candy
Before the box, timer still ticking, Bison decides to trust his shield and block it with his own body. "Come what may, I've got to at least try."
Mostima stays by his side too. "Then I'll watch over you to the very end. Maybe I can even soften the blow a little."
The timer hits zero, and with a roar, something bursts open. But it's not hot at all.

blocks.cap: Instead of an explosion, candy rains down across the whole sky. The box had never held a bomb—it was full of candy from the very start.

blocks.x: A single note falls with it. "Happy Sourin." It was Rat King's final prank. The bomb the Siracusans supposedly hid never existed anywhere at all.
And so, the loudest night's farce in Lungmen finally draws to a close.
Epilogue. The Young Master Leaves, the Rumors Remain

An after-party is held at the ruined 'End of the Earth.' Everyone clinks glasses as if nothing happened, but Bison simply can't adjust to it. "What in the world were all those things that happened tonight even for?"
Mostima answers calmly. "Just someone livening up the party with his own life, that's all." Then she asks—will he stay with Penguin Logistics?
Bison shakes his head. "I won't be staying here. But I do think a courier like this, a way of living like this, can exist." His internship was over, but what he'd learned stayed with him.

At the same time, in front of the graveyard, Mostima offers up a prayer alone, exchanging a brief spat with the mysterious Laterano supervisor who's watched over her for so long. This person's codename changes every month—this month it's the 'Testifier of Tribulation.'

blocks.cap: "Pfft, what? Why?" Old comrades teasing each other, and one secret of Laterano no one else knows.

blocks.x: Behind the slums, Rat King and Lungmen's real power broker Wei Yanwu quietly clink glasses. "The road we walk—no one's left to carry it on after us anymore." A single offhand remark, left lingering with unusual weight.
Around that time, in an alley elsewhere, a familiar wolf appears before the barely-escaped Capone and the dying Gambino. "Family, huh, that's a nostalgic word. You two happen to run into Texas?"
It was **Lappland**, an old acquaintance from Siracusa. What happened to the two of them after that, nobody confirms.

The next day, rumors spread all over Lungmen. "They say Emperor got stabbed in the gut and just walked out fine." "Heard he's taken bullets and bombs before too, and walked away just as fine." Nobody quite believes it, but nobody can fully deny it either.

In the end, everyone settles on the same conclusion. "Well, he's Emperor, isn't he? Wouldn't be called an emperor for nothing." And so the night of Sourin fades away, leaving behind one rumor, sweet and absurd as candy.

act3d0.tagline: A mayor's daughter sets out to save her hometown from an imminent volcanic eruption, only to discover that the town was already fated to be abandoned. A story with a refrain that says some things never disappear, no matter what.

chars.name: Ceylon
chars.desc: The mayor's only daughter and an Originium researcher. Even though the observatory says there's nothing wrong, she chases signs of volcanic abnormality alone.

chars.name: Schwarz
chars.desc: Ceylon's bodyguard since childhood. Gentle like an older sister, but hiding a chillingly sharp set of skills.

chars.name: Provence
chars.desc: A Catastrophe Messenger. Free-spirited, and loves poking around everywhere under the excuse of 'field surveys.'

chars.name: Skyfire
chars.desc: A scholar who studies volcanic ecosystems. No matter who she's talking to, she has to say everything on her mind.

chars.name: Hellagur
chars.desc: A veteran of Rhodes Island. Reads any situation with the cool eyes of someone who's seen it all.

chars.name: Ifrit
chars.desc: A young caster who wields an Originium flamethrower. Has an uncanny nose for a good fight.

chars.name: Vigna
chars.desc: A cheerful fighter who kicks down doors first and asks questions later, delivery-themed.

chars.name: Amiya
chars.desc: Rhodes Island's deputy head, on vacation in Siesta with the Doctor.

chars.name: Cronin
chars.desc: The mayor's secretary and Siesta's Catastrophe Messenger. Dismisses Ceylon's warnings, insisting there's nothing to worry about with the volcano.

chars.name: Herman
chars.desc: The mayor of Siesta and Ceylon's father. Has been away a long time, supposedly on a business trip to a new development zone.

terms.name: Siesta
terms.desc: A tourist city set between a volcano and the sea. Famous as a source of obsidian, it hosts the Obsidian Festival every year.

terms.name: Obsidian Festival
terms.desc: A music festival held citywide for half a month. During this stretch, Siesta is packed shoulder-to-shoulder with tourists.

terms.name: Obsidian
terms.desc: A special ore found in Siesta's volcano. Once mined heavily for its supposed Oripathy-suppressing properties, mining is now banned.

terms.name: Originium Bug
terms.desc: An infected creature that turns violent in response to Originium. Siesta's Originium bugs live deep in the volcano, feeding on obsidian.

terms.name: Catastrophe Messenger
terms.desc: A profession dedicated to investigating and spreading word of catastrophe-related dangers. Both Provence and Cronin do this work.

terms.name: Oripathy
terms.desc: An incurable disease caused by Originium contamination. In Siesta, the infection rate is said to be low thanks to obsidian.

blocks.x: Once a year, Siesta holds the Obsidian Festival. Tourists buy obsidian souvenirs, and bands set up stages all across the city through the night. Amiya and the Doctor have come to this resort town, recommended by Kal'tsit.
There was exactly one thing nobody noticed: the temperature at the volcano east of the city wasn't quite what it usually was.

Provence and Skyfire, wandering the volcano's foothills on a 'field survey,' were the first two to notice the anomaly. Soon, Provence's voice grows serious over the radio. 'Doctor, this might be a matter of life or death for this whole city...'

Meanwhile, Amiya ends up helping Gummy sell mango smoothies at a beach stand. 'Following the Doctor around all day is exhausting you more than it should!' Gummy grabs Amiya and puts an apron on her before she can protest.

On the radio, the mayor's aide, who also serves as the city's Catastrophe Messenger, is bragging about obsidian. 'The volcano is like a friend to us — please, everyone, rest easy.' The voice belonged to **Cronin**.
1. Three Catastrophe Messengers Meet at the Volcano (One of Them Is the Mayor's Daughter)

Ceylon was on her own at first. She'd handed the observatory her volcanic anomaly data, only to get a verdict of 'no abnormality.' So there was only one option left: find real experts among the tourists flooding in for the festival.

blocks.cap: The mayor's daughter's volcano survey report. The observatory sent it back with three words: 'no abnormality.'

blocks.x: Around then, at the volcano's foothills, Provence and Skyfire were bickering over an obsidian sample they were digging up. Strange smells, abnormal temperatures, and now a sudden swarm of Originium bugs.
After rescuing a young woman being chased by that very swarm, they learn her name: **Ceylon Doicos**. The mayor's daughter, and an Originium researcher.
Ceylon, who'd been investigating alone, and Provence and Skyfire, who'd just noticed the same anomaly — the three of them reach the same conclusion: something is wrong with this volcano.

For Skyfire, honestly, this was never just a vacation. 'As a researcher myself, I was quite curious. Chances to observe a volcano's ecosystem firsthand are rare, after all.' Provence, having given up trying to stop her, is half-resigned to it.
I need your help, so that Siesta can get through this crisis safely.
2. The Mayor's Daughter Brings Evidence to City Hall, Gets Treated Like a Wanted Criminal
After a 40-minute remote analysis from volcanologist Eyjafjalla, the conclusion is in: this volcano will erupt within two to four weeks.
Ceylon takes this data to city hall — to **Cronin**, the secretary running the government in place of her father, who's away on business.
Cronin's answer is crystal clear: 'These suspicious tourists have deceived you, miss.' He then orders his guards to seize them all.
Cronin is almost impressively glib about it. He claims rumors of a volcanic eruption have popped up before, and dismisses this one too as just a scheme by people jealous of Siesta's prosperity.
In an instant, the mayor's own daughter becomes a wanted fugitive.
Round up every last one of them trying to ruin the Obsidian Festival.
Fleeing to the beach, Ceylon runs into a familiar face — **Schwarz**, the bodyguard who's watched over her since childhood. But what comes out of her mouth isn't what Ceylon expected.
'You need to listen to Master Cronin.'
3. A Young Caster, an Old Soldier, and a Bodyguard's Unfamiliar Face

Cornered, the group is suddenly joined by a young caster wielding an Originium flamethrower, **Ifrit**. 'If something fun's happening, you gotta call me!' — and with that, she roasts the guards on the spot.
Right after, Rhodes Island's veteran soldier **Hellagur** shows up and easily subdues Schwarz's remaining men. But the moment he crosses blades with Schwarz herself, his expression stiffens.

Silver hair, golden eyes, a Feline woman wielding a black crossbow. Hellagur realizes he's seen that face before — the legendary assassin who reportedly toppled an entire family in Columbia, and vanished a year ago.
If that killer we just talked about really is her, she's clearly a serious threat to us as well.

Hellagur has one more rumor to share. In the Columbian frontier, a lawless patrol that had been pillaging the region vanished, member by member, hunted down in the mountains over the course of a month. The one survivor who made it back to the city came back completely out of his mind.
Hellagur leaves Ceylon, who'd been eavesdropping, with one last remark: even the person you trust most isn't guaranteed to have no secrets.
4. The Story of a Kid Who Used to Build Sandcastles
All Ceylon asked the Doctor for was a walk. As a child, hardly anyone came to this beach, so she used to build sandcastles alone.
Her mother died giving birth to her. From that day, her father buried himself in work, and father and daughter lived like strangers sharing the same house.
Schwarz was the one who stayed by her side instead. Like an older sister, and her only friend.
During her time studying in Victoria, Ceylon never quite adjusted to life there. But by the time she came back, she'd grown closer to being a Victorian than a Siestan.
But that Schwarz was suffering from Oripathy. There was exactly one reason Ceylon started studying Originium: to cure her.
I'll cure that, whatever it takes! I'll beat Oripathy myself.
And now that same Schwarz is treating her as an enemy. Ceylon quietly asks herself — was everything she had, perhaps, fake all along?
Hearing the Doctor's answer, Ceylon stands back up. The one she should be angry at isn't the darkness in front of her, but Cronin — the one truly threatening this city.
Even if no one stands with me, I will save this city!
5. Special Delivery (Door Included)
Ceylon's plan was simple: seize the broadcast station next to Garrison Amusement Park and tell the whole city the truth. But Schwarz was already waiting in front of the station.
'This will only cause greater chaos.' Schwarz blocks her path. Ceylon doesn't back down either. 'Why won't you treat me the way you used to, Schwarz!'
Meanwhile, at Cronin's hideout, another commotion is unfolding. A knock at the door, followed by a young voice: 'Anybody home? Special delivery!'
Open up! Delivery!
The whole door goes flying. **Vigna** waltzes in, all innocence, and makes short work of Cronin's men.

'Meteorite told me, sometimes a little violence is necessary!' Vigna, looking mighty proud of herself, points at Cronin's back as he flees out a window. 'That guy's in for it now — should've just stayed put instead of running.'
6. The Answer Was 'Friend,' Not 'Bodyguard'
In front of the broadcast station, Ceylon finally asks the question she really wanted to ask. 'What was I to you, really?'
Only then does Schwarz open up — about everything she's done, about her life before she came to the Doicos household.
Ceylon already knew. Not all of it, but enough. And knowing it, the only thing she has left to say is:
'I'm sorry.'
That's what friendship is supposed to be, isn't it? It's not friendship if only one side keeps giving.
Schwarz finally gives the answer she's held back for so long. 'You are my friend, miss. Always.
And in front of Cronin's men, Schwarz reveals her real mission — one more thing besides the mayor's orders: what Ceylon had just asked of her — to protect her.
Vaulting off walls, spinning through the air, dodging every bullet without a wasted motion — Ceylon watches, utterly spellbound. 'Wow, that's incredible! You're amazing, Schwarz!'
7. How It Feels When Your Subordinate of 15 Years Stabs You in the Back
Just as Ceylon and Schwarz retake the broadcast station, Cronin turns up again, having fled there. And standing there with him is Mayor **Herman**, supposedly away on a business trip.
Herman already knew everything — that the banned obsidian was still in circulation, that Oripathy patients were being mistreated. All of it was Cronin's doing.
Everything I did was for this city! Who do you think taught me that!
Cronin brings up his days drifting with nowhere to go, sleeping the nights away in a trash bin. The one thing he learned then: a new life needs money, nothing else.

Cronin admits it outright. Rather than spend obsidian profits treating the infected, he'd rather let the volcano erupt. His plan was to hide out through the disaster, then return once the city was ash to mine it all over again.
Cornered, Cronin plays his last card. The volcano.

blocks.cap: The ground shook. Cronin's final card wasn't a threat — it was a real catastrophe.
'Turn to dust with this city!' With Cronin's declaration, the volcano flares red-hot.

blocks.x: The evacuation route is already under Cronin's control. That's when Eyjafjalla cuts in urgently over the radio — there's a way to slow the volcanic activity.
8. Read as 'A Bug,' Spelled 'A Walking Volcano'
Provence, Skyfire, and Ceylon head off in search of that method — deep into a cavern carved along the obsidian vein.

Skyfire's explanation is simple. All these anomalies are caused by the volcano's Originium bugs struggling to expand their territory. All they need to do is subdue the one queen bug leading the swarm.
But the moment that 'one bug' reveals itself inside the cavern, all three of them go silent.

blocks.cap: A roar echoes through the cavern. A shape all but a walking volcano rises slowly out of the darkness.

blocks.x: 'Can you even call that a bug?' Provence mutters. Skyfire charges in instead of answering.
After a fight that leaves them scorched all over and Skyfire's skirt in tatters, the three manage to drive the giant Originium bug back to its original habitat. The immediate crisis is over.
'But we need to get back to Siesta. There are still people waiting for us...' Ceylon murmurs, on the verge of collapse. Provence quietly offers a shoulder to lean on.

'If people hadn't destroyed the Originium bugs' habitat first, this probably wouldn't have...' Provence trails off. Skyfire waves her hands, not wanting to hear the rest, muttering that she's never had a vacation that burned her skirt up this badly before.
Epilogue. What Remains in a City Bound to Vanish
Waiting for the returning Doctor was Amiya, arms crossed. 'Starting something this dangerous in a city we don't even have a contract with!' Gummy patched up the wounds, but couldn't stop the lecture.
Still, Amiya soon smiles and changes the subject. 'Just be more careful next time, okay?' Then she drags the Doctor off to the beach, insisting on collecting seashells.

A few days after everything settles, Herman asks the Doctor for a walk. The whole way, he talks about his wife, Barbara — how, along this sea where she now rests, he built Siesta into a paradise for her.
And Herman says he already knew — about Cronin's corruption, about the volcano's danger. But what he was really hiding was something else.
Siesta was no longer his paradise.

According to newly confirmed geological data, this city will eventually be swallowed by lava. That's why Herman has long since been preparing a new nomadic city. Once this Obsidian Festival ends, Siesta's people will move to that new land.
As long as the people of Siesta exist, Siesta will exist forever. Young people like them — they're the real 'Siesta.'
Ceylon asks to join Rhodes Island. Schwarz comes with her. Her father's blessing was already given.
So, Doctor — I'll be counting on you from here on out too.

blocks.cap: Bags packed, the two walk out side by side. The young lady of the Doicos house and her bodyguard, now Rhodes Island's newest recruits.

blocks.x: The sandcastles Ceylon built as a child would always crumble once the waves came in. Siesta, too, will vanish one day, just the same. But the hand that always helped her rebuild that sandcastle is still right there beside her.

1stact.tagline: A tale of a small mounted officer tangled up with a woman rumored to have 'wiped out an entire village,' who ends up bleeding in someone else's place while chasing a dead knight's treasure. What's left behind afterward are faces quite different from the rumor.

chars.name: Grani
chars.desc: A former Victorian cavalry officer, now a Rhodes Island spearwoman operator on assignment in Kazimierz

chars.name: Chloe
chars.desc: The young chief of Dewdrop Village. Willing to become a hostage herself to protect her village

chars.name: Big Bob
chars.desc: A seasoned bounty hunter skilled in wilderness survival and trap disarming. Offers cooperation with mysterious conditions attached

chars.name: Skadi
chars.desc: A mysterious bounty hunter rumored to have wiped out an entire village. Everyone who crosses her path fears her

chars.name: the Captain
chars.desc: Leader of the bounty hunter squad rampaging through the Dewdrop Village area. Relentlessly pursues Grani's group

chars.name: Specter
chars.desc: A Rhodes Island operator plagued by recurring nightmares, whose memories of herself are far from whole

chars.name: Nearl
chars.desc: The senior Rhodes Island operator who assigned Grani her mission

chars.name: Kal'tsit
chars.desc: The one who leads Rhodes Island. Every deal runs on the strict principle of fair exchange

terms.name: Kazimierz
terms.desc: A mountain nation beyond Victoria's border. The homeland Grani's ancestors left behind, and the stage for this incident.

terms.name: Dewdrop Village
terms.desc: A small mountain village on Kazimierz's northern border. Became a target for bounty hunters because of the legend of a dead knight's treasure.

terms.name: the Blood Saint
terms.desc: A fearsome nickname whispered among bounty hunters, trailed by rumors that she once wiped out an entire village.

terms.name: Reunion
terms.desc: An armed organization made up of the Infected. Big Bob was once part of it, before breaking away.

terms.name: Columbia
terms.desc: A distant nation said to let even the Infected own their own land and fields. The destination Big Bob and his brothers are heading for.

terms.name: the Treasure Key
terms.desc: A key to a treasure chest passed down only through generations of village chiefs. It drains the blood of anyone who uses it without Kazimierz blood in their veins.

blocks.x: Kazimierz is an unfamiliar country even to Victorians across the border. The knights of the capital had no time to spare for a remote mountain village.
Into that gap crept the legend of a dead knight — treasure that only Kazimierz's ancient bloodline could unlock, said to be sleeping somewhere in a nameless mountain.

On the day bounty hunters chasing that rumor turned the border settlement of Dewdrop Village into a wreck, a young spearwoman on assignment from Rhodes Island sets foot on Kazimierz soil for the first time.
1. The Hanging Chief and the Little Cavalry Officer
In a dark room, the hanging girl hadn't had a drop of water in over a day. The bounty hunters just kept pressing her for the treasure's location.

blocks.cap: A full day strung up, and still she hadn't said a word.

blocks.x: Then an unfamiliar voice cuts in. "To think that all the way out here in Kazimierz... I'd find thugs running wild like this."
A little girl with a single spear knocks the bounty hunters flat in an instant and frees the girl. Her name is **Grani** — a former Victorian cavalry officer, now an operator of Rhodes Island.

Before her savior, the girl introduces herself. "...Yes, I'm **Chloe**, chief of Dewdrop Village. I've only held the post for half a year, but I still have a chief's pride." A chief of half a year, and she'd been hanging there as a hostage the whole time.
2. The Dead Knight Is Still Paying the Village's Wages

Barely past introductions, Chloe already had Grani pegged wrong. "Are you the newly appointed knight of Kazimierz?" Grani corrects her. "No, no! I'm neither Kazimierz nor a knight. I'm a Victorian cavalry officer! ...Oh, and I'm a girl!"
Dewdrop Village has lived off this mountain land, self-sufficient, for generations. Not wealthy, but at least peaceful.

blocks.cap: A mountain village carried on since the ancestors' time — Dewdrop Village.

blocks.x: The peace broke a month ago, when a bounty hunter found a knight's coffin and gold coins deep in the mountains.
When an ancient Kazimierz knight meets death, their spirit and wealth are buried in some nameless corner of their homeland, still guarding that land even after death.
The rumor snowballed. Even baseless talk that "someone in Dewdrop Village knows where the treasure is" spread, and the village had already become a den of bounty hunters.

blocks.cap: A single old Kazimierz gold coin swallowed an entire village whole.

blocks.x: What Chloe actually wants isn't the treasure itself. Hire mercenaries with it, drive out the bounty hunters, and bring peace back to the village — that's all.
3. The Woman from the Rumors Appears
As Grani's group tries to slip out of the village, a bounty hunter squad appears. But it isn't Grani the squad members actually fear — it's someone else.
A man called the Captain goes flying clear into the river in an instant, and his men start fleeing, shouting "It's the Blood Saint!"

blocks.cap: Everyone who stands before me dies. Every weapon swung at me shatters.

blocks.x: A red-eyed woman mutters in an unfamiliar tongue. (Ægirian) "If you want to die, step forward." Grani finds that back oddly familiar. "That person... might be one of my comrades..."
The problem is, that "comrade" seems to have never even heard of the mission Grani was given.
4. I'd Like to Hire a Bounty Hunter
Next to appear is another bounty hunter, **Big Bob**. He clears out a swarm of bugs with a stun grenade, then calmly opens with a proposal.
"Hmph, fatty? I'll let you call me 'well-built.'" From that shameless opening line alone, it was clear he wasn't just any bounty hunter.

Big Bob offers cooperation in exchange for his wilderness know-how and trap-disarming skill. Chloe doesn't trust him one bit, but Grani ultimately decides to accept, conditionally — sharing only a rough direction instead of the exact location.
5. Skadi, Right?
Big Bob reads the coded stone markers left by bounty hunters, dodging the traps of the Tarat Mountain forest one by one. "This forest is far more dangerous than you'd imagine."
But partway through, they find an unfamiliar marker. Even Big Bob has never seen this code before. "A monster...?"
The marker's meaning becomes clear soon enough. Behind a fleeing bounty hunter, its true form appears.
Grani asks, already certain. "I knew it was you! Skadi... it's Skadi, right?" Only then does the woman confirm her name. "...So you're the one holding Dewdrop Village's chief."

Grani barely has time to feel glad before Skadi lunges out of nowhere, demanding Chloe be handed over. While they flee in confusion without even knowing why, the Captain's ambushing squad joins in as well.
Grani barely slips away in the chaos and leaves one line behind. "Tell me about your mission, and I might just consider telling you where Chloe is! Go cool your head over there and think it over!"
6. Thanks, Kid Without Prejudice
After finally shaking off their pursuers, a wound on Big Bob's leg comes to light. Grani doesn't hesitate to start wrapping it in bandages.
Big Bob asks. "Do you always help the Infected like this? Aren't you afraid...?"
Plenty of my comrades are Infected too. Honestly, I can never really tell just by looking, and I've never much thought about who's infected. Well, I do find out at bath time, though.
He'd expected a serious answer, but it ended on a note about baths.
Only once the bandage was done did Big Bob offer a short word of thanks. "Thanks, kid without prejudice."
7. So What Has Rhodes Island Actually Done

In front of the Tarat Mountain mine shaft, Big Bob sharpens his tone again. "You Rhodes Island types raise 'Blood Saints' like Skadi and still have the nerve to call yourselves an organization for the Infected. What were you doing while the Infected were locked in quarantine zones, dying?"

There are things we can face head-on with strength and solve, but there are also things beyond our reach. Still, knowing that, we can't just leave innocent Infected suffering right in front of us. Rhodes Island included.
Big Bob still isn't satisfied. "Hmph, what's so great about Rhodes Island. There's nothing in this world worth more than this little gold coin!" Grani only offers a short apology. "Sorry."
Just then, footsteps sound from the mine entrance. A masked group approaches, leaving the fallen Captain behind. "You're... Reunion?!"
Big Bob freezes for a moment, then sides with Grani to fight together. "Enough talk, let's go!"
With his leg injured, Big Bob ultimately chooses to stay behind on his own two feet. "Take Chloe and go on ahead! Hurry! I'll catch up somehow later!"
Grani leaves with just one promise. "I'll leave your share, so make sure you come back safe."
8. I'll Guard the Entrance
Leaving the forest, Grani asks Chloe if she's scared.
Chloe's answer was short. "I'm terribly scared. But if it's to bring back the village's quiet days and protect our homeland's peace... then I have to do my part too."

Reaching the cave entrance, Grani announces it. "You know the way to the treasure, so go on ahead. I'll guard the entrance." Chloe objects. "Facing that many bounty hunters alone is far too dangerous!"
There's no such thing as a task not worth risking your life for. Even if a barbarian's vicious blade pierces my heart, a royal cavalry officer's spear never falls to the ground.
And she adds one more line. "No matter what happens, I'll protect you. I decided that the moment I saved you in the square. I won't let anyone take so much as a strand of your hair."
Chloe laughs, exasperated. "...Honestly, you... you're still such a little kid..." Grani bristles. "I—I'm not a kid! I'm just a little short!"
Left alone, Grani fends off the bounty hunters swarming in, one by one. In the middle of it, Skadi shows up too, pressing her. "Hand over Chloe. I don't care about anything else."
Grani doesn't back down. "As long as Chloe is my client, that will never happen." Neither of them even knowing what Skadi's mission is, they just keep clashing spear against blade.
Having barely shaken Skadi off, heading deeper into the cave, something strange catches her eye. A hole, blown open as if by an explosion. Not something Skadi had made.
Grani guesses. "So, you've decided what to do." The voice belongs to exactly who she expected.
9. Three-Way Standoff at the Cave Mouth
Waiting inside the cave was Big Bob. Leading his dying brothers, he'd staked everything on one last gambit for the treasure.

blocks.cap: A band of the Infected and the Blood Saint — three forces tangled before the narrow cave.

blocks.x: Big Bob finally reveals the real story. "We can go to Columbia. It's an unimaginably distant land, but there, even the Infected can have their own village and fields. I have to get this treasure for my brothers, no matter what."
Right then, Skadi also breaks through the Reunion group and appears. "Clear the way." With the three of them gathered in one place, the cave mouth instantly turns into a three-way battle.
Skadi speaks as if she couldn't care less about anyone's circumstances. "Reunion, bounty hunters, all the same to me." And she presses Grani again to give up Chloe's location.
Grani refuses. "I can't hand over Chloe or the treasure to any of you, not as things stand. This is the request I accepted." It comes right to the edge of blades clashing.
At that moment, Chloe appears, struggling to drag the treasure chest along. "Grani! I found it! I found the treasure!"
Grani rushes to stop her but is a step too late. An explosive Big Bob threw goes off right beside Chloe, and Skadi wraps herself around Chloe at that very instant.
From that one scene, Grani understands. "You have no interest in the treasure, do you? And you never meant to hurt Chloe either. If you had, you'd have grabbed the treasure and run."
I don't need the treasure. All I want is a certain something this child is carrying.
10. The Key Demands Blood
Skadi warns Chloe. "The moment you use that key to open the treasure chest, you die. If you don't carry Kazimierz blood, the key will tear your hand open and drink it dry."
Chloe's expression was already resolved. "The most important duty of my role as chief is to protect this key, passed down through generations. That's what my father taught me..."
Only the bloodline of Kazimierz, unafraid of danger and undeterred by sacrifice, can remove every obstacle.
Chloe grips the key. "Grani, if something happens to me, please make sure the money from this treasure reaches everyone in the village. I'm sorry, Father..."
Grani stops her hand.
"Chloe, would you show me that key too?" After a brief look, she makes up her mind. "I'll open the treasure chest."
Chloe cries out. "What?! But if you do that, Grani would—" Grani had already made up her mind. "It's fine. Chloe, sorry. I'm opening it!"
Grani leaves Skadi with one line and turns the key. "Skadi, if I fall, catch me properly."
And then, Grani collapsed.
Epilogue. Back to Where Each Belongs
