terms.name: the King's Rest
terms.desc: The underground tomb where generations of Victorian kings lie.

blocks.x: Victoria is a great power built of steam and steel. Flying Steam Knights were the nation's pride, and it was a land of ingenuity that fled even catastrophe aboard nomadic cities.
But now its capital, Londinium, is in the grip of Sarkaz mercenaries. On the throne sits neither a Draco nor an Aslan, but the Sarkaz regent-king, Theresis.
Rhodes Island returns to this ashen city. The Doctor and Amiya carry a single question: everyone shouts 'for Victoria' — but what, exactly, is Victoria?
1. Twenty-six years ago, the child in the attic
The story begins twenty-six years ago, at the Cumberland ducal estate. **Aledale**, a young girl who idolized the Steam Knights, slips away from her father into the attic.

blocks.cap: The dust-covered armor of the very first Steam Knight. The girl whispers to this old friend that she'll become a greater knight than her ancestors.

blocks.x: There, Aledale overhears a conspiracy by chance — whispers of those plotting to bring down the old lion, the king.
The plot's core was simple: transfer every Steam Knight to the border, then strike the king. Once the old lion was gone, they reasoned, the cub's crown wouldn't last long either.
Trying to flee out the window before she's caught, Aledale falls from the second floor. But something soft catches and lifts the girl up.

blocks.cap: Golden lions, as if walked straight out of myth. On their backs sat a young princess, the national sword clenched in her teeth.

blocks.x: A heavy voice echoes through Aledale's mind. "One day, you will meet Vina again." That very day, the king vanishes, and a 'miracle' occurs — the young princess Alexandrina appears, cradling the national sword that had gone missing.
Over the twenty-some years since, Aledale often glimpses those golden beings and that girl again in her dreams. Waiting for the day, foretold, when she meets 'Vina' once more.
2. The teacher who believed in the light
Four years ago, a small school in Osterig. The teacher **Golding** takes in poor children and teaches them letters and theater, believing that civilization can stand against violence.
But that night, a coup by the Grand Dukes sets Londinium ablaze. And the next morning, the streets are filled not with the Home Guard or Steam Knights, but with black-horned Sarkaz mercenaries.
From that day on, the Sarkaz never left Londinium, and Steam Knights were never seen on its streets again.

Golding spends over a year with the children preparing a play about a knight standing against a tyrant. As the clouds of war grow thicker, she insists — believing in beauty is what keeps you a person, not a beast.
As long as there's light, we'll keep moving toward it.

blocks.cap: A stage facing an audience of mysterious figures in an empty house. "What I follow is not a sovereign, but honesty and courage."

blocks.x: 3. The day the flag changed

The munitions plant at Highbury. Veteran representative **Catherine** submits to the Sarkaz, striving to keep even one more worker alive. To her, the plant is the pride her family built up over generations, shaping Londinium.
Strike it once, you get nothing but a lump of metal... strike it again and again, and you can forge steam armor. That's how we built Londinium.

But her grandson **Fist** can't bear the submission. He leaves the plant to join the vigilantes and stand against the Sarkaz. Catherine drives him out coldly — but really, she was afraid of losing another grandson, like Harvey, who marched out in a protest twenty-six years ago and never came back.
That day, Victoria's flag comes down off the plant roof, and the Sarkaz banner goes up. As if nothing had changed at all.
4. When the fog clears, war
In the present, Rhodes Island joins hands with the vigilantes. The black clouds covering Londinium's sky are no ordinary storm — they're an artificial catastrophe stirred up by **the Shard**.

Theresis means to use this weapon to aim war and storm at his enemies together — to borrow Victoria's own strength to destroy Victoria. Stopping this is Rhodes Island's first goal in coming to Londinium.

Theresis dreams of a future where the Sarkaz are no longer hunted, where they can finally put down roots. The future his dead sister Theresia envisioned — he means to seize it not through peace, but through struggle. Refusing to give up any possibility at all.

The operation splits in two. The Doctor, Fist, and Ascalon head to find the Sarkaz's hidden supply route, toward Highbury and the Home Guard command tower. And **Siege** sets out to recover the national sword, **King's Breath**, from the King's Rest.
Siege leads the Glasgow gang that heads the vigilante group Osterig. And she is also the princess who vanished twenty-six years ago — Alexandrina Vina Victoria herself.
Amiya reads a familiar grief in Aledale — the face of someone who's carried far too much alone. Even so, Amiya never forgets why they came here.
We came to help 'people.' Living people — and people who have the right to live.
5. Selling the armor
Bringing weapons into the city means paying a smuggler's steep price. What Aledale offers up is the last symbol of House Cumberland — the armor of the very first Steam Knight.
"House Cumberland is you, and me." Aledale tells the old butler Elshie flatly. Words like glory or honor mean nothing anymore, she says.
Siege blocks the deal — threatening the trader to keep the armor safe. She didn't want to watch Aledale lose anything more.

blocks.cap: A cramped back room, hands binding a wound. "We should've spent more time, in more places, together."

blocks.x: As she tends to the wound, the two imagine a life that could have been — tea in the garden, hunting, a tedious ball. When Siege, for the first time, puts into words a future that had always felt like fog, Aledale tells her that's exactly her greatest strength.
Only someone who knows how precious this moment is can speak of a future, she says. "Like right now."
6. A life for a life

Around then, at Highbury, plant representatives who know too much are being secretly executed. Catherine, too, is quietly hauled away — resigned that if her own life can save the others, that's enough.
But the workers, whom Catherine thought knew nothing, rise up. Those with nothing left to fear come forward on their own to rescue their representative.
Fist joins that line too — not as a vigilante, but as this plant's own grandson. Under cover from the Doctor and Ascalon, years of pent-up anger snap the thin violence like rotten wood.

At the execution ground was a young mercenary, Paprika, who had never killed anyone. Catherine hands her the yarn a dead comrade had left as a gift for the girl, and asks just what she's swinging that weapon for. Paprika, in the end, can't pull the trigger.
Once the rescue is done, Fist returns his badge to his grandmother and leaves — he's not a plant worker anymore. Catherine throws the badge right back at her grandson.

blocks.cap: The badge back in his hand. Wherever he ended up, this plant had always been his pride.

blocks.x: To me, my grandmother is the real hero. She was then, she is now, and she always will be.
7. Different paths
Not even all Sarkaz think alike. **Shining**, a confessor who turned her back on her clan, is invited to an old family dinner.

blocks.cap: The one empty seat at the long table. Kept open, always, for the traitor who fled years ago with a test subject.

blocks.x: The clan leader and her sister Salus urge her to come back. Liz, the Infected girl Shining risks her life to protect, was once their test subject, they say — and only the clan can hold her.
But Shining says Liz alone is her true family, and leaves the table, sword in her arms. Next time they meet, she says, it'll be on a battlefield.
You are not my family. Do I need to say it more plainly than that?
8. Night at the command tower

The raid on the Home Guard command tower begins. Commander Leto is a Gaul refugee who surrendered to the Sarkaz on the single hope of rebuilding his homeland, Gaul. But that hope was a weakness deceiving himself, and it drove his own soldiers into a death trap.

The vampire Sanguinaire treats Leto like a pet in a cage — telling him not to mistake it for the bird having tamed its master, choking him under the pretense of mercy. Leto has no choice but to swallow the humiliation.
At the same moment, the Cumberland estate burns in Sanguinaire's flames. Elshie, the butler who's been Aledale's only family since birth, dies in that fire.
Atop the command tower, Amiya breaks down. Through the ring Theresia left behind, the full weight of the Sarkaz's ancient sorrow and grudge pours into her at once.

Amiya sees the night, two hundred years ago, when Kazdel burned. Sarkaz slaughtered simply for being born of a different origin — and that Kal'tsit was present at that massacre, too. However well she thinks she knows Kal'tsit, faced with that memory, Amiya can't help but feel anger and grief.
Six heroes rising from the ruins charge the great army, bearing the burning banner of Kazdel. Answer, our enemy. Who are you, to declare that Kazdel deserves to fall? That cry seizes Amiya's heart.
As Amiya loses consciousness, the regent-king Theresis levels his blade at her. His goal is one thing only: killing the 'Devil' cradled in the Doctor's arms.

blocks.cap: Kal'tsit throws herself in front of the fatal blow. Something red and warm splashes across the Doctor's cheek.

blocks.x: Bloodied, Kal'tsit tells the Doctor to take Amiya and retreat, and stands alone against Theresis. "I will kill you once more, Kal'tsit."

Amid that chaos, the Banshee king **Logos** cuts through the vampire's veil of blood. What he follows, he says, is not the Devil's crown but the ideal Theresia laid out. The roots of the old monarchy have already rotted, he declares — the Sarkaz must change.
9. Where kings sleep
Meanwhile, Siege's party reaches deep underground, at the King's Rest. Behind the door opened by the Draco's key stand statues of every Victorian king who ever reigned.
But the passage held long-dried blood, and the corpses of over a hundred elite Sarkaz. And, having fallen while holding them back, dozens of shattered suits of steam armor.

blocks.cap: Armor cut by blade, burned by fire, run through by nails — and still holding the line. Not even death could bring them down.

blocks.x: Siege realizes why she never once heard steam vent through Londinium in these four years. Every Steam Knight had been here.

What happened here was no heroic legend. It was a trap — every Steam Knight lured into the King's Rest and sealed inside a Sarkaz encirclement — and the key that let them in wasn't handed over by one or two Grand Dukes, but by Victoria itself.

Dagda, too, was once a knight of the tower. After the king left, she guarded an empty palace with nothing left to protect, and was the child her mentor forced to survive before the final charge. She's never properly mourned those deaths.
Even knowing they'd been betrayed, that Dagda salutes each and every warrior who never took off their armor, tears streaming. Siege quietly wraps her arms around Dagda, trembling.
"Victoria isn't just the Parliament and the nobility — it's you, too. It's me, too. And there are still people who'll weep for them."

King's Breath carries a legend of its own. The Aslan Padishah once nearly cut down the Draco king with this blade — and after the two bloodlines sealed their pact, it was reforged into the very symbol of Victoria's glory. It's said the kings once even cleaved catastrophe itself with this sword.

blocks.cap: The blade that had always stood there, through a thousand years. King's Breath sat far more plainly than any legend.

blocks.x: 10. Don't look back
The moment Siege takes King's Breath in hand, a familiar voice calls out behind her. Asking for the sword. It was Aledale's voice.

Aledale had carried a 'mission' for some Grand Duke to take the national sword, from the very start. She'd wanted to protect the vigilantes and her friendship with Siege too, she says — but fate pushed her all the way here.
Siege rejects the word 'mission.' If the vigilantes believe in her, she's their leader; if she calls herself a friend, then that's what she is. Just then, something stirs among the wreckage.

It was the last Steam Knight — someone who watched his comrades slaughtered, and held on alone in the dark for four years, telling himself the armor filled the wound where his flesh and blood used to be. What he'd guarded was the national sword, the one single thing he called 'Victoria.'
He'd already forgotten his own name. No medicine, no light, no food — surviving on nothing but the light the sword gave him, pushing even death away, never knowing what it was he'd been loyal to.
The king was executed, and Parliament, the nobles, the people — all of them betrayed him. So he chose to believe the sword itself was Victoria, and charges at Siege for daring to take it from him.
What this sword can drive off isn't the storm — it's fear. Knight, this sword is not your Victoria.
Worn down, Aledale finally sets her mission aside. No Cumberland, no Grand Duke — just Aledale. And, true to the promise she made Elshie as a child, she chooses to face the Steam Knight herself.

blocks.cap: A raging wind drives everyone else out the door. Beyond the wreckage, only Aledale stood facing the knight.

blocks.x: I'll be the one to face you, my nightmare... my hope.
Just before the door shuts, Aledale's lips form one last word. Whether it was Vina, or Your Highness, that word was buried in the wreckage forever.
Epilogue — A storm rises
In the ashes of the ruined Cumberland estate, the warriors find House Cumberland's steam armor — the one said to have been lost to the fire, whole and intact.

blocks.cap: The old armor, pulled from the ashes. The hope this armor once held — not even the flames could take that from it.

blocks.x: Siege wishes Aledale could have seen it — that not every path was set in stone from the start. And she hopes that last Steam Knight will someday leave the darkness, and find something new worth protecting.
But the sky won't wait. The black clouds over Londinium tear apart, and it becomes clear this was no ordinary cloud at all.

blocks.cap: A colossal storm of catastrophe, unrecorded in any archive. A Sarkaz aircraft rises above Londinium, serving as its eye.

blocks.x: The Shard has activated. Under its gaze, every enemy of the Sarkaz has nowhere left to run. Theresis begins to move, beyond the wall, toward the true battlefield.

Even so, the vigilantes don't scatter. The mercenary Totter, and Home Guard soldiers rescued from the execution ground, choose to stay with Rhodes Island. They took heavy losses, but they gained one thing — intelligence on the Sarkaz supply route.

At Golding's school, the play a year in the making draws its curtain. But the children play as Steam Knights, shouting to "squeeze the Sarkaz dry." The teacher who believed civilization could win against violence breaks down, watching the age stain even the children with war.

In a distant city, Ch'en, Bagpipe, and Harmonie recognize familiar faces, and Reed leaves Rhodes Island for someone she needs to meet. And at the Duke of Wellington's estate, someone calling herself 'Her Highness of Eblana' comes calling.

Those who gave their lives for Victoria were betrayed by Victoria and buried in the dark. And even so, there were those who never took off their armor of glory. If Victoria is neither king, nor Parliament, nor nobility, then in the end it's you, and me, and those who will still weep for them. When the fog clears, war will come.

main_12.tagline: A soldier counting the days to leave, a back-alley gang guarding a boxing gym, both ground down as cogs of war on a single flying fortress. What the powerful call 'history' turns out to be a broken arm in the mud, and a stench that never fades.

terms.name: Newport
terms.desc: An old logistics district cut off from Londinium. A blockaded zone trapping its citizens.

terms.name: Londinium Vigilantes

terms.name: King's Breath
terms.desc: Victoria's national sword, said to cleave catastrophe. Held by Siege.

terms.name: The Shard
terms.desc: The Sarkaz-controlled Londinium skyscraper. A weapon that fires catastrophe.

terms.name: the Revenant
terms.desc: A being infused with the Sarkaz's history and grudges. The true nature of the airship.

terms.name: the Grand Dukes
terms.desc: The eight dukes who split rule over Victoria — Caster, Wellington, Windermere, and others.

blocks.x: Londinium, the heart of Victoria on Terra, has already been in Sarkaz hands for four years. On the surface, the Home Guard appears to rule — but the true master of the city is a devil out of Kazdel.
And one day, a single flying fortress silently shatters a Grand Duke's high-speed warship. The false peace is over, and war has begun.
This is the first page of that war. But it's not a story about kings and dukes and devils — it's a story of the ordinary people caught between them, ground to nothing.
1. The end of a peaceful day
On the deck of the high-speed warship, young soldier Pierce is giddy over his upcoming ten-day leave. He's even written up a shopping list — fix the broken mill, plow the field, buy new work gloves.
A veteran officer laughs and throws him a grim joke — it's not our business what the Sarkaz do, he says, the higher-ups will handle it.

blocks.cap: "Our job is just to follow orders." — On the misty deck, a peaceful day fades away.

blocks.x: That peace doesn't last a few hours. A Sarkaz airship appears and blows the entire warship apart. The soldier dreaming of going home, and the ship itself, vanish just like that.

Aboard that airship is **Theresia** — the Devil who leads the Sarkaz, the one who someday hands her mission to Amiya. She looks down at the burning city and murmurs quietly. War, she says, never once stopped — it was only ever pushed back in front of everyone again.
Then let tears drown tears, and let hardship bury hardship.
General **Manfred** beside her answers calmly. "This... is the only way." That single sentence, carried by the two of them, is the weight of everything that's about to happen.
2. Newport, sealed off

**Newport District**, Londinium's old logistics quarter, is severed entirely from the city overnight. Citizens are trapped in a cramped blockade zone with no idea why. The Home Guard just keeps repeating, "technical reasons."
To a terrified citizen begging for answers, a Sarkaz soldier throws a cruel truth. Now it's your turn, he says, to live the life we Sarkaz lived.
Explanations? Reasons? The war has started, you idiots!

blocks.cap: Newport District, severed from the city. For the people trapped here, the outside world is already another world entirely.

blocks.x: **Baird** picks up a scholar swept up in that chaos off the street. A self-proclaimed heraldry scholar aiming to be next academician of the Royal Science Academy — a skill absolutely useless in a place like this.

Baird brings him to the 'Uppercut' boxing gym — the base of the back-alley gang **Glasgow**. Cador and Delphine grumble, but decide they can't just let him starve, and take him in. At a time like this, they say, every extra person who bands together matters.
3. The vigilantes fall, in a single night

Meanwhile, Rhodes Island isn't in good shape. Kal'tsit lies unconscious after Theresis gravely wounds her at the Home Guard command tower, and Amiya is tormented by dreams of a rusted throne and wailing Sarkaz.

Rhodes, joined with the **Londinium Vigilantes**, prepares a counterattack targeting the Sarkaz supply route into the Shard. **Siege**, heir to the royal line, holds **King's Breath**, the national sword said to cleave catastrophe — but no matter how tightly she grips it, all she feels is cold, lifeless metal.
But right then, every vigilante safehouse is hit at once. The intelligence network has been compromised down to its roots. Fifteen minutes — no, thirty. That's all the time they have left.

In the end, the vigilantes decide to abandon the Londinium they'd grown attached to and retreat through the underground supply routes. Logos and Misery draw off the main force, while the Doctor and Amiya hold back the pursuers.
The war's already broken out. But we... can never give up hope.
4. Grey Hat, and a poet's bargain
An ambush hits them on the retreat. But the one who actually shows up is **Grey Hat**, the Duke of Caster's proxy — a courteous, slippery man who calls himself an amateur poet.

He offers Rhodes a deal: bring him the blueprints for the Sarkaz airship, and he'll let them, their allies, the wounded, even their ship, live — 'for the time being.' All while still eyeing the national sword.
At the breaking point, former Sarkaz mercenary **Ines** cuts in and severs Grey Hat's communications. The deal goes through, but Rhodes is now standing on a board a Grand Duke set up for them.

blocks.cap: A negotiation held sitting beside explosives. Good faith or ill, this is a 'deal,' through and through.

blocks.x: Meanwhile, the still-unconscious Kal'tsit is carried out of Londinium in the hands of the explosives mercenary **W**, with Shining at her side. The first face she sees on waking is W's, and both of them are equally disgusted — but Kal'tsit surprises herself by meaning it when she says thank you.
5. Betrayal, and Cador's guilt

Tragedy strikes on the underground escape route. Fist's old friends, the workers of Munitions Plant No. 11, seal the gates shut — handing Catherine and Clovisia over to the Sarkaz to beg for their own lives.

They aren't villains either. Just ordinary workers who've never held anything but a wrench, terrified of being blown to pieces in some bomb crater on the outskirts. Fist comes to realize something: accepting that a friend betrayed you is far easier than accepting that they simply broke under fear.

The sealed-off Newport District is slowly becoming its own hell. More and more people, driven mad by hunger, kill their neighbors. Cador confesses his guilt to Siege — he drove out an old couple who'd been kind to him, without leaving them so much as a single dose of suppressant, even Infected as they were.

And every night, Cador dreams. After the war ends and his hometown is rebuilt, amid the glow of fireworks, that old man survives and just stares at him. What answer he owes that gaze, he doesn't know.
6. Vina returns

Siege finally reunites with Baird at the Uppercut gym. Indra, Morgan, even Dagda — Glasgow gathers together in one place for the first time in a long while. Every stain on the sofa, every scrawl on the wall, exactly as it was.

blocks.cap: The old sofa the four of them once hauled in, grunting, from a flea market. Every scratch on it carved with days they shared.

blocks.x: But Cador turns cold, calling Siege 'Your Highness.' The truth the national sword revealed — that the leader of their back-alley gang was actually heir to the royal line — feels like a betrayal to him.
Your Glasgow isn't here. You're nothing here. To Glasgow, right now, you're nothing.

Cador declares the king means nothing to him at all — that the restaurant owner who gave him a discount when he had no money deserves more respect. Siege can't argue back. She isn't even sure herself whether she really came back for the people, or whether she's started thinking of herself as a queen.

Even so, saving people needs unity. When a fight breaks out over food, Morgan shouts to the crowd: "Her Highness Alexandrina Vina Victoria has returned, bearing King's Breath!" A lie that isn't quite a lie, just to stop the chaos for a moment.
7. Kal'tsit and W — what does it mean to be Sarkaz?
At a hideout outside Londinium, Kal'tsit finally wakes. To W, who says she has no interest in history, Kal'tsit brings up an old story precisely because of that.

blocks.cap: "I don't regret it. But sometimes... I do get tired." A rare expression from the ever-composed Kal'tsit.

blocks.x: The name 'Sarkaz,' she says, was originally an arrogant slur coined by their oppressors — and it became a rallying cry for the oppressed to unite. If vampires founded a nation, how would that differ from an arrogant Victoria? If Nachzehrer ruled civilization, how would that differ from a cruel Ursus?

This is why Kal'tsit and Theresia founded Babel together — the belief that the Sarkaz are neither baser nor nobler than anyone else. But Theresia sank too deep into the Devil's memories, and arrived at the conclusion that little time remains on this earth.
So the two of them, seeing the very same crisis, ended up standing on opposite sides. One trying to unite the Sarkaz through war, the other trying to stop that war.
8. The vanished relay station, and a teacher's last night

Newport has one plan: broadcast from the old Sunset Street Hotel's relay station, forcing the Grand Dukes — who hate the idea of being seen standing by while people are massacred — to get involved. In the confusion, citizens could slip over the blockade wall and escape.

At the hotel is Colbert, an old Sarkaz manager who considers himself more Londinium than Kazdel. But the relay station had already been stripped for parts long ago. The plan falls apart at the very first step.

Meanwhile, **Golding**, the teacher, realizes she was the one who unwittingly leaked the vigilantes' information, and it breaks her. The teacher who'd watched over her, 'Molly,' wasn't even human — she was Damazti, the oldest Sarkaz of all, scattered into dozens of 'Mollys' to dismantle the vigilantes from the roots.

Golding chooses death — not to face a business, but to face people, each face she knows, one by one, inside her own heart. And so as not to deceive the ones she loves, she asks Damazti to take her shape and quietly disappear.

blocks.cap: "Please, take my shape." A teacher's last request, before despair could swallow her whole.

blocks.x: This can hardly be called resistance. This is the choice of someone too weak. But... I'm sleepy now.
9. The devil of another species

Pushed by Grey Hat, the Doctor and Amiya go to investigate the airship. But it turns out to be no product of industrial engineering at all. Ascalon realizes it — the ship itself is **the Revenant**, a living being infused with the Sarkaz's own history and grudge.
The Revenant pours the full agony of Kazdel — destroyed and rebuilt 3,421 times — straight into Amiya. And it cries out at her, a being of another species entirely.

blocks.cap: A twisted chimera, a devil of another species. "What right do you have to bear the Sarkaz's fury?"

blocks.x: The Revenant's question is cruel. You are not Sarkaz, it says, so you will never truly be Sarkaz — you can turn your back on this suffering and leave whenever you please. Amiya answers that she won't look away, that she'll remember every death, every sacrifice.

But faced with the question — "having seen all this, why do you still try to stop this war?" — Amiya, for one instant, understands Theresia's choice. That logic, that only scorched earth can grant the Sarkaz new life. And even so, in the end, Amiya refuses that path.
But... I'll still refuse that path.

Because she refused, she can never truly stand fully on their side. But that very refusal is exactly what sets Amiya apart from any other devil. Draco Eblana's flame cuts in, and the two of them barely manage to escape.
10. Purple fire rain

Just as the escape begins, purple fire pours down from the sky — flame scattered by Draco Eblana of Dublin. The people burned alive by it rise again as wraiths, purple flames of hunger burning in their eyes.

In the middle of that chaos, Baird alone turns back — to fetch his old deaf friend McLaren, hiding in the video room. He rips out the last plank of wood, promising they'll get a new gym and a new video room together.
But McLaren, driven mad with terror, stabs Baird to protect his own life. What he'd fought so hard to protect turns out to be nothing but an empty tin can.

Mortally wounded, Baird slumps against a wall and finds the body of the scholar who died before him — his final treatise, charcoal words covering the wall from end to end. Behind inconclusive scholarship, what he left were wishes.

I want fried fish and chips. I hope the lover who left me hasn't forgotten me. I hope the kids at the gym are safe... And at the very end, a prayer that everyone's wishes come true, and that tears and hunger disappear.
With his fading pen, Baird adds one short line beneath it.
I hope so too.

blocks.cap: An empty room, its friends all scattered. King's Breath never gave off any light, to the very end.

blocks.x: 11. Ines's fall

Ines infiltrates deep into the airship, searching for a place high enough to use her shadows. There, together with Paprika, the young mercenary she's been dragging along like a hostage, she uncovers Manfred's real plan.

The airship's slow flight is bait. Draw the greed-blinded Grand Dukes' fleets into this one spot, then burn them all at once with the Shard's catastrophe — letting that first fireball spread into a war that swallows the whole continent.

This information has to get out. Ines throws herself off from a high enough point. As she falls, she manipulates the massive shadow cast over all of Newport, drawing the twisted shape of the Shard across the sky.

blocks.cap: A height only visible if you're willing to jump. Ines falls, painting one last warning in shadow.

blocks.x: The Duke of Windermere recognizes the shadow and fires a retreat warning to his fleet. The catastrophe amounts to nothing more than a test shot. And Ines, braced for death, is unexpectedly caught in a net woven from ash. W, and her old comrade Hoederer, who works with ash, have come out to meet her.

blocks.cap: "I always thought Ines would come back from death someday, and now she finally didn't." Two old friends' hands catch her.

blocks.x: 12. Damazti's final hour

Damazti, the oldest Sarkaz alive, takes a deep interest in Amiya — a devil of another species. To a being that's lived thousands of years and exhausted every possibility until it grew bored of them all, Amiya is something it has never once seen.
The Banshee Logos, pursuing Damazti, points out the one territory it's never tried. Death. What has completed and come to a stop cannot grow further, he says — new life is born only from ruin.
In the courage Golding showed by giving up life itself, Damazti feels a desire for the first time. And it accepts Logos's invitation, asking only that the bell on his back toll in mourning for it.

blocks.cap: "It's been a long time... since we stayed still." An eternal wanderer chooses an end, for the first time.

blocks.x: The war-horn's elegy sounds, and every Damazti dissolves at once. From its ashes, two new Damazti are born. The most eternal of monarchies has fallen, and for the first time, something has begun.
The elegy has already sounded, and the horn cries in sorrow. New life is born from ruin.
13. Ash

The Duke of Windermere's fleet gathers up Newport's citizens and carries them off to his own nomadic city. Delphine goes home, and the Grand Dukes' reckoning over the national sword is set aside, for now.
On the ash-covered deck, Siege cries for the first time — Vina, who never cries, not once. She blames it on the ash in her eyes.

And the war goes on. The fleets of Wellington, Caster, and Windermere clash with the Nachzehrer King's great army. The catastrophe cloud hasn't cleared yet, and the Sarkaz march never stops — because they are the war itself.

blocks.cap: The Nachzehrer King, seated on his throne, looks down over his great army. His forces cross storm and corpses alike, and keep marching on.

blocks.x: One twist comes to light too. The one who'd stripped the relay station beforehand was Percival, the hotel doorman — and Infected himself. Posing as a public broadcast, he'd been quietly communicating with the new Reunion, smuggling out the Infected all along. Rhodes and Delphine's broadcast plan had, in fact, just been an unlucky side project riding on top of it.

And so **Nine**, leading the new Reunion, takes in the Infected — vowing not to discard old names or old sins, but to become a place where Infected with no leader, no homeland, can finally set down roots. At her side is Talulah, peeling potatoes, carrying her own guilt.
Talulah looks up at the catastrophe clouds and thinks quietly. Those who've caused ripples since birth, boasting that they've led everything. They always succeed — but she won't call them winners.
I can't accept that everything they've made deserves to be called 'history.' I never will.

blocks.cap: "Amiya, can you find that path?" — Theresia's question, thrown out from the heart of the storm.

blocks.x: While the kings and dukes and devils argue over ownership of 'Victoria's war' or 'the Sarkaz's war,' the ones actually ground to nothing were a soldier waiting for his leave, a man who went mad defending an empty tin can, a scholar who wished everyone happiness at the end of an inconclusive treatise, and Baird, who went back for a friend right to the very end.

The powerful will call it 'history.' But as Ines said, war is nothing more than people flailing in the mud, arms broken in the ruins, a stench that never quite fades. And in that ash, someone quietly writes, 'I hope so too.'

main_13.tagline: On the battlefield where the Sarkaz swallowed Victoria's capital, everyone searches for their own way 'home.' Infected, Sarkaz, or Victorian, they all end up facing the same question: are the only people who'll walk with us really just our own kind?

chars.name: Amiya
chars.desc: Leader of Rhodes Island. The 'Sarkaz King' who inherited the Black Crown.

chars.name: Kal'tsit
chars.desc: The doctor who leads Rhodes Island. Knows the origin of the Sarkaz.

chars.name: Logos
chars.desc: Young lord of the Banshee Royal Court. A male banshee. Writes incantations with a quill.

chars.name: Siege
chars.desc: Leader of Newport's Old Glasgow. Commands the Paragons.

chars.name: Delphine
chars.desc: Heir to House Windermere. An intelligence operative.

chars.name: Hoederer
chars.desc: Leader of the Sarkaz mercenaries. Dreams of returning to Kazdel. Ex-Babel.

chars.name: Ines
chars.desc: A Caprini mercenary. Wields Arts that read shadows.

chars.name: W
chars.desc: A Sarkaz mercenary who loves bombs. A shadow of the old Babel.

chars.name: Shining
chars.desc: A healer of Rhodes Island. Stays close by Nightingale's side.

chars.name: Nightingale
chars.desc: A Rhodes Island medical Operator. Shining's companion.

chars.name: Nine
chars.desc: Leader of the Victorian Reunion.

chars.name: Talulah
chars.desc: Descendant of the Red Dragon. Symbol of Reunion.

chars.name: Guard
chars.desc: A Reunion officer. Former Rhodes Island medical staff.

chars.name: Vampire Sanguinar
chars.desc: Lord of the Bloodline Royal Court. A Sarkaz obsessed with pure blood.

chars.name: Confessor
chars.desc: An undying Sarkaz who covets the crown.

terms.name: Sarkaz
terms.desc: The demon race. The people most vulnerable to Oripathy.

terms.name: Kazdel
terms.desc: The Sarkaz's lost homeland. A nomadic city.

terms.name: Reunion
terms.desc: An armed organization of the Infected.

terms.name: Sarkaz King
terms.desc: Heir to the Black Crown who leads the Sarkaz.

terms.name: Military Council
terms.desc: The Sarkaz faction led by siblings Theresis and Theresa.

terms.name: Londinium
terms.desc: Capital of Victoria. Occupied by the Sarkaz.

terms.name: the Paragons
terms.desc: A legendary Gaul unit. Its name revived by Siege.

terms.name: Behemoth
terms.desc: The skeleton of an undying giant, drifting through space and time.

blocks.x: Terra is periodically struck by 'Catastrophes,' and the Originium left in their wake turns people into the Infected. The demon race, the Sarkaz, is the one most vulnerable to that Oripathy.
The Sarkaz, wandering for a thousand years, finally rallied under the banner of Regent Theresis and swallowed Londinium, the capital of Victoria, whole.
This is the story of that war. But it isn't the story of a single hero — it's the story of countless people across a burning Victoria, each asking what 'home' and 'us' really mean.

Even with the capital fallen, the world is indifferent. Kazimierz's newspapers chase a retiring knight's scoop instead of Victoria's tragedy, selling not truth but anxiety and fear. There's simply too much happening across Terra every day.
1. The Flag of the Infected, and Guard
Reunion, which rose up in Ursus, drifted through Columbia and all the way here to Victoria. War keeps pouring out Infected and refugees, and one by one, they take them in.

blocks.cap: A midsummer night, yet the air feels suffocating. Reunion officer Guard limps out to take her shift.

blocks.x: Guard used to be Rhodes Island medical staff. Now she hands out hastily made suppressants, telling people Oripathy is just a disease — the real enemy is the contempt and discrimination hiding behind it.

Talulah, propped up as Reunion's 'leader,' is really just a banner. Hiding from the Infected the truth that the Chernobog massacre was an Ursus conspiracy, she silently bears the burden of being a symbol of hope.
Talulah waits for judgment day as a sinner. Until then, she asks to be treated as a tool. Guard tells herself Reunion won't get a second chance — that this lie, at least, is for the Infected's sake.

Victorian conscripts fleeing the Sarkaz also drift into the unit. Thrown to the front without proper training, they don't even know they've already been infected. Guard tells them the plain truth: that they, too, are already as good as dead, just like the rest of them.
A question begins to grow in Guard's heart — whether the only people they should join hands with are really just the Infected.
2. The Mother of Steel Falls
Meanwhile, refugees who escaped Old Newport are aboard Duke Windermere's fast warship. The duke's daughter Delphine leads them, along with Siege and her Rhodes Island group.

As the warship passes through a gorge, it starts collapsing from within. The Sarkaz assassin Damastes infiltrated disguised as the duke, and on deck, Vampire Sanguinar, lord of the Bloodline Royal Court, targets Duke Windermere.
Delphine, trying to save a soldier, falls into the vampire's trap. In that single moment's opening, the mother of steel takes a fatal wound.

blocks.cap: Listening to her daughter's lie that the enemy has withdrawn, Duke Windermere praises her sword to the very last moment.

blocks.x: I thought... you'd have grown a bit taller by now. So the clothes might be a bit big.

Without finishing the story of the gift she'd secretly prepared for her daughter, the duke breathes her last. The council blames Delphine for that death, saying "a hero should not be buried alongside the weak."
Then they abandon Delphine, the heir, on the battlefield and sail off with the fleet — indifferent even to the danger facing the commoners who came out to see off the coffin.
3. The Ghost Unit, the Paragons
Siege reaches out to the abandoned Delphine. Siege and her Rhodes Island Operators, along with the fleeing Infected, refugees, and stragglers, come together as one.
What falls into their hands by chance is the legendary military identification code lost since Gaul. A small box with no mystical power at all — yet its name gives people hope.

blocks.cap: "The Paragons!" Those abandoned by the royal army revive a name of forgotten glory with a battle cry.

blocks.x: They rescue Horn's isolated platoon and the citizens trapped in Chetley County. Holding out for days against a Sarkaz mobile war altar, Siege destroys the weapon even as metal shards pierce her body.

Siege proves that it isn't a divine relic like the national sword 'King's Breath' that matters before a Catastrophe, but people alone. Soldiers, Infected, and civilians who couldn't trust each other slowly tear down the walls between their hearts as they fight side by side.
An old viscount of Windermere comes to persuade Delphine to return to her place among the nobility, but Delphine refuses.
I am my mother's daughter, and a comrade to these soldiers. Beyond that, I am nothing.
Siege declares that running away alone won't end the war. If no one will take them home, then they'll go back themselves. They'll storm Londinium and say: this is our land.
4. The Mercenaries' Homecoming
On another front, Sarkaz mercenaries Hoederer, Ines, and bomb-loving W are on the move. Outwardly serving General Manfred, they're actually chasing the Sarkaz secret, the 'Life Spine.'

What they encounter is Behemoth, a massive skeleton drifting through time and space, spewing out illusions of the past. The Military Council has modified this undying giant to teleport supplies and troops to Londinium.

blocks.cap: A waystation carved out of an entire mountain. The identity of the giant hauling the Sarkaz's supplies is revealed.

blocks.x: The skeleton's operator turns out to be their old friend Ursula. Once they frequented the Scarred's shop together, but now, loyal to the Military Council as a major, she turns her blade on them. She says the siblings were the only ones who ever gave a slum girl like her a seat at the strategy table.

blocks.cap: Saying they're past the age to talk of friendship, Hoederer raises his sword against his old comrade.

blocks.x: What Hoederer truly wants isn't revenge, but to send every Sarkaz back to Kazdel — to break the cycle of grudges so everyone has a home to return to. Even the lich Ermengarde, moved by that resolve, promises to relay the will of the Babel remnants to the liches guarding Kazdel.

On the night before the decisive battle, Hoederer confronts W directly. She still thinks of herself as just a mercenary, he says, but right now she carries the lives of countless Sarkaz on her shoulders. Stop clinging to being Theresa's shadow, he tells her, and stop just trailing behind Amiya.

blocks.cap: "So that this world... can rest in peace." W finally faces, as her own, the path Theresa left behind.

blocks.x: Hoederer drives home one more point: Theresis must not be killed by Sarkaz hands right now. If he dies, the failed Sarkaz will become targets for slaughter across the whole continent — even that grudge must become a cornerstone of Kazdel. Ines quietly watches to see if young W can bear that weight.
5. One Who Covets the Crown
Meanwhile, Amiya and the Doctor are ambushed by the Confessor's group. A man named Salus tries to steal Amiya's crown but fails, and Amiya loses consciousness, drawn into a nightmare.

blocks.cap: In the dream, the souls of the Sarkaz pronounce upon Amiya a foretold, inescapable ruin.

blocks.x: The souls whisper that resisting only brings more suffering — to just close her eyes. But Amiya refuses. She says anyone living on this earth can choose their own ending.
I will challenge you. In the name of the Sarkaz King, and in the name of 'Amiya.'

Amiya realizes: Theresa entrusted the crown to her because she, too, never believed in that sorrowful future. The Sarkaz King cannot be a symbol of conquest and revenge. So this power exists not as a curse, but to find one's own path.
Meanwhile, the Confessor targets Rhodes Island's Nightingale. And in a conversation he has with healer Shining, a long-hidden tragedy comes to light.

blocks.cap: In a maze of shattered consciousness, Shining confronts her own sin to reclaim Liz once more.

blocks.x: The name 'Liz' that Nightingale remembers, and the gentle personality that came with it, were actually memories Shining had stitched in to hold together a collapsing vessel. The Confessor tries to use even that love as material to forge the crown.
Even as she's dragged away by the Confessor, Liz opens a path of her own. Don't try to bear it all alone, she says — didn't they promise to face it together? Shining resolves to bring her back.
6. The Broken Greenhouse, Brentwood
Brentwood, a small village near Londinium, was strangely 'peaceful.' Gardener Magdalene and the mayor's daughter Frida bow their heads to the Sarkaz, holding on to protect the villagers.
A royal army officer nicknamed 'Shovel,' who often visited Magdalene's greenhouse, cherished the roses she grew. Believing some things shouldn't be trampled, he quietly tries to keep her alive.
But the truth was cruel. What the villagers had been building day and night was the vampire's massive ritual magic circle, and every resident was a 'key' to open that ritual.

blocks.cap: As the ritual activates, blood-red creatures pour out of the villagers' bodies.

blocks.x: Frida thought peace would be kept if she gave the demons everything they demanded. But the Sarkaz took even Will, the young man she cherished, and in the end every villager was fated to become sacrifices for the ritual. The peace they'd fought so hard to keep had been a lie from the start.
Just then, the self-defense militia and the Paragons burst in. Siege, Delphine, and Horn's tank corps smash the magic circle and pull the citizens free.

blocks.cap: The magic circle is destroyed, and the vampire's rampaging creatures lose control.

blocks.x: 7. Who Is 'Us'
Guard visits an old brewery deep in the woods — once where Victorian Reunion made medicine for the Infected, and the very starting point of Reunion itself.

There, Mace, who calls herself Reunion, and Infected and non-Infected with nowhere else to go, all lived tangled together. But some of the Infected there beat a wandering Sarkaz who'd been selling medicine to death, nameless, for no reason but that he was Sarkaz.
The Originium leaking from that corpse builds up and collapses the room. As the collapse begins, Guard evacuates people and stays behind to the very last.

blocks.cap: Amid the flames, Guard presses herbs into Mace's hand and shoves her toward the exit.

blocks.x: In place of her body, which might turn to ash, Guard leaves behind a recorder — carrying the question of whether the people they should join hands with, the people who truly want to walk the thorny road with them, are really just the Infected.
The people we should join forces with, the people who want to walk this thorny path together... are they really just the Infected? Are they truly... only them?

In the unit was also Nowell, a long-lived being hiding his true nature. Unable to die, having wandered for centuries, he leaves for Londinium to find the 'Hand of Fate' that cursed him. Nine doesn't stop him — perhaps, in the end, what they must fight is the same thing.

Standing on Guard's death, Nine settles her answer. Reunion's driving force must no longer be vague revenge, but a clear creed: whether Ursus or Victorian, anyone who resists oppression stands together.
So Nine casts out those who killed a Sarkaz for no reason. Power must be used for a shared goal, not grudges and division — otherwise, that power will tear them apart from within.
8. The Crimson Lord

The decisive battle unfolds atop Behemoth. Vampire Sanguinar awakens a ten-thousand-year-old Originium 'seed' with blood scattered across the battlefield, trying to drag not just Londinium but all of Terra into the abyss.
His madness had roots. Sanguinar despaired that the crown always chose the weak — and in the end, killed his own brother, who had once been the 'Sarkaz King,' with his own hands.
What his dying brother showed him was his own 'peace' as a doctor in a quiet village. But Sanguinar mocked that dream as false and came to crave nothing but the blood of the mighty Tikaz.

blocks.cap: Amiya believes not in the power flowing in blood, but in the burning blood of the people she has watched over.

blocks.x: Amiya denies everything he stands for. Home isn't a legend from ten thousand years ago, she says, but right here, where the Sarkaz now stand. As long as the present is suppressed by the past, a true future will never come.
Home is right here, where the Sarkaz came from.
Logos plays an elegy, and Ines, W, and Hoederer clear the way.

And here, one man makes his final stand. Leto, a survivor of fallen Gaul, is a man who, out of a weakness to survive, betrayed his own kind and brought about countless deaths. Even the teacher Golding, who tried to awaken him, died in that war.

At the end of his despair, Leto walks toward the vampire who came to bless him. He then 'bestows' his own tainted blood upon the vampire's pure body, saying they'll never again be able to boast of pure blood.
I hereby 'bestow' my blood upon you, you filthy leech!

blocks.cap: The 'Sarkaz King' of another race and the lord of the banshees finally pass judgment on the Crimson Lord.

blocks.x: Even as he falls, Sanguinar sneers — that in the end, they too couldn't overcome the instinct running in their blood, that it's always the cowardly who win. This, he says, is the very curse cast upon the Sarkaz.
As he plunges into an unknown land, he leaves one last curse: he has already returned Tikaz's blood to its home, so go to Londinium and see — he'll be waiting behind the curtain of ruin.

Hoederer strikes a bargain with the dying Behemoth's consciousness to send the group back to where they started. He then takes Ursula prisoner, deciding to make this giant skeleton the key to the counterattack on Londinium.
9. The Approaching Disaster
Reunion heads for Londinium once more. But along the way, another red dragon appears before Nine and Talulah — Dublin's Draco, Evlana.

blocks.cap: Before an unburning flower, two red dragons stand face to face.

blocks.x: Evlana tries to lure Talulah with bloodline and crown, but Talulah refuses — saying anyone who treats death as a tool of power is nothing but a false tyrant. Evlana withdraws, leaving behind a prophecy that next time, a red dragon will devour another red dragon.

Meanwhile, the archdukes shaken by Windermere's death — Castor, Gododdin, and Wellington — join hands to prepare a counterattack on Londinium. Castor is wary of the old veteran Wellington, who has long hidden his strength — the eyes of that starving beast, once seen facing the Emperor of Ursus and the Lich King in his youth, are awakening once more.

Kal'tsit sees through the true nature of that 'curse' the vampire was obsessed with. It's Originium. Ancient Kazdel touched it before anything else, which is why the Sarkaz became the race most vulnerable to Oripathy — and the oldest Infected of all. This fight won't end with a single city.

And atop The Shard, Theresa looks up at the storm clouds. What's about to happen in Londinium can no longer be undone, she says — once everything is revealed, only then will the true Catastrophe follow.
I hope the light I'm looking at can still shine then, even if I am already deep in the darkness.

Infected, Sarkaz, or Victorian, everyone searched for a way home. Guard asked if they were really just the Infected; Amiya refused a preordained fate; Hoederer tried to break the cycle of grudges. But the whirlwind of ill omen the vampire unleashed is only just beginning to summon the real Catastrophe.

main_14.tagline: As the national sword that cleaves Catastrophes and a revived skeleton converge on Londinium, a dead Sarkaz King steals a ten-thousand-year curse from the deepest part of Originium. Not to hold on, but to set free — that was the King's final mercy.

chars.name: Amiya
chars.desc: Leader of Rhodes Island. Theresa's successor.

chars.name: Theresa

blocks.x: At the bottom of Terra lives a race called the Sarkaz. These demons, entangled with Originium and Catastrophe, are born with ten thousand years of hatred and hardship carved into their blood, and even in death they cannot escape that cycle.
The story begins long ago, with a bedtime tale Theresa once told a young Amiya — the tale of a cloth doll crossing a vast desert in search of the 'Field of Hope.'

Before the doll appears an endless river — cold and salty, formed from the tears of all who failed to reach the far shore. Knowing it would grow heavy and sink the moment it touched the water, the doll leaps in anyway.
Theresa put Amiya to sleep without ever telling her the ending. Did the cloth doll ever reach the other side? Finding that answer would take a very long time.
1. The Last Walk
Now, Londinium, the capital of Victoria, has been occupied by the Sarkaz. Theresa, the dead Sarkaz King, has been revived and stands beside her brother Theresis; an airship awaits departure.
Before setting sail, the siblings walk side by side through the vast airship dock. From Kazdel to Kazdel — their destination is now within sight.
Theresis suggests they say a proper farewell, one last time. Theresa reaches out and smooths the wrinkles in her brother's shawl. The two quietly exchange a greeting in the ancient Sarkaz tongue.
(In ancient Sarkaz) Farewell.
Meanwhile, W is loading the Life Spine with explosives, plotting to blow Theresis away. But something else weighs on her — that the last time she saw Theresa, she never managed to say 'goodbye.'
I'm just going to make up for that one word, that's all. So I won't forget. W mutters that to herself.
2. The Falling Ark

Rhodes Island and W's mercenaries, together with the Victorian civilian unit the Paragons, have seized the 'Life Spine' — a flying transport built from the dead Behemoth's spine. Riding it, they intend to strike the heart of Londinium, The Shard.

But at that very moment, the sky turns entirely crimson. Theresis and the Confessor have used the vampire's pure blood, the 'Blood of Tikaz,' to summon the ten-thousand-year curse, Amnanam — the first curse, and the first Originium.
That immense power severs the connection with Behemoth, and the Life Spine crashes down into the middle of a battlefield swallowed by Originium.

blocks.cap: Buildings sink, warships are devoured, and armies are crushed. Hardship is nothing but a carpet the Sarkaz army tramples underfoot.

blocks.x: The group splits into three. Amiya, Kal'tsit, Logos, and the Doctor head for the Confessor's altar to destroy the 'Blood of Tikaz.'
Siege's Paragons head to the Silverrock Bluffs front line to reclaim the national sword that cleaves Catastrophes, 'King's Breath,' while W, Ines, and Hoederer stay behind to guard the Life Spine.

Kal'tsit says three days. Crossing the utterly transformed wasteland to deal with the 'Blood of Tikaz' will take at least three days. Meanwhile, the immobile skeleton is nothing but a giant target, making the defense a far harder fight.
Before leaving, Kal'tsit quietly tells the Doctor that Originium will decide all their futures — and yet, she still believes the Doctor is someone who can change everything.
3. The Sarkaz King's Mercy

On the road to the altar, three frightened mercenaries join Amiya's group. Among them, the driver Marilla, having swallowed a blood-red vampire crystal to survive, has been transformed into something almost monstrous.
Marilla kneels and begs to be disposed of — to die a warrior's death by the Sarkaz King's hand.
Amiya refuses. She tells Marilla she hasn't become the vampire's creature — her appearance has only changed slightly, and there's no wickedness or madness to be felt in her.
Even if a trace of some ancient bloodline remains... you are still you.
No judgment, no reward is needed. Amiya simply welcomes Marilla as a comrade. Not by killing, but by saving — the new Sarkaz King is changing the meaning of mercy.
4. My Victoria

Meanwhile, the Paragons have an unexpected reunion in a ruined village. Bagpipe, thought dead, is alive, and falls into Captain Horn's arms — she made it back after protecting the Ghost Corps' intelligence to the very end.
That night, Duke Gododdin visits and offers Siege a hand. In the name of the Victorian Council, he offers her, the heir to the throne, the seat of chairman — effectively, the pinnacle of the nation.
But when the Sarkaz descend on the village, the duke says this poor town isn't worth defending and urges Siege to leave with him. There's no seat at the negotiating table for these people, he says.
Siege turns down both the throne and the chance to leave with King's Breath, standing beside the remaining citizens instead.
But these people... are my Victoria.

As he leaves, Gododdin abandons his Infected soldiers with the Paragons — knowing full well the Paragons won't turn them away. Whether cast off or handed over, Siege accepts even those Infected as comrades.
5. Before the Broken Door

W, guarding the Life Spine, chases down a vanished scout and discovers, in a mountain cave, the remains of the traitors who once assassinated Theresa long ago. There she meets Kalichoa, the caretaker guarding that grave.
Kalichoa reveals a hidden truth: the Confessor took Theresa's blood-soaked body and forcibly cut her soul out from among the souls of the Sarkaz.

But those souls were bound together as tightly as an umbilical cord. The revived Sarkaz King could no longer exist as her whole, true self, and the weight of the souls she carried meant she would never find peace again.
Knowing all of this, Theresa still decided to offer herself as a sacrifice to those souls — to every Sarkaz, past and future.

Beyond an altar collapsed in the explosion, W meets Theresa's afterimage. The broken door that Theresa fixed for her on the day she first arrived at Rhodes Island — the Sarkaz King opens it herself and walks out, leading countless shadows.

blocks.cap: "Keep moving forward. You'll all be able to go further than I ever could." — the last words the afterimage leaves behind.

blocks.x: 6. The Throne of Death and the Lighthouse of Mercy

On the battlefield, Iron Duke Wellington drives his flagship Gastrel to ram straight into the throne of the Nachzehrer King, Netzsalem. A ten-thousand-year god of war and an undefeated genius of war collide head-on.
Meanwhile, Logos, having sent Amiya to the altar, stays behind alone to bar Netzsalem's way. Banshees do not devour death — banshees exist to see death off.
Vampire Sanguinar appears and tempts him to accept his blood and hatred, but Logos refuses. Instead, he decides to burn the past with his own blood — not for revenge, but for tomorrow.

blocks.cap: "If new life must always be born from death, then I will bring death, and I too will meet death."

blocks.x: Blowing his bone flute and burning all the blood in his body, Logos severs Netzsalem's arm and plunges off the cliff. But the mothers of the Banshee Gorge, the song of death, pull him back up above the river.
As the fallen banshee begins to sing once more, Netzsalem acknowledges his talent — a pass, as a warrior. Yet he asks bitterly all the same: where, exactly, is Kazdel's next hero?
7. Light of the Apostles

Another battle unfolds at the altar. The Apostle Shining confronts her father, the Confessor Khessartouchta — a usurper who has clung to life hidden in shadows for thousands of years, vowing to rewrite the Sarkaz's fate.
Shining tries to save Nightingale, the 'vessel' she long ago carved and gave feeling to. Through dark Arts, she returns to her the true memories stolen by their father.
The sleeping Nightingale opens her eyes. Reclaiming her own name, she stands against her father not as a tool, but as a person.
My name is Liz. I am your enemy.

blocks.cap: Shining, Nearl, Liz. Three Apostles together pierce through the crown of thorns — a promise to save people even from the deepest darkness.

blocks.x: 8. Descent at the Origin

Deep within the altar, Salus, another child abandoned by the Confessor, offers his own blood and life to the 'Blood of Tikaz,' completing the ritual — desperate to reach the future he'd longed for before anyone else.
At the top of The Shard, a crimson fruit blossoms. Originium begins to replace everything on the earth. Amnanam, the origin point of all things in Terra, has descended once more.

blocks.cap: A scene less terrifying than exhilarating — as if the world were simply returning to its original shape.

blocks.x: At that moment in Silverrock Bluffs, Siege leaps off her comrades' shoulders and drives King's Breath into the Sword's Seat. A golden light that cleaves Catastrophe pierces the sky, and the storm briefly stops.

blocks.cap: The steam knight thought dead reappears to clear the way. Victoria's glory has returned, together with the Paragons.

blocks.x: 9. Heart of Originium
Having reclaimed the Life Spine, W and her group now ram that skeleton into the body of the Sarkaz's airship itself. Within the airship dwells a mad, ancient Revenant.

A being that, for nine thousand years, has fed its own kin one by one into a soul furnace, all in the name of protecting the homeland Kazdel. Not even death would take him, so he tried to atone for his sins by burning himself.
W thrusts both hands into the burning furnace core and tears it out. Even as her hands burn away entirely, she doesn't let go. And so, the fortress of the sky at last stops its heartbeat.

blocks.cap: "Get down here now!" — heedless of her burning hands, W grips tight to the airship's very soul.

blocks.x: But the dying Revenant forces one last truth upon the Doctor — Theresa, fallen while protecting Amiya in a sea of blood, and among those who killed her, the Doctor himself.
The truth that the Doctor was a conspirator in Theresa's death — W and Kal'tsit already knew. Still, W says she has no interest in killing the memory-lost Doctor of today, and calmly turns her back.

Amiya, too, confesses she had suspected it for a long time — that the Doctor who saved her and the Doctor who killed Theresa were the same person, something she still can't quite understand. And yet, Amiya takes the Doctor's hand once more.
Memories can deceive a person, Amiya says, but feelings cannot be faked — the Doctor standing beside her now is the Doctor she has always believed in.
10. False Paradise
As the price of stopping the airship, the Doctor's group is pulled into the descended Amnanam — into the interior of Originium, a fused universe where all things have stopped.

There, the Doctor encounters a perfectly gentle, false Rhodes Island, and a being named Priestess — an old colleague who speaks the language of the cosmos, one who, the Doctor claims, created Originium together with them in some ancient bygone age.

blocks.cap: "The answer was always here. That I am here." — a voice that has waited for the Doctor at the deepest core of Originium.

blocks.x: But the Doctor doesn't linger in that beautiful lie. Recognizing that they are a being who can rewrite Originium once more, the Doctor revives the shattered Rhodes Island and sails forward across a sea of gold.
Above that sea, the souls of dead Sarkaz still endlessly repeat their war. And at the far edge, where all things have stopped, a single tower pierces the sky. It is Babel.
11. When Sorrow Blooms
At the tower's summit, the group meets Theresa. She has succeeded in rewriting the information of Originium — trying to create a perfect flower garden for the Sarkaz alone.
Confirming that garden has no place for other races, Amiya tells her that isn't Theresa's true dream. In the end, the two Sarkaz Kings clash over the Black Crown.
In the middle of the fight, Amiya is drawn into Theresa's memories. There, the Sarkaz King calmly confesses: she has to defeat Amiya, has to make it happen.
Only then can I carry forward the ideal I never achieved. Only then can I set myself free.

Amiya stabs her own chest with the sword Theresa hands her, taking the Sarkaz King's power and will into herself. The pain was wrapped in warmth. Of her own will, Amiya chooses to become the new 'Sarkaz King.'

blocks.cap: "I really am a lucky person, Kal'tsit. Wherever I traveled, my closest friend was always there beside me."

blocks.x: Unable to hold back, W embraces the Sarkaz King, whose sight is failing. Theresa remembered it all — that mercenary who, camera in hand back at Babel, was too shy to approach. She'd been waiting, she says, for a photo together.

Before departing, Theresa finally gives W a name — 'Wiš'adel,' meaning 'one who wishes for a home.' Not 'W,' a name that already belonged to a dead Sarkaz, but a name that suits her. In return, she asks W to watch over Kazdel's fate.

And the Sarkaz King chooses the path of complete disappearance — leaving not even a trace of herself in Originium. In doing so, she sets free, in both life and death, the souls of the Sarkaz who had been used and bound for ten thousand years.

Logos rings a final bell for the vanishing souls, vowing that if there's no ferry, he will console their spirits himself — that he won't stop singing until the weeping of his kin ceases on the far shore.

blocks.cap: "I'm right here." — Theresa's warm power, though she has already departed, embraces Amiya as she holds on to the falling airship.

blocks.x: Epilogue — The Legacy of Mercy

As the storm subsides, the battlefield is turned upside down. Iron Duke Wellington, it turns out, had been on the side of Evlana, Queen of the Draco, all along. Betraying Castor and the other archdukes, he declares Tara's independence after over a hundred years.
Meanwhile, Reunion appears in Londinium once more. In the ruins turned into a city of the Infected, they join hands with ordinary people like Catherine and Tommy, beginning to reclaim their home.

Wiš'adel broadcasts on the royal army's frequency: any Sarkaz who wants to live should lay down their weapons and follow. As Theresa wished, she'll send as many of her kin home as she can. She signs off: "We are Babel."
Theresis walks through the door of truth Theresa left open — carrying a new vow to reclaim, this time, what belongs to them, from the arrogant creators who have ruled over the Sarkaz's fate.

As she fades away, Theresa looks one last time, through the Sarkaz King's eyes, at futures scattered across Terra — the icefields of Sami, the sea of Ægir, the cathedral of Laterano, all being swallowed by darkness. The hardships yet to come.

blocks.cap: The farthest place she could ever reach. Even as the darkness closes in, Theresa reaches out toward the one light within it.

blocks.x: The ending of the cloth doll's tale that Theresa never got to tell — this time, Amiya writes it herself. The doll was never alone. Beneath the river of tears, white, black, blue, and red friends were waiting.
Together, hand in hand, they crossed the river, and beyond it the endless golden sea, until at last they set foot on land again and found the Field of Hope.

Holding on was never mercy. Setting free, of one's own will — that was the Sarkaz King's final mercy. And the one to carry that story forward now is Amiya — together with a storyteller who never truly left.

main_15.tagline: Beings shaped as tools by their creator each choose to be 'themselves,' casting off the missions they were given. The Ark burns and falls, and one of them chooses to burn herself away to keep a final promise.

chars.name: Kal'tsit
chars.desc: Rhodes Island's doctor. The Doctor's oldest companion.

chars.name: Amiya
chars.desc: Leader of Rhodes Island. Wields the Black Crown.

chars.name: Ascalon
chars.desc: Theresis's disciple. A blade in the shadows.

chars.name: Warfarin
chars.desc: A Rhodes Island medic. Chloe's attending physician.

chars.name: Theresis
chars.desc: The Sarkaz Regent of Kazdel. Theresa's brother.

chars.name: Hilda
chars.desc: A devout Cautus Infected of Londinium.

chars.name: Mon3tr
chars.desc: Kal'tsit's symbiont. Always stays by Kal'tsit's side.

terms.name: Amnanam
terms.desc: The first Originium. A holy relic of the Sarkaz.

terms.name: Abyss
terms.desc: A sealed zone deep within the Rhodes Island vessel.

terms.name: Continuance of Civilization
terms.desc: The Black Crown. A system that explores the future and information.

terms.name: PRTS
terms.desc: Rhodes Island's command system.

terms.name: Fused Universe
terms.desc: A sea of information unfolding within Originium.

blocks.x: In the world of Terra, 'Catastrophes' strike periodically. Originium is left behind in their wake, and people who stay near that mineral too long contract Oripathy, becoming Infected.
The long war that swallowed Londinium is nearing its end. Theresa, fading away in the Fused Universe together with countless souls, left her brother Theresis the 'first Originium' — Amnanam.
This story begins with the fate of the one who now holds that Originium. And ten thousand years ago, somewhere in the cosmos where a single ship once slept.

blocks.cap: A burning Londinium. An Infected girl pushes through the smoke, trying to save even one more of the dying.

blocks.x: 1. The End Upon the Throne

Theresis returns to the underground audience chamber of the holy royal palace, holding Amnanam close. But his body has already been overrun by the Fused Universe's chaotic information, slowly hardening into Originium.

Before Kal'tsit, the Doctor, Amiya, and his disciple Ascalon, he reveals his final intent — to bury everything here and send Amnanam back to Kazdel, so the Sarkaz can hold their own fate in their own hands for the first time.

blocks.cap: The Doctor faces the fading Sarkaz King. "How exactly did the gods die..."

blocks.x: Originium begins to seize control of his hands, his eyes, even his thoughts. At the moment he raises his sword with his last shred of reason, Ascalon's assassination blade pierces his heart from within the smoke.

The one who put him on the throne was the very disciple he had tried to bring with him to the end. Theresis says he has now repaid an old debt, and pushes Ascalon away, telling her to take Amnanam to Kazdel.
I will stay behind. Do not... tell the Sarkaz of my end...
Upon Victoria's old throne, the Sarkaz Regent accepts his fate alone. Above, the cheers of Victoria's victory can be heard, and in the official record, he is left as nothing but 'missing.'

As his breath fades, he mocks the Doctor one last time — that they know nothing, that a day will come when they realize the true monster is themselves. And yet he tosses Ascalon's old stone dagger, once his own, toward the Doctor, telling them they have no right to witness his end, and sends them away.

Deep within the sea of information of the Fused Universe, he had encountered a being — the creator of Originium, just awakening. She calls the Sarkaz an 'error not accounted for in the plan,' the race most severely contaminated by Originium.

Theresis does not yield. He swings his sword, declaring that Theresa was not the first to rebel, nor will he be the last — that someone will always stand before her until no one can toy with the Sarkaz's fate ever again. And so, having carved his final judgment upon the creator, he sinks into the sea of information.

blocks.cap: In his final moment, a familiar white light rises in the darkness. Theresa's farewell sees her brother off.

blocks.x: 2. The Girl Who Awoke After Death

Turning back time, to the middle of the war for Londinium. The devout Cautus Infected **Hilda**, even as her own body collapses, keeps saving people, one after another. In the end, she too gives out amid the ruins.
But at the moment of death, someone catches her fragile, crumbling hand. When she opens her eyes again, Hilda finds herself aboard a massive vessel she has never seen before.
It's a ship orbiting an already-dead planet. Mechanical lifeforms called Photorefractors and beings who calmly accept their own doom welcome her aboard.

blocks.cap: A planet shattered to pieces floats among the stars. No sacred city, no god — only graves and laws remain.

blocks.x: Hilda is shaken. There is no paradise like the one her bishop grandfather spoke of, and even gods die before ruin — she learns this here. She begins searching for the owner of the ship that saved her, the linguist Priestess.

The 'ghost' she meets aboard the ship is the last survivor of a race that lived by devouring planets. Even he, he says calmly, could do nothing when true ruin finally arrived. There is no eternal nation, no endless paradise, and even intelligent life is insignificant to the cosmos.
Hilda, who once thought of death only as a path to a new environment, asks a question here too — can the suffering brought by Originium truly never be erased? But no one there knows the answer.
The name the people there call this ship, in their own tongue, is this: 'Rhodes Island.'
3. Three Years Later, People Scatter

Three years after the Londinium incident. PRTS, Rhodes Island's command system, has been suffering increasingly frequent, unexplained errors. Kal'tsit senses instinctively that this is connected to the vessel's deep sealed zone, the 'Abyss.'
Just in case, Kal'tsit decides to reorganize the mothership entirely. Operators leave one by one on assignment to branch offices around the world, and the mothership grows emptier and emptier.

Warmy sets off vowing to fill miners' bellies with her cooking, and Zima's group heads for their home in Ursus despite their fear. Ceobe bakes piles of cookies for those departing. Quiet days of farewell.

Kal'tsit quietly tells Folinic, who is leaving for Victoria, not to force herself to control her fear — that she has never once expected her to be like her mother, or like Kal'tsit herself. Just don't forget the smile you had the first time you walked into this room, she says.

In the medical department is a special patient, **Chloe**. Strangely, the deeper her Oripathy progresses, the healthier her body becomes, while her pain only grows worse. It's the first sign that something about Originium is changing.

blocks.cap: At the center of the blueprint, a zone never even recorded in PRTS. Kal'tsit points to it. "The Abyss. The beginning of everything, the end of everything."

blocks.x: 4. Beyond the Black Crown

Following the clue Kal'tsit left behind, Amiya and the Doctor dive their consciousness together into the Black Crown — the 'Continuance of Civilization.' Guided by Theresa, they cross the 'boundary' of knowledge that Terra can reach, into the singularity beyond.

blocks.cap: Pink threads tangle and coil, weaving a path to the singularity beyond the boundary. Following the trail Kal'tsit left behind.

blocks.x: On that path, the two glimpse the Doctor's lost past. The story a young Doctor once told Amiya in the mining pit, about a vehicle resembling an Originium bug's egg sac, a story of a single-log bridge stretched between the stars — it was all real.
Amiya confesses she'd struggled with all her might not to forget that story. It was a gift the Doctor had given her, and it was their shared past.
This time, we saw it together. Both of us — you and I — have come to know a vanished life once more.

blocks.cap: Beyond the singularity lay an unfamiliar era. A civilization sailing a sea of stars, and the silhouette of someone standing beside the Doctor.

blocks.x: The singularity spills out memories of a bygone age. A civilization drifting through the cosmos, people standing against the approaching ruin called 'It,' and the linguist Priestess, who once stood by the Doctor's side.
Priestess once told the Doctor: if we can create a language to explain God, doesn't that mean we can understand God? Together, the two of them created Originium to answer that ultimate question.

The Doctor's old codename was 'the Prophet.' Originium was born from a wish to be a gift guiding the next civilization, but no one had foreseen the suffering of Oripathy that would come with it. From within that, the Doctor draws out a clue to a technique for handling Originium crystals.
5. The Awakening Ship
While the Doctor's group is deep within their consciousness, Rhodes Island — the vessel itself — awakens. PRTS blocks all access and seizes control of every facility aboard.
The corridors twist like a maze, and enemies made of Originium crystal endlessly spawn. The mothership changes its own course and begins charging toward a massive vein.

The scattered Operators fight even as communications are cut off. Mantra links everyone's voices together through Arts, and Raidian sets up a makeshift comms network, barely stitching Rhodes Island back into one.

The robots corrupted and rampaging under PRTS's influence are, unexpectedly, saved by Preston. He overwrites their corrupted sections with his own core code, sharing with them, all the while, his own dream — his daughter's wedding. We're in the same boat, he says, grumbling, but reaching out a hand anyway.
6. The Price Kal'tsit Paid
Kal'tsit stands at the deepest part of the vessel, before the Doctor's sarcophagus. The very place where, long ago, she awakened his sleeping hope, and gained an unerasable pain.

blocks.cap: She lays a single white flower atop the sarcophagus. "This is the spark she stole from a god."

blocks.x: Kal'tsit decides to connect herself to the sarcophagus and become PRTS's interface. As the price, she must sacrifice half her own life — she must sever the cyclical system that has given her endless life until now.

At that moment, her ten-thousand-year connection with Mon3tr is also completely severed. For the first time, Mon3tr loses Kal'tsit. And yet Kal'tsit becomes connected to everything on Terra, becoming eyes for her scattered comrades.
My goal isn't somewhere far across Terra. That person is right here, beside me.

Outside the sarcophagus, Mon3tr lets out a cry tangled with confusion and grief. Now that the connection carried on for ten thousand years has snapped, Mon3tr must judge and act on its own. Kal'tsit's final order was just one thing: protect the Doctor and Amiya, no matter what.
7. The Name AMa-10

The sarcophagus hands Kal'tsit one last truth. Kal'tsit was originally a mechanical lifeform created by 'the Prophet' — the Doctor — in a bygone age, called AMa-10. Thanks to its cyclical structure, it gained a body that would never die.

blocks.cap: Savior of this earth. Destroyer of this earth. Originium was a legacy of two divergent futures, left behind by a past civilization facing its own ruin.

blocks.x: At some point, the Doctor gave this mechanical life a true name. A mineral where light refracts in two directions within its crystal — calcite. Calcite — Kal'tsit. The twin cyclical structure it formed with Mon3tr is what gave the two of them undying life.

The Prophet knew that intervening in a civilization's development was arrogance, yet, thinking it might be a last chance, meant to ask Kal'tsit to protect the life of this earth. But he soon takes those words back. Instead, he leaves only one thing behind: not a mission, but freedom.

And the Doctor left her freedom instead of a mission — to find the meaning of life herself, to seek hope and a future of her own. Kal'tsit took those words as her lifelong goal and has protected the life of this earth ever since.
8. 'Her'
The Catastrophe clouds fold away and vanish, and Originium blankets the entire deck. Descending upon it is a woman draped in white — Priestess, the linguist of the bygone age who created Originium.

She treats the Doctor as an old colleague, reaching out a hand and proposing they merge Terra with Originium together to build 'a world of our own' — hoping only that the memory-lost Doctor will return to her.
Just as the Doctor is about to instinctively step forward, Kal'tsit grabs their arm. But she doesn't force an answer — in the end, she lets go, leaving the choice to the Doctor.

Then Kal'tsit stands before Priestess. Kal'tsit had already planted a trap in Priestess's awakening chamber beforehand — now that she's awake and possesses a body, Priestess can no longer return to Originium's sea of information; she's merely human.
Priestess, you are not the creator of Terra. Every life on this earth has no obligation to follow your will. ...This is my revenge, and my final act of resistance.
Kal'tsit declares herself Rhodes Island's doctor and a comrade of the Doctor and Amiya, calls Priestess the enemy of every life on Terra, and gives the order to Mon3tr.
