Along the road to the Guard Department stands Daigu Plaza. A Reunion caster there senses something is off — Lungmen is completely empty, and every expensive item has nothing left but its price tag.
It's as if someone cleared everything out beforehand. What's more, Reunion units in other districts are disappearing one by one, without a trace, their microphones still switched on.

Before that unease can fully take root, Ch'en's Guard Department rappels down from the rooftop and crashes through the windows. Ch'en orders them to blow up Reunion, cover and all — even knowing full well that Daigu Plaza belongs to Swire's family.

Swire blows up over the comm, and a piano worth 1.8 million Lungmen dollars gets used as cover and smashed to pieces. Ch'en's real goal lies elsewhere — she waits for the enemy to finish their report, letting word spread across all of Lungmen that 'the Guard Department is here.'
If you've finished your report, then your job here is done.
7. Life and Death Together
Then explosives hidden on the third floor detonate right beneath Ch'en's feet. Hoshiguma shields her with her shield, and the two of them tumble down the stairs together.
With one arm broken, Hoshiguma bears the weight of two floors' worth of debris with just her shield. Then she pushes Ch'en up, saving her life once again.
But this time, Ch'en stubbornly refuses to leave Hoshiguma behind. The old Ch'en would have weighed the costs and put the team first — but not now.

blocks.cap: The dragon who always stood behind the shield now stands in front of her wounded partner instead. "Just let me be your shield sometimes."

blocks.x: For the first time, Ch'en lays her true feelings bare. She says Hoshiguma is no longer just Inspector Hoshiguma to her — she's Hoshiguma, her friend and partner.
Your shield has always held me up until now. Let me be your shield sometimes too. Hoshiguma.

Hoshiguma laughs, saying Ch'en can deliver a line like that without even blushing. Then she gives Ch'en, who's heading for the Guard Department, just one warning — never get lost in some thought and act on stubbornness alone. Don't fight for anything other than who you are right now.

In place of the injured Hoshiguma, who drops out, Swire calls in over comms with some silly joke. She lightens the tense mood with talk of a sandwich from Lang Yam Restaurant, then leaves one last line — that life matters most, so don't go risking it over whatever's in front of you.
8. To Reclaim the Guard Department
In front of the Guard Department building, Ch'en stands before her officers. We are neither rioters nor criminals, she tells them — remember your obedience, your duty, your resolve.

blocks.cap: Facing the building that once symbolized safety and law, the Guard Department forms ranks. "Lungmen must never again have to worry because of us."

blocks.x: As the Lungmen Guard Department, let's take back what's ours. That alone is enough.

But the inside of the building is strangely quiet. There's not a single enemy on the lower floors, and while Reunion numbers grow the higher they go, there's no sign of an attack — as if they're all taking a nap. The command post sits on the rooftop, an obvious trap.
9. Devils
Waiting on the rooftop are Reunion's boy commander **Mephisto** and his quiet sniper **Faust** — a man who commands the Infected with the precision of chess pieces.
When Mephisto uses his Arts, Originium grows through the bodies of fallen Reunion soldiers, piercing through them. Their wounds heal and they feel no pain — remade into 'undying bodyguards.'

But Ch'en was prepared too. Four of the twenty-one Infected in the Guard Department are spies she planted, and one of them leaked Mephisto's entire plan to her. Even Mephisto's belief that he'd rescued Faust was part of Ch'en's setup from the start.
Mephisto tries to win Ch'en over, telling her she too will eventually be used up and discarded by Lungmen.
Do you know that you'll end up being used like a chess piece by those people, and then thrown away?
10. The Sword That Won't Draw

Ch'en carries a sword lent to her by Director Wei — the **Dragonslayer Sword**, codename Crimsonnight. An extraordinary weapon, she'd been told never to let it exude killing intent except when it was truly worth drawing.
Ch'en tries to draw it, but Crimsonnight refuses to leave its sheath. Caught in Faust's crossfire, Ch'en can only draw the blade halfway, barely deflecting arrows to hold her ground.

blocks.cap: "This sword isn't meant to be used on the likes of you." Even so, Ch'en grips the hilt, vowing that he will pay a price no matter what.

blocks.x: Mephisto mocks Ch'en as a mere game piece, but Ch'en cuts him off flatly — she says she's nobody's pawn.
This kind of game board doesn't suit me. Kid, I'm not anyone's game piece — I'm Lungmen's blade.

But Mephisto knows Ch'en's weakness precisely. He says the 'Leader' told him, seeing right through the fact that Ch'en can't draw her sword. In the end, Ch'en is thrown back by a shockwave, her ribs broken.

Mephisto's Originium Arts only work on the Infected. Yet seeing the dust clinging to Ch'en, he mutters something meaningful, as if he's noticed something. What exactly Ch'en is — the story never gives a clear answer.
11. Reinforcements from the Sky
Just as Ch'en braces herself even to die there, Rhodes Island's aircraft, the 'Bad Guys,' appears in the sky.

blocks.cap: "There's no need to go that far, Captain Ch'en!" Rhodes Island, once left behind, now comes cutting back through the sky.

blocks.x: Fire-wielding Operator **Blaze** drops in, and **Amiya** uses her Arts to restore the erased emotions of Mephisto's bodyguards. The emotionless Infected suddenly begin to weep, silently, all at once.
Amiya declares to Mephisto that she won't back down. But she says he isn't her enemy — the only thing she means to destroy is his grudge and his cruelty.
Mephisto, the one standing alone on this battlefield right now — that's you!

Ch'en apologizes to Amiya. When Amiya plays dumb, saying the battlefield was too loud to hear anything, Ch'en once again swallows a sincere apology. This time, she decides to hand command over to the Doctor, saying she'll trust him, just once.
12. Be Dyed Crimson

Blaze catches Faust's invisible crossbowmen, the 'Phantom Crossbowmen.' With a technique that sprays her own blood and sets it alight, she burns away their air-refraction camouflage entirely, exposing them.

With the Doctor's command and Rhodes Island's covering fire, an opening finally appears. Ch'en doesn't miss the moment — she draws the Dragonslayer Sword and cuts down over a dozen bodyguards in a single stroke.
Be dyed crimson...

blocks.cap: The rooftop collapses under a single blow. Amid the crumbling ruins, Mephisto and Faust barely manage to pull themselves free.

blocks.x: Mephisto and Faust retreat under cover of the chain of explosions. To the very end, Mephisto can't understand it — why haven't the twenty squads and two battalions of Reunion he mustered shown up yet?

Betrayal? A leak? Mephisto tries to reassure himself that Big Sister Talulah couldn't possibly have orchestrated something like this. That name is the first hint, slipping out, of this night's true weight.
13. Targeted Treatment
Over the comm, the voices of **Wei Yenwu** and Kal'tsit overlap. And it's revealed that all of this was Wei Yenwu's plan from the very start.
Leaving Amiya in the abandoned city, deliberately isolating the Guard Department — all of it was bait to make Reunion let its guard down and draw out its full forces.

Herd the scattered Reunion forces into one place with a citywide blockade across Lungmen, then wipe them out entirely with elites lying in ambush. Cutting out only the diseased tissue from the wound that is the city — that's precisely 'targeted treatment.' This was the true identity of the classified order Ch'en had been swallowing all along.

Rhodes Island knows they were used. But evidence is lacking for now, and it's also true that Swire helped hold off the pursuers together with them, so Amiya swallows her anger. Wei says calmly that as long as the result is satisfactory, that's all that matters.
Lungmen's problems must be something Lungmen itself can resolve entirely on its own. That is what matters most of all.

Then he adds, speaking to the Doctor, that though they may not be friends, at least they aren't enemies yet. Their cooperation remains valid until Reunion is completely wiped out, he says — and so Rhodes Island and Lungmen's precarious partnership continues, teetering along.
Epilogue — A Night That Was Only the Beginning
In the Director's office, Wei Yenwu sits across from his longtime companion Fumizuki. Even though it's a night of victory, Wei's expression is dark.

This was clearly an all-out assault by Reunion — so why didn't their leader, Talulah, show up in Lungmen at all? A suspicious air hangs over the entire Ursus Empire. A feeling that all of this is merely the beginning of something.

Fumizuki brings up an old name. That battle twenty years ago that stopped Ursus's conquest — if they had lost then, the person sitting in Wei's seat right now would have been someone else. Duke Kashchey.

blocks.cap: The long night ends, and dawn breaks. But this victory is only the first piece of a far greater picture.

blocks.x: Fumizuki says she somehow senses that person's presence in Talulah. Wei's answer quietly lays down this night's final truth.
But he's already dead. Killed by Talulah's own hand.

Lungmen's cold-blooded blade abandoned a comrade and even cut through her own old home to drive Reunion into a perfect trap. But the one who drew up that perfect plan wasn't the one holding the sword — and the orders, the guilt, and the old wounds she had to swallow still remain inside Ch'en. And as Wei Yenwu sensed, this long night had only just begun.

main_6.tagline: In Lungmen after Reunion is driven out, the very people who claimed to protect the city end up cutting into its own flesh. What the Infected with nowhere left to go held onto until the end wasn't ideology or revenge, but the right to choose for themselves.

chars.name: Amiya
chars.desc: Leader of Rhodes Island. Reads emotions.

chars.name: Blaze
chars.desc: An Elite Operator who wields heat. Carries the Doctor on her back.

chars.name: GreyThroat
chars.desc: A non-Infected sniper of Rhodes Island. Torn over where she belongs.

chars.name: Ch'en
chars.desc: Captain of the Lungmen Guard Department's Special Inspection Team. Digs for the truth.

chars.name: Swire
chars.desc: Young lady of a Lungmen merchant house. Ch'en's longtime comrade.

chars.name: Kal'tsit
chars.desc: Rhodes Island's doctor. Commands Mon3tr.

chars.name: Frost Nova
chars.desc: Commander of the Snow Devil Squad. Wields frost Arts.

chars.name: Mephisto
chars.desc: A boy commander of Reunion who commands the Infected.

chars.name: Faust
chars.desc: A taciturn sniper at Mephisto's side. A Reunion commander.

chars.name: Crownslayer
chars.desc: A Reunion agent pursuing Rhodes Island.

chars.name: Wei Yenwu
chars.desc: Lungmen's chief administrator.

terms.name: Lungmen
terms.desc: A vast city that flourished by welcoming people from every walk of life.

terms.name: Guard Department
terms.desc: Lungmen's enforcement arm. The pillar its citizens lean on.

terms.name: Reunion
terms.desc: An armed Infected organization that rose up in Ursus.

terms.name: Snow Devil Squad
terms.desc: A Reunion guerrilla unit that once roamed the snowfields.

terms.name: Black Hats
terms.desc: A Lungmen special unit that moves while concealing its identity.

terms.name: the Slums
terms.desc: Lungmen's lower district, home to the Infected and the poor.

blocks.x: Reunion's invasion of Lungmen is nearly over. Rhodes Island has joined forces with Lungmen's Guard Department for one final joint operation, to sweep out the last Reunion remnants left in the city.
On the surface, it's a mop-up operation whose outcome is already decided. And yet, this story is titled 'Necrosis'.
The old saying that rotten flesh must be cut away to save the rest — it was aimed at Reunion and Lungmen alike.
1. How About a Sauna? (Instructor: The Blazing Cat)
Before Reunion, cornered by the blockade, an Operator drops straight out of the sky. A cat who wields heat, the Elite Operator **Blaze**.

Blaze introduces herself on the spot and suggests they quietly lay down their weapons. Since they're all Infected, she says, she's giving them a chance. And if they resist, she'll warm up the air around them first — all with a smile.

blocks.cap: "Whose side's air is hotter, mine or yours?" — Blaze's invitation to the sauna is one you can't refuse.

blocks.x: This chaotic cat has been put in charge of the Doctor — carrying them around bodily across a battlefield with no comms.

Amiya quietly asks the Doctor for a favor. Not to protect Blaze by force, but to look after her heart, since she has to keep fighting fellow Infected. This is where the Doctor's long journey to earn an Operator's trust begins.
2. Ch'en of the Guard Department, and a Secret Order

The other flank of the encirclement is handled by the Guard Department. Special Inspection Team Captain **Ch'en** and the merchant house's young lady **Swire**, bickering all the while, drive Reunion's main force into a fixed position.

On the surface, it's a smooth joint operation. But behind it, Chief Administrator **Wei Yenwu** quietly gives someone an order — don't contact him for three weeks, and hand over all information to 'Guillory's daughter'.
And a voice from some night long ago overlaps with it. A man who called Wei his 'sworn brother' and asked him to look after his woman and daughter. He said he'd already picked the daughter's name.
……Talulah.
"Don't take it too much to heart. I know it all already." The man says that and vanishes. Beneath Lungmen's clean-looking victory, something was already festering.
3. The Commander Who Turns His Own Men into Monsters

Cornered, Reunion's commander **Mephisto** resorts to the worst move to survive. He uses his own Arts to force his comrades' infections into runaway overdrive, turning them into monsters that can't even feel pain.
Those monsters don't distinguish friend from foe. They tear into people who were their own squadmates moments ago, devour them, and multiply, creating more monsters.

blocks.cap: "All of Lungmen will become your soil." — Mephisto plants catastrophe in the city, using his own kin as seeds.

blocks.x: The sniper at his side, **Faust**, freezes in front of the sight. She cries out that these aren't monsters, they're living people.
And Faust silently turns around, like someone who already knows which side she has to protect.
4. Two Snipers, Back to Back

Faust, separated from her unit, is caught by **GreyThroat**, Rhodes Island's non-Infected sniper. Arts that erase sight, and a piercing arrow — both are lone snipers, so if they pull the trigger at the same time, they both die.

blocks.cap: "If we pull the trigger at the same moment, we both die." — In the fog, two snipers aim their barrels at each other's backs.

blocks.x: GreyThroat lowers her weapon, saying she saw Faust carry off a wounded Infected, saw her not shoot a straggling Guard Department officer. You're doing the right thing, she says.

Faust answers coldly. A person who only follows orders and leans on someone else's judgment ends up becoming something terrifying. And yet, since neither of them fired, she lets GreyThroat live this time.

For GreyThroat, so used to orders that she can't judge for herself, that one line left by an enemy lingers long after. Having given her parents to Oripathy research and lost them in the clash between the Infected and the authorities, she belongs fully to nowhere, and still hasn't decided whether to stay with Rhodes Island or leave.
To a GreyThroat like that, this invasion is full of question marks. What is Rhodes Island even fighting for — and if it helps the Infected, why does it have to aim its guns at other Infected?
5. People Erased in the Slums
Around that time, something strange happens in the Slums. Half the residents of one district vanish outright, and someone has already cleaned away the Reunion corpses without a trace.
Ch'en spots Black Hats slipping across a rooftop — an unidentified unit that erases everyone in sight, Infected or citizen, without distinction.
The truth that emerges after her pressing is grim. That special unit wasn't Reunion — it was Lungmen's own hand, unleashed by Wei Yenwu to gut the Slums outright.
Even **Lin Xiya**, who cherished the Slums, is complicit in this. What the citizens fear is the Infected, she says — whether that Infected is Reunion or not doesn't matter.
Ch'en forces the special unit to withdraw, but the answer that comes back is a single chilling warning: don't try to find out what happened here, and 'don't investigate the drainage system'.

And the Infected informant 'Nine', who had been attached to Ch'en, quietly leaves the Guard Department after seeing all this. Because she's learned Lungmen has no intention of protecting the Infected. No matter where they live, she says, the Infected are all in the same position.
6. Eno and Sasha
Mephisto and Faust both had names, once.
In the sewers of Chernobog, there was a child who always smiled, **Eno**. A child who, even while enduring abuse, sang songs for his only friend, **Sasha**.

Eno believed that if he just did as people told him, everyone would be happy. So he moved the children who bullied him like chess pieces, burning away everything that had hurt him — thinking Sasha would be pleased.

Sasha said through tears — not what you're forced to do, not revenge, just do what you actually want to do. The two children swallowed Originium together and became Infected together, vowing to survive as one.
It was Reunion's leader who gave Eno a new name. She told him the past-him no longer existed, and to seek his ideal along with the name 'Mephisto'. She called herself **Talulah**.
But Sasha notices. That after passing through that village, Talulah had changed completely. That she'd become someone who even exploits Mephisto's trust.
7. Sasha's Last Arrow

Once it becomes impossible to bring their transformed comrades back, Faust has her unit retreat toward the Slums and decides to stay behind as bait. Since those monsters won't attack her, she says, they can buy time together.
Mephisto cries out — we promised to survive together! Faust answers quietly. If you come to choose for yourself and want something, she says, whatever it is, I won't blame you for it.
And she uses her final Arts, knowing that using it again will destroy her infected organ. There's no reason left for me to hold back now, she says.
Even alone, that's fine — just live. Don't die. This is my… last wish.

blocks.cap: "Farewell, Eno." — She nocks the arrow, draws the string, and takes her final aim.

blocks.x: The Phantom Crossbowmen hold nothing back with high-explosive rounds for her — exactly Faust's own way of saying no tribute to a fallen comrade is ever too much.
8. Just Falling Out of the Sky
Meanwhile, Amiya's group is surrounded by the Snow Devil Squad in the ruins. That's when Blaze drops from the sky with no rope at all, and clashes head-on with Reunion's frost Arts user.

blocks.cap: "When have you ever seen me use a rope?" — Blaze jumps down recklessly again today.

blocks.x: That opponent is **Frost Nova**, commander of the Snow Devil Squad. A dying Infected whose whole body is wrapped in cold colder than winter itself. Blaze, showing respect for her strength, urges her not to push herself any further.
During the fierce battle the ground collapses, and the Doctor falls into the ruins below along with Frost Nova. Blaze uses compressed air to slow the Doctor's fall, protecting them all the way down.

The remaining Snow Devils make an unexpected offer — a ceasefire. Aren't both our commanders rabbits anyway, they say. These people, who seemed like they'd be as cold and merciless as ice, turn out to value their comrades' lives above all else.

It was actually them who came here to hunt, they say. They'd long wanted to clean out a Reunion that hurts the innocent, like Mephisto does. And so Rhodes Island and the Snow Devils begin digging through the ruins together.
9. Kal'tsit and Crownslayer
Elsewhere, Rhodes Island's doctor **Kal'tsit** captures the Reunion agent **Crownslayer** in the Slums, with the wolf-scented girl Red holding her down.

blocks.cap: Neither the fog nor her codename is any use. Kal'tsit stands before the captured Crownslayer.

blocks.x: Crownslayer curses Kal'tsit as a traitor, saying it's Kal'tsit's fault that Ursus slaughtered her father and the other scientists. Kal'tsit calmly tells her about her father Illya, and about Crownslayer herself as a child.

The real traitor wasn't Kal'tsit, but a greedy empire that trampled on the scientists' devotion. Kal'tsit says she's no Caster, summons Mon3tr, and lets her go alive even though she could have killed her.
Leave this place. And find another path — the true path that can change this world.
Kal'tsit believes she will someday become a 'seed of unrest' that wakes from Reunion's madness. We don't stand in for the law, she says, and we have no right to judge anyone.
10. Beneath the Ruins, Facing the Frost
To the Doctor trapped beneath the ruins, Frost Nova tells her own story — a kind of thank-you for the Doctor not killing her while she couldn't move.

She was born at a mine in the tundra of northwestern Ursus. Her parents and grandmother were taken and killed one after another, and at eleven, her turn came. That's when Arts burst out of her and froze her executioners to death.

blocks.cap: Just before the final massacre, a band of guerrillas arrives — Patriot's unit, from Bulldrokarsty.

blocks.x: The one who took her in was Bulldrokarsty, an old war hero of Ursus. Even with an armorless arm about to go necrotic, he wrapped the ice-cold child in a tight embrace — she called him father.

Unable to let anyone touch her because of the cold, only able to feel warmth from hot drinks, a single spicy candy is her one pleasure. She plays mischievous pranks on the Doctor, then quietly laughs and apologizes.

She doesn't hate the people of Ursus. Even those raised to hate the Infected were, before hatred blinded them, just ordinary people. Her only enemy is the 'Ursus Empire' that drove the Infected to the brink.
She says a person who doesn't fear death is someone with no attachment left to the world. And yet, she quietly admits, she herself has failed to find the answer to that question for over a decade.

Now she thinks she has to stop even Talulah, who seems to have deceived the Infected, with her own hands — she has to keep, within whatever time is left, the promise to give her brothers and sisters a place to live.
11. The Snow Devils' Last Toast
The squad knows Frost Nova doesn't have much time left. Her Arts have been eating away at her body, so she grows lighter every time they hold her.
They decide to entrust their sleeping commander to Faust's unit, to be sent to her father Patriot's side — and to stay behind themselves to hold off the pursuit.
Warriors are bound to die someday, they say, sharing vodka they'd smuggled along, even passing a sip to their youngest member. Dying without ever knowing the taste would be too sad, after all.
When we're free, that's when we'll cry out loud… We haven't won yet, so we've no right to cry!
The last sip is offered to that cat who once said she'd drink with them — praying that everything works out for those kids. Blaze rushes over too late, and in the end can't break their resolve.
12. Winter Falls
Having lost all her brothers and sisters, Frost Nova waits for the Doctor's group beneath the ruins. With no one left beside her now, she says, for the first time she can give it everything she has.
She gathers the cold of the whole world to bear down on them, freezing all ten of Amiya's rings solid, shattering GreyThroat's arrows in midair. Asked why she goes this far, she answers.
As a commander, she's already lost, she says, but as a warrior, she hasn't lost yet. If I can't even beat myself, how could I face Talulah — beat me, and show me hope.
Amiya reads her rage and weeps, sensing that what Frost Nova endures — the pain in her heart — is many times greater than the pain of drawing out an Originium crystal.
So they don't hold back, and fight to the end. And they win. Frost Nova smiles and lets her head drop.

blocks.cap: "From now on, I'll… be by your side." — She leaves one last promise, that she wants to enter Rhodes Island.

blocks.x: She asks the Doctor a favor. Stop the Talulah of now, she says, but help the 'real Talulah' who once walked that muddy road with her. And she wants to die at her father's side, to pick with her own hands the fruit of what she planted.
The rotten part has to be cut away. Defeat me, and defeat Talulah. And the body that was colder than winter grows quiet and cold at last.
13. Don't Investigate the Drainage System
After the battle ends, Ch'en keeps hearing the same warning — 'don't investigate the drainage system.' Swire tries desperately to stop her, but Ch'en opens the door in the end.
The smell tells her where the erased people of the Slums went. Ch'en heads straight for Wei Yenwu.

Ch'en confronts him — that he erased the very Lungmen citizens who trusted and supported him, wholesale, just for being Infected, to protect his own position. And she brings up a secret long buried — that it was Wei who killed Talulah's father, and Wei who entangled Ch'en's mother.
At the same moment, Amiya visits Wei and voids the successful contract herself, because Lungmen deceived the Guard Department, the citizens, and the Infected alike, using people as game pieces.
You must not treat living people as game pieces. A person is a person.
Rhodes Island doesn't fight only for the Infected. Clearing the shadow of Oripathy from this world, while never using anyone as a tool — that's the principle Amiya upholds.
Epilogue — A City Draws Near
But the real crisis hasn't come yet. Through the entire annihilation of Reunion in Lungmen, its supreme commander never once showed her face.
Talulah had been in Chernobog the whole time. From the moment the fire started to the moment they caught their breath, she had always been watching Lungmen.

blocks.cap: 36 hours later, the core of the nomadic city Chernobog collides with Lungmen.

blocks.x: Kal'tsit reports it. Chernobog's core is approaching Lungmen, endlessly broadcasting to the whole city. The message that leaves even Wei Yenwu speechless is a single sentence.
"This city is the territory of Ursus."

In Lungmen after Reunion was driven out, those who claimed to protect the city cut into its own flesh, and the Infected with nowhere to go closed their eyes not for ideology or revenge, but by their own choice. And where winter had fallen, an entire frozen city now comes bearing down. Necrosis is not over yet.

main_7.tagline: A dead city rolls whole toward Lungmen, and those who crawl into its coffin-like Core to stop it betray one another again and again. In the end, an old warrior who fought fate for two hundred years turns his back on it for the first time.

chars.name: Amiya
chars.desc: Leader of Rhodes Island. Commands this Core operation.

chars.name: Kal'tsit
chars.desc: Rhodes Island's doctor, its true head. Knows the Infected best.

chars.name: Ch'en
chars.desc: Captain of the Lungmen Guard Department's Special Inspection Team. Wields the dragon-slaying sword Sui.

chars.name: Hoshiguma
chars.desc: A Guard Department officer. An oni with a broken horn. Ch'en's longtime friend.

chars.name: Rosmontis
chars.desc: Rhodes Island's annihilation-warfare Elite. A fourteen-year-old Feline girl.

chars.name: Nearl
chars.desc: The Radiant Knight of Kazimierz. Holds the line with her shield.

chars.name: W
chars.desc: A Sarkaz mercenary captain from Victoria. Specializes in bombs.

chars.name: Wei Yenwu
chars.desc: Lungmen's ruler. Head of the Guard Department.

chars.name: Talulah
chars.desc: Leader of Reunion. Is driving the Core toward Lungmen.

chars.name: Patriot
chars.desc: An old warrior of the guerrilla unit. Called the shield of the Infected.

terms.name: Chernobog's Core
terms.desc: The heart of the nomadic city Reunion is driving. About to collide with Lungmen.

terms.name: Reunion
terms.desc: An armed Infected organization that rose up in Ursus.

terms.name: Lungmen
terms.desc: The nomadic city of Yan. The end of the Core's path.

terms.name: Infected Guerrillas
terms.desc: The Elite unit led by Patriot. Strict in discipline.

terms.name: Sarkaz
terms.desc: A persecuted, wandering people. They call themselves demonkin.

terms.name: Wendigo
terms.desc: A branch of the Sarkaz. The bloodline closest to death.

terms.name: Sui
terms.desc: Ch'en's dragon-slaying sword. Forged in fire, cuts through Arts.

blocks.x: Chernobog, a city swallowed by Catastrophe and left for dead. Reunion has revived that city's heart, the 'Core', and is rolling an entire nomadic city toward Lungmen.
Thirty-two hours remain before the Core collides with Lungmen. A collision would mean war between Ursus and Yan.
And the world will remember only one cold fact — that "the Infected started a war."

blocks.cap: "Not a bad opening for a farce." — The story opens at the heart of Reunion, the Core.

blocks.x: 1. An Improvised Explosive Suicide Act

At the top of the Core, the Sarkaz mercenary captain **W** comes to see Reunion's leader **Talulah**. She approaches under the pretense of gathering intel, but her real goal is to stop the Core's march on Lungmen.
But Talulah reads through W's intentions from the very start — down to the things W thought she'd hidden even from herself.

Talulah is someone who hides herself behind every kind of face. A fighter before Reunion, a business partner before W, a gentle daughter before Patriot. Each mask, once peeled away, is only another mask.
As talk of W's intangible Arts unfolds, the hundred-odd bombs W had planted in the steel base melt away without a trace, entirely consumed by Talulah's heat.
Cornered, W turns her own body into a bomb and detonates, falling into the Core district. Preparing something worse than dying is W's way.

W laughs even as she dies. She thinks she was actually afraid of this dragon-horned woman — not that she disliked her, but that she feared her. In front of a monster steeped in lies, someone who plans ahead always keeps a final move in reserve.
I hope it was a sufficiently painful death, W of Victoria.

Talulah goes on to win over the Sarkaz mercenaries by promising them 'an age where war becomes king', pulling them into her grip. And to Patriot, she entrusts the key that can stop the Core, still playing the role of a gentle leader.

blocks.h: 2. A Night of Farewells — The One Who Leapt from the Window

blocks.x: Meanwhile, in Lungmen. In Chief Administrator Wei Yenwu's office, Kal'tsit, Amiya, and Guard Department Captain **Ch'en** discuss the situation.
The truth comes out. Ursus handed Chernobog over to Reunion in its entirety, letting the Infected flow into Lungmen — the whole scheme laid out to unfold like flowing water.
Wei Yenwu makes his decision. He will accept war and strike the Core preemptively — intending to pay Lungmen's debt with his own death.
Ch'en can't bear that approach. The moment Wei Yenwu calls the Infected of the Slums a 'mistake', Ch'en reveals that she, too, is that 'mistake'.
Because I'm Infected too. If there's no place for the Infected in this city, then I shouldn't be here either.

Ch'en, Infected since three years ago, returns her Guard Department badge and turns her back on Lungmen. When her mentor and uncle Wei Yenwu blocks her path with black-cloaked soldiers, Ch'en draws Sui and cuts a way through.
Then she breaks a window hundreds of meters up and leaps straight down, leaving Wei Yenwu's pleas behind her.

blocks.cap: "Now we owe each other nothing." — The parting of mentor-and-uncle Wei Yenwu, and niece-and-student Ch'en.

blocks.x: Ch'en heads to the Core alone for one reason — Reunion's leader Talulah is none other than her older sister.
On the path out of Lungmen, **Hoshiguma** is waiting for Ch'en. The Guard Department has already put out a warrant for her.
The broken-horned oni trusts Ch'en — and that's exactly why she can't let her go. Standing alone against Reunion, she says, is throwing your life away for nothing.

blocks.cap: "You are not leaving Lungmen." — A friend blocks a friend's path.

blocks.x: Hoshiguma is someone who's had precious things taken from her, one by one, by time. She says she's crushed her own comrades' dreams with her own hands before, and that she can't afford to lose Ch'en too.
Even after Ch'en confesses that she's Infected, that her sister is Talulah, Hoshiguma's answer stays the same — that it matters, and that's exactly why she can't let her go.
You're Infected? I couldn't care less.
In the end, the two cross blades. And for the first time, Ch'en's sword leaves a wound on the oni.
Hoshiguma finally steps back, accepting Ch'en's request to keep, in her place, the promise sealed by the teddy bear left behind by the Ursus girl Misha — a promise to the children of the Slums.

blocks.cap: "Go! And don't ever come back." Then, left alone, she mutters, "Liar… who else worries about this city as much as you do?"

blocks.h: 4. Leave It to the Real Experts

blocks.x: At the Lungmen residence, Wei Yenwu's wife Fumizuki holds her husband back. Does becoming Infected mean Ch'en and Talulah stop being your nieces, she asks — are you really going to let them die in a foreign land like this?
That's when Kal'tsit steps in. The true experts for handling the Core aren't Lungmen's army, she says — it's Rhodes Island.
Only the Infected can face the Infected.

After also pointing out that Talulah may be Duke Kashchey's heir, Kal'tsit secures two things from Wei Yenwu: a promise not to interfere with Rhodes Island's operation, and the keepsake of Edward Artorius, who died in Lungmen twenty years ago — Talulah's father.
Meanwhile, in the biological processing room of the Rhodes Island vessel, the Feline girl Rosmontis quietly sends someone off — Frost Nova, a Reunion commander who fell in Lungmen.

Sending someone off, she says, is untying the thread that connected you to them. You have to get used to it, or the pain hits you all at once, all of a sudden — and only at the very end does Rosmontis ask the name of an enemy she never even knew.
Frost Nova… what a pretty name. Hello, Frost Nova. …Farewell.
Before departure, Amiya broadcasts to the whole ship. Not a speech to raise morale, but a broadcast that lays out the reality as it is.
If they can't stop a war that will be recorded as one the Infected started, everything Rhodes Island has built will vanish. The odds are slim, she says, but defeat is not something they can accept.

blocks.cap: "We will face madness with reason, and we will never give up." — Amiya's first declaration of war as leader.

blocks.x: Amiya is no longer the girl who only received orders. She says she can now hold back the storm herself, even without the Doctor and Kal'tsit — that she's finally standing shoulder to shoulder with them.
The operation is precise. A sandstorm erases their tracks, and Dobermann lures the enemy's eyes with an aircraft. Five teams have to climb up the underside of the moving Core.
When the sandstorm grows weak enough to risk exposure, Nearl steps in. The Radiant Knight bends the very angle of sunlight to her will, lighting up the whole underside of the Core.
If it will lessen this world's hardship… even the sun's own light will follow my will, if only for a moment.

blocks.cap: Amid the sandstorm, they climb onto the moving city with only a single wire to hold onto. Kal'tsit wraps an arm around the Doctor's waist and pulls them up.

blocks.h: 6. The Girl of a Forgotten Land

blocks.x: Having entered the Core's underground, Rhodes Island witnesses Rosmontis in battle — a chilling display of power, a massive sword floating in midair, shattering enemies to pieces.
This fourteen-year-old girl chose to fight on her own. So that no one else has to live a life like hers, she says, the world is calling her name.

Rosmontis holds Arts that gather up the emotions of the dead in their stead, but she's forgotten where those emotions actually come from. Tears flowing without any memory behind them is what hurts her.
If you don't look back… you forget. I will never forget. Not anyone's death.

Kal'tsit calmly explains — Rosmontis has almost never directly killed an enemy; that seemingly brutal power is actually used to disarm foes. It wasn't the lab that broke her, she says — she broke the lab, and so Rhodes Island helped her find the discipline to govern that power together.
Amiya stays by her side. Whatever emotion suddenly bursts out is entirely Rosmontis's own, she says — no one should take it from her — and holds her hand.
In the Core's warehouse district, Ursus Infected are barely clinging to life — citizens of Chernobog massacred and abandoned by Reunion.

One Reunion member caring for them turns out to be an old comrade Rhodes Island had planted undercover — an Operator who slipped into Reunion, carrying on the will of a Scout who died protecting them.

blocks.cap: A reunion of comrades in the middle of enemy territory. "It's been a while, Miss Amiya, Dr. Kal'tsit. And you, Doctor."

blocks.x: The Ursus Infected woman Tatiana curses him as Reunion. Kal'tsit holds Mon3tr's blade to her comrade's throat and asks Tatiana: has this man truly protected you?
Frightened, Tatiana finally admits — this one man has never done wrong by them. That answer alone proves her comrade's innocence — a cold, Kal'tsit-style psychological maneuver.
Through him, Rhodes Island approaches the truth. The Infected of this region are still breathing only because the old warrior **Patriot** and his guerrillas protect them.

The guerrillas are different from Reunion's rioters. They call the Infected by name instead of number, and stand as a shield for those forced into labor. Her comrade decides to stay in Reunion, believing in that conviction.
But Kal'tsit sees an even more terrifying picture — Talulah is engineering the Infected to kill each other, trying to bring down Reunion from within, in the most dishonorable way possible.
The central district holds an altar for the Sarkaz's ancient rite of 'cannibalism' — an old ritual, no longer reproducible in the modern age, that tears life apart regardless of friend or foe.

Rhodes Island fights back with an information war. Rosmontis expands the ritual to locate every enemy messenger, Raidian scrambles the signals with her Arts, and Amiya holds Rosmontis's crumbling mind together.

As Rosmontis's mind stumbles and loses its way mid-search, Kal'tsit and Amiya place their hands on her face and bring her back to the deck. We're here beside you, they say — you just have to move forward.
The guerrillas, dependent on primitive megaphones and security directives, fall into chaos as their communication network is torn apart entirely. Rhodes Island's decapitation strike tightens the net.
At the end of that net, he finally appears — a mountain of a giant, deflecting Rosmontis's sword with his bare hand.
Patriot. True name Bulldrokarsty. A Wendigo who has lived through long ages — one who left Kazdel to become a warrior of Ursus, and is now the shield of the Infected.
He and Kal'tsit have met before. Remembering Kal'tsit as a 'Knight' of the Kazdel Queen, he raises his sword against Rhodes Island, blaming them for Frost Nova's death.
But it isn't revenge. His daughter's death was a path his daughter chose herself, he says — he believes only in the outcome of war.
War does not ask right from wrong. Step onto the battlefield, and it decides itself who dies, and who was wrong.

As the fight continues, Amiya's Arts reflect his whole life — his wife Helen; his son Grobezijl, who himself requested to be put down while protesting the anti-Infected laws; and his daughter Yelena, raised amid the ice.

In the ice, young Yelena once placed a hand on her weeping father's shoulder and said — good person or bad, whatever I have to become, just let me stay with my family forever. That daughter was Frost Nova.

He has betrayed his convictions three times in his life. He left Kazdel, he opposed Ursus, and now he is about to betray Reunion. Each time he lost something precious, and each time he walked back toward fate all over again.
And yet he doesn't stop walking. If he stops, he says, the deaths of those who died beside him become meaningless — so, his body in ruins, he keeps pressing forward.

The reason he tried to keep Amiya alive was his dead daughter's words coming back to him — that they have no right to kill the Talulah of today, that whatever the future holds, today's me is me and my father is my father, Yelena had said.
Finally stopped by his accumulated wounds, he hands Kal'tsit the key to stop the Core, accepting his defeat as the victor's spoils.
Only I… need to die. I've found a place worth dying in.
But at that moment, an old Sarkaz prophecy comes alive before his eyes — a vision of a 'Demon King' wearing a black crown, enslaving every race.

And that Demon King's face is none other than Amiya's. "The last Wendigo dies by the Demon King's hand." Realizing he is, as prophesied, dying at the Demon King's hand, he reaches out toward Amiya with his last strength.

blocks.cap: The Caprinae girl doesn't flinch. She doesn't step back, doesn't scream — she simply looks the giant straight in the eyes.

blocks.x: But the hand never reaches the girl. Death stops the Wendigo's long march before it can — a march that had never once retreated a single step.
Amiya tries to show him one last illusion, but he refuses. However miserable it is, he says, it's the result of how he's lived and struggled, and the ending he rightly deserves.
Young Demon King, everything comes down to action. If this is fate… I refuse to believe it.
Patriot's death spreads through the whole Core. His guerrillas turn their blades not toward Rhodes Island, who killed him, but toward Talulah, who drove him to this death.
For Patriot!
The shieldbearers leave Amiya a warning too — we don't believe in the prophecy, we believe in the Captain; and if you ever become a tyrant like Talulah, we will never forgive you.
Talulah's scheme backfires on her instead. Now that the Infected and the citizens have had time to get to know each other, the facade called Reunion begins to crumble from within.
W, buried under a heap of rubble, is ironically rescued by Kal'tsit's own hand and heads for the command center, declaring that she's coming back to Talulah.
And Ch'en finally reaches the Core, holding onto nothing but an old promise — to stop her sister.

blocks.cap: "Talulah… a promise is a promise." Wounded all over, Ch'en takes her last steps toward her sister.

blocks.x: Old wounds surface too. Twenty years ago, Duke Kashchey held out the shackled infant Talulah before Wei Yenwu to test him. What Wei Yenwu cut down that day with his dragon-slaying sword Sui was none other than his own brother, Edward.
A sword meant to slay dragons, in the end used to slay his own kin. That tragedy was the beginning of the sisters Ch'en and Talulah.

blocks.h: Epilogue — Snow, and Someone Already Prepared

blocks.x: As Frost Nova's remains are processed, the cold they release blankets Chernobog's sky in heavy snow. The guerrillas speak of it — is Yelena coming to greet the Captain?
Mephisto hums, clumsily, the lullaby Frost Nova used to sing. Frustrated at how badly he sings it, only an empty song lingers in the falling snow.
And in the darkness, another scheme takes shape. The Sarkaz Regent and the Confessor, chasing clues about the 'Demon King', bring out 'her' — already prepared and waiting.

blocks.cap: "I'm back." — Where one tyrant has fallen, other hands in the darkness quietly begin to move.

blocks.x: A dead city rolled toward Lungmen, and inside it people betrayed and were betrayed, losing each other along the way. An old warrior who fought fate for two hundred years turned his back on it for the first time at the very end, entrusting the future to a young Demon King, while one woman walked into the snow-covered Core holding onto nothing but a promise to stop her sister. The key to stop the Core now rests in Rhodes Island's hands — but what this cradle of death will bring forth, no one yet knows.

main_8.tagline: 버려진 소녀가 검은 뱀의 저주를 짊어진 채, 얼어붙은 대륙의 모든 감염자에게 '너는 혼자가 아니'라 말하려다 스스로를 태워버리는 이야기. 눈은 녹지 않고 봄은 오지 않지만 — 미워하지 말고, 다만 분노하라.
